title: Frog Farming Has Returned in Midnight
author: POSTED 20 HR 7 MIN AGO BY POPULARTOPULAR
content:
As players continue exploring the Early Access period of Midnight, some have stumbled upon a new hyper-spawn, just outside of Silvermoon City. Originally discovered in Mists of Pandaria, and then in MoP Remix and Legion Remix, Frog Farming is back. However, this time it's not valuable loot that the frogs are dropping, but an abundance of profession materials.

Midnight Skinning Profession Overview
Midnight Skinning Profession Overview
 Midnight Leatherworking Leveling Guide
Midnight Leatherworking Leveling Guide


Frog Farming, originally practiced in Mists of Pandaria, became even more infamous in MoP Remix when players began grinding hyperspawn groups of Frogs on the Timeless Isle for Threads and Bronze. Now, even though there's no player power associated with this farm, there is money on the line!

Players can find these hyperspawning Gloombelly Toad just south of Silvermoon City, at Stillwhisper Pond. When Skinned, these Toads can produce Void-Tempered Leather , Mote of Primal Energy, Mote of Light, and Mote of Wild Magic.

Tip: Click map to zoom
Hide pins
Eversong Woods


Joining a group will allow players to easily farm these frogs and share Skinning targets among the group.



Currently, Void-Tempered Leather can be sold on the Auction House for 36-74 gold, depending on quality. As more players continue to farm frogs, the price of these Skins may decrease as the supply increases.

title: Midnight Dungeon Guides Now Live: Everything Coming for Launch & Season 1
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY POPULARTOPULAR
content:
Just in time for Midnight's Early Access release, Wowhead's Dungeon guides are now live! Midnight will release with 4 level-up dungeons, 4 max-level dungeons, and 4 additional dungeons from previous expansions will be introduced in Season 1.

Midnight Dungeon Locations, Guides, & Details
Midnight Dungeon Locations, Guides, & Details
LEVEL-UP DUNGEONS
On your way to level 90, you'll be introduced to the 4 new level-up dungeons - Windrunner Spire in Eversong Woods, Murder Row within Silvermoon City, a reimagined version of Magister's Terrace on the Isle of Quel'Danas, and Den of Nalorakk in Zul'Aman.

Windrunner Spire Dungeon Overview
Windrunner Spire Dungeon Overview
 Murder Row Dungeon Overview
Murder Row Dungeon Overview

Magisters' Terrace Dungeon Overview
Magisters' Terrace Dungeon Overview
 Den of Nalorakk Dungeon Overview
Den of Nalorakk Dungeon Overview


MAX-LEVEL DUNGEONS
Upon reaching the new max level, players will have four new dungeons to tackle, including The Blinding Vale in Harandar, Maisara Caverns in Zul'Aman, and Nexus Point Xenas & Voidscar Arena within the Voidstorm.

The Blinding Vale Dungeon Overview
The Blinding Vale Dungeon Overview
 Maisara Caverns Dungeon Overview
Maisara Caverns Dungeon Overview

Nexus Point Xenas Dungeon Overview
Nexus Point Xenas Dungeon Overview
 Voidscar Arena Dungeon Overview
Voidscar Arena Dungeon Overview


SEASON 1 HEROIC & MYTHIC DUNGEONS
In addition to four of the new dungeons - Magister's Terrace, Maisara Caverns, Nexus Point Xenas, and Windrunner Spire, Season 1 will also feature four dungeons from previous expansions. These include Algeth'ar Academy from Dragonflight, Pit of Saron from Wrath of the Lich King, Seat of the Triumvirate from Legion, and Skyreach from Warlords of Draenor.

title: Everything Unlocked During Midnight Early Access and March 3rd Weekly Reset
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY IMX3
content:
Midnight Early Access begins February 26th, with the full expansion launching March 2nd. Here's all of the content available to players with Midnight Early Access.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
Blizzard
View Original
In Midnight, players experience the next chapter in the Worldsoul Saga as things become more dire for the world of Azeroth. Xal’atath, the Harbinger, poses our greatest threat as she unleashes chaos in her wake with the ensuing invasion striking into the heart of the blood elf homelands in Eversong Woods.
Midnight Content Releases
With the release of Early Access, players who have pre-purchased Midnight will have the opportunity to begin their new adventures through the expansion. Here’s what players can expect as they take their first steps.
What’s Available in Early Access:
Level up to 90
All Reimagined and New Zones: Eversong Woods, Zul’Aman, Harandar, and Voidstorm.
World Quests
Renown Tracks
Housing and Endeavors
Delves tiers 1-7
Dungeons: Normal and Heroic difficulty
Prey: Normal Difficulty
Please note that dungeon unlocks will work a little differently in Midnight. As you level through the storyline on your first character through each of the zones, you’ll encounter quests that tie into the ongoing narrative. Alternatively, all dungeons also become available at level 90 whether these quests have been completed or not. Finding and undertaking the associated quests for each dungeon allows players the opportunity to get into dungeons a little sooner should they choose.
Dungeon Quest Level
Windrunner Spire Windrunner Spire: Haunting Melodies Or reach level 81 on any character.
Murder Row Murder Row: One Fel Swoop Or reach level 83 on any character.
Maisara Caverns Maisara Caverns: Maisara Hungers Or reach level 85 on any character.
The Blinding Vale The Blinding Vale: Lightbloom Roots Or reach level 88 on any character
Den of Nalorakk Den of Nalorakk: Unforgiven Or reach level 88 on any character.
Magisters’ Terrace Magisters’ Terrace: Homecoming Or reach level 90 on any character.
Nexus-Point Xenas Nexus-Point Xenas: Eclipse Or reach level 90 on any character.
Voidscar Arena Voidscar Arena: Breaking the Triad Or reach level 90 on any character.
There are select features that will not be available until after the weekly reset during the week of the full launch of World of Warcraft: Midnight worldwide .
What’s Available with the Reset the Week of March 3:
Haranir Allied Race: Players will need to have completed the Harandar zone level-up campaign to gain access to this new playable character.
Profession Weekly Quests
Arcantina: Visitor Quests
Prey: Hard Difficulty
Mythic 0 Dungeons: Preseason (includes all eight Midnight dungeons which will be on a weekly lockout until March 24 then will go to a daily lockout.)
Midnight Season 1 will begin on March 17.
Arriving over the glittering elven kingdom of Quel'thalas, the looming Voidstorm threatens to extinguish all light and cover the world in darkness. Players will level up to 90 and explore four new and reimagined zones to uncover the heart of Xal'atath's machinations while seeking out new allies among the light and shadows alike.

Face Xal’atath, the Harbinger and the Void in Midnight with Early Access launching on February 26 at 3:00 pm PST. Midnight goes live worldwide March 2 at 3:00 pm PST.
MAIN CAMPAIGN AND LEVELING
During Midnight Early Access, players will be able to level their characters up to the new level max of 90 while progressing through the next chapter of the Worldsoul Saga. The starting quest for the Midnight campaign is fittingly named Midnight, and can be started in Stormwind City, Orgrimmar, or Dornogol.
NEW ZONES IN MIDNIGHT EARLY ACCESS
Players first begin their Midnight journey in the Isle of Quel'Danas, fighting back against Xal'atath's invasion on the Sunwell. After completing this introduction, it’s up to you how your story progresses beyond with four new zones to explore, including:
Eversong Woods, including an updated Silvermoon City
Zul'Aman
Harandar
The Voidstorm
Each of these new zones will include unique rares and interesting treasures to seek out. For a more detailed look into each zone's rares and treasures, check out our guides below.

Eversong Woods Rares & Treasures
Eversong Woods Rares & Treasures
Zul'Aman Rares & Treasures
Zul'Aman Rares & Treasures
Harandar Rares & Treasures
Harandar Rares & Treasures
Voidstorm Rares & Treasures
Voidstorm Rares & Treasures
APEX TALENTS
While leveling to 90, players will gain access to Apex Talents, a new set of talent nodes added to the bottom of each specialization talent tree. These talents are designed to modify and enhance signature abilities for each specialization in new and exciting ways. Apex Talents consist of three nodes, with each node unlocking at Levels 81, 84, and 90.



For a deeper look into Apex Talents, including a list of each specialization's talents, check out our guide below.

Apex Talents Overview
Apex Talents Overview
DUNGEONS IN MIDNIGHT EARLY ACCESS
As you explore and level throughout Midnight's new zones, all of the new Midnight dungeons will be available on Normal and Heroic difficulty during Early Access.

Level-up Dungeons Max-level Dungeons
Den of Nalorakk Maisara Caverns
Magisters' Terrace Nexus-Point Xenas
Murder Row The Blinding Vale
Windrunner Spire Voidscar Arena


After Early Access, these dungeons will open on Mythic 0 difficulty. Seasonal Mythic 0 and Mythic+ dungeons release later with Midnight Season 1.

For more information on each dungeon in Midnight, check out our overview guide below.

Midnight Dungeons Overview
Midnight Dungeons Overview
DELVES IN MIDNIGHT EARLY ACCESS
Midnight Early Access brings 8 new Delves and a brand-new Delve companion, Valeera Sanguinar on difficulties 1-7. Some Delves will not be available until after the full launch of the expansion. Bountiful Delves will not become available until Midnight Season 1. Here is a list of the Delves available with Early Access:
Twilight Crypts
Atal'Aman
The Grudge Pit
The Gulf of Memory
Collegiate Calamity
The Shadow Enclave
Shadowguard Point
Sunkiller Sanctum


Delves in Midnight
Delves in Midnight
RENOWN FACTIONS
Midnight Early Access allows players to begin earning reputation with the four new Renown factions:
Silvermoon Court
Amani Tribe
Hara'ti
The Singularity
Reputation gains will be limited mostly to campaign quests, zone rares, treasures, and World Quests during Early Access, with other ways to earn Reputation coming with the full expansion launch.



Silvermoon Court Renown Guide
Silvermoon Court Renown Guide
Amani Tribe Renown Guide
Amani Tribe Renown Guide
Hara'ti Renown Guide
Hara'ti Renown Guide
The Singularity Renown Guide
The Singularity Renown Guide
PREY
Prey is the new outdoor activity in Midnight, where players complete unique objectives to hunt special targets throughout each zone.

In our interview with Blizzard, Prey was confirmed to be available in Early Access, but only Normal Hunts can be completed. Normal Hunts will drop Adventurer-track gear upon completion. Heroic hunts will unlock with the full release.

Prey Guide
Prey Guide
MIDNIGHT PROFESSIONS
Professions will be available to level with the Early Access. This, however, does not include weekly quests and patron orders used to level Profession specializations which are unlocked on Midnight release. Early Access is a great opportunity to gather new materials and make some quick gold at the start of the expansion.

For a closer look at each Profession available in Midnight, check out our overview guide below.

Midnight Professions Overview
Midnight Professions Overview
HARANIR ALLIED RACE
The Haranir will not be available to unlock during Early Access, the first steps towards unlocking this new race can be completed. To unlock the Haranir, players must complete the Harandar zone questline, which can be completed retroactively during Early Access as part of the main campaign.



Haranir Allied Race Guide
Haranir Allied Race Guide

title: 45 New Mounts to Collect with the Launch of Midnight
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY IMX3
content:
The Midnight expansion adds a whopping 45 new mounts to collect on expansion launch. Here is a short preview of these new mounts and how you can earn them.
MIDNIGHT MOUNT HIGHLIGHTS
Midnight brings new mounts from a wide array of different activities and achievements. One of the more popular mounts comes from the new raid, March on Quel'Danas. The last boss of the raid, Midnight Falls, drops the Ashes of Belo'ren on Mythic difficulty. This mount features the first boss of this new raid, Belo'ren, Child of Al'ar. Since this is one of the first raids of the expansion, there will be plenty of opportunities later to farm this beautiful mount.

Another mount highlight comes from Prey, the new outdoor activity. With Prey, players will hunt special targets throughout each new zone of Midnight. With three difficulties of Prey, players can earn the Preyseeker's Nightmare mount reward for defeating every Prey target on the Nightmare difficulty. Similar to Delves, Prey has its own Journey track, with mount rewards available at Ranks 5 and 10, the Preyseeker's Hubris and Preyseeker's Wrath respectively.

These are just four of the many new mounts available in Midnight, with more coming later in future patch updates. Some mounts also don't have sources yet, even though they've been datamined; we're sure we'll find more as Midnight's full release approaches.

If you're looking for models, our Midnight Mount Guide has everything you'd want to see what the mounts will look like as you ride through Quel'danas and beyond!

title: Midnight Guide Compendium: Every Guide You’ll Need for Launch
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY WHISPYR

title: Extra Reagents and High-Value Targets: What's New with Midnight Skinning?
author: POSTED 1 HR 20 MIN AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Skinning! If you're looking to learn everything about Skinning in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Skinning Guide, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Skinning in Midnight
Skinning in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT SKINNING?
Traditionally, Skinning has always been a bit of an oddity among the Professions, and Midnight Skinning isn't an exception. The base mechanics of Skinning haven't changed, but Midnight sees the addition of one mechanic, making the profession at least slightly more interactive.

Skinners will want to enable a new tracking feature, Find High-Value Beasts. This will allow you to see special enemies - indicated by both a minimap icon as well as sparkle particles around them - that will provide significantly more reagents than normal when skinned.


 

Other than this new feature, Skinning is as basic as it has been in previous expansions. Lures are still available for those who wish to summon high-value mobs to skin, and Diffusers are available for those who are looking to farm Motes alongside leather and scales, but otherwise, the gameplay loop should be the same!

title: Midnight Hotfixes for March 1st - Valeera Level Cap Implemented
author: POSTED 15 HR 28 MIN AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has posted hotfixes for today which includes adding a level cap for Valeera.
Blizzard
View Original
March 1, 2026

Classes
Hunter
Fixed an issue causing Stomp and Wild Thrash to damage enemies through walls.
Mage
Fixed an issue causing Comet Storm to pull enemies through walls without damaging them.
Fixed an issue causing Shatter to pull enemies through walls.
Fixed an issue causing Duality Pyroblasts to pull enemies through walls.
Delves
Valeera’s maximum level is 45 until Midnight Season 1 starts.
Items
Contained Stormarion Defender and Vivacious Chloroceros should now appear in the mount collection.
Player versus Player
Voidscar Arena
Fixed an issue where the arena event could fail to progress.
Prey
Players who were unable to access the Hunt Table in Astalor’s Sanctum after completing a Prey quest should once again be able to accept new hunts, as long as they haven’t completed the 4 weekly hunt maximum.
Developers’ notes: There is currently a display bug for those who were affected by the previous issue that may show they have 1 more hunt available than they actually do. This will be fixed with weekly restarts.
Quests
Creatures in the Midnight introduction that takes you to the Isle of Quel’Danas now have lower health and deal less damage.
Players should be able to continue handing out fliers to folks during "Mobilize! Enlist! Recruit!” if they leave and then come back to their task.
Fixed an issue where Rage-Riddled Drifter would take a long time to respawn for “Overcoming the Unknown” world quest.
User Interface and Accessibility
To ensure that macro usage aligns with our overall goals for addons, there have been some minor changes made to macro capabilities:
Macros are prevented from setting a target marker on more than 3 units within a very short time.
And the following restrictions only apply while the player is in an active encounter:
Macros are no longer allowed to send chat messages to chat channels that are not exclusive to the group (custom chat channels, guild, communities, etc).
Macros are still allowed to send chat in group-exclusive channels (/raid, /party, /rw, etc), but are prevented from sending multiple messages to those channels within a very short time. In addition, all members of the group must be inside the instance or macro chat will not be allowed in these channels.
Macros are still allowed to send chat in whispers but the whisper target must also be in the instance. Macros are also prevented from sending multiple whispers within a very short time.

title: Macro Changes Now Live to Prevent Workarounds for Addon Restrictions
author: POSTED 17 HR 42 MIN AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced that some macro changes are live to prevent workarounds for addon restrictions.

One of the more common workarounds that has been suggested was to press a macro when you get a mechanic. This macro would send a whisper or a message through a chat channel to someone outside the raid, where addons are unrestricted. Thus, a computational addon could easily find the result, and whisper or message players back. This seems to be addressing that problem.
Blizzard
View Original
To ensure that macro usage aligns with our overall goals for addons, there have been some minor changes made to macro capabilities that were recently deployed to the live client:
Macros are prevented from setting a target marker on more than 3 units within a very short time.
And the following restrictions only apply while the player is in an active encounter:
Macros are no longer allowed to send chat messages to chat channels that are not exclusive to the group (custom chat channels, guild, communities, etc).
Macros are still allowed to send chat in group-exclusive channels (/raid, /party, /rw, etc), but are prevented from sending multiple messages to those channels within a very short time. In addition, all members of the group must be inside the instance or macro chat will not be allowed in these channels.
Macros are still allowed to send chat in whispers but the whisper target must also be in the instance. Macros are also prevented from sending multiple whispers within a very short time.

title: Obtain the Sunwing Hatchling Pet from Rookery Cache Treasure in Silvermoon
author: POSTED 18 HR 21 MIN AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
Having trouble looting the Rookery Cache treasure in Silvermoon City? Follow these quick tips to obtain the Sunwing Hatchling dragonhawk pet to journey with you in Midnight.

Midnight Pet Collection Guide
Midnight Pet Collection Guide
Eversong Woods Rares & Treasures
Eversong Woods Rares & Treasures
ROOKERY CACHE TREASURE & SUNWING HATCHLING
With Early Access out, there's plenty to do and collect in the new expansion and you don't have to travel far to find a new pet. Nestled on the west side of Silvermoon city you can find the Rookery, the location of the Rookery Cache Key. Obtaining the new pet can appear bugged initially, but let's walkthrough exactly how to nab the iconic red dragonhawk.

The Rookery Cache is located in Silvermoon City (/way #2393 24.38 69.58) on a floating platform just above the sprawling metropolis. Before you can open the caches, you'll need to obtain the key. First, buy Tasty Meat from Farstrider Aerieminder located right next to the cache.

  

Do not eat the meat. Simply, place the Tasty Meat on a yellow transparent platter located next to Mischevious Chick and lootable ring of Rookery Cache Key should appear. If you don't see the platter initially, then either relog or briefly flyaway to have the platter reappear.

  

With the key in hand, simply loot the Rookery Cache and obtain the ruby dragonhawk Rookery Cache Key pet.


This treasure is just one part of the Treasures of Eversong Woods achievement, so be sure to check out our Eversong Woods Rares & Treasures Guide for all the rest and obtain your very own Sootpaw.

title: Buff Mechanic Out, Stat Bonuses In: What's New with Midnight Blacksmithing?
author: POSTED 22 HR 19 MIN AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Blacksmithing! If you're looking to learn everything about Blacksmithing in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Blacksmithing Guide, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Alchemy in Midnight
Alchemy in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT BLACKSMITHING?
As a whole, Midnight Blacksmithing hasn't changed much: players familiar with the Blacksmith's crafts won't see much difference in how they approach Midnight's version of the profession. What has changed, though, is that things are a little easier to manage now that the Everburning Ignition buff system has been removed.



While the buffing system in The War Within was a novel idea, it added an annoying step for crafters, who felt that they had to micromanage buffs every time they wanted to make goods. Now, players will instead put Knowledge Points exactly where they want them for buffs, with dedicated wheels for major boosts to Ingenuity, Multicraft, and Resourcefulness for all crafts, not just when using a buff item like The War Within's version.

Blacksmiths will also be busier than normal in Midnight, since Profession Tools are now getting a third upgrade with Epic versions. Blacksmithing provides the most Profession tools and accessories of all the Crafting options, so adding another tier of rarity will make sure that those specialized in creating Profession tools will be rolling in gold.

title: First Darkmoon Faire of Midnight - Profession Skill, Knowledge Points & XP Buff
author: POSTED 1 DAY AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
The Darkmoon Faire has returned for the first time in Midnight. Complete profession quests for Midnight skill and knowledge points or level quicker with the 10% XP buff.

Darkmoon Faire Guide
Darkmoon Faire Guide
Professions in Midnight
Professions in Midnight
 Table of Contents
Profession Quests
Profession Shopping List
Reputation & Experience Buff
DARKMOON FAIRE PROFESSION QUESTS
Looking to hit a new skill breakpoint for your Midnight professions or maybe just earn some extra knowledge points? With the return of the Darkmoon Faire, profession quests have returned as well.

Completing any profession quest rewards:
+2 Midnight Profession Skill
+3 Midnight Profession Knowledge
Similar to the War Within with Artisan's Acuity, the Midnight profession quests do not award Artisan's Moxie.


For those just beginning their Midnight profession journey, you'll likely want to leave these profession quest turn-ins for the later skill levels as those tend to be more difficult skill ups. Just don't forget to pick these up before the Darkmoon Faire ends.

Professions in Midnight
Professions in Midnight
Profession Shopping List
Gather up the materials to complete the quests ahead of time as some profession quests require certain items that cannot be purchased on Darkmoon Island:
Alchemy: 5x Moonberry Juice, 5x Fizzy Faire Drink
Inscription: 5x Light Parchment
Leatherworking: 10x Shiny Bauble, 5x Coarse Thread, 5x Blue Dye
Tailoring: 1x Coarse Thread, 1x Red Dye, 1x Blue Dye
Cooking: 5x Simple Flour
DARKMOON FAIRE REPUTATION & XP BUFF
Portals to the Darkmoon Faire are open once again allowing players to participate in mini-games, earn Darkmoon Tickets, and obtain the WHEE! buff. Level to 90 a bit faster with the additional 10% experience buff.

To obtain the buff, simply purchase a Darkmoon Top Hat or ride the Darkmoon roller coaster or carousel.

As previously reported, Blizzard hotfixed in a debuff that prevents the Darkmoon Faire buff from applying to Midnight Renown factions.

There's no benefit to hoarding your Midnight reputation quest turn-ins for the Darkmoon Faire.


 Although the reputation buff won't work for Midnight Renown factions, you can still check out our full suite of Midnight Renown guides below.

title: Fast Travel to Silvermoon City with the Personal Key to the Arcantina Toy
author: POSTED 1 DAY AGO BY IMX3
content:
While the Arcantina is a cozy tavern for adventurers to gather, it also offers a fast way back to Silvermoon City with the Personal Key to the Arcantina toy!

The Arcantina Quest Hub Guide
The Arcantina Quest Hub Guide
PERSONAL KEY TO THE ARCANTINA
The Personal Key to the Arcantina toy can be earned by completing Arator's Journey, featured in the Midnight campaign. To start this questline, head to the Scouting Map in Silvermoon City and select Arator's Journey. This will start you out with the quest, Arator.

Completion of this questline can be monitored through the Arator's Journey achievement. Once you have completed this questline, you will unlock the Arcantina hub and be rewarded with the Personal Key toy.


Arator's Journey starts by selecting the quest on the Scouting Map in Silvermoon City.


The Personal Key has a one-minute and thirty-second cooldown and directly teleports the player to Arcantina. Once there, players can leave the Arcantina, and they will be back in Silvermoon City's portal room! This is a quick and convenient way back to the main hub city for the expansion, not to mention being on such a short cooldown.

title: Abundance Event Bugged in Early Access and Does Not Work - Do Not Waste Your Shards
author: POSTED 1 DAY AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
The Abundance World Event is bugged and does not allow players to contribute anything to the shrines. Don't waste your Dundun shards!

World and Zone Events Guide
World and Zone Events Guide
Abundance Bugged
Abundance is the Amani Renown World Event. After unlocking Abundance, players can do a tutorial in Zul'Aman and after that players can head to a specific location in any of the 4 zones to talk with Dundun, the Loa of Abundance to participate in the activity.

After the Tutorial, the Abundance activity no longer lets you pick up any treasures while inside.


It's impossible at this moment to contribute anything to Abundance or complete the weekly Abundant Offerings quest at this time.
Abundant Harvest and Shards of Dundun
One of the Abundance caves will be special, an Abundant Harvest. If you head to this cave, you can trade in a weekly capped currency of Shard of Dundun to receive Unalloyed Abundance at the end of the Harvest based on how you did. Because you can't actually do anything inside the cave right now, spending your shards is essentially wasted. Unalloyed Abundance is used for cosmetics which can be seen in our Abudance Guide.

title: Recycling Simplifies the Process: What's New with Midnight Engineering?
author: POSTED 1 DAY AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Engineering! If you're looking to learn everything about Engineering in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Engineering Guide, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Engineering in Midnight
Engineering in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT ENGINEERING?
Engineering is historically one of the most complex Professions, so thankfully, Midnight Engineering is easing that burden slightly for players. The War Within introduced the Dig Through Scrap mechanic, where players had to farm Pile of Rusted Scrap then process it for a chance to learn new recipes and discover prototypes for new recipes. Now, this system has been revamped to Recycling, significantly streamlining the process and allowing for easier progress through Engineering leveling.



Recycling works similarly to Scrapping in that you're taking a resource and destroying it to potentially learn something new, but the difference now is that Recycling takes existing Midnight crafting reagents, such as ingots and tailoring bolts, to use instead of a soulbound farmed material. You'll also gain Engineering materials such as Aetherlume and Evercore while you're at it, so you're always getting something for the craft.

This will make Engineering significantly more accessible for alts, and will allow players to powerlevel the craft - as long as they have the gold for the materials to search through. This is a great change that makes an interesting existing process more accessible to players.

title: Purchase Trolli Products to Claim Gummi the Glow Wyrm
author: POSTED 2 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
A new Trolli promotion has appeared, allowing players to obtain a new Gummi the Glow Wyrm pet in WoW and several Diablo 4 cosmetics weapons when purchasing participating products.


Gummi the Glow Wyrm has an abundance of natural predators, as it is known for their rubbery texture and sweet flavors.



We have only limited info on the Diablo 4 cosmetics, but we can see a preview of them in the promotional screenshot.

Similar to the previous promotion featuring Gummi, obtaining these new in-game cosmetics also requires purchasing a participating product and uploading the receipt, which also requires registering with a valid U.S. address and phone number.



Additionally, Trolli is also hosting a separate giveaway for free Xbox swag with no purchase necessary, though it is also open only to U.S. residents.
OPEN TO US ONLY?
Although the promotion is exclusive to residents of the United States, the codes generated for the WoW rewards can be redeemed by any Battle.net account, with the players being able to redeem it even on accounts that only play on EU realms.

So as long as you have a friend in the U.S. to make the purchase, and forward the code, this means that non-US players can still obtain the new cosmetics.

title: (Delve Rotated) Quick Way to Level Alts in Midnight Early Access
author: POSTED 2 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Players have found a quick way to level alts in the Midnight Early Access by using Tier 1 Delves.

 Midnight Delves Guide


The delve story has rotated and is no longer worth doing as of 2/28
Players have started to hit level 90, and between leveling your Renown to get Champion pieces of gear, players are starting to level alts. Leveling in Midnight is a little odd this time around, as just doing the main campaign doesn't get you nearly enough experience to hit level 90. No matter what, you're doing some form of side content while leveling, whether that be side quests, Delves, dungeons, Prey or World Quests.

As questing seems to give a lower amount of experience this time around, players have looked towards simply farming mob experience in Delves -- it's not 10,000 boars, but a lot of creatures in the Shadowguard Point Delve in Voidstorm.

Tip: Click map to zoom
Hide pins
Voidstorm


Players have been doing mass pulls in the Shadowguard Point Delve on Tier 1 difficulty today, which seems to result in about 20-30 minute levels. You do 1 mass pull at the start of the Delve, quickly leave and do it all over again. It's very repetitive but efficient. It's unknown what future stories of delves hold after tomorrow's reset at this time and if this farm is as good.



The reason players have been farming this Delve is because its relatively open, and has great mob density. Tier 1 Delves also don't decrease experience gains by much, while significantly reducing incoming damage and enemy health. This method is easiest to do as a Tank, but survivable DPS can do it as well.

title: The PoddyC Interviewed Blizzard to Talk About Midnight
author: POSTED 2 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
The PoddyC interviewed Blizzard, with Associate Game Director Paul Kubit and Lead Encounter Designer Dylan Barker discussing the evolution of Mythic+ affixes, multiple Season 1 raids, addons, Labyrinths, and more coming in Midnight!

NEW RAID LAYOUT
There were a few reasons for splitting the raid up into multiple tiers. Part of it was the story, with the Voidspire leading into new campaign quests taking us into March on Quel'Danas.
The other reason was just novelty. The past several raid tiers have been large epic areas full of bosses, but it doesn't always have to be that way. Changing their approach and splitting the raids up also affects player psychology, and so they're interested to see how players engage with them.
Introducing a new mini-raid during the midpoint of both the first and second seasons also gives players the opportunity to face another challenge after their initial progress may be over, though they're not intended to be insanely difficult on the level of Uu'nat in Crucible of Storms.
MYTHIC+ PHILOSOPHY
When they started, Blizzard was just building dungeons based on the principles of what makes a good dungeon, which Mythic+ got layered over top of. Over time, both the casual and competitive Mythic+ community has influenced that design, largely for the better.
The level of detail perceived by players tends to get higher with each expansion, which puts a burden on the developers to ensure that new mechanics are managed well. It's a really big game though, so ensuring that detail work is done properly is very challenging, but one that they're up for.
For the most part, good dungeon design does translate well into Mythic+, and it's philosophically about shifting places where they want challenge to exist on trash or on bosses into big moments that are visually clear in the world, as opposed to something that's just a math scaler which eventually blows players out.
They're also looking for opportunities to have variety between dungeons, while still maintaining a parity between time spent in each. All eight dungeons in a given season ought to feel like things players want to engage in. 30 minutes feels about the right amount of time to ask players to stay switched on.
The new low level Mythic+ affix is designed to be sort of a "racing line" for newer players, especially those still dipping their toes into tanking. It isn't really meant to be optimal, so much as express the general direction and cadence of the dungeon.
They certainly want a lot of agency for players in Mythic+, but they don't always enjoy the idea of players only ever engaging in a third of the content, so they'll sometimes put their finger on the scale by making some creatures more valuable or others actually mandatory to engage with.
The design space of affixes right now feels very clean. There's a lot of gradual experimentation, and they can't be super specific at the moment, but they like that the current style of affixes feels very easy to read for players just getting into Mythic+.
Before being formally titled, Labyrinths were internally referred to as giga-delves, and they fill some of the same design space as Mega-dungeons have in the past.
Mega-dungeons are awesome ways to explore big iconic areas like Tazavesh or Karazhan, and although Labyrinths fulfill some of the same feeling of a big explorable space to get lost in, it doesn't mean mega-dungeons won't come back in the future.
It's a little early to get into Labyrinth rewards, but they're closely related to Delves, so you will see cosmetics and some power rewards coming from them.
MYTHIC+ REWARDS
Rewards changed around a few times during the beta, initially offering increased rewards at higher Mythic+ levels, but they ultimately decided to revert them, as Mythic+ has not fundamentally changed enough to warrant a new rewards structure.
Crests were also iterated on, and they have been simplified greatly since Dragonflight and The War Within. At one point they experimented with incrementing costs, but it ultimately felt too mathy, so it was streamlined back to a consistent cost.
Players have already gotten some hints as to how they'll be able to make their gear more powerful in Midnight, using the Void Forge. They'll work on it alongside a fun dominaar character called Decimus, who has a complicated relationship with Xal'atath. More details will come around the 12.0.5 timeframe.
Generally speaking, they want to keep giving players goals throughout the season. It should be possible to get best-in-slot gear, but there should continue to be small sub-goals along the way. Turbo Boost has been a pretty good way to ensure that players can continue to optimize throughout the season.
ADDONS CHANGING ENCOUNTER DESIGN
When players know the fight timings, they know what assignments will be coming, and they can put markers on the ground, a lot of decisions can be made without players having input, minimizing the role of the shot caller or raid leader. That automation space in particular is what they've tried to target with the addon changes.
There is a mechanic during the final boss on March of Quel'Danas that they don't think they could have made as satisfying with computational addons solving it for players. The way they're able to make the mechanic easier for lower difficulties and harder for higher ones wouldn't have worked because of the number of elements and way they're adjusted in each difficulty would still boil down to the addon telling each players where to go, taking out any sense of coordination.
Now that coordination is playing a higher role, it means they can tune back other elements of the fight during that mechanic, to help focus on it. There are still going to be a lot of other opportunities to shoot bullets and put swirls under your feet to create that frenetic feeling, but it shouldn't need it to happen as constantly anymore.
The first boss of Voidspire also has a similarity to this in requiring the raid group to play tic-tac-toe. It may be the easiest game in the world, but doing it in the middle of a raid still requires players to coordinate with each other. Players still have a variety of tools to do this, from voice comms, to world markers, or the ping system, but it still requires some kind of coordination rather than just being solved with an addon.
While some players will undoubtedly find creative solutions, it's a big priority and commitment for the developers to ensure that these mechanics are still approachable for players who don't use addons.
Class mechanics are another area where they want to ensure the game is approachable even for players who don't use addons. The cooldown manager is part of that, but so is just streamlining certain class mechanics that players can't reasonably keep track of without complex solutions.
Assessing future addon changes will be done on a case-by-case basis. The ultimate goal is to create a level playing field, ensuring one player doesn't have an express advantage over another due to a particular combat-solving addon.
NOT YOUR GRANDMA'S WORLD QUESTS
The Legion Remix Heroic world tier worked out really well and it was interesting to see how players navigated its challenges. One of the great things about a temporary self-contained system like Remix is that they can try new things to see how they might work out in the main game.
Prey sort of scratches those itches, and its also just a cool concept entrenched in the fantasy of Midnight, working alongside the Blood Elves to hunt bad guys down while they're simultaneously hunting you. Especially as you get into Nightmare difficulty, the gameplay gets much more chaotic - it's not your grandparents world quests!
BIGGEST SURPRISES OF MIDNIGHT
The last boss of Quel'Danas will have some moments, even aside from the previously described mechanics, that are very visually satisfying and the pinnacle of their recent VFX work, even coming on the heels of Dimensius.
The final encounter in the Voidspire is also a huge spectacle with really fun mechanics. It's also narratively loadbearing, with a bunch of max level campaign predicated on the events that happen in and after that fight. They can't wait for players to see what comes next!
[/ul]

title: Get 4 Pieces of Champion Gear by Leveling Renown
author: POSTED 2 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
You can obtain 4 pieces of Champion gear by leveling your Renown in Midnight!

Silvermoon Court Renown Guide
Silvermoon Court Renown Guide
Amani Tribe Renown Guide
Amani Tribe Renown Guide
Hara'ti Renown Guide
Hara'ti Renown Guide
The Singularity Renown Guide
The Singularity Renown Guide
Table of Contents
Champion Gear from Renown
How to Increase Renown in Midnight
Champion Gear from Renown
Midnight comes with 4 new Renown tracks for players to level, one for each zone. At a certain point on each Renown track, the faction will offer you a quest awarding you with a piece of Champion 1/6 gear when completed. If you're interested in getting a slight advantage when heading into Season 1, make sure you're getting your Renown reputations up to hit this benchmark.

Each faction awards the piece of gear at a different Renown rank, and has a different slot.
Faction Renown Needed Slot
Silvermoon Court Rank 9 Helm
Amani Tribe Rank 9 Neck
Hara'ti Rank 8 Belt
The Singularity Rank 7 Trinket
Some of these quests aren't available in Early Access, but should become available with the full release on March 3rd (March 2nd for NA):
Guarded Treasures is available to be completed.
Legendary Prosperity cannot be completed.
Courting Success is not available.
An Abundance of Wealth is not available.
How to Increase Renown in Midnight
Reputation for each Renown is Warband-wide meaning you can only do it once per account. There are quite a few ways to earn reputation:
The main campaign for each zone awards a lot of reputation.
Side Quests
World Quests
Weekly Quests
Rares
Peaks Exploration Achievements
Lore Objects
Contracts
Normally, players would save up the majority of these things for when the Darkmoon Faire rolls around. However, through datamining, we've found a spell that seems to disable bonus reputation gains for the Midnight factions.

title: *FIXED* Bind-on-Equip Items Bugged in Midnight Early Access
author: POSTED 2 DAYS AGO BY IMX3
content:
Blizzard has announced a bug regarding Midnight Bind-on-Equip items having an incorrect level requirement. Midnight BoE items will have a level requirement of 90 once this fix has been deployed.

This issue has been fixed. Midnight BoE items now display proper level requirements and proper item level values.
MIDNIGHT BOES BUGGED
Blizzard
View Original
Today we’ve discovered an unfortunate bug in Midnight content where Bind-on-Equip items that are meant to have a minimum level requirement of 90 have been dropping with a minimum of level 80.

We’re working on a fix, and when we deploy that fix, BoE items that should have had a level 90 requirement will operate as intended.

Thank you!

title: Warcraft Short Story: "The Quiet at the End of Us" - Orweyna & Amarakk
author: POSTED 2 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
In a new Blizzard Short Story "The Quiet at the End of Us by " by Catherynne M. Valente, learn about the friendship between Orweyna and Amarakk. Check out the narrated video or illustrated PDF.

Hara'ti Renown Guide
Hara'ti Renown Guide
Haranir Allied Race Guide
Haranir Allied Race Guide

Blizzard
View Original
As Orweyna grew from seed to sapling in the wilds of Harandar, Amarakk was always at her side—the steady voice of reason who helped ground her wilder impulses. Amarakk became both her confidante and co-conspirator until the day Orweyna learned how to open a rootway to a place long forbidden to the Haranir, Azeroth. With one visit to the surface world, the bond between these two children became so much more.

Discover a vibrant tale of friendship, loss, and the lengths friends will travel to save each other ahead of World of Warcraft: Midnight. Join Amarakk for a special fireside telling of our newest short story—The Quiet at the End of Us.
Download the PDF
Written by Catherynne M. Valente | Illustrated by Surfside3D | Narrated by Ike Amadi as Amarakk
Adventure is calling you home! Experience the next chapter in the Worldsoul Saga as things become more dire for the world of Azeroth when World of Warcraft®: Midnight™ launches worldwide. Keep an eye on worldofwarcraft.blizzard.com/news for more details.

title: Cosmetic Crafting: What's New with Midnight Tailoring?
author: POSTED 2 DAYS AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Tailoring! If you're looking to learn everything about Tailoring in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Tailoring Guide, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Tailoring in Midnight
Tailoring in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT TAILORING?
Fundamentally, Midnight Tailoring hasn't changed much with the new expansion. In Midnight, Tailors are still the only source of naturally dropping cloth, and all of the standard crafts that have come to be expected since the Dragonflight rework are present and accounted for. New to Midnight, however, is a fun new category for Tailors to work with: cosmetic items!

  
  

Normally, any gear that Tailors have crafted has been functional for combat. These six capes, however, are purely cosmetic - and this sets a new standard for the profession that we're excited to see explored in further content updates. So far, only cloaks are available, but they should nicely match armor sets easily available in the first season of Midnight. Hopefully, we see more fun cosmetic outfits and ensembles in Tailoring's future!

title: Darkmoon Faire Reputation Buff No Longer Applies to Midnight Renown
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
With the launch of Early Access, we've learned that the Darkmoon Faire reputation buff will no longer apply to the new Midnight faction renown!

Several hotfixes have been pushed over the past few days, including a new spell linked to the Darkmoon Top Hat and WHEE! buff gained from the carousel ride. Ominously named [DNT] I Don't Feel So Well..., it seems someone had a little too much fun at the faire, because it applies a reputation penalty to specific renown added in the Midnight expansion - canceling out the usual gain.


The WHEE! buff effects on the left are now offset by Midnight renown-specific debuffs from [DNT] I Don't Feel So Well....


The list of affected reputations includes everything coming in Midnight, as well as the third season of The War Within Delves, which is still technically ongoing. Because it only affects these specific renowns, this means the Darkmoon Faire buff still has a valid use in speeding up gains from previous expansions, it just won't help you get ahead on any of the new ones. And because of the way this additional buff was set up, and when it was hotfixed in, existing Darkmoon Faire buffs from the last Darkmoon Faire likely don't have this penalty applied and still provide a reputation bonus.
Hara'ti - Harandar
Amani Tribe - Zul'Aman
The Singularity - Voidstorm
Silvermoon Court - Silvermoon City
Magisters - Silvermoon sub-faction
Farstriders - Silvermoon sub-faction
Blood Knights - Silvermoon sub-faction
Shades of the Row - Silvermoon sub-faction
Prey: Season 1
Delves: Season 1
Delves: Season 3
Valeera Sanguinar
Assuming this works how we expect it to, there would no longer be any reason to horde your quests or reputation turn-ins for the Darkmoon Faire this Sunday... in fact it might even be detrimental! Because of the way these spell effects are multiplied, it seems that you may actually lose a small amount of reputation compared to not having gotten the buff in the first place. It should only be a 1% difference, but it would be a humorous and relatively harmless backfire for players trying to gain an edge on the new expansion renown grind.

title: Official Midnight Content Update Patch Notes for Early Access
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
Blizzard has released the official patch notes for the Midnight content update as part of Early Access.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
Midnight Roadmap
Midnight Roadmap
Blizzard
View Original
Arriving over the glittering elven kingdom of Quel'thalas, the looming Voidstorm threatens to extinguish all light and cover the world in darkness. Players will level up to 90 and explore four new and reimagined zones to uncover the heart of Xal'atath's machinations while seeking out new allies among the light and shadows alike.
TABLE OF CONTENTS
ADVENTURE THROUGH A NEW CAMPAIGN CHOOSE YOUR PATH
EXPLORE NEW AND REIMAGINED ZONES UNLOCK THE HARANIR, A NEW ALLIED RACE
UNLEASH YOUR SKILLS IN MIDNIGHT'S NEW DELVES BATTLE THROUGH THREE NEW RAIDS
CHALLENGE EIGHT NEW DUNGEONS FOUR NEW WORLD BOSSES
SYSTEMS AND ACTIVITIES COME HOME TO AZEROTH WITH HOUSING
DEMON HUNTER DEVOURER SPECIALIZATION AND VOID ELF DEMON HUNTERS USER INTERFACE UPDATES
TRANSMOGRIFICATION UPDATES PLAYER VS. PLAYER TRAINING GROUNDS
UDPATED LEVELING EXPERIENCE CLASS COMBAT DESIGN UPDATES
DUNGEONS ITEMS
PROFESSIONS USER INTERFACE AND ACCESSIBILITY
ADVENTURE THROUGH A NEW CAMPAIGN
Experience the next chapter in the Worldsoul Saga as things become more dire for the world of Azeroth. Xal’atath, the Harbinger, poses our greatest threat as she unleashes chaos in her wake with the ensuing invasion striking into the heart of the blood elf homelands in Eversong Woods.
CHOOSE YOUR PATH
You’ll first begin your journey in Eversong Woods, but it’s up to you how your story progresses beyond this first zone. There are three available paths you can tackle in any order: brave the ancient troll kingdom of Zul'Aman, investigate the gnarled hidden roots of Harandar, or accompany Arator on his journey to better understand his relationships with both his family and the Light. [LEARN MORE]
EXPLORE NEW AND REIMAGINED ZONES
SILVERMOON CITY
With the press of the Void’s incursion and assault on the Sunwell, the forces of the Horde and Alliance fortify within the streets of Silvermoon City. Serving as a hub for both factions, approximately one-third of the city is designated exclusively for the Horde, while the remaining two-thirds are accessible to both faction members as they work together to push back Xal’atath’s forces. The city has been rebuilt from the ground up and the path of destruction the Scourge left behind is mostly a memory. [LEARN MORE]
EVERSONG WOODS
Immerse yourself in a revitalized Eversong Woods. With the Voidstorm looming overhead and renewed with the power of the Sunwell, Eversong Woods is familiar, but changed—filled with wonder and beauty, and a sense that something bigger is unfolding. [LEARN MORE]
ZUL'AMAN
The volatile neighbors to the blood elves, the Amani trolls, reside in their capital of Zul’Aman. They are a proud people who have fought hard for their land and overcome significant hardships and challenges. Comprised of old forests and tall mountains with periodic rainfall, you’ll take a journey deeper into their culture and history as you explore all this zone has to offer. [LEARN MORE]
HARANDAR
The home of the haranir is a fungal jungle where the roots of all the world trees converge. The haranir travel through the rootways to move around the world, observing Azeroth in secret but never intervening openly in the world’s affairs. In the zone lies the Rift of Aln, a primordial wound where the barrier between dreams and reality grow thin. [LEARN MORE]
VOIDSTORM
This hostile land is host to Void creatures of all varieties. Everything here is hungry and ready to devour and consume weaker creatures for their power. Xal’atath and her Devouring Host rule over this world of cosmic predation. Chief among her followers are the domanaar: some of the most powerful void creatures the world has ever known. Safety here is not guaranteed and you’ll need to work with your allies to carve your way through this vast and all-devouring wasteland. [LEARN MORE]
UNLOCK THE HARANIR, A NEW ALLIED RACE
Earn the trust of the Haranir through a series of quests to unlock this new allied race. Haranir will be able to join either the Horde or the Alliance and play as a Druid, Hunter, Mage, Monk, Priest, Rogue, Shaman, Warlock, or Warrior.

An unfamiliar people from a forgotten time, Haranir will arrive with a variety of customizations available to them. They can be adorned with bioluminescent paint on their face and bodies in a variety of patterns that can be selected when customizing your character. [LEARN MORE]
UNLEASH YOUR SKILLS IN MIDNIGHT'S NEW DELVES
Take on new challenges through ten new Delves and one new seasonal Nemesis Delve alongside a new NPC companion—legendary blood elf rogue Valeera Sanguinar. You’ll experience more of the story of Midnight interwoven through new quests and even experience the great outdoors—taking the adventure outside for the first time. [LEARN MORE]
BATTLE THROUGH THREE NEW RAIDS
Season 1 of Midnight begins the week of March 17, and with it, players will take their first steps into the Voidspire and Dreamrift raids with the March on Quel’Danas raid released shortly after. Face off against nine bosses split across three raids over the course of Midnight Season 1. [LEARN MORE]
CHALLENGE EIGHT NEW DUNGEONS
Experience eight new dungeons, each with unique environments and storylines as you level up through the campaign. Face new challenges, defeat new enemies, and claim your rewards! [LEARN MORE]
Windrunner Spire
Magister’s Terrace
Murder Row
Den of Nalorakk
Maisara Caverns
Blinding Vale
Nexus-Point Xenas
Voidscar Arena
We’ll also have a mix of four new and four fan-favorite dungeons as part of Midnight Season 1. All eight of these new dungeons will also be available in Mythic0 during the pre-season period at launch.
Season 1 Dungeons:
Magisters' Terrace (Midnight)
Maisara Caverns (Midnight)
Nexus-Point Xenas (Midnight)
Windrunner Spire (Midnight)
Algeth'ar Academy (Dragonflight)
Pit of Saron (Wrath of the Lich King)
Seat of the Triumvirate (Legion)
Skyreach (Warlords of Draenor)
FOUR NEW WORLD BOSSES
Face four new world bosses that rotate weekly when Midnight Season 1 begins: Lu'ashal, Cragpine, Thorm'belan, and Predaxas.
SYSTEMS AND ACTIVITIES
POWER UP WITH NEW APEX TALENTS
In Midnight, players can expand their specializations further with new Apex Talents, taking you deeper into the fantasy of your class. Available beginning at level 81, you’ll unlock the first of these and provide your character with an extra boost of power as you take on new challenges.

Apex Talents are intended to be the pinnacle of specialization trees going a little beyond the average talent. These new talents are focused on the core fantasy of each specialization and hone into core gameplay mechanics in new, powerful ways. [LEARN MORE]
PULL UP A CHAIR IN THE ARCANTINA
A refuge to the road-weary and those seeking a respite from the trials and travails of the life of an adventurer, the Arcantina serves as a crossway for all kinds. It is a magical tavern at the crossroads of nowhere and everywhere catering to travelers and heroes from all walks of life. It’s here you’ll meet up with some familiar faces for stories and quests sending you back out into Azeroth once more. [LEARN MORE]
GO ON THE HUNT WITH THE PREY SYSTEM
Track powerful targets throughout the zones of Midnight in three difficulties: Normal, Hard, and Nightmare. Successfully find and defeat your quarry to earn valuable new rewards as you progress through the system. [LEARN MORE]
TAKE ARMS IN A NEW BATTLEGROUND: SLAYER'S RISE
Set in the Voidstorm, Slayer's Rise is a 40 vs 40 epic battleground will pit the Horde and Alliance against each other in a race for control and conquest. Players with War Mode turned on will also be able to engage in World PvP in the outdoor area.
COME HOME TO AZEROTH WITH HOUSING
Build, decorate, and personalize your home in Azeroth with the new Housing feature. Join a Neighborhood, take on Endeavors, and make yourself at home. [LEARN MORE]
DEMON HUNTER DEVOURER SPECIALIZATION AND VOID ELF DEMON HUNTERS
Introducing the Devourer Demon Hunter, a third specialization that utilizes the power of the Void instead of Fel, like other Demon Hunters. This glaive-wielding, soul-harvesting, and planet crushing spellcaster operates from mid-range with the full suite of mobility you'd expect from Demon Hunters. Use specialization and Hero Talents to augment your playstyle, enabling you to dive into melee and unleash devastating combo attacks.

With the introduction of a new specialization, Demon Hunters also gain a new Hero Talent tree—Annihilator. This new tree is shared between Devourer and Vengeance Demon Hunters. Devourer Demon Hunters will also gain access to the Scarred Hero Talent tree (formerly called Fel Scarred).

Players will also gain access to the new Void Elf Demon Hunter combination through a short quest line as a part of the Midnight pre-expansion content update, along with the Devourer specialization. [LEARN MORE]
USER INTERFACE UPDATES
The User Interface is introducing powerful new features, improved customization options, cleaner raid frames, expanding display-scaling controls and more. You’ll be able to configure how information and damage meters appear within the base UI and customize them to fit your personal play style without the need to download additional add-ons.Learn more about some of the changes and updates to Nameplates, Damage Meters, Boss Alerts, and more in our previously published article. [LEARN MORE]
TRANSMOGRIFICATION UPDATES
Take your transmog game further with the ability to unlock and save full outfit sets across characters for any occasion. The updated transmog system also provides an additional opportunity for adding more customization and self-expression. You’ll be able to unlock and save dozens of outfits. Unlocking outfit slots will cost gold, but once they are unlocked, switching between saved outfits is free.

Each outfit can be added to your action bar, allowing you to manually update your look any time you want without having to visit a transmog vendor. Whether you’re fighting in a dungeon, exploring a new area, or taking a rest in your house, you’ll be able to dress for the moment. [LEARN MORE]
PLAYER VS. PLAYER TRAINING GROUNDS
For players new to Player vs. Player (PvP) combat, you can learn the PvP ropes in the Training Grounds. Players will join a battle against smart game-controlled opponents in Arathi Basin, Silvershard Mines, and Battle for Gilneas. You’ll become more comfortable with how these battlegrounds work and how classes can work within them, gaining confidence and opening the door to more PvP experiences in the game. While you can expect more victories than losses, the game-controlled opponents will not be pushovers. Skill and coordination will be required to achieve those victories.
UDPATED LEVELING EXPERIENCE
New Player Experience Updates
Bring your friends and family into Azeroth with an updated New Player Experience in Exile’s Reach. They’ll learn more about World of Warcraft while honing their character’s skills and diving into the story of Dragonflight along the way.
Returning Player Experience
Return to Azeroth with confidence as you undertake a new focused experience. You’ll get your feet wet without the weight of trying to discern where you left off last and get back into action as you reacquaint yourself with your character. [LEARN MORE]
CLASS COMBAT DESIGN UPDATES
Every class in World of Warcraft receives major combat design updates. This means new rotations, stronger class identities, and fresh talent choices.

Across all of the classes, talent trees have been rebuilt with fewer mandatory filler points and more paths that support specific playstyles, letting you tailor your character to the gameplay that you enjoy the most.
DUNGEONS
MYTHIC+
Learn the basics of the Mythic+ keystone and affix system in the Adventure Guide Dungeon Journal, found under the Current Season tab.
New Mythic+ Affix: Lindormi’s Guidance – Available in keystone levels 2-5. While Lindormi’s Guidance is active, certain non-boss enemies will be afflicted with Temporal Sands, visually highlighting them and reducing their health and damage done by 5%. Defeating these marked enemies will fulfill 100% of the Enemy Forces count, providing a basic route through the dungeon. Additionally, the affix also prevents player deaths from reducing the timer.
Xal’atath’s Bargain affixes have moved one level deeper into the system, from Mythic+ level 4 to Mythic+ level 5.
Dungeon titles are available for players who complete the highest keystone level dungeons each week.
The Mage Tower challenges are now a maximum level only experience.
Developers' notes: To help preserve the intended difficulty and pacing of the encounters, the Mage Tower is now a maximum level only experience. In addition, each encounter has been rebalanced to provide a more consistent experience across all specializations.
ITEMS
Wild pets in Midnight zones do not require battling to capture.
Class set and raid weapon appearances will now change after an item has been fully upgraded to 6/6 on its upgrade track.
Acquiring a class set 4-piece bonus now unlocks a character-specific Feat of Strength that will enable Catalyst charges to drop from raid bosses, Mythic+, Bountiful Delves, and Rated PvP on that character for the remainder of the season.
Developers' notes: The decision to spend Catalyst charges can be difficult early in a season as players anticipate looting more powerful armor in the same slot in the near future. For example, catalyzing a Veteran or Champion helm may feel like a mistake knowing that a Hero helm could be around the corner in your next Mythic+ dungeon. If a player decides to spend that charge and then receives that Hero helm, it may have to sit in their bags for up to two weeks before it can effectively be equipped. This change should empower players to chase their seasonal class set bonuses early on without reservation with the knowledge that additional Catalyst charges will likely be farmable for them just several weeks into the season. In addition, armor pieces with desirable secondary stats in non-set slots such as belts and boots should feel like more viable options earlier in the season.
Claim any unclaimed Great Vault rewards before Midnight Season 1 begins as they will be no longer be available.
PROFESSIONS
New Midnight recipes have been added.
Lowered the number of qualities for stackable reagents and consumables to 2 (was 3).
Developers' notes: One goal of having qualities is so that players who invest time and effort into their professions can produce more valuable goods. We believe that, as far as reagents and consumables are concerned, this goal is better accomplished by having 2 qualities, normal quality and high quality, with the added benefit of simplifying the system a little.
Secondary stats from Professions Tools and Accessories will now apply to previous expansions, but will not apply to a higher expansion Profession.
Example: Sunforged Needle Set is a Tailoring accessory that can be crafted in Midnight. When equipped, the secondary stat bonus will apply to Dragonflight, The War Within, and Midnight Tailoring, but will not apply to The Last Titan Tailoring.
Fractured Sparks no longer need to be combined into whole Sparks before they can be used in crafting. Whole Sparks drop instead of Fractured Sparks and Spark costs have doubled.
USER INTERFACE AND ACCESSIBILITY
Engage with World of Warcraft content in a meaningful way thanks to a brand-new feature tab in their Adventure Guide—Journeys—with a significant update to how you track your in-game progress.
Added a setting that allows the player to automatically reset data when entering an instance.
To view all content update notes, click here.

For World of Warcraft customer support, please visit our Support Site or our Customer Support discussion forum. If you’ve found a bug, please let us know about it in our Bug Report Forum.

Back to Top

Midnight Overview

title: Midnight Early Access is Now Available on Live Servers
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Midnight is now live for early access players to travel to Silvermoon, participate in the campaign against the Void, make their way to level 90, and more!
Table of Contents
Midnight Campaign
Specialized Apex Talents
New Delves and Dungeons
Pull Up a Chair in the Arcantina
Go on the Hunt for Prey and Rewards
MAIN STORY CAMPAIGN QUESTS AND LEVELING
The Midnight Campaign is open to all early access players, starting with a desperate defense of the Sunwell on the Isle of Quel'Danas, before introducing players to Silvermoon City and new adventures around the northeastern lands. During this time, players will be able to level their character up to the new cap of 90, unlocking new class, hero, and specialization talent points.

The expansion campaign begins with the aptly named Midnight quest.
SPECIALIZED APEX TALENTS
As players make their way to level 90, they'll unlock three new class talents, three new hero talents, and four new specialization talents. At the same time, players will also gain access to a new four-point node of Apex Talents at the bottom of the tree, intended to be the pinnacle of specialization trees and focused on the core fantasy of each specialization's gameplay mechanics. While the four new specialization talent points are not required to go into these Apex talents, it is generally recommended.

 Apex Talents Overview Guide


NEW DELVES AND DUNGEONS
During the leveling experience, players will meet up with Valeera Sanguinar, who will join us in exploring 10 brand new delves! While most Delves should be available during early access, our new nemesis in Torment's Rise will not be available until Season 1 begins, The Darkway delve opens on March 24th, and Parhelion Plaza delve becomes available on March 31st.



Likewise, there are 8 new dungeons coming in Midnight, which players will be able to play on Normal difficulty during Early Access.

Level-up Dungeons Max-level Dungeons
Den of Nalorakk Maisara Caverns
Magister's Terrace Nexus Point Xenas
Murder Row The Blinding Vale
Windrunner Spire Voidscar Arena
PULL UP A CHAIR IN THE ARCANTINA
Another new questing hub unlocked by completing Arator's Journey during the Midnight leveling campaign, this warm and cozy tavern features familiar faces who send you back to old zones in search of items from their pasts, rewarding players with decor for both The Arcantina and their own homes. The Arcantina is an evergreen feature, meaning that we can look forward to more content and rewards being added to it in the future.
GO ON THE HUNT FOR PREY AND REWARDS
Upon reaching Silvermoon City, the Prey system of Hunts can also be unlocked, by completing the Prey: Astalor's Initiative introductory quest. A new type of world activity that rewards players hunting down dangerous foes, players are tasked with completing various objectives to determine their prey's location and defeat it, though they may also be ambushed in the process! Normal Hunts can be completed while leveling, but higher difficulties will unlock upon reaching max level.
