title: War Mode Experience Bonus May Have Been Nerfed to 15% for Leveling
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
On live and Beta servers, the War Mode experience bonus may have been nerfed to 15% when outside of towns.
Midnight Leveling starts soon and many players were going to use War Mode as it provides 25% to 40% experience bonus to quests and mob kills. With some last minute hotfixes, it appears that Blizzard has nerfed this experience bonus to a flat 15%. Oddly, the value doesn't appear to have changed on the War Mode button or when in town, but once you step out of town, the buff Enlisted appears to have changed to now stay 15%. This interaction appears to be the case on both live (with a character lower than level 80) and Beta (with a level 80 character), and across factions.

We've also confirmed that there's an unlabeled hotfix that changed the following values on something:
25 -> 15
25 -> 15
30 -> 15
30 -> 15
35 -> 15
35 -> 15
40 -> 15
40 -> 15
Considering there are two of each value, which would apply to one for each faction, and a range which matches the typical range of War Mode experience bonuses, this does seem to confirm the theory. If this is the case, most War Mode leveling routes were set to a 25% experience boost and will likely need to be rerouted.

title: Same Style, New Slots: What's New with Midnight Enchanting?
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Enchanting! If you're looking to learn everything about Enchanting in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Enchanting Guide, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Enchanting in Midnight
Enchanting in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT ENCHANTING?
One of the least changed Professions heading into the new expansion, Midnight Enchanting will be very familiar to players. Nothing material has been gained or lost with the traditional expansion changes, even keeping the Shatter Essence buff system that was introduced in The War Within (and then subsequently removed in Midnight for other Professions, like Blacksmithing). What is new, though, is that players will have different slots to enchant this time around, and some of them are less common!



In Midnight, players will lose the Bracers and Cloak Enchantments from The War Within, and instead will gain Helm and Shoulder enchantments. This is notable because traditionally, these two slots have not been available for Enchanters; instead, any time Helms or Shoulders have been augmented, it's been through other professions (such as Boon of the Builder from Engineering in Legion) or specific Reputation-or Quest-based boosts (like the Incandescent Essence). In fact, through all previous expansions, there has ever only been one Shoulder enchant for the profession, and no Helm enchants at all: Boon of the Scavenger in Legion.

This slot change-up could be exciting, because it may mean Blizzard has something in store for those underutilized (for Enchanting at least) slots - but we'll have to wait and see!

title: Honor Reset During Midnight Early Access - PvP Currency Schedule
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has confirmed that Honor will be reset during Early Access, and revealed the remainder of the PvP currency schedule.
Blizzard
View Original
Hello everyone.

With Early Access for Midnight beginning at 3:00 p.m. PST on February 26, we want to outline how PvP currencies and gear unlocks will work over the coming weeks.
During Early Access (February 26 - March 2)
Honor from The War Within is set to zero for all characters.
Honor gain is capped at 4000 per character on your account.
Honor gear is not purchasable from vendors.
Warband Honor transfer is disabled.
After March 2 at 3:00 p.m. PST
Conquest from The War Within is set to zero out for all characters.
The Honor cap is increased from 4000 to 15,000 per character on your account.
Honor gear becomes purchasable.
Conquest gear is not purchasable until Midnight Season 1 begins.
Warband Honor transfer remains disabled.
With the launch of Midnight Season 1 (March 17)
Warband Honor transfer is re-enabled.
Conquest currency is now obtainable for all characters.
Conquest has an initial cap of 1600, increasing by 800 every week.
Conquest gear is now purchasable.
Bloody Tokens from The War Within will zero out for all characters.
Bloody Tokens can now drop.
Bloody Tokens have an initial cap of 1600, increasing by 800 every week.
War Mode gear becomes purchasable.
With the changes above, we intend to provide everyone with a fair experience as Midnight gets underway, and a smooth experience obtaining, transferring, and using PvP currencies.

We look forward to seeing you in Slayer’s Rise, Training Grounds, Battlegrounds, and Arenas during Midnight!

title: Earn A New Pet and Two New Plushies from Support a Streamer and Twitch Drops
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Starting on February 26th, you can earn a new pet and two new plushies from the Support a Streamer and Twitch Drops program!
Blizzard
View Original
Earn the Fishmonger May pet by supporting your favorite World of Warcraft channels with the gift of 2 subscriptions on Twitch and collect the Cuddly Alliance Blue Grrgle and Cuddly Horde Red Grrgle plushies* Housing decor items by watching your favorite World of Warcraft Twitch channels when World of Warcraft: Midnight goes live.
Support a Streamer on Twitch and Earn the Fishmonger May Pet
World of Warcraft is partnering with Twitch to host Support a Streamer during the launch of World of Warcraft: Midnight! Twitch viewers will be rewarded for gifting a total of 2 subscriptions to across any eligible streamers who are Twitch Partners or Twitch Affiliates while the streamers are live streaming World of Warcraft with the channel category set to “World of Warcraft.” The campaign runs from. February 26 at 3:00 p.m. PST until March 26 at 3:00 p.m. PDT and is available in all regions where World of Warcraft and Twitch are accessible. Support your favorite, eligible streamers and you can receive the Fishmonger pet to join you in your adventures.
“You can never have too many fish according to May.”
Event Duration
Start Time: February 26, 3:00 p.m. PST
End Time: March 26, 3:00 p.m. PDT.
How to Participate
Log in to your Twitch account (if necessary, first create a Twitch account for free at Twitch.tv).
Visit any eligible Twitch channel streaming World of Warcraft.
While the channel is live streaming World of Warcraft, purchase and gift at least one subscription of any tier; once you’ve purchased and gifted a cumulative total of two subscriptions during the campaign period, you’ll earn the reward.
* Please note that the campaign will begin on February 26, 2026, at 3:00 p.m. (PST). Any subscriptions purchased before the above start time will NOT be counted towards the campaign. Any subscriptions purchased while the channel is not live streaming World of Warcraft with the category set to “World of Warcraft” also will NOT be counting towards the campaign.
Frequently Asked Questions
Q. Which regions are participating in the Support a Streamer reward campaigns?
A. Anywhere World of Warcraft & Twitch are available.

Q. How can I confirm which channels are eligible for the campaign?
A. Open to all Twitch Partners & Affiliates broadcasting World of Warcraft gameplay.

Q. How do I purchase a subscription on Twitch?
A. Users must first log into Twitch, using either a web browser or mobile device. Purchases may be made on either a web browser or mobile device. Detailed information on purchasing subscriptions can be found here, and information on purchasing gift subscriptions can be found here.

Q. How many subscriptions do I need to purchase to earn the Fishmonger May Pet for World of Warcraft? Which Tier(s) are eligible for the campaign?
A. To qualify for the reward, users must purchase and gift a cumulative total of two subscriptions of any tier. Each subscription must be purchased and gifted during the campaign period. Prime Subs are excluded.

Q. I purchased two subscriptions prior to the campaign. Am I eligible for the reward?

A. No. The reward can only be earned by purchasing two subscriptions to a stream that is streaming World of Warcraft and using the World of Warcraft category during the campaign period.

Q. What should I do if I have a problem completing subscription purchases on Twitch?
A. First, make sure that you are trying to complete a purchase in one of the channels of the World of Warcraft Category. Most commonly, refreshing the page, or trying a different payment method can help. For mobile purchases, please allow a moment to refresh. If you require further assistance, contact the Twitch Support team by reading common troubleshooting FAQs and common fixes here.

Q. Can I track how many eligible subscriptions I have done for the campaign?
A. While there is not a centralized location to track progress in the campaign, users may refer to their Twitch Payment History to review recent purchases of subscriptions to eligible channels. This should be used only as a reference for successful subscription purchases and does not guarantee the subscriptions qualified for the campaign.

Q. Where can I find my campaign rewards once I've earned them?
A. To receive your rewards, you must claim the drop on the channel you’re watching or in the Drops Inventory menu on Twitch.
Offers applicable with purchase of two gift subscriptions or recurring subscriptions of any tier on eligible channels live streaming ‘World of Warcraft’ category. Offer not available where World of Warcraft is not available for sale. Twitch reserves the right to modify or cancel this offer at any time. Offer is non-transferable, may not be resold, and may only be redeemed once per user. Offer will be invalid if you violate any of these terms. Subscriptions are subject to the Twitch Terms of Sale. Please visit help.twitch.tv for questions or support regarding receipt of reward.
Twitch Drop: Cuddly Alliance Blue Grrgle and Cuddly Horde Red Grrgle Plushies
Greetings, adventurers! With the release of the World of Warcraft®: Midnight™ players return to the elven kingdom of Quel'Thalas as Xal’atath’s invasion of Azeroth begins, and the Voidstorm threatens to cover the world in darkness. Watch any World of Warcraft stream on Twitch.tv from February 26, 3:00 p.m. PST, until March 26, 3:00 p.m. PDT to earn the Cuddly Alliance Blue Grrgle and Cuddly Horde Red Grrgle plushies* Housing decor items.
Eligibility
To participate, you must link your Twitch account by visiting https://account.battle.net/connections. If you have changed your password or made any changes to your Twitch or Battle.net account, you will have to relink your accounts. When you relink, there will be a 7-day cooldown before you can relink a new Twitch account. After you’ve claimed your rewards on Twitch, please make sure you’re logging into the Battle.net region you’d like to receive the drop(s) on, as the first region you log into will be where your items will be delivered. It can take up to 24 hours to receive your reward(s) in game after claiming. Read our Frequently Asked Questions section for more information.
How do I Earn Twitch Drops?
Start Time: February 26, 3:00 p.m. PST
End Time: March 26, 3:00 p.m. PDT

Watch 4 hours of World of Warcraft content while these Twitch Drops are active on any channel to earn the Cuddly Alliance Blue Grrgle and Cuddly Horde Red Grrgle plushies Housing decor items.
Frequently Asked Questions
Q. How do I link my battle.net and Twitch accounts?
A. Sign into your Battle.net account at https://account.battle.net/connections and click the Connect link for Twitch, then follow the instructions to sign in and link your Twitch account to Battle.net.

Q. What devices can I use to earn and claim drops?
A. You can earn and claim drops using your PC/Mac web browser or on the Twitch app for Android or iOS. Twitch apps on game consoles, smart TVs, and other TV apps do not support drops.

Q. Do I need to watch the same channel the entire time to earn my drops?
A. You can earn progress towards a drop on any eligible channel in the World of Warcraft category on Twitch. You can, for example, watch half an hour on one channel and then switch to another channel without losing your progress. You cannot earn progress faster by watching more than one channel at a time.

Q. Will I receive my drop rewards in-game automatically?
A. To receive your rewards, you must claim the drop on the channel you’re watching or in the Drops Inventory menu on Twitch.

Q. Do Twitch Drop rewards expire?
A. Earned Twitch Drop rewards will expire seven (7) days after they’ve been claimed if a Battle.net account has not been linked.

Q. As a streamer, how do I get Twitch Drops on my channel?
A. All you need to do is ensure your Twitch and Battle.net accounts are linked and that you’ve opted into Drops (see Drops Streamer Home).

Q. What regions are eligible for these Twitch Drops?
A. Participating regions include North America, Latin America, Europe, The Middle East, Africa, Japan, Taiwan, Southeast Asia, Australia, and New Zealand.
We’ll see you on Twitch!

Blizzard has the right to change, pause, or cancel these Twitch Drops and their terms if they cannot run as planned for any reason, including but not limited to technical issues or unexpected factors beyond Blizzard’s control.

*Not available in World of Warcraft Classic® games. Requires World of Warcraft® Subscription or Game Time.Use and placement of decor items is limited to the Housing feature, which requires access to World of Warcraft®: Midnight.

title: Incremental Power Increases for Raid Are Returning in Midnight: Blizzard Update
author: POSTED 3 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has provided Wowhead with a statement on the incremental power increases for raid, noting that they will be returning in Midnight.
In Wowhead's recent interview with Dylan Barker and Paul Kubit, no Raid Renown in the Season 1 raids was confirmed. When asked a follow up question if the scheduled damage buffs are being replaced, Wowhead got the response of "Not to my knowledge. If I'm wrong I'll have someone correct me."

Blizzard has now provided an updated statement to Wowhead on these incremental power increases, noting that they will be returning and providing the same incremental power increases as in the War Within, starting about one month after launch.
Blizzard
As I had shared previously about the stacking raid buff, we have an update to share. Previously with the removal of the raid renown track, we shared our goals in keeping an eye out and seeing if gear progression will support player progression. We will indeed have a stacking raid buff over time that will kick in via a quest completion about a month after the raid opens. This will provide the same incremental power increase players saw throughout The War Within.
The idea of no incremental raid buff was controversial. While some players enjoyed the idea of progressing naturally only using gear, other players enjoyed having breakpoints to help them overcome large spikes in difficulty. There's still some missing questions here such as how much the damage buff is going to be, and the maximum scaling value, but this is confirmation that it's returning!

title: Every New Mount That Can Be Earned From Faction Renown in Midnight
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY POPULARTOPULAR
content:
In preparation for the Midnight expansion release, we've created Guides for every new Faction Renown! Here, we'll preview every new Mount that each Faction offers. Make sure to check out our full Guides to learn more about the Player Power, Cosmetic, and System Rewards that can be earned from each new Renown.
SILVERMOON COURT
The Silvermoon Court is likely the first new faction that players will interact with while leveling in Midnight. Headquartered in Silvermoon City & Eversong Woods, this faction is further divided into four sub-factions that players can earn reputation with and obtain additional cosmetics, Profession recipes, & Battle Pets.

Silvermoon Court Renown Guide
Silvermoon Court Renown Guide


At Renown 17, players can purchase the Crimson Silvermoon Hawkstrider from the Silvermoon Court for 6000.



The pride of the Silvermoon Court, these hawkstriders are prized for their vibrant red plumage and purebred elegance.


At Renown 19, players will be able to purchase the Fiery Dragonhawk from the Silvermoon Court faction for 8000.



A dragonhawk whose wings shine as brightly as the leaves of its Eversong home.
AMANI TRIBE
Trolls of the Amani Tribe are descendants of Zul'jin, hailing from the zone of Zul'Aman. Players can earn Renown with this faction by completing World Quests and Special Assignments in Zul'Aman, defeating rares weekly, participating in the Abundance weekly quest, and opening Bountiful Caches in Delves.

Amani Tribe Renown Guide
Amani Tribe Renown Guide


At Renown 17, players can purchase the Amani Blessed Bear from the Amani Tribe for 6000.



Once symbols of conquest, these war-bears now stride again, reborn through Nalorakk's blessing and the renewed spirit of the Amani.


At Renown 19, players will be able to purchase the Amani Windcaller from the Amani Tribe faction for 8000.



Swift as the storm and sharp as lightning, these sacred eagles bear the mark of Akil'zon, carrying their riders on the winds of her blessing.
HARA'TI
Informally introduced in The War Within, the Hara'ti faction is comprised of Haranir, residing in the zone of Harandar.

Hara'ti Renown Guide
Hara'ti Renown Guide


At Renown 16, players can purchase the Fierce Grimlynx from the Hara'ti quartermaster for 6000.



This grimlynx has seen many battles against the creatures in the Rift of Aln.


At Renown 19, players will be able to purchase the Cerulean Sporeglider from the Hara'ti for 8000.



This sporeglider likes to gently nuzzle its riders.
THE SINGULARITY
Within the final zone of the Voidstorm, players will encounter a faction of Ren'dorei led by Magister Umbric, called The Singularity.

The Singularity Renown Guide
The Singularity Renown Guide


At Renown 17, players can purchase the Ravenous Shredclaw from The Singularity for 6000.



Fast and dependable, Shredclaws were one of the first creatures tamed in the Voidstorm. Their owners learned quickly not to leave them alone with their pets.


Finally, at Renown 19, players will be able to purchase the Voidbound Stormray from The Singularity for 8000.



With its razor-sharp teeth, the Voidbound Stormray look eager to bite you. It will, but only if you let it.
Which faction Renown will you focus on first? Are there any specific rewards you're after? Let us know in the comments below!

title: Prepare for Extra Hazards: What's New with Midnight Herbalism and Mining?
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Herbalism and Mining! If you're looking to learn everything about Herbalism and Mining in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Herbalism and Mining Guides, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Herbalism in Midnight
Herbalism in Midnight
 Mining in Midnight
Mining in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT HERBALSIM AND MINING?
Gathering Professions traditionally don't see many changes from expansion to expansion, and Midnight is no different. For Midnight Herbalism and Midnight Mining, though, the biggest difference will be the level of danger that players face during the process.

Two of the themed Herbalism and Mining nodes for Midnight, Voidbound and Wild, come with significant hazards to keep in mind as players farm. Especially as players are working towards mounted gathering, there will be few ways to avoid the effects of these nodes.

 

Voidbound nodes send a wide circle of void energy around the herb or ore, drastically slowing movement speed. If you don't escape using movement speed boosts or other abilities, it will throw you to the opposite side of the void ring, which can be dangerous if there are hostile mobs or a long drop nearby. This thankfully can be circumvented once players can gather while mounted, because flying straight up will get you out of range easily. This works for Druid flight form, too!

Gathering Wild nodes causes slightly more of an issue: these will summon four Vengeful Lasher mobs (for Herbalism) or two Rumbling Oreling mobs (for Mining) that will attack you. The mobs themselves aren't deadly (unless you immediately afk after gathering), but they are significantly annoying - this will greatly slow down the gathering process until players can fly away still mounted.

title: Max Quality Gems Easier to Make: What's New with Midnight Jewelcrafting?
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Jewelcrafting! If you're looking to learn everything about Jewelcrafting in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Jewelcrafting Guide, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Jewelcrafting in Midnight
Jewelcrafting in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT JEWELCRAFTING?
Jewelcrafting in The War Within saw some major changes, adding significant depth to the crafting system, and that depth continues in Midnight with Midnight Jewelcrafting. The specialization trees to hone in on specific cuts are still around, so prepare for picking your favorites and making a beeline towards them - but one big change should make things easier all around for high-value Eversong Diamond cuts.



Meta gems have historically been some of the most valuable gem cuts Jewelcrafters produce, both monetarily and stat budget and effect-wise. Because of this, they are incredibly difficult to craft at high quality, especially in the earlier parts of an expansion cycle. The War Within is a prime example of this: to craft a max quality Blasphemite gem, max skill, +40 skill from Profession equipment, and 170 Knowledge Points invested in the proper specializations were required.

Now, in Midnight, the new Meta gem will require only a 30 Knowledge Point investment, plus max skill and Profession equipment bonuses. This is because the skill level required is being reduced from 585 to 280, making the gems significantly more accessible - and much earlier than anticipated. They only take one node's worth of investment into the Glamorous Gems Specialization!

title: Measuring the Success of a Season: Interview with Raider.IO
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In an interview with Raider.IO, Associate Game Director Paul Kubit and Lead Encounter Designer Dylan Barker discussed the upcoming Midnight launch and how they'll measure the success of Season 1!

THREE RAIDS, ONE TIER
The first raid tier was split up into multiple instances because the fights and their story takes place across a couple different locations: Dreamrift is part of the story, but it's not part of the campaign, while the final fight of the Voidspire is a part of the main campaign which will take players into the March on Quel'Danas.
Of course, players just playing for the campaign will still be able to see and do everything on Story Mode too.
Balancing three raids isn't a whole lot different from how they'd normally balance just one, but the split is more approachable for casual groups trying to decide where to spend their time. The developers think it will make pugging more interesting, especially with the way Mythic lockouts work. It's nice to be able to change up the format.
Cutting back on addons has resulted on a greater focus on visual clarity for things players would have depended on addons to keep track of. They also just wanted more of the gameplay to happen in the world and between players. Chat and pings are more interesting forms of communication than having an addon tell players where to be at all times.
There's going to be a pursuit of optimization with Mythic raids that feels like the use of certain classes is required, even if its technically doable without. Normal and Heroic are tested to ensure they can be done without them.
DUNGEON DESIGN
The developers have seen feedback that folks enjoy being able to work towards unlocking each level of resilient keys, so no changes planned there. The focus in Season 1 is on lower difficulties, with the new guidance affix helping on-ramp newer players and especially tanks.
Developers have discovered that 30-minutes is a good baseline for Mythic+, since 40+ minutes is a long time to ask players to stay switched on for. They also want to ensure that dungeons have similar runtimes within the same season, since outliers tend to get skipped if they're too much longer or binged if they're noticeably shorter.
There aren't any plans to specifically remove RP from dungeons, though they have been known to trim it down for keystones. More important is making sure that the amount of RP time in a keystone dungeon is going to be the same each time, so that there's no variance.
Three dungeons are part of the level up campaign, as opposed to one in The War Within. They want it to feel like part of the expansion story, though past expansions often treated them separately due to being group content. The advent of follower mode has given pretty much everyone the ability to go in and complete a dungeon, so they can be used as more narrative spaces.
Part of the goal of tuning the Mythic+ rewards system is making sure that the highest tiers are still available and fun, without feeling mandatory because of their rewards. They experimented with changing this on the Beta, but felt that there hasn't been enough change in the expectations of Mythic+ to warrant increasing that "mandatory" point higher.
There are all sorts of metrics for determining if a season is successful, which largely boil down to measuring player sentiment: whether they're having a good time. Dungeon durations, success rates, and determining win rates for the new PvP training grounds feature. While well-matched PvP is meant to have around a 50% win-rate, training mode can afford to be higher, since it's solely against AI - it shouldn't be 99.9% because they don't want players to afk and still win, but they'll use metrics like that to assess the difficulty and success of the mode.
STORY OF AN ONGOING SAGA
One of the great things about Midnight is that it's part of a trilogy. It's chapter 2 of the World Soul Saga, created by the same team that's been building story over the last several years, and will carry forward into The Last Titan. There are some intentional story beats being setup that won't pay off later, as dominoes that were setup long ago finally fall.
The name of the expansion is Midnight, so there is some expectation that its a darker, middle chapter. They're already building off of some fairly dark story in The War Within, which culminated in Xal'atath summoning the Void Storm to kick off her assault, so it is starting off somewhat dark. This is World of Warcraft though, so they try to balance the drama with a bit of levity; features like the Arcantina or player housing neighborhoods may generate some of their own drama, but there aren't going to be subject to any void incursions.
No spoilers, but forging will play a role in the campaign of Midnight. Won't say whether there will be a new Quel'Serrar or Quel'Delar type weapon, but we will end up working with Blood Elven forges to piece togehter relics of the past in a meaningful story building way.
Players will earn a piece of decor by killing all the rares in Zul'Aman. You can already see it in a couple places in the zone, but it's huge: you could put it in your front yard and place your whole house on top of it.

title: Upcoming Arthas Figure Bundled with In-Game Golden Anniversary Backpieces - Pre-Order March 2nd

New Arthas Action Figure by McFarlane Toys

title: Midnight Pre-Patch Hotfixes: February 25, 2026

content:

Blizzard
View Original
Here you'll find a list of hotfixes that address various issues related to World of Warcraft: Midnight, Mists of Pandaria Classic, Season of Discovery, Burning Crusade Classic, WoW Classic Era, and Hardcore. Some of the hotfixes below take effect the moment they were implemented, while others may require scheduled realm restarts to go into effect. February 25, 2026

Classes
Death Knight

Devastation

title: Not Many Changes: What's New with Midnight Leatherworking?
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Leatherworking! If you're looking to learn everything about Leatherworking in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Leatherworking Guide, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Leatherworking in Midnight
Leatherworking in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT LEATHERWORKING?
Good news, Leatherworkers - out of all the Crafting Professions, Midnight Leatherworking received virtually no changes in Midnight! Overall, the Leatherworking Profession should feel extremely similar to its post-Dragonflight rework of previous expansions; if you played around with Leatherworking at all in the past four years, you've got a great idea of what you'll be getting into. There are no new mechanics, only a few adjusted Specialization nodes, and no surprises hidden in the recipe list this time around.



The biggest change that Leatherworkers should be aware of is that it will take a bit less effort this time around to completely learn everything that Leatherworking Specializations have to offer. In The War Within, it took a total of 800 Knowledge Points to complete all Leatherworking Specializations; in Midnight, however, players are looking at a total of 770 Knowledge Points to completely cap the Profession. It's a nice little bonus that means Leatherworkers will be maxed out a few weeks early.

title: Haranir Will Not Be Available During Midnight Early Access
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
One of the biggest new features coming in the Midnight expansion is the introduction of the Haranir, however players may have to wait just a little bit longer to play them, as developer comments suggest that the new allied race will not be available during Early Access.

 Haranir Allied Race Guide


Although the ancient guardians of the rootways were highlighted in Blizzard's recent Midnight Early Access and Worldwide Launch Schedule, the Midnight Developer Q&A last October revealed that Haranir would not be available during Early Access, with the intent to avoid giving early access players notable advantages.

Timestamped


Though it is completely possible that the developer intent has changed over the past six months, it has been pointed out that Earthen weren't available during Early Access for The War Within either, suggesting that this will remain the case.
UNLOCKING THE HARANIR IN WORLD OF WARCRAFT: MIDNIGHT
Unlike their dwarven counterparts, unlocking the Haranir requires completing only the main Harandar campaign quests, which will award the Allied Race: Haranir achievement and an Ivory Grimlynx usable by all characters.
The Frenzied March
Cull and Burn
Stem the Tides
Expeditious Retreat
A Last Resort
Survive
The Gift of Aln'hara
Light Finds a Way
Quelling the Frenzy
Rise of the Haranir
Once they do become available, new Haranir characters feature an astounding amount of customization, and can be played as a Druid, Hunter, Mage, Monk, Priest, Rogue, Shaman, Warlock, or Warrior pledged to either the Alliance or Horde factions.


They even have unique totems and Druid forms!

title: Celebrate Midnight's Launch with Rewards Across Blizzard’s Games
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY POPULARTOPULAR
content:
Ahead of Midnight's Early Access release, Blizzard has announced that players will be gifted with rewards in their other games. By completing the Welcome Home and Echoes of Midnight Achievements, players will earn Overwatch Legendary skins, a Hearthstone cardback, & a StarCraft II console skin!

Midnight Expansion Overview
Midnight Expansion Overview
Blizzard
View Original
To celebrate World of Warcraft: Midnight, game teams across Blizzard have joined forces to create a suite of unique rewards that unlock when players move into their first home and earn the new “Welcome Home” achievement in World of Warcraft, as well as a second wave of rewards after completing the “Echoes of Midnight” achievement, which is earned by finishing Midnight’s introduction questline. 
Earn Two Achievements, Unlock Rewards in Multiple Blizzard Games
This is a celebration across our games of the Midnight expansion launch and Player Housing, and these rewards are a thank you to our incredible community—a community that spans genres and worlds. We invite our entire Blizzard community to put down roots in Azeroth and celebrate alongside us. Welcome home!
Overwatch 
Xal'atath’s treachery knows no bounds. As the Void consumes Symmetra, earning the “Welcome Home” achievement in WoW will unlock the following cosmetics in Overwatch:
Xal’atath Symmetra Legendary Skin
Xal’atath Symmetra Spray
Xal’atath Symmetra Name Card
Dark Heart Player Icon
Earning the “Echoes of Midnight” achievement will unlock the Devourer Genji Legendary Skin.
Hearthstone

Classic Games | StarCraft II and Heroes of the Storm
It’s about time! Whether you shout "Lok’tar Ogar" or “For the Alliance," now you can show your allegiance in every match with these Horde and Alliance StarCraft II console skins after you earn the “Welcome Home” achievement in WoW.
Earning the “Echoes of Midnight” achievement will unlock the Crimson War Bear Epic Mount, so you can charge toward your foes in style!
Putting Down Roots Starts with Midnight
We’re thrilled to celebrate this moment not just in WoW, but across multiple Blizzard titles. No matter which Blizzard world you call home, we’re grateful to celebrate this launch with you. Whether it’s a cozy corner in Azeroth or a legacy across every Blizzard game you play, we can’t wait to see what you build.

title: New Player Starter Guide: Welcome to World of Warcraft!
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
If you're starting your WoW journey in Midnight, check out Blizzard's New Player Starter guide to learn more about what to expect in Azeroth including the updated Exile's Reach experience.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
Exile's Reach Guide
Exile's Reach Guide
Blizzard
View Original
If you’re new to World of Warcraft and seeking to start your adventures with us in Azeroth, we can help get you started with our handy starter guide. Welcome! We’re glad you are joining us.
Beginning in Exile’s Reach
You’ll first begin your expedition aboard a ship where you’ll start to learn the basics of movement, interacting with non-player characters, and some starter class abilities. Key World of Warcraft figures will guide you through your first steps. As you progress through Exile’s Reach, you’ll learn more about your new world and hone your skills for your chosen class, gaining familiarity with its abilities and how to interact with your inventory.

After completing Exile’s Reach, you’ll move on to experience the entirety of the Dragonflight story including Story Mode raids leading you up to level 70 and the beginning of the first chapter of the Worldsoul Saga—The War Within.

What is Story Mode?

Experience the game's rich storytelling without the challenges of traditional raid mechanics. Story Mode opens the opportunity to follow the questline and enter the raid to enjoy the narrative at your own pace—allowing you to face the final boss in the encounter either solo or with four other friends. This mode is available in Dragonflight and The War Within raid encounters only.

You’ll level up to 80 through the story in an accelerated play-through of the expansion. Within just a few hours of playtime, this experience will take you through the complete story, beginning on the Isle of Dorn and delivering you to the broken world of K’aresh. Along the way, you’ll view key cinematics and follow the story of The War Within's main characters.

At the end of the experience, players with the Midnight expansion can join in the continuing story of the Worldsoul Saga.
Test the Waters with a Free Trial*
Begin the journey to a world filled with wonder and boundless adventure without needing a Subscription or Game Time. All playable content is free up to level 20, including level 20 Dungeons and quests, with a Free Trial. After the initial trek through Exile's Reach, characters on fresh accounts will be directed to Dragonflight™ content to continue to level beyond 20; however, a Subscription or Game Time is necessary.

The Free Trial is available to all players with a new or existing Battle.net account or those with a pre-existing account but inactive Subscription or Game Time.

Once you upgrade your account with a WoW Subscription or Game Time, all game content beyond level 20 and up to level 80 become available, including higher-level Dungeons, Raids, and social features like guilds, unrestricted in-game chat, and the Auction House.

More details about the World of Warcraft Free Trial, including frequently asked questions, can be read on WorldofWarcraft.Blizzard.com/start.
Getting Started
Download the Battle.net desktop app for Windows or Mac and create your account.
Open the Battle.net desktop app and select World of Warcraft from the menu.
Under the Version menu, select World of Warcraft. You'll see a second dropdown for each account if you have more than one World of Warcraftaccount. Select the active account you wish to play on from that column.
Click the Install button. The progress bar will show you when the game is available to play.
Once the installation is complete, click the Play button.
You'll need to select the realm you want to play on from the available realm types. Make sure you pick the same one your friends play on—in World of Warcraft Classic, characters can only group with others on the same realm!

If you're looking for a group of like-minded players, you can head to the New Guild Listings forum to begin your search. Or you can check out the new Ambassador Program to find helpful Discords communities.
Realm Types
There are several different realm types to choose from. Choose carefully, as each one has a distinct play style.
Normal—All zones are considered contested except enemy faction cities, where players will be automatically flagged for PvP. Players won't be able to attack each other in contested areas unless they turn on War Mode, allowing players on any realm to participate in open-world PvP with like-minded players. The toggle for War Mode can be found at the bottom of the Talents interface once a character reaches level 20. It can only be activated in a Faction or Capital City and can be deactivated at any rest location.
RP (Role-Play)—This realm type is best for those who wish to participate in role-play with others. This realm type uses the Normal ruleset and does not have open-world PvP.
/New Player Has Joined the Chat
After creating your new character and beginning your journey into World of Warcraft for the first time, you will automatically be added to a Newcomer Chat channel, where you can talk with other new players and seek advice from experienced players on the same faction who will be your guides as you level through the new starting zone of Exile's Reach. New players and guides will be able to recognize one another in the chat channels and by icons above their characters' heads that only they can see. Upon reaching level 50, new players will continue the journey independently and no longer have access to the Newcomer Chat.

If you're a veteran player and interested in guiding new players as they experience World of Warcraft for the first time, head over to Stormwind to speak with guide recruiter Celeste Periwinkle at the Stormwind Embassy or Orgrimmar to speak with guide recruiter Eyla Pathfinder at the Orgrimmar Embassy once you've met the following criteria:
Have an account in good standing
Reach level 80
Complete 3,000 quests
If you no longer wish to be a guide, you can speak to the guide recruiter once again.
Pre-purchase Midnight from the Blizzard Shop today to join in the next chapter of our journey. Learn more about the new expansion in our information round up. Adventure is calling you home!

*Free Trial not available for WoW Classic games.

title: Non-Seasonal Mythic 0 Dungeons Available with Full Release of Midnight
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY IMX3
content:
In our interview with Associate Game Director Paul Kubit and Lead Encounter Designer Dylan Barker, it was confirmed that non-seasonal Mythic 0 dungeons will be available after the March 3rd Weekly reset.

Wowhead's Season 1 Developer Interview
Wowhead's Season 1 Developer Interview
 Midnight Dungeons Overview
Midnight Dungeons Overview
NON-SEASONAL MYTHIC 0 DUNGEONS
Right at the launch of an expansion, Mythic 0 dungeons are a popular way for players begin gearing their characters for end-game content. With the Midnight expansion, non-seasonal Mythic 0 dungeons will be available with the weekly reset on March 3rd. The full list of non-seasonal Midnight dungeons is provided below:

Level-up Dungeons Max-level Dungeons
Den of Nalorakk Maisara Caverns
Magisters' Terrace Nexus-Point Xenas
Murder Row The Blinding Vale
Windrunner Spire Voidscar Arena


Midnight Season 1 launches on March 17th, leaving us with two full weeks until all gearing sources become available. title: No Raid Renown in Midnight Season 1: Wowhead Exclusive Interview
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
In our interview with Paul Kubit and Dylan Barker, we learn that there is no Raid Renown Track in Midnight Season 1, and likely no scheduled weeks of bonus damage and healing.

Midnight Raids Hub
Midnight Raids Hub
 Full Season 1 Interview
Starting in Patch 11.1 with Liberation of Undermine and continuing into Patch 11.2 with the Manaforge Omega, raid renowns were added and gave players bonuses for clearing the raids week over week. Players would get cosmetic rewards, access to skips, quality of life improvements, and a damage and healing buff around every 3 to 4 weeks.

Raid renown tracks seemed to be received positively, though catch-up was a concern as you could only gain up to 2 renown levels a week. This was a problem if you were starting late in the season as you would just be behind in damage increases with no way to catch up faster. In addition, some players weren't a fan of the damage increases as it would make a tough boss, suddenly trivial the next week. It seems that Blizzard is moving away from this, as Paul Kubit confirms that there is no Raid Renown track in Midnight Season 1.
Paul Kubit said:
We don't have a renown this time. You should be able to approach raids however often you like and whenever you want to.
Dylan Barker follows up talking about the scheduled damage buffs that were on the Raid Renown Track in War Within, noting that he believes that they're just going to be keeping track of completion rates and are hoping that gear helps enough to defeat the encounters.

Blizzard has provided Wowhead with a statement that confirms scheduled raid buffs are returning. The below statements have been left in the article below but are no longer accurate.
Dylan Barker: I think we're basically just going to be keeping tabs on completion rates and what not over time. I think we're hoping for gear to grow people into it organically as much as is possible [...]

Squishei: So there's no more "this is the week where we're getting 3% more damage and healing"?

Dylan Barker: Not to my knowledge. If I'm wrong I'll have someone correct me.
While raid renown tracks were only present for 2 seasons of the War Within, the scheduled damage buffs were actually present in all 3 seasons, with Nerub'ar Palace having the weekly turn in of Nerub-ar Finery. This will be the first raid in the last 3 raid tiers without a ramping damage buff other than gear.

title: Blizzard Talks Midnight Season 1 Raids, Dungeons and End-Game - Exclusive Wowhead Interview
author: POSTED 4 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In a new interview with Wowhead, Associate Game Director Paul Kubit and Lead Encounter Designer Dylan Barker discussed dungeons, raids, and other end-game features coming in Season 1 of Midnight!

GEAR AND UPGRADES
WoW has pivoted away from the borrowed power of previous expansions, and Midnight is no different. Apex talents are a new introduction to the talent tree, but are intended to stick around for awhile. Although borrowed power has kind of a negative connotation now, temporary bonuses that your character can play around like legiondaries were kind of cool, so nothing is off the table for the future.
Changing crests for each upgrade track was a simplification from the upgrade systems of Dragonflight and The War Within. The previous overlap between crests with varied names created a lot more confusion, when now players can much more clearly associate Heroic crests for Hero-track gear.
As far as the number of crests are concerned, that's simplification of a different type. They initially tried scaling costs, with each subsequent upgrade becoming more expensive, but ultimately decided the flat cost would be a lot less mathy for players just trying to figure out what to do with their resources each week. Players should be able to make some sort of "rule" in their head like "weapons first, then trinkets, then chest" or whatnot, but trying to do that while also dealing with incremental costs required too much calculation.
As far as changing tier tokens, it's a mix of wanting to try something different and aligning armor types to make each token more obvious. They just want to see how it plays out.
RAID PROGRESSION
There is no raid renown track in Season 1 of Midnight, which they hope will make it a little easier for players to approach raids however and whenever they want. With the absence of a raid-specific buff unlocked over time, they'll be keeping tabs on completion rates and nerf as appropriate, but are also hoping that gear will help players grow in power organically over time.
Blizzard has provided Wowhead with a statement that confirms scheduled raid buffs are returning. The below statements have been left in the article below but are no longer accurate.
With three different raid locations each with a different number of bosses, skips and teleports shouldn't feel as necessary, and they can simply prioritize which raid to tackle based on how much time they have on a given day.
Designing boss mechanics without computational addons and Weakauras has been a big focus for the team. The very first boss of Voidspire asks players to play tic-tac-toe, which is something they want to play out in the world. It's a simple game, but they want players to keep their eyes on the battlefield, at what the enemy is doing, and to think about how to counter it.
The biggest difference, and the thing they've struggled with in the past, is getting players to communicate more, rather than deducing what is going to happen based on an automated aura or macro.
With Mythic difficulty in particular, they want everyone to participate in mechanics. Sometimes they'll let players choose who is going to do what and when, while other times its randomly assigned in order to add an extra degree of difficulty... which often got flattened out by computational addons. Many of the newer encounters now have more static assignments, which should help lead players to developing more of a rhythm in learning the cadence of the fight without as much need for addons to keep track of what's going on.
DUNGEONS, AFFIXES, AND REWARDS
Dungeons have been getting a little shorter, but more important is trying to create parity between the different dungeons of a given season. Players always tend to want to skip the long one and binge the short one until they're sick of it, so the team is trying to do better at keeping them all at a similar playtime. The 30-minute dungeon has felt pretty right and it feels like a good length of time to ask players to remain engaged.
Great Vault rewards shifted around on the beta as the developers experimented with new ideas, but ultimately, gameplay in Mythic Plus has not changed enough between the War Within and Midnight to to warrant changing the rewards structure. Even if the difficulty remained the same, players have an expected value for the "rewards cap" and so to change it so drastically from 10 all the way up to 18 would have been a pretty big change in expectation, so they brought it back down.
The new Routing Affix as targeted to provide a racing line for newer tanks, intended to reduce the anxiety burden placed on them by streamlining part of the process and hopefully encourage more players to pick up a new role. Just like a racing game, that line which the computer provides is good, but ultimately not meant to be optimal - as they come to understand more about the game, players should start to learn where they can should play safer or be more aggressive.
Non-Seasonal Mythic+0 dungeons should become available on March 3rd, after early access ends.
ADDON CHANGES IN REVIEW
The goal of the addon changes has been largely about fairness, making sure that players feel like they are on equal ground whether they have add-ons or not. The developers think it's gone about as anticipated, but there's still work to be done for sure.
Add-ons are still welcomed in plenty of forms, from adjusting the way that information is presented, to managing non-combat related things like collections, and accessibility reasons. In a lot of ways, the team is happy to see folks continue to modify their gameplay experience, but whether or not class and encounter design feels fair will often fall back on those designers, rather than the add-on side.
THE FUTURE OF SEASON 1
Because the Sporefall raid is arriving later in the season during Patch 12.0.7, most players who have already been running content should find it to have an appropriate difficulty and rewards. It won't be the hardest raid they've ever released, but it's going to be a lot of fun, they can promise us that!
These mini-raids would be an interesting place to play with new rule sets, like smaller raids sizes, which is something to think about going forward.
We'll meet a dominaar in the Voidstorm named Decimus, who is a complicated individual, sort of an employee of Xal'atath but also not. Hell help us run the Voidforge, which will help make our gear stronger. We'll start this process at launch, collecting fragments to empower the forge for something upcoming later in the season, with more details coming around the 12.0.5 timeframe.
No comment on whether there will be another Turbo Boost in Midnight, but the developers believe it has succeeded in its goal of providing players with new mid-season gearing opportunities, some new amount of power to chase after they've exhausted other activities earlier in the season.
The team is constantly iterating and experimenting with interesting systems for players to find cool stuff to make their character stronger. Cyrce's Circlet is one example, being closely tied with the Siren Island, while Turbo Boost being more thematically associated with the Goblins of Undermine is another.
The team is going to continue playing around with different systems, but philosophically, they want to retain novelty. They don't want it to end up feeling like players will expect the same thing in every x.5 or x.7 patch, they want players to look forward to the unknown. That's why the Turbo Boost was different than the Circlet and why some things we might see in the future will be different than what we've in the past.
EXCITING UPCOMING FEATURES
Dylan is most excited for the Murder Row minigames, but that's not in the Season 1 dungeon pool. From Season 1, it would be Maisara Caverns, as it features an incredible vista while approaching the final boss and from its encounter mechanic. The whole dungeon is begging for high keys.
Internal playtests for the final boss of March on Quel'Danas have also been a blast. Without spoiling, its mechanic is not something they could have done in a world where computational add-ons could take dynamic assignments and automatically correlate them to static responses. The team is going to be able to play a lot more with making the fight easier or harder based on coordination, instead of just flooding the arena with one shot danger orbs.
Paul is partial to the Den of Nalorakk, which isn't in Season 1, but is part of the Zul'Aman campaign. Players will be entering the dreams of the bear loa for the majority of the dungeon, in a sort of dreamlike space, which is about what you'd expect the the dreams of a bear to be about about - gathering food and surviving the winter. Seasonally, he's more excited to go back to Pit of Saron, which is the earliest dungeon they've reached back to for Mythic+.
Raid wise, Paul is most excited for the final encounter of the Voidspire, Crown of the Cosmos. They've been intentionally shy about exactly who we'll be fighting there, but if you go in game and you dig into the dungeon journal, you'll probably figure it out. The fight itself is spectacular and leads into some really good story that fits in-between the March on Quel'Danas raid.

title: More Decor, but More Knowledge Needed: What's New with Midnight Inscription?
author: POSTED 5 DAYS AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Inscription! If you're looking to learn everything about Inscription in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Inscription Guide, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Inscription in Midnight
Inscription in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT INSCRIPTION?
While every Crafting Profession is getting new Decor options in Midnight, Inscription is the clear winner - with 12 new pieces, including some highly sought after bookshelves with books like the Magnificent Towering Bookcase, scribes will have plenty of opportunities to make gold with the influx of people trying Player Housing as it reaches its full release.



The rest of Inscription's Midnight changes should feel relatively normal to those familiar with the Profession. There's not much new, aside from the addition of a bow to the craftable repertoire: Aln'hara Sprigshot. The only other thing of note is that, unfortunately, Scribes will have to work a bit harder in Midnight to fully max out their specializations. While The War Within required 780 Knowledge Points to fully max the Profession trees - already a staggering number - Midnight raises this even higher to 870 Knowledge Points total.

title: Prey Release Schedule Revealed - Staggered Difficulty Release from Early Access to Season 1
author: POSTED 5 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
Join the open world hunt with Midnight's Prey system. In our latest developer interview, Prey's release cadence has been revealed with a staggered difficulty launch starting in Early Access.

 Wowhead's Prey Interview


Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
Everything About Prey
Everything About Prey
PREY'S RELEASE SCHEDULE IN MIDNIGHT
While War Within veterans are used to the content release schedule of an expansion, not much has been known about the content cadence for Prey, the new open world opt-in hunting system coming in Midnight. In our interview with Kim Flack, Senior Game Designer (Prey), we finally learned about the staggered difficulty release schedule:
Normal Mode: Available in Early Access (Feb 26th) starting at Level 90.
Hard Mode: Available at Journey Rank 1 after the first weekly reset for Midnight's Launch (March 3rd).
Nightmare Mode: Available at Journey Rank 4 with Season 1 (March 17th).
All unlocks are account-wide, so you'll only need to complete the requirements on your first character. Subsequent characters will have Prey available starting at Level 80. Completing the 4 hunts per week should be sufficient for reaching Journey rank unlock requirements.
Kim Flack, Senior Game Designer (Prey) said:
Once you reach level 90 and complete the main level up campaign,...you're going to get a quest to meet Astalor Bloodsworn in Murder Row. He's going to send you on a short tutorial to learn the ins and outs of the system. After that you'll unlock Normal Mode account-wide, you won't need to do that on every single character. Normal mode is available during Early Access.

Hard Mode is unlocked very early at Journey Rank 1, so effectively the first reset after Early Access or the regular Midnight launch. Nightmare mode is unlocked shortly afterward at Journey Rank 4 at the start of Season 1. Both of these unlocks are also account-wide, you won't need to do it multiple times.

Once you've unlocked Prey [on your main], any of your alts that are at least level 80 will have access to Prey while they're leveling. Upon reaching level 90, they'll naturally then have access to Hard and Nightmare modes if they're unlocked on your account already.
Players are only going to be able to reach Rank 3 before the start of Season 1. Normal Mode is locked until Rank 4. ...If you're doing those 4 hunts per week to get your big chunks of Journey Progress, you're going to be right on pace to unlock Nightmare Mode with Season 1. You won't need to grind out random hunts.
PREY REWARDS
With the staggered difficulty release for Prey, the item level of the gear obtainable will also be capped till Season. Completing a Hunt will grant Preyseeker's Journey progress, gold, and a Preyseeker's Adventurer Chest containing a variety of goodies:
A piece of gear that will increase in item level based on difficulty.
Normal: Adventurer Track
Hard: Veteran Track
Nightmare: Champion Track
Upgrades crests based difficulty: Adventurer Dawncrest, Veteran Dawncrest, and Champion Dawncrest
Voidlight Marl with a chance at Tormented Tantalum and other profession items.
 Are you excited to start your Prey hunt in Midnight? Do you like the staggered difficulty release for Prey?

title: Blizzard Talks Prey and Zul'Aman in Midnight - Exclusive Wowhead Interview
author: POSTED 5 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In an exclusive interview with Wowhead, Senior Game Designer Kim Flack and Game Designer Jake Shillan discussed the story beats of Zul'Aman and brand new Prey system of hunts coming in Midnight!

HUNTING DOWN PREY
Prey has a journey track similar to Delves, lending itself to progression over time, titled Prey Season 1. We can infer that this means there will be a Season 2, but the developers can't confirm that right now.
Midnight launch will feature 30 Prey targets across three difficulties, so there are a lot of permutations, but the developers are eager to hear what players find interesting or challenging. One of the harder torments actually causes you to lose progress on death in Hard mode, and resets it entirely on Nightmare, but it does not appear in Normal mode.
The Prey introductory quest becomes available upon reaching level 90 and completing the main campaign, but the unlock is account-wide, so subsequent alts can participate while leveling. Normal mode will be available during Early Access, Hard unlocks with Journey Rank 1 when the expansion launches in full, and Nightmare at Rank 4 with the start of Season 1.
Players will have access to up to 12 Prey Hunts each week (one per zone per difficulty) awarding Adventurer/Veteran/Champion-track crests and gear based on difficulty. The first four hunts per week give a large amount of account-wide Journey progress, while subsequent hunts give significantly less. As long as you're doing four per week, you should be on track to unlock Nightmare by the time Season 1 begins.
There are random repeatable Hunts unlocked at Rank 2, while Custom Hunts are can only be completed once per week for more general rewards instead of gear.
Hunts can also be done in a group, even on different difficulties. If one player advances to the second stage, everyone's will, allowing players to help with each other's bosses without waiting for anyone to catch up. Hunts can also be done in War Mode and they're going to be very spicy, but there are some fun achievements for the brave!
The goal is for all difficulties to be soloable with sufficient gear and skill, but certain classes or specs will always have an easier time than others.
Evergreen Prey sounds like a great movie title, but they can't say yet whether it will be a long-term system or not. They're eager to see how players respond.
Please listen to the music in Murder Row, it's a fantastic place to AFK.
STORY AND WORLD DESIGN
The Zul'Aman storyline focuses on what's been happening over the last few decades between the elves and trolls. It's not so much of a truce as a stalemate, which each withdrawing to their own side, but with Xal'atath sort of "poking the hive," the Trolls and Elves are going to have to come together, even if they have to agree to disagree.
Trolls are one of the oldest races in Warcraft, but while Darkspear are an original playable race and we explored the Zandalari in Battle for Azeroth, they want to keep the current spotlight on the Amani. We're finally busting down those gates to see what's been going on over the past 15-17 years; they have their own ecosystem, their own culture, and their own characters to explore.
Much of the Zul'Aman story revolves around the four Loa that we saw in the Zul'Aman raid so long ago, and how the threat of the Twilight's Blade is affecting them. They're not going to divulge anything right now, but there will be many stories to share.
Everyone on the team was very excited as soon as they heard the idea of adding capybara creatures to the zone. Making one a loa was just kind of an extension of that, as there are are quite a few creature loa who aren't necessarily major ones, including a pangolin, rat, and the capybara. Each of them have their own domain, a physical shrine similar to the major loa temples, and Capybara are well associated with hot springs in the real world, which led to Fi'lo, the capybara loa of children (pronounced "fee-low").
There are a lot of iconic troll characteristics throughout Zul'Aman; they're strong, honor driven, brutal, but having a pocket shrine of Fi'lo to offset that came as a refreshing reprieve.
When it comes to revamping old zones, the team goes where the story dictates. It's not a quick redo or complete rebuild from nothing, but a careful revitalization and update of an area that you remember leveling through. Quite a bit of time has passed in game, so it's their chance to show that evolution along the timeline of World of Warcraft.
Verticality is another huge addition to the world, looking for opportunities to give players somewhere to go while Skyriding. They've always managed to create amazing vistas, but now they need to think about how players will see these areas from on high and down low, not just walking along a predetermined path. There's a lot to consider when it comes to giving players cool spaces to fly in, like the top of the temple on an enormous mountain or that great mysterious island way out in the ocean which the team isn't going to talk about right now. They just want to make sure they can satisfy players who are just flying around, and give them cool things to look at.
Open world delves are a new addition to Midnight which also plays into world design. As they created new spaces, such as transforming the Zul'Aman raid into a proper world zone, they started to think about what else they could do with those areas. While the Delves occur in the "open world" they're still instanced, so they don't actually take up the same space at the same time.

title: RNG Recipes Are Gone: What's New with Midnight Alchemy?
author: POSTED 5 DAYS AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Midnight introduces some new changes to both Crafting and Gathering Professions. While players expect Profession updates each time a new expansion launches, Midnight is iterating on the previous Dragonflight and War Within concepts of each profession, adjusting difficulties, adding new mechanics, and providing new types of crafts.

We're taking a look at what's new and different with each Profession - this time, with a look at Alchemy! If you're looking to learn everything about Alchemy in the new expansion, you can find extensive information in our Midnight Alchemy Guide, as well as overall Profession information in our Profession Overview.

Alchemy in Midnight
Alchemy in Midnight
 Profession Overview in Midnight
Profession Overview in Midnight
 WHAT'S NEW WITH MIDNIGHT ALCHEMY?
In what may be the biggest change to any profession heading into Midnight, Midnight Alchemy completely revamps how players interact with the Recipe Discovery system - and that means no more RNG!

In The War Within, players had to roll the dice with Wild Experimentation and Meticulous Experimentation to learn new Alchemy recipes, which meant that with bad luck, you'd be blowing yourself up quite frequently and starting a lockout for further experimentation. It's never fun to design a system that punishes you for trying to interact with it, and that's exactly how many players felt this last expansion.



Now, Midnight instead uses deterministic research to learn Alchemy recipes: introducing Camberon's Cauldron!

This supplementary trainer allows players to unlock new recipes by completing tasks within Alchemy - simple things that players are likely to already be doing, such as "Craft 5 Midnight Healing Potions" or "Craft Void Potions 10 times." While recycling flasks and potions are still here to stay, the removal of RNG factors should make for a much smoother experience with Alchemy as a whole.

That's not all that's changing with Alchemy this time around, though. Those players who are fond of using Transmutations might be in for a bit of a struggle, since Midnight's transmutation options ( Bouquet of Herbs, Box of Rocks, and School of Gems) all share a cooldown - one that's 18 hours long. There are cooldown reductions for the new Wondrous Synergist transmutation, which will be a heavily used item for things such as the Voidlight Potion Cauldron , but sadly, there doesn't seem to currently be a way to adjust the general transmutes.

title: Many Healer Spells No Longer Secret Aura on Midnight Launch
author: POSTED 5 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced that many healer spells will no longer be secret for Midnight Launch allowing for better customization of raid frames!

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
User Interface Hub
User Interface Hub
HEALER SPELLS NO LONGER SECRET AURA ON MIDNIGHT LAUNCH
Despite some recent hotfixes to healer buff sorting, the lack of raid frame customization by Blizzard has led to significant frustration amongst healers. Healers accurately assess the healing battlefield by relying on quick identification of critical buffs and debuffs; most are finding that task difficult in the current state of addon lockdown.

Our Restoration Shaman writer, Harreks, has been trying to resurrect some of the raid frame customization lost by healers since the Midnight Pre-Patch. We previously highlighted some of the workaround solutions as well as the first incarnation of his addon, but the latest version has since been patched out of the game as referenced in the response below.

Below is a recent post from Blizzard on the WoWUI Discord about their plans for buff filtering in the near future including whitelisting certain healer spells at Midnight launch until a full system that allows for players to hide or emphasize buffs directly via the Spellbook arrives in a later patch.
Blizzard via WoWUI Discord said:
Hello again from Blizzard!

Today we have an update regarding which spells we are going to be flagging as non-secret for Midnight launch. These changes are planned to go out in a data hotfix in the next couple days, but we wanted to give you all a heads up beforehand so you can plan any necessary changes to your addons.

Over the course of Beta, as well as recent weeks on live, one of the biggest recurring points of feedback has been from healers who wish that our default UI provided more robust options for filtering or emphasizing specific buffs on raid frames. In the past month we have added a few layers of extra logic to buff sorting and visibility, and while we've heard positive feedback from many healers about these improvements, we realize that our solutions are leaving others still wanting more customization.

While we had originally hoped to be able to deliver a streamlined default experience, it has become clear that there are common use-cases that a single set of logic cannot cover. Some players may want to make simple tweaks like highlighting the target of their Preservation Evoker's Echo, or their Holy Paladin's active Beacon, while others would consider that to be distracting clutter. We are now beginning work on a system to allow players to hide or emphasize buffs from their spellcasts on a per-spell basis, via the Spellbook. We expect that solution to be ready for players in a later Midnight patch.

In the meantime, we have also seen a few addons popping up that use non-secret information to make very reliable guesses about players' spellcasts in order to drive this sort of filtering logic. Breaking the API calls driving this functionality would cause significant collateral damage to common addons, and we are not planning to do go down that path. Ultimately, this design space is mostly about customizing the display of information, rather than objectively advantageous computational solutions, so we want to treat it the same way we approached resources like Holy Power or Maelstrom.

Thus, rather than requiring addon developers to jump through hoops to support an implementation that will have some frustrating inaccuracies, we are planning to flag a number of short-cooldown rotational healer spells, and very long duration buffs, to exclude them from our Secret Value system. We will likely re-protect these spells once our own filtering solution, with its own addon hooks for display customization, is in place.

The full list of spells being flagged is below, broken down into several categories.

title: Live, Laugh, and Love Your Way in the March Trading Post - Two Flying Quilts Available
author: POSTED 5 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
The March Trading Post items have been revealed and include Two Flying Quilts!

The Trading Post Guide
The Trading Post Guide

Blizzard

Whether you’re living your best life (or unlife) in Azeroth, still aglow from the Love is in the Air holiday, or having a good laugh with friends as you go on adventures together, the March Trading post is where it’s at. Take part in a variety of activities to earn the month’s reward— get the Comfy Bel'ameth Flying Quilt mount hand-woven by Ainderu Summerleaf.

February’s Anniversary Outlet is almost over! If you haven’t bought everything you wanted to, it’s not too late. Head over to the Trading Post today to make your final purchases before the March Trading Post arrives! Check out the full inventory in our previously published article.
   
Visit T&W** (Tawney and Wilder) just outside the Mage District in Stormwind or the Zen’shiri Trading Post next to Grommash Hold in Orgrimmar to check out all the latest items*** they have available. Players can also visit the Trading Post in the capital cities of Dornogal (The Foregrounds) on the Isle of Dorn and in the renewed Silvermoon City (with the launch of Midnight) where representatives of both Tawney and Wilder and the Zen’shiri Trading Post are open for business.

This Month's Bonus Reward
Comfy Bel’ameth Flying Quilt
Mount
“Ainderu Summerleaf's hand-woven quilts are popular among the children of Bel'ameth.”
Complete activities to fill the bar at the top of the Traveler’s Log found in the Adventure Guide (Shift-J)***, and you'll receive this month's bonus reward—the Comfy Bel’ameth Flying Quilt mount.
How it Works
Get Tender Every Month
At the beginning of each month, players with an active account in good standing will receive 500 Trader’s Tender automatically every month from a chest called the Collector’s Cache. If you don’t have active game time on your account or aren’t a subscriber at the start of a new month, don’t worry. Your Tender will be awarded on the first day of the month when you do have game time or an active subscription. This will be the same amount for everyone, regardless of how you pay for your game time.
The Traveler's Log
Complete monthly activities listed in the new Traveler’s Log. Each month features a rotating, themed set of activities. These activities provide the opportunity to earn up to 500 additional Trader’s Tender. There’s only a set amount of Tender you can earn each month through completing activities, so you won’t need to complete all the ones in the log each month. You’ll be able to pick and choose from a variety of fun in-game activities to easily earn Tender. Players can choose to earn by continuing to play the game as they already do, such as completing quests, competing in battlegrounds, participating in holiday activities, and even running Mythic+ dungeons. But you can also choose to take part in activities uniquely designed for the month.
Freeze an Item
Before the month comes to an end, if you haven’t purchased that one “must-have” item yet, don’t worry. You can “freeze” an item so you can purchase it later. When you freeze an item, it will stay available month over month until it is purchased or replaced.
If this month’s offerings didn’t have the items you wanted, you can hang on to your Trader’s Tender to spend later. It won’t go anywhere, and unspent Tender will continue to accumulate each month.
Learn more about the Trading Post from the news post on the official site.
*The Trading Post feature is not available in WoW Classic titles.
**Requires World of Warcraft Subscription or Game Time
***Requires Level 10 to access the Adventure Guide.
