title: The State of Player Housing and Upcoming Features - New Showcases and Visitor Codes
author: POSTED 5 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Find out the current state of Player Housing and some upcoming features in the latest Blizzard blog where they announce a new Showcases feature and Visitor codes!
Blizzard
View Original
From decor storage to exterior lighting, Endeavors, exporting houses and more, we’re looking into the future of Housing and what you can expect from the launch of Midnight and beyond.
Hello Neighbors!

Housing Early Access has been out for a few months now and we’ve loved seeing your creativity and builds. As a reminder, Housing is an evergreen feature that we will continue to build and iterate on. So, as we approach Midnight, we want to discuss what we’re currently working on and what you can expect to see in the months to come.

Our usual disclaimer applies here: We’re discussing this far in advance of when we’d normally talk about in-development features because we want your feedback early and often. This means there’s plenty of time for things to change, priorities could shift, names or aspects of features might be different by the time they launch, and things might be pushed up or back (or even cut entirely). Keep this in mind as you read on.
Hot Topics
We want to first acknowledge that there are some things that we’ve seen come up often in community conversations around Housing and we’re actively working on them. Here’s what we mean:
Maximum Decor Storage - Now that we’re seeing usage numbers, we plan to increase the maximum amount of decor storage by around 50% in the coming weeks.
Decor storage and deletion – As you approach the maximum amount of decor storage, it can be confusing because some items don’t count towards the cap but also can’t be deleted while others do count. It’s not clear which is which by looking in the House Chest. This makes it difficult to delete items to free up room and players can end up “soft-locking” themselves without much recourse. To address this, we’ll be changing things to allow these items to be deleted while leaving the original as undeletable since you can’t get it again. So, if you get an item through an achievement, and buy too many extras, you can delete the extras without deleting the achievement item.
The weird rotation bug – We’re aware of a bug that causes some items to slightly rotate themselves when you re-enter your house. This is caused by certain angles of rotation being saved and reloaded incorrectly. We have a fix for this but because it touches a lot of fundamentals in decoration, we want to put it through some rigorous testing. We’ll roll it out when we’re confident that it’s both safe and fixed.
House XP loss – There’s been sporadic reports of House XP (experience) loss with updates. We’ve added additional logging here to try and isolate what’s happening. We’re working on it, but don’t have any additional updates right now.
Exterior limits – Lastly, we still want to increase the exterior decor limit, but we’re gathering some more data here (see down below).
What’s Coming in Future Updates
Some things you can expect with nearly every update: new decor, bug fixes, quality of life improvements, and more. These things will be consistent throughout updates, but what about the larger features? Here are some things we’re working on right now that we’ll be introducing later.
Exterior Lighting and Exterior Limits
In the Midnight alpha, we had to disable exterior lighting due to performance concerns on the client. We’re currently working on a way to re-enable this in a way that remains performant. At the moment, this is taking the form of proximity restrictions so two light sources outside cannot be placed too close to one another (which is where the bulk of the performance impacts come from). This restriction will only exist outdoors and you’ll be free to place all the lights on top of each other indoors like you can today.

Once this is implemented, we’re going to run additional performance tests to see how exterior lights affect performance in a Neighborhood with a lot of players and every plot fully decorated. Assuming these numbers come back favorably, we’ll be increasing the exterior decor limits again as well.
More Endeavors
More Endeavors are planned for the future with more cultures coming to your Neighborhoods. They’re excited to share their traditions, activities, and let’s be honest, most excitingly, their decor with you. We’ve been watching what tasks folks are enjoying and which ones they’re shying away from in our current set of Endeavors. Future Endeavors will continue to experiment with new types of tasks while adding more of the kinds of tasks people like and pruning those that are less popular.
“Layouts” and Import/Export
We’ve mentioned this in a few different interviews, but let’s talk more about them! At their core, Layouts allow you to import and export the decor and rooms of your house, allowing you to effectively save and load various configurations and states. These can be used strictly for yourself or shared with other players, but regardless of how you choose to use them, they enable a host of interesting functionality:
If you’re looking for inspiration or just a base to build on, importing a Layout gives you a great starting place.
If you’ve built a holiday room and want to “take down the decorations” after the holidays, it’s simple to swap back to your “normal” decor (and then put things back up next year).
If you’re about to embark on a major redesign, export your current layout and use it as a “save point” that you can rollback to if things don’t go how you thought they would.
If you want a house for different characters, Layouts let you build one for each and swap between them as desired.
If you make a “build guide” for a house or room, you can include example Layouts for people to pull apart to really understand it.
But that’s not all! Layouts can also be used as a stop-gap solution to get around some of the current limitations in the room system. If you want to change around your hallways without having to redo the rooms on the ends from scratch, just export them and reimport when your new hallways are ready and rooms are ready.
Currently, we’re planning for Layouts to work on a room by room, an exterior plot, and a “whole dang house” basis but we’re also exploring some other finer-grained options here too.

It’s important to note that the sharing of a Layout is an explicit choice and up to the sole discretion of the house owner. Unless you, as the owner, decide to export and share a Layout, players (even those who have permissions to your house) cannot access or import it.

Another important note is that when you import a Layout, you don’t need to own all the decor, but you will need to have any necessary rooms or houses unlocked. We’ll try our best to make the import work (leaving out objects you don’t own, not coloring objects you don’t have the dye for, etc), but some things would break the bounds of physics too much for a direct import!

There’s a whole lot of logistics for us to figure out still since we want these to be very flexible and usable by everyone, but we’re excited to talk more about Layouts in the future!
 Bring Your Pets Home
What’s a home without a cute and fluffy (or oddly tentacled or precisely machined) friend to come back to after your adventures? In a future update, you’ll be able to place (almost) any of your collected pets.

To do so, you’ll place a “pet bed” decor item (or other thematically appropriate piece of decor), and from its customization menu, you’ll be able to select a pet as well as whether it should stay still or wander around. (We see you tripping hazard cats.)

There are a few limitations on pets, however:
When we launch pets-as-decor, they will be placeable both inside and out, but pathing will only work inside your house. There’s more work to be done on exterior pathing and that will be enabled further down the line. We’d rather you have placeable pets sooner rather than later!
Pets will NOT be limited by the amount you own, assuming you have at least one collected. If you want to go full-on cat person with a bunch of Black Tabby Cats, we won’t stop you.
Pets will have their own limit on placement, separate from decor limits, to prevent performance problems with 100s or 1000s of tiny things wandering around your house. (Also, tripping hazards again.)
Lastly, a very small number of pets will not be placeable due to various concerns, some technical and some functional.
“Showcase” and Visitor Codes
One more thing before we go! We’re also in the early stages of something we’re calling Showcases, which gives players a way to submit their houses to an either timed or themed showcase as well as browse and visit houses that have already been submitted. While visiting a house in a Showcase, if you like it, you can leave an upvote. At the end of the Showcase, the houses with the most upvotes will earn a small trophy they can show off as decor.

Our goal is to give players an easy way to check out interesting houses in a central in-game location for inspiration. Going a little further to enable easy sharing of your house, we’re also going to implement “Visitor Codes” which let you generate time-limited codes that can be shared to allow other players to visit your house directly.

It’s important to note that when you submit to the Showcase or a Visitor Code is generated, we’re going to “snapshot” your house and that snapshot will be used to spin up a new instance for visitors browsing via the Showcase or visiting via the code. This means 1) you’re free to (re)decorate without worrying about someone showing up mid-remodel and your place being a mess, and 2) players aren’t entering “your” space and you won’t be bothered by a stream of lookie-loos checking things out while you’re washing your hair or something. These visitors will be in their own shared instance together rather than in your private one. Your actual house’s permissions will still be respected while allowing you to show off your creations.

We’re also experimenting with a Guest Book as a part of this, where players can leave pre-written notes for the owner. When the owner logs in, they’ll be able to see who visited and any notes left for them.

We’ll have more to share on the Showcase and Visitor Codes later!
Onwards!
This is just a quick look at what we’re working on right now, but there’s so much more coming your way in the coming months (and years) with Housing. As things come online and firm up, we’ll be sharing more. In the meantime, keep your feedback coming and send along any concerns you have, what excites you, and definitely keep sharing your amazing builds!

title: Blizzard Reveals Overwatch Rush: Top Down Mobile Shooter in Development
author: POSTED 5 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has revealed an early gameplay preview for Overwatch Rush, a brand new top-down mobile shooter set in the Overwatch universe! Much like the main game, Rush features several familiar heroes and objective based gameplay, it appears very similar to the core Overwatch experience, albeit on a new device and from a new perspective.



Based on the preview, returning heroes include Lúcio, Mercy, Pharah, Reaper, Reinhardt, Soldier 76, and Tracer, with a character skin system as well as customizable talents and mods, though no specifics regarding any new systems have been shared. The interface is also very similar to Diablo: Immortal, though it does not appear to be developed alongside an external studio, instead being worked on by an internal Blizzard team distinct from Overwatch proper.

  View Original

Welcome, heroes!

We’ve got something new to share. While it's still early days, we want to share updates on our progress throughout the development process.

Today, we’re introducing Overwatch Rush, a new top-down Hero shooter designed specifically for mobile set in the Overwatch universe.

Overwatch Rush is being developed by a dedicated team at Blizzard, separate from Team 4, all of whom have deep mobile experience. Team 4 remains fully focused on Overwatch, which will continue to bring exciting and fresh new experiences for PC and console players in its recently launched Season 1 and beyond!

Overwatch Rush is an entirely new game – not a port – built specifically for mobile players. As we prepare for testing Overwatch Rush in select countries and regions, check out our official Discord server for updates on the game.

While there remains a lot of work to do, we’re eager to see how players enjoy what we’ve built so far, and to hear from them on what we should focus on in the coming phases of development.

Our mission is to continue to expand the Overwatch universe by bringing fresh new adventures to players across all platforms. We'll continue to keep you updated on how things are progressing.

—The Overwatch Rush Team

title: Challenge With Communication & Coordination, Not More Bullets: Midnight Interview with PC Gamer
author: POSTED 6 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
In an interview with PC Gamer, Lead Encounter Designer Dylan Barker discusses how the addon changes have paved a way for new encounter mechanics and design philosophy in Midnight.

 Addons & Encounter Design with PC Gamer
Midnight Season 1 Overview
Midnight Season 1 Overview
Midnight Raids Hub
Midnight Raids Hub


ADDONS & THE TRICKLE DOWN EFFECT
World First raid teams would enlist programmers to create WeakAuras to counter or solve certain mechanics. Mechanics would then have to be designed with these programmers in mind leading to an escalating back-and-forth cycle.
Creates a nightmare scenario for both design and gameplay with the bottom line being: You probably shouldn't need coders to raid.
The issue trickles down into slower progressed raid teams and even the faster paced Mythic+ where interrupt tracking and enemy management was key.
Dylan Barker said:
Let's say, in a Mythic+ scenario, [we're changing] what mixes of trash we're suggesting is reasonable for players to pull … up until the number of creatures we expect makes it unmanageable.
Midnight Pre-Patch has pared back a lot of addon capabilities by restricting the data required for solving combat encounters.
To compensate, WoW's classes were redesigned and simplified since player's would no longer have addons to deal with complex mechanics. Additionally, Blizzard created alternatives to the most crucial addons.
Computational addons forced the challenge to rely on dexterity or reflex mechanics since addons could solve strategy but couldn't pilot your character out of fire.
OPENING UP ENCOUNTER DESIGN
Part of the difficulty of a similar MMO, Final Fantasy 14, are mechanics called 'limit cuts' that force players to strategize. Addons like WeakAuras often nullified those opportunities in WoW.
Expect to see more limit cut-style mechanics in WoW with significant complexity; designed for people, not computers and coders.
Dylan Barker said:
I think there are a couple of mechanics in the raids for this opening tier that are possible in Midnight that might not have been possible in the recent past, and we're able to challenge players [via] puzzle and communication and coordination and strategy more.
Difficulty progression will test your skills beyond just shooting more bullets at the player.
The addon disarmament offers a lot of opportunities for encounter designers to develop something that hasn't been possible for years. It's super exciting to see what the dev team can pull off in this environment.
Dylan Barker said:
It's cool, because the way that gets harder from Normal to Heroic to Mythic is not 'we shoot more bullets at you'. It is harder on a communication axis, on a coordination axis.

title: Now Fixed: Pinterest Promotion Temporarily Disabled to Fix Account Suspensions
author: POSTED 6 DAYS AGO BY WHISPYR
content:
After the newest World of Warcraft promotion went live, players quickly noticed their Pinterest accounts were being suspended due to "some activity that appears to violate our policies against spam." The Pinterest crossover features a new Pin-o-Matic camera and social integrations for the photo-sharing platform.

As of this afternoon, Blizzard has re-enabled Pinterest photo-sharing and all suspended accounts have been restored.
Blizzard
View Original
Update 2:00 p.m. PST February 23
The Pinterest photo‑sharing feature has been enabled again for all players, following confirmation from Pinterest that they have fixed the issue and restored all suspended accounts.

As a part of the fix, all previously linked Pinterest accounts were disconnected. Players who already linked accounts will need to re‑link their Pinterest account before posting photos again.

Thank you for your patience!
Blizzard has now jumped in to resolve the issue, disabling the Pin-o-Matic camera and preventing uploads directly to Pinterest until the issue is resolved on their end.

We’re aware of this issue and we’ve reached out to Pinterest.

Until it is solved, we’ve disabled the function.

We’ll keep an eye on it and get it turned back on as soon as it’s safe to do so.

title: Power Up with New Apex Talents in Midnight
author: POSTED 6 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has released a blog covering the upcoming Apex Talents in Midnight!

Apex Talents Overview
Apex Talents Overview
Blizzard
View Original
In Midnight, players can expand their specializations further with new Apex Talents, taking you deeper into the fantasy of your class. Available beginning at level 81, you’ll unlock the first of these and provide your character with an extra boost of power as you take on new challenges.

Apex Talents are intended to be the pinnacle of specialization trees going a little beyond the average talent. These new talents are focused on the core fantasy of each specialization and hone into core gameplay mechanics in new, powerful ways.

Apex Talents can be found in your Talent Tree beneath the (currently) final section of the specialization tree. They will cost a total of four specialization talent points. The first point is spent on an initial keystone that provides a large boost in power and introduces new gameplay. The next two-point node emphasizes the Apex Talent or a few core abilities for the specialization. The final point applies to a capstone that brings the Apex Talent into a complete and cohesive package.
To give you an idea of what is in store for you and your character, we’ve rounded up each classes’ Apex Talents for their specializations.
Death Knight Demon Hunter Druid
Evoker Hunter Mage
Monk Paladin Priest
Rogue Shaman Warlock
Warrior
(All talents subject to change.)
Death Knight
Blood
Frost
Unholy
Dance of Midnight Chosen of Frostbrood Forbidden Knowledge
Rank 1

While Dancing Rune Weapon is active, Parrying an attack has a chance to make your next Heart Strike cost no Runes and deal 150% increased damage. Rank 1

Frostwyrm’s Fury deals 100% increased damage to the first enemy it hits and grants you 15% Haste for 12 seconds. Rank 1

Army of the Dead siphons the power of the fallen, transforming Death Coil and Epidemic into Necrotic Coil and Graveyard for 30 seconds.

Necrotic Coil: Unleash a vortex of necrotic energy and bone dealing Shadowstrike damage up to 3 foes and Shadow damage to all pierced enemies.

Graveyard: The dead erupt beneath all foes with Virulent Plague, dealing Shadow damage and Shadow damage to enemies nearby.
Rank 2 (2/2)

For each Dancing Rune Weapon active your damage is increased by 6% and your damage taken is reduced by 4%. Rank 2 (2/2)

Frostwyrm’s Fury extends the duration of an active Pillar of Frost by 4 seconds.

Enduring Strength grants an additional 8% Strength. Rank 2 (2/2)

Putrefy causes your next 1 Scourge Strike to summon a Lesser Ghoul and grants you 6% Mastery for 12 seconds.
Rank 3

When you consume a Rune, you have a chance to call a Dancing Rune Weapon to your aid for 6 seconds. Rank 3

All Frost damage increased by 10%.

Frostwyrm’s Fury deals 100% increased damage. After the Frostwyrm flies away, you may cast Frostwyrm’s Fury again to recall it at 50% effectiveness. Rank 3

Each Magus of the Dead under your command increases the damage of Necrotic Coil and Graveyard by 8%.

Dread Plague has a 20% chance to rouse the dead, causing a Lesser Ghoul to unearth and leap at the target to Putrefy at 60% effectiveness.
Demon Hunter
Devourer
Havoc
Vengeance
Midnight Eternal Hunt Untethered Rage
Rank 1

Collapsing Star always critically strikes. Rank 1

The Hunt empowers your next Eye Beam, increasing damage by 100% and affecting enemies in a wider area. Rank 1

Soul Cleaveand Spirit Bomb have a chance per soul fragment consumed to grant Untethered Rage, allowing Metamorphosis to be cast without incurring its cooldown and lasting 10 seconds.
Rank 2 (2/2)

All Cosmic damage dealt increased by 6%.

Collapsing Star’s critical strike damage is increased by 50% of your critical strike chance. Rank 2 (2/2)

The Hunt has a 30 second reduced cooldown, deals 30% more damage, and its damage over time effect applies to 4 additional enemies. Rank 2 (2/2)

Soul Cleave and Spirit Bomb can consume an additional soul fragment and deal an additional 10% damage for each consumed.
Rank 3

Entering Void Metamorphosis causes your soul to erupt, generating 5 Soul Fragments and granting immediate access to Collapsing Star. Rank 3

Blade Dance damage increased by 20%.

Fully channeling Eye Beam causes your next Blade Dance to refresh its own cooldown. Rank 3

Each cast of Soul Cleave and Spirit Bomb that does not grant Untethered Rage instead applies a stack of Seething Anger.

Seething Anger grants 1% Agility and increases your chance to gain Untethered Rage. Lasts 12 seconds and cannot be refreshed after gaining Untethered Rage.
Druid
Balance
Feral
Guardian
Restoration
Ascendant Eclipses Unseen Predator Wild Guardian Everbloom
Rank 1

Activating an Eclipse makes your next Wrath or Starfire instant. The first 2 Starsurges or Starfalls you cast during each Eclipse deal 20% increased damage. Rank 1

When you Ferocious Bite, you have a 15% chance per combo point spent to flicker to an enemy within 30 yards and deliver an Unseen Attack.

Unseen Slash: Slash the target, dealing Physical damage and additional Bleed damage over 6 seconds. If this effect is reapplied, any remaining damage is added to the new Bleed.

Unseen Swipe:Swipe nearby targets, dealing Physical damage. Damage reduced beyond 5 targets. Rank 1

After you cast Berserk or Incarnation: Guardian of the Ursoc, you gain access to Wild Guardian.

Wild Guardian: Spirits of the wild come to your aid, causing your next 2 casts of Ironfur, Maul, and Frenzied Regeneration to be echoed at 150% effectiveness. Rank 1

Lifebloom stacks every 5 seconds stacking up to 3 times.
Rank 2 (2/2)

Your critical strikes dealt during an Eclipse cause their victims to languish for an additional 24% of damage dealt for 6 seconds. Rank 2 (2/2)

Unseen Attacks increase your damage done by 15% for 5 seconds. Additional applications extend this effect. Rank 2 (2/2)

Mastery increased by 5%. Maul is always empowered and deals 40% additional Nature damage over 12 seconds. Rank 2 (2/2)

30% of Lifebloom’s healing splashes to 2 allies within 30 yards.
Rank 3

Activating an Eclipse also launches Solar or Lunar Bolts at enemies within 40 yards that always critically strike.

Solar Bolt: Entering Solar Eclipse launches Solar Bolt at 1 enemy that deals Nature damage.

Lunar Bolt: Entering Lunar Eclipse launches 3 Lunar Bolts at enemies that deal Arcane damage to them and nearby targets. Deals reduced damage beyond 5 targets. Rank 3

Tiger’s Fury causes you to perform an Unseen Attack after your next 2 combo point generating attacks. Rip and Unseen Attack damage increased by 30%. Rank 3

When you cast Wild Guardian, you gain 2 charges of Dream Guide.

While Dragonrage is active you gain Rising Fury every 6 seconds, increasing your Haste by 4%, stacking up to 5 times. Rank 1

Essence abilities have a chance to infuse your next Reversion with the power of the green dragonflight, upgrading it to Merithra’s Blessing.

Merithra’s Blessing: Spring a bloom of life from your target, healing them and up to 4 other allies within 30 yards. Blooms may heal your primary target multiple times if no nearby injured allies are present.

Any time you extend Ebon Might, your Duplicate is also extended, equal to 100% of the amount you extended Ebon Might by. Rank 2 (2/2)

Memories of the Titans’ destruction flash in your mind when you evoke the Dark Titan’s power, increasing critical strike by 8% and Haste by 8% for 6 seconds.
Rank 3

Damage dealt by Haunt has a chance to release the demonic soul haunting its target. When released, it unleashes a devastating attack before being banished back to Nathreza. Rank 3

While Dominion of Argus is active, Hand of Gul’dan refunds 1 Soul Shard.

Demons summoned by Dominion of Argus last an additional 3 seconds. Rank 3

Casting Chaos Bolt, Shadowburn, or Rain of Fire evokes an echo of the Dark Titan’s power at 40%, 50%, or 60% effectiveness respectively.

If no target is found, a random target suffering from your immolate will be selected.
Warrior
Arms
Fury
Protection
Master of Warfare Rampaging Berserker Phalanx
Rank 1

Your single target melee abilities have a chance to upgrade Slam to Heroic Strike. All modifiers and talents that affect Slam also affect Heroic Strike.

Heroic Strike: Powerfully strike an opponent, causing Physical damage and gaining a stack of Master of Warfare, allowing your attack to penetrate 3% armor for 30 seconds. Multiple applications of this effect may overlap. Rank 1

Rampage damage increased by 10% and Rampage makes you go Berserk, increasing your Strength by 3% for 8 seconds. Multiple applications of Berserk may overlap. Rank 1

Thunder Clap causes your next Shield Slam to unleash a wave of force, dealing Physical damage to all enemies in front of you, and reducing the damage they deal to you for 5% for 8 seconds.
Rank 2 (2/2)

Each stack of Master of Warfare increases Mortal Strike, Cleave, and Overpower damage by 5% and critical strike damage by 5%. Rank 2 (2/2)

Rampage’s Rage cost is reduced and damage is increased by a further 10% during Recklessness. Rank 2 (2/2)

Damage dealt by Thunder Clap and Phalanx’s wave of force is increased by 20%.
Rank 3

Heroic Strike reduces the cooldown of Colossus Smash by 2 seconds. Rank 3

Recklessness grants 3 stacks of Berserk and duration of Recklessness increased by 50%. Rank 3

While Shield Block is active, Shield Slam deals 10% additional damage and has a 20% increased chance to critically strike.
We look forward to seeing just how far you can go in your adventures with these new enhancements to your specializations. We’ll see you in Midnight!

title: No Class Tuning Planned Until March 17th - Class Tuning Roadmap for Midnight Season 1
author: POSTED 6 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has released a class tuning roadmap for Season 1 announcing that there is no class tuning planned until March 17th.
Blizzard
View Original
With the official launch of Midnight upon us, we want to take the opportunity to give you a sneak peek at our tuning and class update roadmap as we head into the first season of Midnight.

With the Midnight expansion, players will gain access to the new Apex Talents and their additional talent points as they level toward 90. Similar to how we approached The War Within class changes, we will continue to look at class performance and any emergent pain points to see where we might have opportunities to make changes during major content updates. In Midnight, many specializations have undergone large changes in order to meet our design goals for the expansion. While the scope and full list of class updates are still being worked on, we are paying close attention to both internal metrics as well as player sentiment as we assess how to proceed.

When it comes to tuning adjustments, our next planned pass is expected to arrive with the Midnight Season 1 launch on March 17. Our next pass will follow immediately afterwards utilizing data gathered during the week Heroic difficulties are available; these changes will go live with the launch of Mythic raid difficulty and Mythic+ on March 24.

Beyond these dates, we will look to perform more tuning passes based on data gathered during the starting weeks of Mythic difficulty for Season 1 and the March on Quel’Danas available the week of March 31.For these two passes we err on the side of caution with the amount of tuning we do until their respective Mythic end-bosses die to avoid being overly disruptive to progression. After these key dates we will have several check-in passes planned roughly a month apart. Once players obtain higher item level and better optimized gear sets, the throughput of several specs will shift up or down relative to others and we feel it’s important to be diligent in looking at the current state of class balance as time goes on.

For a more specific timeline we’ve listed the dates we plan to perform tuning adjustments below. Please note that this is not an exhaustive list of Season 1 tuning, just the ones immediately coming up. All of these changes will be applied with weekly restarts in each region.
March 17: Midnight Season 1 Launch
March 24: Mythic Raid Difficulty and Mythic Keystone Dungeon Launch
March 31: March on Quel’Danas Live
April 7: Post March on Quel’Danas Release Worldwide
We want to emphasize that these dates may change as needed. We may also hotfix any especially disruptive bugs or tuning issues at any point in time; the above dates are only for our currently planned tuning passes.

Thank you for your continued feedback and we look forward to seeing you in Midnight!

title: New Arator Animated Short: Son of Two Worlds - World of Warcraft: Midnight
author: POSTED 6 DAYS AGO BY POPULARTOPULAR
content:
Blizzard has released a new animated short and Character Profile focusing on Arator "The Redeemer" Windrunner!

 Join Arator for a New Journey for the Light in Midnight


Blizzard
View Original
Elf and Human. Mother and Father. Darkness and Light. For Arator, division has defined his entire life; embracing these dualities has given it purpose.
Character Profile: Arator "The Redeemer" Windrunner
Arator Windrunner bears the enduring heart of one born to a family defined by sacrifice. A half-elven child born from two of Azeroth’s greatest heroes and yet raised a stranger from them both. Shaped by loss and strengthened by faith, Arator forged ahead determined to realize a future of hope. One far removed from the heartache that defined his earliest days.
The beloved son of ranger-captain Alleria Windrunner and high general Turalyon, Arator entered the world mere months before his parents vanished amid a doomed mission beyond the demonic Dark Portal. Infant Arator was entrusted with his aunt, Vereesa Windrunner, who raised him with tenderness knowing the weight of his heritage and the legacy he carried.
Though Arator never knew their voices, he returned time and again to the Valley of Heroes in Stormwind City where statues of Alleria and Turalyon stood in solemn tribute to their ultimate sacrifice. In those quiet pilgrimages, the solitary child learned to love them despite their ever present and painful absence.
Arator followed the paths of his legendary bloodlines, ultimately choosing the one paved by his father as one of humanity’s first holy paladins. His service within the Order of the Silver Hand earned him the title “The Redeemer,” though the prophecy woven into it remained his alone to discover.
When the Dark Portal suddenly reopened and a chance to track his parents emerged once more, Arator did not hesitate. The paladin pledged his sword to the Sons of Lothar, taking up their cause as his parents had done before him to both honor them and be closer to the truth of their fate.
Years later, as the demonic Burning Legion invaded Azeroth for the final time, Arator proved himself not just to be a seeker of the past but a champion of the present. He met every trial set against him with unwavering resolve, fighting alongside his comrades of the Silver Hand and aiding the Grand Army of the Light in ending the Legion’s all-consuming threat once and for all.
Every hardship, every ordeal led him to the moment he had longed for: reunion. Yet the joy of seeing his parents again, one each family member had spent decades sacrificing for, was delicate. The infant the parents had left behind stood before them a grown man. Edged with the understanding that love can outlast lost time even when a family cannot be rebuilt in a day, they vowed to move forward together. No matter how uncertain the path forward.
In the years that followed, Arator made countless allies and continued to serve the Silver Hand with distinction. Yet the familial bond he sought most remained more complicated than ever. His family, reunited yet strained, remain pulled by duty toward different horizons. Even as fate pulls them toward separate paths, Arator remains his parents’ quiet anchor. And so, his heart endures it once more, ever holding to the hope that one day they will become the family that duty had denied.
“I am greater than the sum of my parts. I am not half anything. I am complete.”

title: Join Arator for a New Journey for the Light in Midnight
author: POSTED 6 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
As part of the Midnight level up campaign, you'll venture alongside Arator, son of Turalyon and Alleria Windrunner, as he seeks out relics of the Light.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
Blizzard
View Original
One of the available paths through the Midnight campaign, players will undertake a new questline alongside Arator, son of Turalyon and Alleria Windrunner. At the request of Alonsus Faol, you’ll recruit Arator for this continent-spanning adventure to seek out relics of the Light used by priests and paladins at the Sunwell when their reserves run dry.
Arator's mission brings new experiences to familiar locations, all while he takes another kind of journey grappling with his relationships with both his family and the Light.
Oh, the Places You (and Arator) Will Go
  
Here are just some of the places you’ll travel to:
Seek out relics of early paladins at Light's Hope Chapel and defend it from Scourge attack.
Fight through the darkened corridors of Scarlet Monastery to put to rest those who would use the Light for their own hateful deeds.
Work with Sunwalker Dezco in Hammerfall to help Arator discover the paladin he aspires to be.
Arator's story reaches its climax in the shadow of Blackrock Mountain, where alongside the Sons of Lothar he must face the weight of his father's legacy and decide once and for all which path to take as a servant to the Light.
At the end of your journey, you’ll also encounter the Arcantina for the first time. It is a refuge for the weary, gathering spot, and tavern to both Horde and Alliance. You'll also have the opportunity to claim the ground mount, the Relinquished Scarlet Charger, as a part of your journey when you complete the quest, "Relinquishing Relics".
Whichever path you take, all roads lead to an impending confrontation with Xal’atath and the Void. We look forward to seeing you in Azeroth!

title: Visit Friends' Neighborhoods for Last-Minute Endeavor Decor
author: POSTED 6 DAYS AGO BY WHISPYR
content:
The first round of player housing endeavors is nearly done, but you don't have to wait to get the housing decor you want; get it from your friends! Because Charter and Guild Neighborhoods get to choose the Endeavors to complete, every Endeavor is available at the same time if you look hard enough.

Neighborhood Endeavors Guide
Neighborhood Endeavors Guide
BUYING HOUSING DECOR FROM OTHER NEIGHBORHOODS
With Endeavors being specific to Neighborhoods, so too are their rewards. Luckily, you can hop over to a friend's neighborhood and buy the decor from a different Endeavor's vendor (and it's likely that, at the end of the Endeavor cycle, all rewards are unlocked and available). By far the easiest way to do so is to work your way down your Battle.net friend list, right-clicking them, and then selecting "View Houses".


While some neighborhoods might have the same Endeavor as yours, you're almost guaranteed to find someone with the specific Endeavor you're looking for. To buy the decor you want, just go to the town center like normal. It's important to note that you will still need to purchase the decor using Community Coupons, so make sure you have them on hand - or transfer the currency from other characters that might have some here or there.
NEIGHBORHOOD ENDEAVOR VENDORS
Endeavor decor vendors can be found in their respective town centers, usually slightly off to the side behind a set of tables. Decor from the vendors can be found on our Endeavour Guide or viewed directly from the specific vendors:
Brother Dovetail - Grummles
Harlowe Marl - Niffen
Hordranin - Draconic
Aeeshna - Ethereal
Pascal-K1N6 - Mechanical


Because Endeavors last so long, this is a great way to snag some decor ahead of when your neighborhood might actually see it - so stock up and get ready to decorate!

title: Collect Endeavor Coffer Before Endeavor Cycle Ends Soon
author: POSTED 6 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
For those in a Neighborhood that’s completed the Endeavor Initiative, be sure to collect the Endeavor Coffer for House XP and Community Coupons.

Player Housing Overview
Player Housing Overview
Neighborhood Endeavors Guide
Neighborhood Endeavors Guide
ENDEAVOR COFFER COLLECTION REMINDER
For Neighborhoods that started an Endeavor when first available, the first cycle is winding down with fewer than 10 days remaining. If you haven’t yet completed the Initiative, then you still have some time to do some tasks, earn that favor, and fill the Endeavor progress bar. Remember, completing tasks with neighbors gives 20% bonus rewards.

If you’re in a savvy Neighborhood that’s already completed the initiative, then be sure to collect the Endeavor Coffer near the Neighborhood steward and message board for additional rewards:
250 House XP
30x Community Coupons
Alliance neighborhoods can find theirs at (/way 53.03 39.99), while Horde neighborhoods have theirs at (/way 55.17 57.57).

 

 
Horde & Alliance Coffer Locations


Don’t leave these valuable rewards unclaimed. There is a 2250 XP cap for House XP that can be earned per Endeavor for tasks; coffer collection brings the total to 2500 XP. Community Coupons are spent at Endeavor vendors for themed decor items.
Did you enjoy the first Endeavor cycle? What was your favorite piece of decor added to your collection?

title: Hyper-Detailed World of Warcraft Characters, Weapons, and More by Infinity Studio
author: POSTED 7 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Infinity Studios is bringing World of Warcraft to life with hyper-detailed recreations of some of its most famous characters, from Varian Wrynn to Sylvanas and even the Lich King himself!

Modeled after his appearance in the Legion cinematic trailer, is a highly exclusive and very expensive collector's piece for World of Warcraft fans.


Although showcased and sold by Sideshow Collectables, the bust is actually made by Infinity Studio and was originally revealed at the Shanghai Wonder Festival in October 2024, alongside several other lifelike creations big and small.

Featuring an initial ¥50,000 RMB (~$7,237 USD) price tag, the same statue is now listed for only $5,005 by Sideshow Collectables, so you can actually get it at a bit of a discount now! A similar Slyvanas bust was also showcased at the Shanghai Wonder Festival, alongside Valeera Sanguinar, the Warglaives of Azzinoth, Frostmourne, and smaller figures of Illidan Stormrage, Mercy from Overwatch, and many more.


Although not for public sale at the time, Varian was listed at $7,237 USD, Valeera for $2,703 USD, and Sylvanas for $4,339 USD.



The plush Lightforged Gorehowl commemorates the 15-meter version made by NetEase. I also have one, and it's very cute.


Both busts again appeared at China Joy this past August, alongside a gigantic Arthas Menethil riding Invincible! Measuring 6.6 feet tall and nearly 6 feet wide, the larger-than-life Lich King also has a very reasonable price of ¥288,888 (~$41,815 USD).

 
 




So if you have the means, check out Sideshow Collectibles and/or Infinity Studios for some incredibly detailed statues. Those of us who don't have that kind of cash can just enjoy the pictures instead.

title: All Your Teleports in One Spot with Porter Add-On
author: POSTED 7 DAYS AGO BY IMX3
content:
When it comes to transportation, portals are a common way to instantly navigate throughout Azeroth. With Porter, all of your portals are in one easy-to-access spot!

User Interface Hub
User Interface Hub
PORTER ADDON
World of Warcraft has portals to almost everywhere throughout Azeroth. Whether it's your Hearthstone, dungeon, and raid portals, or certain toys, it can be tedious managing all of them. With Porter, all of your portals are in one place, including:
Hearthstones and cosmetic Hearthstone toys
Class teleports and portals
Equippable-teleport items (cloaks, guild tabards, rings, etc.)
Engineering toys
Unique-destination toys (Dalaran Hearthstone, Garrison Hearthstone, etc.)
Dungeon and Raid portals
Racial abilities (Dark Iron Mole Machine, Vulpera Camp)


Additionally, Porter offers Mythic+ support. Porter tracks which Keystone your character has, and will highlight the dungeon portal for that Keystone if you have earned it. Dungeon and Raid teleports are also categorized by Current vs. Legacy and will keep all seasonal dungeon content in one convenient location.

There's even a small interview happening right now with Lead Designer Toby Ragaini and Sean from Raider.IO, which covers a lot of the excitement around upcoming housing features, such as allowing players to import and export their own housing designs!

Mark your calendars for an epic community event. Attendance is free, and the event will stream live for those who cannot attend in person. Northrend Live gathers creators, players, and fans from coast-to-coast-to-coast for one immersive, high-energy experience, both in person and online.

title: Endeavor Vendor Purchase for Upgrade Crests in Latest Midnight Player Housing Weekly Quest
author: POSTED 8 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
Last week we previewed a new Midnight Player Housing quest that awarded upgrade crests. In what appears to be a rotating Housing weekly, a new quest has popped up that requires participation in Endeavors for similar rewards.

Player Housing Overview
Player Housing Overview
Neighborhood Endeavors Guide
Neighborhood Endeavors Guide
Midnight Season 1 Overview
Midnight Season 1 Overview
Table of Contents
Community Engagement Player Housing Weekly
The "No Player Power" Rule
COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT PLAYER HOUSING WEEKLY
With the weekly reset on the Midnight Beta, Housewarming is no longer available but has been replaced with the Community Engagement quest still from Vaeli, the dracthyr postmaster in Silvermoon. Again listed as Weekly in datamining, we believe this will be a new rotating Player Housing weekly quest.

 

To complete the new quest, simply make a purchase with Community Coupons from a Neighborhood Endeavor vendor available in any Neighborhood with an active Endeavor. For completing the quest, you'll be rewarded with:
Your choice of:
20x Hero Dawncrest
40x Champion Dawncrest
60x Veteran Dawncrest
500x Voidlight Marl
3x Void-Touched Augment Rune
As well as the base rewards of:
Essence of Lumber
500x Voidlight Marl
25x Community Coupons
34 16

  
In order to obtain the minimum 5x Community Coupons to make a purchase, players will need to have a Player House and engage with an active Neighborhood Endeavor. This typically involves completing Endeavor tasks or the Neighborhood initiative. Tasks can range from neighborhood maintenance activities to completing delves, defeating raid bosses, and more.


The Endeavors UI


Player Housing weeklies also award Community Coupons, so it is possible to save the previously earned coupons for this specific quest.
THE "NO PLAYER POWER" RULE
In the previous article, we covered Blizzard’s strict stance on ‘no player power from player housing’ from multiple interviews. While we asserted that these new weekly quests do break that rule, it was a fairly soft break in a bit of a gray area for a few reasons:
Fairly easy completion requirements.
Myth Dawncrest are unobtainable.
Weekly crest caps maintain quest as optional.
Increased foot traffic to neighborhoods.
While most of the above still stands for the latest quest, asking players to engage with one of Player Housing’s core systems like Neighborhood Endeavors feels like a more egregious break of the rule.

To start, players will need to own a House in a Neighborhood with an active Endeavor to earn Community Coupons. There may be a few loopholes around it like previous weekly quest rewards, but the most assured method is homeownership. Then the quest will require Endeavor task completions. Players may earn coupons passively through activities they’re already completing like Mythic+, but it definitely feels one step further than gathering friends inside a Player Home.


The Horde Endeavor Coffer


This quest will certainly increase engagement in Player Housing, but I’m not sure this one does as much for exploring other Neighborhoods and Player Houses like the previous one. If your Neighborhood doesn’t have an active Endeavor for the required vendor then you can visit a friend’s though those circumstances will be rare. That sense of adventure and showcase of design tipped the needle for me last time, and without that here this quest feels less forgivable in keeping with the original tenets of Player Housing.
Did this new quest sway your opinion on the player power in Player Housing debate? Do you mind having player power from Player Housing or how far is too far?

title: Phil Spencer Resigns: New Leadership and the Future of AI at Microsoft Gaming
author: POSTED 9 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Big changes were announced at Microsoft today, as CEO of Microsoft Gaming Phil Spencer and President of Xbox Sarah Bond have both resigned, with CoreAI President Asha Sharma and Head of Xbox Game Studios Matt Booty set to take over.

Along with Bobby Kotick, Phil Spencer was principally responsible for the $75.4 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard in 2023, so far the largest video game acquisition in history, which made Blizzard Entertainment and World of Warcraft a part of the Microsoft Corporation. Considered by many to be a "real gamer" among executives, Spencer was highly regarded by fellow players and investors alike, responsible slashing console prices, supporting backwards compatibility, launching the Xbox Game Pass, and leading a significant expansion of Microsoft's gaming business through the acquisition of Mojang, ZeniMax, and Activision Blizzard.



As current Head of Xbox Game Studios, Booty's name probably rings a bell, but Sharma's may not. The former VP of Product and Engineering at Meta and former COO of Instacart joined Microsoft in 2024 and is currently the President of CoreAI, overseeing teams and technologies powering AI across Microsoft and its enterprise customers.

EXECUTIVE EMAILS
Although no specific reason for their departure has been given, Spencer's email to staff asserts that the decision to leave was a long time coming and revealed that Asha Sharma would be taking over as the new head of Microsoft Gaming, while Mat Booty would be promoted to the position of Chief Content Officer.

Perhaps even more surprising is the revelation Sarah Bond also decided to leave Microsoft. Widely believed to be poised as Spencer's successor, very little detail was given and she has so far refrained from commenting.
Phil Spencer
Asha Sharma
Matt Booty
Satya Nadella
Phil Spencer, outgoing CEO of Microsoft Gaming said:
Subject: A New Chapter for Microsoft Gaming

When I walked through Microsoft’s doors as an intern in June of 1988, I could never have imagined the products I’d help build, the players and customers we’d serve, or the extraordinary teams I’d be lucky enough to join. It’s been an epic ride and truly the privilege of a lifetime.

Last fall, I shared with Satya that I was thinking about stepping back and starting the next chapter of my life. From that moment, we aligned on approaching this transition with intention, ensuring stability, and strengthening the foundation we’ve built. Xbox has always been more than a business. It’s a vibrant community of players, creators, and teams who care deeply about what we build and how we build it. And it deserves a thoughtful, deliberate plan for the road ahead.

Today marks an exciting new chapter for Microsoft Gaming as Asha Sharma steps into the role of CEO, and I want to be the first to welcome her to this incredible team. Working with her over the past several months has given me tremendous confidence. She brings genuine curiosity, clarity and a deep commitment to understanding players, creators, and the decisions that shape our future. We know this is an important moment for our fans, partners, and team, and we’re committed to getting it right. I’ll remain in an advisory role through the summer to support a smooth handoff.

I’m also grateful for the strength of our studios organization. Matt Booty and our studios teams continue to build an incredible portfolio, and I have full confidence in the leadership and creative momentum across our global studios. I want to congratulate Matt on his promotion to EVP and Chief Content Officer.

As part of this transition, Sarah Bond has decided to leave Microsoft to begin a new chapter. Sarah has been instrumental during a defining period for Xbox, shaping our platform strategy, expanding Game Pass and cloud gaming, supporting new hardware launches, and guiding some of the most significant moments in our history. I’m grateful for her partnership and the impact she’s had, and I wish her the very best in what comes next.

Most of all, to everyone in Microsoft Gaming, I want to say “thank you”. I’ve learned so much from this team and community, grown alongside you, and been continually inspired by the creativity, courage, and care you bring to players, creators, and to one another every day.

I’m incredibly proud of what we’ve built together over the last 25 years, and I have complete confidence in all of you and in the opportunities ahead. I’ll be cheering you on in this next chapter as Xbox’s proudest fan and player.

Phil

XBL: P3
































































Their messages are fairly standard, but the biggest question is what Sharma has planned for the future of Microsoft Gaming, particularly with her background in AI and consumer platforms rather than gaming. She appears to address this emphatically, firmly stating that games are art created by people, while condemning "AI slop," a term used to decry content created by generative artificial intelligence, often considered as lacking in effort, quality, or meaning. On the surface, this sounds promising, though as an executive overseeing the development of AI tools over the past few years, it also begs the question of whether her idea of "AI slop" really matches that of consumers.
To meet the moment, we will invent new business models and new ways to play by leaning into what we already have: iconic teams, characters, and worlds that people love. But we will not treat those worlds as static IP to milk and monetize. We will build a shared platform and tools that empower developers and players to create and share their own stories.

As monetization and AI evolve and influence this future, we will not chase short-term efficiency or flood our ecosystem with soulless AI slop. Games are and always will be art, crafted by humans, and created with the most innovative technology provided by us.
BLIZZARD ISN'T USING GENERATIVE AI... YET
Blizzard Entertainment has historically rejected the idea of using generative AI, with Lead World Artist Kristy Moret stating that "you can't replace passion" in an BlizzCon 2023 interview, and former Warcraft General Manager John Hight unequivocally stating that "we're not using generative AI within WoW" following his talk at the 2024 Games Developer Conference.

What remains to be seen is whether anything will change under Microsoft's new leadership. Earlier this month, Executive Producer Holly Longdale spoke about Blizzard's relationship with Microsoft on The Game Business Show, stating that their new parent company had been very hands off since the acquisition, and that the team hasn't felt their presence.

However, the technology has continued to spread within the industry and AI spending is higher than ever. Microsoft continues to focus on AI infrastructure, though investors have begun to show concern over whether AI spending will actually create sustainable margins, as Microsoft shares fell over 17% despite posting record operating margins this year. Much of this uncertainty revolves around their rocky relationship with OpenAI, but while the future is volatile and uncertain, we can be certain that AI isn't just going to disappear: the only question is simply how far it will go.

title: Crest Upgrade Costs Changed Again on Midnight Beta
author: POSTED 9 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
One week before the early access launch of the expansion, the crest cost of upgrading gear has been changed again.
Table of Contents
Crest Costs Nerfed in Midnight
Changes to Upgrade System in Midnight
CREST COSTS NERFED IN MIDNIGHT
We have extensively covered the Crest Cost changes in Midnight, but in the latest set of hotfixes, a curveball has been thrown to change up the crest costs once again. We've datamined some large changes to the crest economy once again:
Upgrades now require a flat 20 Crests per level (was 10/20/30/40/50 in Midnight)
Crafted pieces now require 80 Crests (was 60)
Comparing this to War Within, each upgrade costs 5 more Crests with crafted pieces requiring 20 more Crests. From our last datamining, the Crest Cap per week has been increased to 100 (was 90 in War Within), making income slightly higher. This seems like it will make gearing decisions easier, but gearing will be slightly slower when compared to War Within due to the increased cost of upgrades.
CHANGES TO UPGRADE SYSTEM IN MIDNIGHT
There have been a lot of changes to the upgrade system, so here's a quick summary of what we know. This hasn't been confirmed by Blizzard, and we are going off of datamining and what is currently live on the Midnight Beta, so this is subject to change again.
Crests now cap at 100 per week (was 90)
Crests now cost 20 per upgrade (was 15)
Crafted gear now costs 80 crests (was 60)
Each gear track now only accepts a singular type of Crest (Champion Gear accepts Champion Crests the entire time)
Each track has been standarized with 6 total tiers

title: Upgrade Achievements Cut Crest Costs by 50% in Midnight
author: POSTED 9 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In Midnight, the gear upgrade achievements now reduce crest costs for alts by 50%, which should make it much faster to gear more characters!


Credit to Zorthas on Twitter


Not to be confused with the character-specific discount removing crest costs for upgrading another piece of gear to the same item level on that particularly character, these seasonal achievements mark the point at which that character's gear has grown beyond each upgrade track, unlocking the ability to trade crests upward into their higher versions and a big discount for that particular type of crest on alts. Previously in Dragonflight and The War Within, this discount was only 33%, but has been raised to 50% in Midnight.
Adventurer of the Dawn discounts Adventurer track upgrades for alts, and allows uptrading Adventurer crests into Veteran.
Veteran of the Dawn discounts Veteran track upgrades for alts, and allows uptrading Veteran crests into Champion.
Champion of the Dawn discounts Champion track upgrades for alts, and allows uptrading Champion crests into Hero.
Hero of the Dawn discounts Hero track upgrades for alts, and allows uptrading Hero crests into Myth.
Myth of the Dawn discounts Myth track upgrades for alts.

The discount applies separately for each achievement, so in this case the Champion track is discounted, while Hero is not.


The upgrade system has undergone several other big changes in Midnight, streamlining the use of currencies to try to mitigate the amount of resource hoarding and using drops to save on upgrades.
Upgrades now require a small amount of gold instead of Valorstones
All tracks have an equal six ranks and only use their associated crest, instead of overlapping.
The number of crests required to upgrade increases with each subsequent upgrade along the track.
 

While this requires a lot more crests to finish upgrading overall, this is also probably why the discount was changed, as 33% split evenly from 15 discounted to 10, but is more awkward for upgrades costing 40 being reduced down to 26 or 27. In any event, it should reduce the amount of time farming crests on alts, making them much faster to gear out and catch up to your main.

title: Let Xal'atath Guide Your Path with New Waze Voice Pack
author: POSTED 10 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
World of Warcraft and Waze have partnered to bring a darker presence to your daily commute, with turn-by-turn directions provided by Xal’atath, Harbinger of the Void!


Just don't let Xal'atath take the wheel.


Having previously partnered with Waze on a Thrall voice pack, the Xal'atath pack offers similar features, with new icons for the user's vehicle and mood, like swapping your standard car model for a Dragonhawk! Simply download the latest version of Waze and look for the World of Warcraft banner in the menu!
Waze said:
Link
Drive with World of Warcraft on Waze.
Waze is once again teaming up with World of Warcraft, this time bringing a darker presence to your turn-by-turn directions: the Harbinger of the Void, Xal’atath.

After the heroic and noble Thrall experience, now Xal’atath offers a dark mirror. She's swapping cosmic schemes for turn-by-turn directions, offering biting remarks and cryptic instructions — a preview of her role in the upcoming World of Warcraft: Midnight.

With Xala’tath as your navigator, you can become part of her game of cat and mouse. Choose between the “Void” or “Light” Moods to show your allegiance on the Waze map, and swap your standard car for the custom “Dragonhawk” vehicle to take flight on your route.

This new driving experience is available globally in English, Spanish and German, all using the in-game voices of Xal’Atath. Simply download the latest version of Waze and look for the World of Warcraft banner in the menu or activate it now.

title: Share Your Azeroth Home with the New Pin-o-Matic Camera
author: POSTED 10 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has revealed their new promotion with Pinterest, featuring a Feat of Strength and the new Pin-o-Matic Camera, which players will use to quickly share pictures and short animations from World of Warcraft.

We previously datamined strings revealing the new photo sharing mode on the Midnight Beta, though the site it would be connected to was left ambiguous. Seemingly made to replace the previous Twitter Integration introduced in Patch 6.1, this new version seems nearly identical in form, with the most significant difference being the switch from Twitter (now X) to Pinterest. Blizzard has also launched its own World of Warcraft Pinterest Board as an example for players interested in getting started with the new feature, whether taking pictures of their home or any other in-game content.
View Original
The Housing feature in Midnight opens the door to express your creativity in Azeroth in new ways — and now there’s a way to capture, share, and discover inspiration on Pinterest with a new in-game feature. Players can connect their World of Warcraft account to their personal board and share screenshots directly from the game.

Whether you’re showing off a finished home or collecting ideas for your next build, the Pin-o-Matic Camera makes it easy to bring your vision beyond the game and connect with the housing community.
Getting Started
Connect to your Pinterest account directly from within World of Warcraft:
Open Options
Go to Gameplay → Social
Check Connect to Pinterest
Sign in through the in-game browser and authorize the connection
You can also disconnect your account at any time using the same menu.
Unlock the Pin-o-Matic Camera
The first time you connect your Pinterest account, you’ll earn the “Craft Your World” Feat of Strength ,which rewards the Pin-o-Matic Camera toy. The camera is added automatically to your Toy Box and is ready to use right away.
Capture Your Creations
Using the Pin-o-Matic Camera activates a photo mode:
Frame your shot with a capture overlay.
Choose portrait (2:3) or square (1:1) aspect ratios.
Take a photo or exit photo mode at any time.
After capturing a photo, a short animation plays before you’re prompted to create your Pin.
Create and Publish Pins
Once you’ve taken a screenshot, you can add optional titles and descriptions before publishing your Pin directly to your Pinterest account. A confirmation message lets you know when your Pin is live, and you can continue taking photos or return to gameplay.
Find Inspiration and Connect with the Community
Pinterest is also a place to explore Housing creativity from other WoW players. Browse builds, save ideas for future projects, and follow fellow creators. Inspiration can start on a board or in-game — and flow right back into your Azeroth home.
Craft Your World — For the Board!
The new Pin-o-Matic Camera makes it easy to share what you’ve built, discover new ideas, and stay connected with the WoW housing community. Looking for inspiration to get you started? Visit the official World of Warcraft Pinterest board.

title: Midnight Early Access and Worldwide Launch Schedule
author: POSTED 10 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has released a blog detailing the Midnight early access and full worldwide release, with all the new content and features coming with the new expansion!
View Original
In Midnight, players experience the next chapter in the Worldsoul Saga as things become more dire for the world of Azeroth. Xal’atath, the Harbinger, poses our greatest threat as she unleashes chaos in her wake with the ensuing invasion striking into the heart of the blood elf homelands in Eversong Woods.

Arriving over the glittering elven kingdom of Quel'thalas, the looming Voidstorm threatens to extinguish all light and cover the world in darkness. Players will level up to 90 and explore four new and reimagined zones to uncover the heart of Xal'atath's machinations while seeking out new allies among the light and shadows alike.

Face Xal’atath, the Harbinger and the Void in Midnight with Early Access launching on February 26 at 3:00 pm PST. Midnight goes live worldwide March 2 at 3:00 pm PST.
Midnight Campaign, Leveling, and Zones
You’ll first begin your journey in Eversong Woods, but it’s up to you how your story progresses beyond this first zone. There are three available paths you can tackle in any order: brave the ancient troll kingdom of Zul'Aman, investigate the gnarled hidden roots of Harandar, or accompany Arator on his journey to better understand his relationships with both his family and the Light.
The Story: Adventure Through a New Campaign
Experience the next chapter in the Worldsoul Saga as things become more dire for the world of Azeroth. Xal’atath, the Harbinger, poses our greatest threat as she unleashes chaos in her wake with the ensuing invasion striking into the heart of the blood elf homelands in Eversong Woods.
Choose Your Path
You’ll first begin your journey in Eversong Woods, but it’s up to you how your story progresses beyond this first zone. There are three available paths you can tackle in any order: brave the ancient troll kingdom of Zul'Aman, investigate the gnarled hidden roots of Harandar, or accompany Arator on his journey to better understand his relationships with both his family and the Light. Once the immediate threats to Azeroth and the Sunwell are defeated and you’ve progressed through the campaign in Eversong Woods, Zul’Aman, Harandar, and Arator’s Journey, it will be time to go on the offensive and take the fight to Voidstorm.
Explore Four New Zones
Play through four new zones as you dig deeper into the ongoing story, meet new allies, and amass power to push back against the Void.
Eversong Woods
Immerse yourself in a revitalized Eversong Woods. With the Voidstorm looming overhead and renewed with the power of the Sunwell, Eversong Woods is familiar, but changed—filled with wonder and beauty, and a sense that something bigger is unfolding. Learn more in our overview article.
Zul’ Aman
The volatile neighbors to the blood elves, the Amani trolls, reside in their capital of Zul’Aman. They are a proud people who have fought hard for their land and overcome significant hardships and challenges. Comprised of old forests and tall mountains with periodic rainfall, you’ll take a journey deeper into their culture and history as you explore all this zone has to offer. Learn more in our overview article.
Harandar
The home of the haranir is a fungal jungle where the roots of all the world trees converge. The haranir travel through the rootways to move around the world, observing Azeroth in secret but never intervening openly in the world’s affairs. In the zone lies the Rift of Aln, a primordial wound where the barrier between dreams and reality grow thin. Learn more in our overview article.
Voidstorm
This hostile land is host to Void creatures of all varieties. Everything here is hungry and ready to devour and consume weaker creatures for their power. Xal’atath and her Devouring Host rule over this world of cosmic predation. Chief among her followers are the domanaar: some of the most powerful void creatures the world has ever known.

Safety here is not guaranteed and you’ll need to work with your allies to carve your way through this vast and all-devouring wasteland. Learn more in our overview article.
Arator’s Journey
One of the available paths through the Midnight campaign, players will undertake a new questline alongside Arator, son of Turalyon and Alleria Windrunner. At the request of Alonsus Faol, you’ll recruit Arator for this continent-spanning adventure to seek out relics of the Light used by priests and paladins at the Sunwell when their reserves run dry.
Welcome to Silvermoon City!
With the press of the Void’s incursion and assault on the Sunwell, the forces of the Horde and Alliance fortify within the streets of Silvermoon City. Serving as a hub for both factions, approximately one-third of the city is designated exclusively for the Horde, while the remaining two-thirds are accessible to both faction members as they work together to push back Xal’atath’s forces. The city has been rebuilt from the ground up and the path of destruction the Scourge left behind is mostly a memory.

Soaring towers resplendent in crimson and gold lead to stunning vistas of the surrounding areas. The city is worth exploring in all its many bejeweled facets: the sin'dorei culture soaked in and celebrated having triumphed over many of their trials. See what the city has to offer in our previously published article.
Befriend and Unlock the Haranir, a New Allied Race in Midnight!
Earn the trust of the Haranir through a series of quests to unlock this new allied race. Haranir will be able to join either the Horde or the Alliance and play as a Druid, Hunter, Mage, Monk, Priest, Rogue, Shaman, Warlock, or Warrior.

An unfamiliar people from a forgotten time, Haranir will arrive with a variety of customizations available to them. They can be adorned with bioluminescent paint on their face and bodies in a variety of patterns that can be selected when customizing your character. Learn more from our previously published article.
Come Home to Azeroth with Housing
Build, decorate, and personalize your home in Azeroth with the new Housing feature. Join a Neighborhood, take on Endeavors, and make yourself at home. Learn more.

Players who with the Midnight expansion can begin setting up their new homes with Early Housing Access. Purchase your first home, establish your Neighborhood, and begin designing your space with the use of your decor collection. Experience all Housing has to offer when the expansion goes live!
Demon Hunter Devourer Specialization and Void Elf Demon Hunters
Introducing the Devourer Demon Hunter, a third specialization that utilizes the power of the Void instead of Fel, like other Demon Hunters. This glaive-wielding, soul-harvesting, and planet-crushing spellcaster operates from mid-range with the full suite of mobility you'd expect from Demon Hunters. Use specialization and Hero Talents to augment your playstyle, enabling you to dive into melee and unleash devastating combo attacks.

With the introduction of a new specialization, Demon Hunters also gain a new Hero Talent tree—Annihilator. This new tree is shared between Devourer and Vengeance Demon Hunters. Devourer Demon Hunters will also gain access to the Scarred Hero Talent tree (formerly called Fel Scarred).
Players will also gain access to the new Void Elf Demon Hunter combination through a short quest line as a part of the Midnight pre-expansion content update, along with the Devourer specialization. Demon Hunter Void Elves include new metamorphosis visuals and the Void Elf racial Trait: Spatial Rift.

Learn more in our previously published article.
Unleash Your Skills in Midnight's New Delves
Take on new challenges through ten new Delves and one new seasonal Nemesis Delve alongside a new NPC companion—legendary blood elf rogue Valeera Sanguinar. You’ll experience more of the story of Midnight interwoven through new quests and even experience the great outdoors—taking the adventure outside for the first time. Learn more.
Battle through Three New Raids
Season one of Midnight begins the week of March 17, and with it, players will take their first steps into the Voidspire and Dreamrift raids with the March on Quel’Danas raid released shortly after. Face off against nine bosses split across three raid dungeons over the course of Midnight Season 1. Learn more in our Midnight Raid Overview and Schedule.
Systems and Activities
Pull Up a Chair in the Arcantina
A refuge to the road-weary and those seeking a respite from the trials and travails of the life of an adventurer, the Arcantina serves as a crossway for all kinds. It is a magical tavern at the crossroads of nowhere and everywhere catering to travelers and heroes from all walks of life. It’s here you’ll meet up with some familiar faces for stories and quests sending you back out into Azeroth once more. Learn more.
Go on the Hunt with the Prey System
Track powerful targets throughout the zones of Midnight in three difficulties: Normal, Hard, and Nightmare. Successfully find and defeat your quarry to earn valuable new rewards as you progress through the system. Learn more.
PvP Updates
Take Arms in a New Battleground: Slayer’s Rise: Set in the Voidstorm, this 40 vs 40 epic battleground will pit the Horde and Alliance against each other in a race for control and conquest. Players with War Mode turned on will also be able to engage in World PvP in the outdoor area.
User Interface Updates
The User Interface is introducing powerful new features, improved customization options, cleaner raid frames, expanding display-scaling controls and more. You’ll be able to configure how information and damage meters appear within the base UI and customize them to fit your personal play style without the need to download additional add-ons. Learn more about some of the changes and updates to Nameplates, Damage Meters, Boss Alerts, and more in our previously published article .
Transmogrification Updates
Take your transmog game further with the ability to unlock and save full outfit sets across characters for any occasion. The updated transmog system also provides an additional opportunity for adding more customization and self-expression. You’ll be able to unlock and save dozens of outfits. Unlocking outfit slots will cost gold, but once they are unlocked, switching between saved outfits is free.

Each outfit can be added to your action bar, allowing you to manually update your look any time you want without having to visit a transmog vendor. Whether you’re fighting in a dungeon, exploring a new area, or taking a rest in your house, you’ll be able to dress for the moment. Learn more from our previously published article.

Please note that previously applied transmogs and legacy outfits have been converted into Custom Sets.
Player vs. Player Training Grounds
For players new to Player vs. Player (PvP) combat, you can learn the PvP ropes in the Training Grounds. Players will join a battle against smart game-controlled opponents in Arathi Basin, Silvershard Mines, and Battle for Gilneas. You’ll become more comfortable with how these battlegrounds work and how classes can work within them, gaining confidence and opening the door to more PvP experiences in the game. While you can expect more victories than losses, the game-controlled opponents will not be pushovers. Skill and coordination will be required to achieve those victories!
Midnight Brings Journeys to Your Adventures
Engage with World of Warcraft content in a meaningful way thanks to a brand-new feature tab in their Adventure Guide—Journeys—with a significant update to how you track your in-game progress, once Midnight launches.
Level Up Your Talents in Midnight
With the release of Midnight, a new adventure path opens up, raising the level cap to ninety. Along with these additional levels, we’re expanding talent trees with exciting new abilities and introducing what we call Apex talents.
Updated Leveling Experience
New Player Experience Updates
Bring your friends and family into Azeroth with an updated New Player Experience in Exile’s Reach. They’ll learn more about World of Warcraft while honing their character’s skills and diving into the story of Dragonflight along the way.
Returning Player Experience
Return to Azeroth with confidence as you undertake a new focused experience. You’ll get your feet wet without the weight of trying to discern where you left off last and get back into action as you reacquaint yourself with your character.
Class Combat Design Updates
Every class in World of Warcraft receives major combat design updates. This means new rotations, stronger class identities, and fresh talent choices.

Across all of the classes, talent trees have been rebuilt with fewer mandatory filler points and more paths that support specific playstyles, letting you tailor your character to the gameplay that you enjoy the most.
Get Bonus Content from the Midnight Pre-Purchase
Pre-purchase World of Warcraft: Midnight Epic Edition and unlock a slew of benefits to aid you in your adventures!
Get Early Access to Housing
Put down roots and begin designing your dream home in Azeroth. With Housing Early Access, you'll get a special preview of the new feature—explore, customize, and decorate with a limited selection before the full suite of options arrives with the launch of the expansion.

Midnight Housing Decor
Adorn your new home in Azeroth with Midnight-themed decor, including the Light-Infused Rotunda, Void-Corrupted Rotunda, Light-Infused Fountain, Void-Corrupted Fountain, and four iconic character Paintings.

Voidlight Surger
Embrace both the Light and the Void when you take to the skies on the Voidlight Surger flying mount, which cycles its appearance with unique visual effects!

Enhanced Level 80 Character Boost
Prepare for Midnight with this Enhanced Character Boost that will have you ready to begin leveling in Quel'Thalas.

Transmogs, Mounts, Pets, and More!
Get the Lightstrider and Voidstrider transmog sets, the Lightwing and Voidwing Dragonhawk flying mounts, the Doomfeathers and Hopeflutter Dragonhawk pets, additional Trader's Tender, and 30 Days of Game Time.
Visit the Shop
Choose Your Edition
Epic Edition
Learn more about Liadrin in her Character Profile.
[/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
