title: Midnight Season 1 Raid Overview and Schedule
author: POSTED 10 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has released a blog highlighting the release schedule and overview of the upcoming raids!
Blizzard
View Original
Season one of Midnight begins the week of March 17, and with it, players will take their first steps into the Voidspire and Dreamrift raids with the March on Quel’Danas raid released shortly after. Check out the full release schedule and preview of the bosses you’ll encounter.
Schedule
All unlocks are with each region's weekly maintenance reset, unless otherwise noted.

March 17
Voidspire – Normal, Heroic, and Raid Finder Wing 1
Dreamrift – Normal, Heroic, and Raid Finder Wing
March 24
Voidspire – Mythic, Raid Finder Wing 2, and Story Mode
Dreamrift –Mythic
March 31
Voidspire – Raid Finder Wing 3
March on Quel’Danas – Normal, Heroic, and Mythic
April 7
March on Quel’Danas – Raid Finder and Story Mode
Voidspire
From high above the Voidstorm, Xal’atath has gathered the Devouring Host beneath her dark banner. The towering Voidspire looms like a spear of darkness, a citadel guarded by an army of the void. The forces of the light – led by Alleria Windrunner and the Champions of Azeroth – must rally for one last desperate assault before all is lost.

Location: Voidstorm
Raid Bosses: 6
Difficulties: Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, Mythic, Story Mode
Level: 90
Raid Finder Minimum Item Level: 220
Imperator Averzian
Imperator Averzian wages a relentless campaign, seeking not conquest, but annihilation. His entire will is bent to a singular ambition: to rend the veil between worlds and carve a breach into the Void itself. Through this wound, his infinite host shall pour forth, drowning Azeroth in darkness.
Vorasius
A colossal predator born in the wastes of the Voidstorm, Vorasius grew to an incredible size fueled by an endless hunger. Summoned to the battle raging at the base of the Voidspire by Voidlight Everdawn, this colossal monster now feasts on both sides.
Fallen-King Salhadaar
After turning on Xal’atath, the fallen king Salhadaar has become a captive of the very Shadowguard he once commanded. Tortured, delirious, and infused with the Void, he awaits his death—or a chance to win his people’s freedom from Xal’atath’s grasp.
Vaelgor & Ezzorak
Born as clutchmates in the red dragonflight, Vaelgor and Ezzorak were once the guardians of all living things. Now, twisted by the dark will of Xal’atath, all that remains of their former selves is their unyielding bond to one another.
Lightblinded Vanguard
Once paragons of the Light, Senn, Bellamy, and Lightblood have surrendered to their own blinding zealotry—their faith perverted into a ruthless fanaticism that now compels them to smite all who oppose them.
Crown of the Cosmos
High atop the Voidspire, Xal’atath marshals her dark army from across the Voidstorm. Aided by Turalyon, Arator, and Alleria, the mortals of Azeroth must confront her before she unleashes the might of the Voidspire against her enemies.
The Dreamrift
The dreamrift exists deep within the Rift of Aln, where the dreams of Aln’hara took form long ago. Dark manifestations have grown within the rift in Aln’hara’s absence, devouring each other in an endless cycle. Even the brave haranir dare not delve too deep, for now something greater and more terrible lurks within the Dreamrift’s depths.

Location: Harandar
Raid Bosses: 1
Difficulties: Normal, Heroic, Mythic, Raid Finder, and Story Mode
Level: 90
Raid Finder Minimum Item Level: 220
Chimaerus the Undreamt God
When Aln’hara was taken from her cradle, only pain and madness were left to shape the chaos of the rift. Chimaerus feasted on the void left behind, gorging itself on other nightmarish manifestations. Now it stands as the monster within the rift; a being of agony and rage.
March on Quel’Danas
Darkness has eclipsed the Isle of Quel’Danas, clouding the skies above Silvermoon City. With Arator the Redeemer by their side, the Champions of Azeroth must march on the Sunwell before the light of the blood elves is lost forever.

Location: Quel’Thalas
Raid Bosses: 2
Difficulties: Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, Mythic, Story Mode
Level: 90
Raid Finder Minimum Item Level: 227
Belo’ren, Child of Al’ar
One of the last clutch of Al’ar, Belo’ren was raised for the purpose of protecting the Isle of Quel’Danas. In the aftermath of the Voidspire, Belo’ren has been irradiated by Void. Confused and freshly hatched, the phoenix is focused on its sole task: Keep any invaders from reaching the Sunwell.
Midnight Falls
Once a creature of Light, L’ura fell to the Void long ago on Argus, her essence siphoned by Alleria Windrunner in the Seat of the Triumvirate. Freed from her bindings, L’ura’s dark energies now threaten the very Sunwell itself.
Rally your allies and prepare to face the challenges ahead in Midnight.

title: Farm Fast Darkfuse Solutions Reputation with The G.O.L.E.M. of Progress
author: POSTED 10 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
There's a new method for farming Darkfuse Solutions reputation, allowing groups of players to spawn an endless amount of mobs, collecting Market Research even faster than farming trash piles!
THE WINDS OF PROGRESS
The The G.O.L.E.M. of Progress is a patch 11.1 local story questline in which an inventor seeks to improve life in the Undermine and while the full quest awards the G.O.L.E.M, Jr. toy, Darkfuse enthusiasts are more concerned with the first step, repeatedly respawning Darkfuse Bully to farm Market Research. The downside of this method is that you may have already completed the quest with no way to repeat it again, though alts can be used just as well, since Research was made warbound transferrable in Patch 11.1.


The instigator spawns bullies, which can be repeatedly killed within a raid group, without advancing the quest.


This final week before Midnight early access begins is also an excellent time to take advantage of this farm, as the Winds of Mysterious Fortune event grants even more bonus reputation with each turn-in until February 24th. So, get a couple friends together and check out the simple step-by-step process below.
Adamwcz said:
Link
This quest can be used to farm Darkfuse Solutions rep faster than farming trash piles, assuming you have an effective (non-AFK) group.

After accepting the quest, there is a Darkfuse Instigator NPC you can talk to at /way 28.25, 64.18.
When you click his dialog option, a set of three hostile Darkfuse Bully spawn instantly. They can drop Market Research.
If you are in a raid group, you will be able to spawn a new set the moment the last of the three dies.

How to perform the rep farm most efficiently:
Prepare with some Experimental Goblin-FUEL Supplement and Prototype Auto-Advertiser in order to increase your Market Research drop rate.
Get a group of 5 people, convert to raid.
One person with the quest will be the clicker - they must prioritize clicking the dialog option ASAP when the last bully dies. Here is an addon to auto-select the gossip option as soon as you interact with the Instigator. So if the clicker has this addon, they need only spam right-click the instigator, or spam their interact key.
The other 4 people will be the killers - they must prioritize killing the three Darkfuse Bully ASAP so that the clicker can summon more mobs sooner.
More people having the quest doesn't help; you can only have one set of 3 bullies up at a time, so it is all about the kill speed and gossip click delay.

title: Unleash Your Skills in Midnight's New Delves
author: POSTED 10 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
From the expansive depths to sprawling outdoor zones, explore the 10 new Delves and battle the Seasonal Nemesis alongside the legendary blood elf rogue Valeera Sanguinar.
 Midnight Season 1 Overview
Midnight Season 1 Overview
Blizzard
View Original
Take on new challenges through ten new Delves and one new seasonal Nemesis Delve alongside a new NPC companion—legendary blood elf rogue Valeera Sanguinar. You’ll experience more of the story of Midnight interwoven through new quests and even experience the great outdoors—taking the adventure outside for the first time.
Experience new layouts and enemies with the same variety and replayability as you have grown to love. We’re also taking a couple of Delves into outdoor spaces where you’ll be able to use ground mounts to get around more easily. Players will also experience new quests to carry you through the overarching story in the zone.

Meet up with Valeera Sanguinar in Silvermoon City to get started on your journey through the various Delves and the stories they have waiting for you. Make sure to check the Delver’s Guide while you’re there to find out which Delves are bountiful that day. If you’re doing a little achievement hunting, you’ll also be able to mouseover the map to see what Story Variants the Delve is running. Each Delve has several storylines to play through.

Check in with Naleidea Rivergleam to view all the Delve Treasures available to purchase with Undercoins.You can also speak with Telemancer Astrandis to spend your Voidlight Marl on Renown track items and Housing decor.
Twilight Crypts
Sunkiller Sanctum

The legendary blood elf rogue Valeera Sanguinar will also join you as a new NPC companion taking on all three support roles players are familiar with.
This is just the beginning of what we have in store for Delves in Midnight. We look forward to joining you in these new adventures.

title: Midnight Datamining Reveals Possible Turbo Boost-Like Mechanic for Midnight Season 1
author: POSTED 11 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
A new system called the Voidforge seems to be unlocked in Midnight Season 1 which provides new ways to collect and upgrade items.
Table of Contents
What is the Voidforge?
Speculation on the Voidforge
Unlocking the Voidforge
What is Midnight's Turbo Boost?
Upgrading Items in Midnight
WHAT IS THE VOIDFORGE?
The Voidforge is located in Voidstorm and maintained by Decimus. By talking to Decimus, players pick up the quest, Building the Voidforge which requires players to collect Elementary Voidcore Shard from raids, dungeons and bountiful delves.

 

The rewards from this quest are Transmute Elemental Voidcore and there's also a notification that this quest line unlocks The Voidforge which grants "new ways to collect and upgrade powerful items in Midnight Season 1."

The Voidforge
Instant
Gain access to the Voidforge, granting you new ways to collect and upgrade powerful items in Midnight Season 1.


After completing the quest chain, which seems to be 6 quests long, you unlock the Voidforge.
SPECULATION ON THE VOIDFORGE
While we know the details of the quest chain to unlock the Voidforge, not much is actually known about what the Voidforge actually does. Below, we speculate on what the Voidforge does based on various datamining clues, but this is unconfirmed.
UNLOCKING THE VOIDFORGE
In order to unlock the Voidforge, you need to complete the quest chain. We speculate that this is a 6 week quest chain due to Transmute Elemental Voidcore which mentions:

The Voidforge requires 6 Elemental Voidcores for its completion.


The big unknown question is if the quest chain unlocks right away with Season 1, or will it unlock midway through the season like a Turbo Boost?
WHAT IS MIDNIGHT'S TURBO BOOST?
Based on a hidden currency that we've datamined, this seems to be the new Turbo Boost of Midnight. [DNT] 12.0 Midseason - Voidforge Unlock - Turn-In Tracker has been datamined which links the 12.0 Midseason to the Voidforge. This follows the last two Turbo Boosts which also had hidden currencies clearly linking the Midseason to the Turbo Boost, [DNT] 11.1.5 Midseason - Turbo-Boost Quest Turn-In Tracker and [DNT] 11.2.5 Midseason - Turbo-Boost Quest Turn-In Tracker.

From this, it seems reasonable to suspect that the Voidforge will be a Midseason boost, the question is exactly how?

 UPGRADING ITEMS IN MIDNIGHT
What exactly does unlocking the Voidforge do? The answer is, unfortunately, we can't find any indication of how this would work from datamining. All we have to go off of is the tooltip of the Voidforge:

Gain access to the Voidforge, granting you new ways to collect and upgrade powerful items in Midnight Season 1.


Because this is a forge, it's entirely possible that you will get to "craft" gear or upgrades on your gear. From a "collect" standpoint, the system could be:
Similar to Valor Points from Wrath, where you get currency from bosses and spend it on gear.
Craft wanted gear from unwanted gear (trade 3 unused trinkets for 1 wanted trinket).
Similar to the Dinar or Puzzling Cartel Chip systems
From an upgrade standpoint, possible speculations include:
Crafting additional upgrade tracks on your items
Upgrading a piece into the next tier (turning a Hero piece into a Myth piece)
Crafting an omni-track onto an upgrade similar to Dragonflight Season 4
But until the Voidforge is unlocked on live servers or the Beta, this is just speculation. What do you want to see from the Voidforge?

author: POSTED 11 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced that they are testing the Midnight Launch on Friday, February 20th!
Blizzard
View Original
Please join us in the Midnight Beta on a special test realm for our launch test on Friday, February 20 at 1:30 p.m. PST.

We will run the test for one hour, and during that time, other Beta realms will be closed.

We’re looking forward to seeing you there!

title: Watch Midnight Pre-Patch Trailer to Earn Discord Currency
author: POSTED 11 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
A new Discord quest is available through February 25th is asking users to watch a short Midnight Pre-Patch expansion trailer to earn 700 orbs, which you can finish in under two minutes with no streaming required!

 

Found in the lower left corner of the app, or in the Quests section of the Direct Messages panel, most of Discord's tasks ask users to stream their gameplay for a certain amount of time, but this one simply requires watching the Midnight trailer. Lasting just over a minute, most of us have probably already seen it by now, but its a very quick and low effort way to earn a handful of Discord's virtual Orb currency.


Good question...


Redeemable for perks like Nitro, profile effects, and badges, Discord Orbs are a virtual currency earned exclusively by completing quests, redeemable for perks like Nitro, profile effects, and badges. While 700 orbs won't exactly get you much, there's no cost, no premium real-money purchase, and you can simply save them until another easy to complete quest comes along. There are quite a few to choose from, including six other similar video quests, so you should be able to acquire enough Orbs to get at least a few days of Nitro in less than ten minutes time.


You're not going to get much with just 700 Orbs, but they may help you get something eventually!


Discord has been under fire recently for announcing new age verification requirements coming this March, in which some users will be required to submit face or ID scans in order to access the full range of communities and servers, otherwise being locked into a "teen-appropriate experience." While spokespeople have urged that most existing adult users will be able to bypass this requirement using a new "age inference system" to automatically determine whether they're already adults, it has left users and critics alike skeptical and on edge.... resulting in an overwhelming surge of new users for competitor TeamSpeak instead. Despite this upcoming uncertainty, however, Discord currently remains the foremost platform for World of Warcraft communities, with Blizzard also looking to expand their presence through the World of Warcraft Ambassador's Program, launching on February 26th.

title: Take the Fight to Xal’atath’s Seat of Power, Voidstorm
author: POSTED 11 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
Devour or be devoured. Blizzard showcases the all-consuming cosmic predation of Voidstorm, the seat of Xal’atath’s power and home to the most powerful void creatures the domanaar.

If you're excited to dig into the zones ahead of Midnight's official launch, we've got you covered with our Zone Guides. Check out places of interest, new treasures, and Rare rewards to snag once the latest expansion arrives.

Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
 Voidstorm Rares & Treasures
Voidstorm Rares & Treasures
Blizzard
View Original
This hostile land is host to Void creatures of all varieties. Everything here is hungry and ready to devour and consume weaker creatures for their power.

Once the immediate threats to Azeroth and the Sunwell are defeated and you’ve progressed through the campaign in Eversong Woods, Zul’Aman, Harandar, and Arator’s Journey, it will be time to go on the offensive and take the fight to Voidstorm.
At a Glance:
New Zone- Voidstorm: Void suffuses this hostile zone of all-consuming cosmic predation. The creatures who reside here are rife with deep hunger and keen to devour anything and anyone to gain power. Alongside our allies, we’ll storm the Voidspire raid here as we take the fight to Xal’atath.
Void elves: The ren'dorei were once banished from Silvermoon for their research into the Void, yet now step forward to use their expertise to protect their home. Though the power of the Voidstorm threatens to overwhelm them, their knowledge may be our greatest asset.
Faction: The Singularity
Voidstorm
Xal’atath and her Devouring Host rule over this world of cosmic predation. Chief among her followers are the domanaar: some of the most powerful void creatures the world has ever known.

Safety here is not guaranteed and you’ll need to work with your allies to carve your way through this vast and all-devouring wasteland.
   New Activity:Stormarion Assault
Protect the Void Elves as they activate a Singularity Anchor to purge Stormarion Citadel of remnant Void anomalies. This is an action-packed combat scenario where player skill and well-placed defenses are crucial to holding back relentless waves of attackers.
Dungeons
Nexus-Point Xenas
A fractured facility teetering on the edge of collapse. Once a hub of innovation, its core now pulses with unstable void and arcane energy. Brave adventurers must navigate its shifting wings, confront voidwalking guardians, and uncover the truth behind its unraveling power.
Bosses
Chief Corewrite Kasreth: Chief Corewrite Kasreth and the Shadowguard Ethereals once upheld flawless energy flow within the Nexus-Point’s Corespark Engine. But Lorthraxion’s radiant disruption shattered their balance. Now, cloaked in mistrust and weary of outsiders, they labor to restore the arcane harmony that once sustained the Nexus-Point.
Corewarden Nysarra: Corewarden Nysarra guards Nexus-Point Xenas with waning strength. Lightscarred by Lothraxion’s assault, she broods in her wing, wounded and waiting. The Nullward stirs with battle, and her wrath sharpens for any who dare approach.
Lothraxion: Blinded by betrayal, Lothraxion storms the Nexus-Point alone. With its defenses shattered, he prepares to breach the Nexus Core, risking a chain eruption that could annihilate Silvermoon and ripple across Azeroth itself.
Voidscar Arena
The Voidscar Arena awaits worthy challengers! Overseer Charonus has filled the arena with opponents plucked from across Azeroth, forcing them to participate in his games.
Bosses
Taz’Rah: The domanaar’s prized champion, Taz’Rah, is forced to fight against his will. Taz’Rah demands his own death from every opponent, hoping each battle will finally end his suffering.
Atroxus: Imprisoned by Charonus, this starving behemoth is hoping for a new victim to sate his appetite. Atroxus’s sheer size is almost as dangerous as the poison it spews.
Charonus: The overly confident and ostentatious owner of the Voidscar Arena, Charonus, is impressed by the fighting ability of Azerothians and demands them for his grand collection.
New challenges and great dangers lie in wait within Voidstorm. Fight or become the devoured by the unrelenting Void in Midnight.

title: Befriend and Unlock the Haranir, a New Allied Race in Midnight!
author: POSTED 11 DAYS AGO BY IMX3
content:
The Midnight expansion brings a brand new allied race, the Haranir. Learn about the history of the Haranir, along with playable classes, and more!

Haranir Allied Race Guide
Haranir Allied Race Guide
 Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
Blizzard
View Original
Play through the Midnight campaign to earn the trust of a new allied race, the Haranir. You’ll learn about their history along the way and explore the wilds of their home in Harandar.

Quick Facts:
Home: Harandar, a dangerous fungal jungle aglow with bioluminescence nestled amidst the roots of the World Trees.
Unlockable Allied Race through the level-up campaign
Available Factions: Horde or Alliance
Classes: Druid, Hunter, Mage, Monk, Priest, Rogue, Shaman, Warlock, or Warrior
Mount: Ivory Grimlynx
 
Met during The War Within, the haranir story comes to light in Midnight. Serving as ancient guardians of the rootways, they are led by a council of elders. Ferocious, watchful guardians, the haranir keep an ever-present vigil over the wild domains of their long-absent goddess, in hopes that she might one day return. Some haranir choose to break their lingering connection to join the Shul’ka—defenders against horrors within Harandar. A young haranir, known as Orweyna, is one of those with a very strong connection to her goddess, leading her beyond the bounds of her home and against the haranir traditions of seclusion.

Orweyna: A young haranir, Orweyna was first introduced in The War Within. Her race is little known and has remained reclusive deep under the roots of the world trees. She will be pivotal in introducing us to her people, their culture, and what they are about. Despite being young, she answers the call of her goddess to go where she’s led—even if it means incurring the ire of her people.
             
An unfamiliar people from a forgotten time, Haranir will arrive with a variety of customizations available to them. They can be adorned with bioluminescent paint on their face and bodies in a variety of patterns that can be selected when customizing your character.
The champions of Azeroth will need all the allies they can get.

title: Explore the Bioluminescent Jungles of Harandar in Midnight
author: POSTED 11 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
In a new preview, Blizzard teases the bioluminescent fungal jungles of Harandar for Midnight - home of the Haranir, new dungeons, and the overgrown Lightbloom.

If you're excited to dig into the zones ahead of Midnight's official launch, we've got you covered with our Zone Guides. Check out places of interest, new treasures, and Rare rewards to snag once the latest expansion arrives.

Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
 Harandar Rares & Treasures
Harandar Rares & Treasures
Blizzard
View Original
The home of the haranir is a fungal jungle where the roots of all the world trees converge. The haranir travel through the rootways to move around the world, observing Azeroth in secret but never intervening openly in the world’s affairs.

After completing your journey through Eversong Woods, Harandar is one of three options at the Scouting Map in the Sanctum of Light that you can choose from.
At a Glance:
New Zone- Harandar: The home of the haranir found among the roots of the world trees. It is a fungal jungle alight with bioluminescence, where its people live in harmony with the nature around them.
Haranir: A reclusive people, they serve as ancient guardians of the rootways and are led by a council of elders.
Faction: Hara’ti
Harandar: Home to the Haranir
Travel into the very roots of Azeroth to the home of the Haranir. It is a land teeming with rich plant life and grounded by the people who live in harmony with nature. In the zone lies the Rift of Aln, a primordial wound where the barrier between dreams and reality grow thin. There, the echoes of the Goddess’ anguish are powerful enough to drive most haranir mad. Horrifying, half-formed creatures emerge from within, threatening all of Harandar. Standing against these horrors are the Shul’ka—mystical and mysterious warriors who silence their connection to the goddess to allow them to safely traverse the Rift and defend Harandar from the horrors that dwell within.
 New Activity: Legends of the Haranir
Step into the forgotten past of the Hara’ti Tribe using magical paintings to embark on bite-sized lore-rich journeys. Each adventure reveals more of the haranir’s mysterious history.
Dungeon
The Blinding Vale
The Blinding Vale sprouted instantly. From within the Vale, rutaani emerged and spread the Lightbloom in their wake.

After the events in Harandar, Orweyna began surgical strikes to keep the Lightbloom in check. But the hideous glare of the Blinding Vale hides a garden where the Lightbloom cultivates new creatures and magic to drive forward their one fanatical imperative. Growth unstoppable.
Bosses
Lightblossom Trinity: The lightbloom has suffered a terrible defeat, but like the rutaani it imbues, it changes. These Lightblossomed rutaani fanatically propagate their vicious and invasive garden.
Ikuzz the Light Hunter: Ikuzz is a fearsome carnivore who once stalked prey throughout Harandar. Now the Light Hunter has succumbed to the overwhelming power of the Lightbloom, and his insatiable hunger serves its growth.
Lightwarden Ruia: The once-respected Rootwarden Ruia could not defeat the Lightbloom on his own. His wounded pride led him to drawing power from the Lightbloom and using it to punish all others instead of aiding his allies.
Ziekket: Born from the Lightbloom’s epicenter, Ziekket was spawned and incubated by Light-infested ruaani to create an unstoppable engine of the Lightbloom’s expansion.
Ride the rootways into the depths of a primordial world of bioluminescent splendor, delve into the history of the haranir, and shore up new allies in the fight against the looming darkness of the Void in Midnight.

title: Hunt or Be Hunted with the Prey System in Midnight
author: POSTED 11 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has released a blog covering the upcoming Prey System in Midnight!

 Everything About Prey
Blizzard
View Original
A new opt-in hunting system, Prey, lets you pursue powerful targets across the Midnight expansion zones.
Join the hunt by speaking with Magister Astalor Bloodsworn in Murder Row in Silvermoon City. Here, you’ll take on the challenge of hunting a specific target.
Once you have a target, you can continue your travels through the zones as you normally would. You will also be able to engage with several new Prey-specific gameplay mechanics and unique World Quests. Stay on your toes or your prey may find you before you find them. How and when you detect your prey is meant to be unpredictable, and it could take any number of activities or discoveries before you see them—or they see you.
Unlock three difficulty levels as you progress: Normal, Hard, and Nightmare. While in Normal difficulty other players will be able to come to your aid and help you take down your target in the outdoor world. Once you’ve reached Hard and Nightmare difficulties, you’ll need to contend with extra abilities on your enemy called Torments that make both the hunt and the final fight considerably more challenging. You and your friends will be on your own in your final encounter to take them down. No raid groups here!
Prey Rewards
Earn a variety of cosmetic rewards to add to your collections, including mounts such as mana wyrm-themed mounts and transmog appearances. You will also be able to earn power rewards up to the Hero track and will also contribute to the Great Vault outdoor activity slot each week.
Will you be the hunter or the hunted?

title: Watch the Liadrin Animation: All That is Sacred
author: POSTED 11 DAYS AGO BY IMX3
content:
Blizzard has just released a new animated short featuring Lady Liadrin and the crucial role she has played as a protector of the Sunwell.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
Blizzard
View Original
In Azeroth’s darkest hour, Liadrin stands against the Void, knowing that with the Light by her side, darkness will never prevail.
Character Profile: Lady Liadrin
Lady Liadrin walked the path of devotion as a priestess of the Holy Light, until her prayers went unanswered as her world drowned in ash and undeath. In that moment her faith in the Light shattered and soon only enmity remained.

As the Scourge descended on Silvermoon during the Third War, it was not the undead nor sorcery that ultimately broke the high elven lines but treachery from within. In the chaos that followed, the kingdom of Silvermoon was brought to its knees and their beloved Sunwell, the very heart of their culture, was corrupted.

Amid the ashes of their kingdom, the price of subsisting was steep. Prince Kael’thas was forced to order the destruction of the Sunwell to save those that remained. Deprived of the arcane energies from the Sunwell, addiction clawed at the survivors. Unrelenting, maddening, and fatal. In desperation, the blood elves turned to new methods, siphoning energy from external sources.

When a captured naaru, a being suffused with energies of the Holy Light, was delivered to Quel’Thalas, the magisters discovered a dangerous truth: the Light could be stolen. Liadrin, devastated by the Light’s failure to protect her people, accepted this method without hesitation. Thus, the Blood Knights were born. Paladins who did not seek the Light’s blessing but its servitude. And Liadrin would lead them as their matriarch.

Following invasion and betrayal, the reconstituted Sunwell fell to corruption once more. Lor’themar considered destroying it as they had before when hope appeared in the form of Prophet Velen. Drawing on the essence of the same naaru siphoned by the Blood Knights, Velen transformed the Sunwell into a radiant source of both Holy Light and arcane energy.

The restoration of the Sunwell marked a turning point for the blood elves. For Liadrin, it was a moment of reckoning. She and her Blood Knights renounced their methods and chose to walk in the Light not as conquerors, but as its champions.

Liadrin is a paragon of both conviction and consequence. She is a devout adherent of the Light not through blind faith but through understanding its purpose and responsibility. Tempered by trials and forever transformed by her journey from doubt to devotion.

"…the Light showed me that I was not lost. It helped me to find the strength to survive in spite of all that had happened and all the evil I had wrought."

Now is the time to make your stand.

title: New Heirloom Warglaives Available in the Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 12 DAYS AGO BY POPULARTOPULAR
content:
Blizzard has released a new Heirloom Warglaive in the Midnight Pre-Patch! The Sharpened Arcanite Warglaive can be purchased from either Krom Stoutarm in Ironforge or Estelle Gendry in Undercity/Orgrimmar for 650 gold.

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
 Twilight Ascension Pre-Patch Event
Twilight Ascension Pre-Patch Event




With the Love is in the Air holiday event currently ongoing, players don't even need to spend gold to upgrade this new Heirloom. While Ancient Heirloom Scabbard can be purchased for 200 Love Token, subsequent upgrades will cost 450, or a total of 2,450 Love Tokens to fully upgrade this weapon. This might not be the most efficient way to spend holiday currency, but for those who don't want to spend the gold, it is an option.

It's been a while since we've seen any new Heirloom items, especially weapons. Since the rework of Heirlooms in Shadowlands, many players don't see them as a necessity for leveling any longer, but for collectors (or in this case, players who really want to level new Demon Hunters), the addition of these Warglaives shows that the concept hasn't been abandoned.

title: Now Fixed: Blizzard Reenables Warband Banks
author: POSTED 12 DAYS AGO BY WHISPYR
content:
After a hotfix, Blizzard has restored access to the Warband Banks for all North American players, with European Servers likely to follow soon. This hotfix comes after nearly 2 days of the Bank being disabled.
Blizzard
View Original
With a hotfix that has been rolling out over the last hour, we’ve restored access to the Warband Bank to all players in this region. The underlying issue has been resolved, and no changes were made to the Warband Bank or its contents.

Thank you very much for your patience and understanding.
For European Servers, the fix will roll out shortly after, alongside the weekly maintenance later today.
Blizzard
View Original
With a hotfix that we will implement with scheduled weekly maintenance today, we will restore access to the Warband Bank to all players in this region. The underlying issue has been resolved, and no changes were made to the Warband Bank or its contents.

Thank you very much for your patience and understanding.

title: Blizzard Previews Zul'Aman: The Amani Home Revamped for Midnight
author: POSTED 12 DAYS AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
In a new preview, Blizzard teases the revamped Zul'Aman for Midnight - home of the Amani, new dungeons, and even new Loa!

If you're excited to dig into the zones ahead of Midnight's official launch, we've got you covered with our Zone Guides. Check out places of interest, new treasures, and Rare rewards to snag once the latest expansion arrives.

Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
 Zul'Aman Rares & Treasures
Zul'Aman Rares & Treasures
Blizzard
View Original
The volatile neighbors to the blood elves, the Amani trolls, reside in their capital of Zul’Aman. They are a proud people who have fought hard for their land and overcome significant hardships and challenges. Comprised of old forests and tall mountains with periodic rainfall, you’ll take a journey deeper into their culture and history as you explore all this zone has to offer.
After completing your journey through Eversong Woods, Zul’Aman is one of three options at the Scouting Map in the Sanctum of Light that you can choose from.
At a Glance
New Zone- Zul’Aman: An expanded zone and home of the Amani trolls. It is a temperate region with forests and mountains.
Amani trolls: Once led by the powerful chieftain Zul’jin are now led by his granddaughter, Zul’jarra.
Faction: Amani Tribes
Dig deeper into Zul’jarra in the short story, “Legacy of the Amani.”
Zul’Aman: Home of the Amani Trolls
Meet the grandchildren of Zul’jin: Zul’jarra and Zul’jan. Zul’jarra is the leader of the Amani trolls, and her brother, Zul’jan, serves as her adviser. The uneasy peace that has settled between the Amani and the blood elves has begun to weaken as the threat of the Void continues to drive wedges between nations. It falls to you to venture into this ancient land and learn more of the deep culture of the Amani.
Zul’jarra is searching for a solution to help her people survive the Void onslaught that is coming their way. Having lost touch with the loa and the ongoing tensions with the neighboring blood elves, they are struggling to push back the Void. You’ll explore the abandoned and overgrown Akil’zon (eagle), Halazzi (lynx), Jan’alai (dragonhawk), and Nalorakk (bear) loa temples as you delve deeper into their story.
New Activities: Abundance
Harvest materials for the Loa of Abundance in a fast-paced, super-buffed, professions-themed public event. Available across all Midnight zones.
Dungeons
Den of Nalorakk
Wounded by Zul’jin and Malacrass, Nalorakk turned his back on the Amani, slumbering in his den to ease his pain. Now, the Amani seek to heal the rift between their tribe and the loa. But, to gain Nalorakk’s favor, those who come before him must complete his trials. Assist Zul’jarra as she journeys into the mind of the Loa of War to defeat him and gain his blessing.
Bosses
The Hoardmonger: Driven by voracious greed, the gluttonous Hoardmonger fiercely defends the stashes of food and supplies he has stolen from nearby troll tribes. Nalorakk’s first trial is to eliminate him before the tribes go hungry.
Sentinel of Winter: The seasons can be kind or cruel. Sentinel of Winter unleashes the harshest winter storms to test all who face him. To surpass Nalorakk’s second trial, endure the biting cold and defeat the embodiment of a winter storm.
Nalorakk: Slumbering in his fitful and battle-filled dream, Nalorakk, the Loa of War, will only test the mettle of the worthy. Survive his trial and receive his blessing or die trying.
Maisara Caverns
Beneath the sacred Maisara Hills, Vilebranch trolls sacrifice Witherbark captives to power their necromancy. Their rituals twist stolen souls into fuel for Rak’tul, a monstrous dire troll bound in the depths. If the cavern’s magic is left to fester, Zul’Aman will face a terror it cannot contain.
Bosses
Muro’jin and Nekraxx: Muro’jin is a relentless tracker charged with capturing victims for the Vilebranch’s dark rights. Nekraxx, a reanimated eagle, strikes down any who resist capture by her master. They must not be allowed to supply souls for Vordaza’s necrotic magic any longer.
Vordaza: Vordaza, the Vilebranch’s master of souls, performs forbidden rites to extract the soul essenses of the dead. She disills the suffering of her victims into raw necrotic energy to empower Rak’tul, her monstrous creation. Her vile magic and her monster must be stopped.
Rak’tul, Vessel of Souls: An undead thrall bound to Vordaza, Rak’tul is grotesquely bloated with the essences of tormented souls and necrotic power. Drunk on his newfound power and driven mad by the souls’ endless suffering, Rak’tul stands ready to break Zul’Aman beneath the crushing weight of his fists.
An ancient wounded land, draped in the culture of a fractured people, awaits you in Midnight. Choose your allegiances carefully as you navigate ongoing tensions amid war-torn instability.

title: Priority Sorting for Important Healer Buffs on Raid Frames Added
author: POSTED 12 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced that important healer buffs are now sorted near the beginning of tracked spells!

This is notable because healers have been doing pretty scuffed and unreliable workarounds to track important raid buffs.
Blizzard
View Original
Here you'll find a list of hotfixes that address various issues related to World of Warcraft: Midnight, Mists of Pandaria Classic, Season of Discovery, Burning Crusade Classic, WoW Classic Era, and Hardcore. Some of the hotfixes below take effect the moment they were implemented, while others may require scheduled realm restarts to go into effect. Please keep in mind that some issues cannot be addressed without a client-side patch update. This list will be updated as additional hotfixes are applied.
Hotfixes
February 17, 2026

Classes
The following spells are now prioritized on raid frames and always sorted near the beginning of tracked spells: (Preservation Evoker) Echo, (Restoration Shaman) Riptide, (Discipline Priest) Atonement, (Mistweaver Monk) Renewing Mist and Enveloping Mist, (Restoration Druid) Rejuvenation, Germination, and Lifebloom, and (Holy Paladin) Beacon of Light and Beacon of Faith.
Death Knight
Unholy
Developers’ notes: Recent bugfixes and tuning adjustments caused diseases to deal more damage than intended. We’re rebalancing their output to keep the build viable while also updating Putrefy and Necromancer’s Cunning to trigger Rune of the Apocalypse effects, helping support a wider range of build options.
Virulent Plague damage reduced by 18%.
Dread Plague damage reduced by 18%.
Pestilence now deals 100% of your Plagues’ remaining damage (was 150%).
Epidemic damage reduced by 10%.
Graveyard damage reduced by 10%.
Magus of the Dead’s Shadow Bolt and Frostbolt damage increased by 20%.
The damage from Putrefy and Necromancer’s Cunning can now trigger Rune of the Apocalypse’s effects.
San’layn: Visceral Strength grants 4% Strength (was 8%).
San’layn: Incite Terror stacks up to 3 times (was 5 times).
Demon Hunter
Devourer
All ability damage reduced by 4%.
Fixed an issue allowing Devourer Demon Hunters to be able to cast Void Metamorphosis without it talented.
Fixed an issue causing Burning Blades to benefit Devourer Demon Hunter's auto attacks.
Havoc
All ability damage increased by 5%.
Vengeance
Fixed an issue that prevented Single-Button Assistant from using Fracture.
Added Last Resort's Uncontained Fel debuff as an optional Tracked Buff to the Cooldown Manager.
Druid
Balance
All ability damage increased by 3%.
Feral
All ability and auto-attack damage increased by 3%.
Guardian
Fixed an issue where Dream Guide would activate at 80% health with two stacks, instead of the expected 40%.
Fixed an issue where Red Moon's damage was not being increased by Fury of Nature.
Fixed an issue where Red Moon's damage was not being increased by Lunar Insight.
Fixed an issue where Thrash's arcane damage was not being increased by the correct amount with Fury of Nature.
Fixed an issue where additional Rage consumed by Killing Blow was not being counted by After the Wildfire.
Restoration
Sunfire damage increased by 35%.
Moonfire damage increased by 30%.
Moonfire mana cost reduced by 33%.
Starsurge damage increased by 40%.
Wrath and Starfire damage increased by 25%.
All damage is now increased by 1% per level, resulting in a 15% overall damage increase at level 90.
Evoker
Devastation
Fixed an issue causing Disintegrate damage to be much lower than intended, increasing Disintegrate damage by 76%.
All damage reduced by 4%.
Flameshaper: Conduit of Flame now increases critical strike chance by 10% against targets above 50% health (was 15%).
Flameshaper: Fulminous Roar now causes Fire Breath to deal damage 15% more often (was 20%).
Preservation
All healing increased by 6%.
All damage is now increased by 1% per level, resulting in a 58% overall damage increase at level 90.
Temporal Artificer reduces the cooldown of Rewind by 60 seconds (was 120 seconds).
Rewind reverses 30% of damage taken in the last 5 seconds (was 33%). Does not affect PvP combat.
Fixed an issue where Dream Breath’s heal was slightly less than a 40-yard radius.
Emerald Blossom mana cost reduced by 20%.
Reversion mana cost reduced by 22%.
Fire Breath mana cost reduced by 30%.
Hunter
Corrected an issue where Concussive Shot was not available to be tracked within the Cooldown Manager when talented.
Beast Mastery
Dark Ranger: Dark Hound attack power reduced by 25%.
Pack Leader: Boar area damage now deals reduced damage beyond 5 targets (was 8).
Marksmanship
Windrunner Quiver no longer benefits Focused Aim twice.
Focused Aim now reduces Aimed Shot’s cooldown by 2 seconds (was 1 second).

title: Explore the Zones of Midnight: Eversong Woods
author: POSTED 12 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has released a blog covering the revitalized Eversong Woods in Midnight! A beloved starting zone since Burning Crusade, Eversong Woods is seeing some huge changes. The Dead Scar is finally healed, the Ghostlands are teeming with life, and favorite locations like Windrunner Spire are seeing new life (and no more gloomy lighting).

If you're excited to dig into the zones ahead of Midnight's official launch, we've got you covered with our Zone Guides. Check out places of interest, new treasures, and Rare rewards to snag once the latest expansion arrives.

Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
Silvermoon Hub & Zones Guide
 Eversong Woods Rares & Treasures
Eversong Woods Rares & Treasures
Blizzard
View Original
Immerse yourself in a revitalized Eversong Woods. With the Voidstorm looming overhead and renewed with the power of the Sunwell, Eversong Woods is familiar, but changed—filled with wonder and beauty, and a sense that something bigger is unfolding.

Graphic updates breathe new life into this iconic blood elf homeland. A combination of the original Eversong Woods and the Ghostlands, the zone has been slightly expanded and connected to the Eastern Kingdoms.

Players can take to the skies and Skyride through the zone, without loading screens, while experiencing the majesty of the glittering spires of blood elf architecture and golden foliage of the forests from above.
At a Glance
Zone- Eversong Woods: A part of the kingdom of Quel'thalas, it is comprised of the newly combined zones of Eversong Woods and Ghostlands and has been expanded and reimagined with enhanced graphics. Silvermoon City stands as its shining capital.
Blood Elves: Members of the Horde, this proud race once used the moniker of 'high elves' before adopting the name of the sin'dorei (children of the blood) in the wake of tragedy.
Faction: Silvermoon Court
Eversong Woods: Home of the Blood Elves
The passage of time has healed many wounds in the land, including the Dead Scar, created by the Scourge invasion. Yet, places of death and darkness remain, out of sight and mind.

Your journey will begin here as the sin'dorei protect themselves not only from the Void, but also from other threats, both familiar and new.
New Activity: Silvermoon Court/Saltheril's Soiree
Speak to Lorthemar Theron for the "Midnight: World Tour" quest, dispatching you to Saltheril's Haven.

Amid Silvermoon's intricate politics and quiet struggles for influence, our most distinguished military orders each have matters they wish addressed. Help them tend to their defenses.

Balance your reputation between four of Silvermoon's military factions—Blood Knights, Farstriders, Magisters, and Sunreavers—via public events and weeklies.

Saltheril's Soiree is a … complicated affair. High society. High drama. That said, they do their part to keep Eversong orderly and serene. Prove your worth to these factions, and you may find their gratitude and favor richly rewarded. Each track has its own set of cosmetic rewards.

Be sure to visit Renown Vendor, Caeris Fairdawn, and Decor Vendor, Sathren Azuredawn in Saltheril's Haven, to spend your Voidlight Marl. Faction vendors are also standing nearby, where you can exchange your Brimming Arcana for additional rewards.
Dungeons
Murder Row: Eversong Woods: Silvermoon City
The darkened streets of Murder Row hide a secret fel-smuggling operation that has been preying on the fears of Silvermoon citizens ever since the Voidstorm appeared.
Bosses
Kystia Manaheart: Although an accomplished Magister, Kystia Manaheart was far too devoted to fel magic to give it up entirely. Little wonder she's become a staunch supporter and ally to underground fel smuggling operations in the Row.
Zaen Bladesorrow: Clever, quick-witted, and commanding, Zaen Bladesorrow oversees the shipments of illegal fel artifacts. Those under his command follow his orders to the letter… or pay the price.
Xathuux the Annihilator: Xathuux the Annihilator is one of Lithiel Cinderfury's most powerful summons. Although he loathes serving the warlock, he follows her commands for the sheer visceral joy of destruction.
Lithiel Cinderfury: Lithiel Cinderfury sees the Voidstorm above Silvermoon as both a threat and an opportunity. Her people are afraid, and she has a perfect solution… the power of fel. She claims her smuggling operation protects her people from the dangers above, but the profits she receives are a pleasing bonus.
Windrunner Spire: Eversong Woods
Once the Hallowed home of the Windrunner family, Windrunner Spire now mirrors the scarred relationship between Alleria, Sylvanas, and Vereesa. Animosity during a reunion at the home drove suffering and grief into a haunted manifestation that binds the spirits there, unable to find peace.
Bosses
Emberdawn: Emberdawn settled in the gardens of Windrunner Spire after seeking a place to raise her young. Highly territorial, she guards her nest with a searing ferocity.
Derelect Duo: The banshee Kalis and her loyal abomination Latch lead a group of fanatical followers of Sylvanas Windrunner. They've come to the Spire in search of relics with any resonant essence of the Dark Lady, willing to do anything to obtain them.
Commander Kroluk: Commander Kroluk once fought in the Second War, a conflict that ended the life of Alleria Windrunner's brother, Lirath. In her grief, Alleria slaughtered any Old Horde she could find. The vengeful spirits of those she defeated now haunt the Spire.
The Restless Heart: Born from the grief and wrath of the Windrunner family, the vengeful spirit of a once-proud Ranger-General haunts the halls of its abandoned ancestral home. Only by facing a worthy foe can it be laid to rest and finally know peace.
Magister's Terrace: Isle of Quel'danas
Magister's Terrace is the heart of arcane study for the sin'dorei, including Magister Umbric, long ago. Within its halls lies the Cynosure of Twilight, the key to unlocking the way into the Voidstorm.
Bosses
Arcanotron Custos: This highly advanced arcane construct has been charged with keeping the peace and ensuring that all Magisters adhere to rules and restrictions. Veering from said rules will not be tolerated.
Seranel Sunlash: An accomplished Spellbreaker, Serenel Sunlash has earned the full trust of the Magisters. Charged with the protection of the Cynosure of Twilight. Seranel will fulfill those duties with his very life if necessary.
Gemellus: An otherworldly spawn of Void origins, Gemellus seems to utilize the Void not only for its own protection but to propagate and foster the development of its species as well.
Degentrius: A powerful creature of the Void, Degentrius has taken advantage of the opportunity inadvertently provided by Magister Umbric and launched an assault on the Terrace.
New adventures await you in World of Warcraft: Midnight. Unveil them as you explore Eversong Woods, creating new alliances and discovering answers to age-old mysteries. But watch your back, the journey ahead is teaming with danger as Xal'atath's invasion of Azeroth begins, and the Voidstorm threatens to cover the world in darkness.

title: Azeroth Interiors: Player Housing Challenge Begins Today
author: POSTED 12 DAYS AGO BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Itching for some design ideas for your home sweet home? Blizzard's Azeroth Interiors challenge begins today, and some of the biggest World of Warcraft streamers will be building incredible homes live for viewers to watch.
Blizzard
View Original
Spread the word and prepare to be seated—it's time to tune in to Azeroth's premiere player housing show! Azeroth Interiors is a three-phase challenge in which eight selected duos from around the world develop housing concepts, execute their ideas through building, and take us on a tour of their decorated domiciles live on broadcast.
Enter our Neighborhood and immerse yourself in Housing with the latest Creator Showcase – Azeroth Interiors
Phase One- Ideation
In the first phase of Azeroth Interiors, each duo will personalize a unique Pinterest board with visions and aesthetics that inspire their design styles. From cozy cottages to charismatic castles, stay tuned to find out what drives your favorite creator’s decor!
Phase Two Building
In the second phase of Azeroth Interiors, each duo will gather materials and begin construction on their house to bring their ideas to life. Keep up with each duo’s progress by tuning in to their livestreams on Twitch from February 19 through February 21!
Phase Three– Showcase
In the final phase of Azeroth Interiors, each duo will reveal their final builds on a live broadcast to the audience and our panel of judges. Tune in for a full tour of each interior, complete with furniture spotlights and blueprints—all paired with live commentary from the creators themselves. Catch the full stream live on the official World of Warcraft Twitch channel on February 24 to see how each unique vision is brought to life!
Each duo will be streaming their progress throughout the project, so check the schedule below to learn when to find your favorite creators.

title: Discover Dream Homes and Get Free Decor with Zillow for Warcraft
author: POSTED 12 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has revealed a new partnership with Zillow, featuring fun and fanciful plot listings to promote player housing in Midnight, along with a free piece of decor for everyone!

 Visit Zillow for Warcraft and Claim Free Decor



It's Zillow... for Warcraft!


Part of Zillow's new "Sometime Starts Today" campaign, there's no actual buying or selling of homes, just clever marketing and a chance to share off what player housing is capable of. Though the winner of the Azeroth Interiors competition will receive a custom listing, the exact extent of features is unclear. The site is moving very slowly at the moment, but it does not appear that players can actually create their own custom listings either - everything redirects to pre-purchasing pages. Still, the featured plots are very creative and fully interactable, so it's fun to read the faux listings and enjoy the fantasy of actual home ownership.


Simply click to be redirected to your Battle.net login, and claim the decor.


While these listings are fake and just for fun, the concept isn't that far fetched. Setting up a real-estate type site to showcase, share, or even sell custom-built player homes is exactly the kind of thing players could do on their own, when and if Blizzard eventually allows players to co-own or decorate each other's homes.
View Original
Welcome to the Neighborhood!

Whether you’re looking for a tropical retreat where you can sip Kaja’Cola under the sun, or an idyllic spot where the neighbors are quiet as the grave, Zillow for Warcraft has what you need.

Top agents on Zillow Bek’tar and Hazyl are on the job, ready to guide you toward listings you won’t want to miss.
Visit Zillow.com/Warcraft and see the first homes brought to life. Tour featured listings, meet Zillow partner agents Hazyl and Bek’tar, and more!

While you’re checking out the showcase listings, don’t forget to claim the Naturally Elegant Doormat Housing decor item* to place in your own little slice of Azeroth. Once claimed for your account, you’ll unlock the ability to purchase additional copies in-game from the vendors Tuuran and Gabbi near the Trading Posts in Stormwind and Orgrimmar.
In Azeroth, there’s a place for every hero to call home. You don’t have to wait for “someday” to arrive to find yours, with Zillow for Warcraft, your someday starts today!

* *Not available in World of Warcraft Classic® games. Requires World of Warcraft® Subscription or Game Time. Use and placement of decor items is limited to the Housing feature, which requires access to World of Warcraft®: Midnight. Available through September 30, 2026. Limited to one Naturally Elegant Doormat decor item redemption per Battle.net account.

title: Blizzard Pledges More Player Customization: Interview with Windows Central
author: POSTED 13 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In a new interview with Windows Central, Design Director Maria Hamilton and Principal Artist Jay Hwang discuss long-term plans to improve all manner of player customization, from housing to glyphs and animations!

 Racial Customization, Animations, and More with Windows Central



Nayelea's got a cool house, but goblins and gnomes are still waiting for theirs.
PLAYER HOUSING
More racial styles are coming, so players won't be forced to build their own goblin workshop out of spare decor purchased from Undermine. The team doesn't want players waiting years to make a goblin or gnome house and are already working on a solution. It won't be a painfully slow rollout like heritage armor has been.
Housing mechanics may not always be constrained to specific instances and can function in the main world of Azeroth. This is only the beginning. They've already thought about how it could be used to build quests, activities, and events, but the first priority is getting support and optimization.
Self-made traps, jumping puzzles, and other user generated content is also teaching the developers a lot about what kinds of things players enjoy about the system, and helps developers prioritize.
PLAYER CUSTOMIZATION
More racial cosmetics are coming, though they don't have a roadmap yet. They're looking at a larger project, rather than the odd drop here and there like heritage armor, and have even discussed improving character animations at the same time... but it's not coming soon.
They understand the need for better player customization and expression, and have already started down that road by remaking the transmog system.
There are have been many talents, toys, and abilities over the years that attach audio/visual effects to change how something looks, an astonishing number of things overlapping, but not built to work together.
Working cosmetic glyphs into the new transmog system is the kind of customization they would like to offer. Long term, they want to come up with a more consolidated solution to customizing effects and appearances.
Adding and improving animations have come up more than you might imagine. Players want animations appropriate to the role they're playing, and integrating that into a customization system is all part of a larger project for the future.

title: New and Reimagined Zones in Midnight Featuring Fiamma
author: POSTED 13 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In a new official preview video, Blizzard has partnered with Fiamma to guide us through the new zones coming in Midnight, showing what’s changed, what’s new, and why each area matters as we journey through the next chapter of the Worldsoul Saga!


Explore the new and reimagined zones coming in World of Warcraft: Midnight! From the revamped Eversong Woods and Zul’Aman, to the mysterious depths of Harandar and the shattered remains of Voidstorm, Midnight is packed with exciting new adventures and lore.

title: Lunar Festival Returns with New Cosmetic Weapons and Ensemble
author: POSTED 13 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Come celebrate the new year with another Lunar Festival, adding explosive new cosmetics for players to collect!

 Lunar Festival 2026 Event Guide


This year's offerings may look familiar, as all of the rewards are purple recolors of past cosmetics purchased from the trading post, purchased from Fariel Starsong for a total of 200 Coin of Ancestry instead of spending precious Tender. Obtained by honoring the elders located throughout Azeroth, players can obtain about 100 per character - they are not warbound though, so you'll need at least two characters to collect and purchase all of this year's new cosmetics.

Sunny Pack of Lunar Explosives for 25 Coin of Ancestry


Violet Lunar Lantern for 25 Coin of Ancestry

title: Shadowmoon Lumber Returned to Warlords of Draenor Zones in the Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 14 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Initially missing during the launch of early access housing, Shadowmoon Lumber is now available for harvesting across Warlords of Draenor zones in the Midnight Pre-Patch, allowing players to complete the Lumberjack Warlord achievement!

Professions in Player Housing
Professions in Player Housing
Draenor Decor Farming
Draenor Decor Farming




Player housing launched with a wide range of decor, much of which can be crafted from existing professions, all of them sharing various types of lumber as a new common reagent. Unlocked by completing Lumber For You, twelve different versions of the resource are gathered from axe-wedged trees in each expansion.


Lumber Type Gathering Location
Dornic Fir Lumber Khaz Algar and K'aresh
Fel-Touched Lumber Broken Isle Zones and Argus
Ironwood Lumber Kalimdor and Eastern Kingdoms
Dragonpine Lumber Dragonflight Zones
Bamboo Lumber Mists of Pandaria Zones
Olemba Lumber Outland Zones
Shadowmoon Lumber Draenor Zones
Arden Lumber Shadowlands Zones
Ashwood Lumber Cataclysm Zones
Darkpine Lumber Battle for Azeroth Zones
Coldwind Lumber Northrend Zones
Thalassian Lumber Quel'thalas and Harandar (Midnight)



However, Warlords of Draenor trees were not available for harvesting when early access Housing went live in patch 11.2.7, with a workaround was provided through a vendor exchanging an equal amount of Outland Olemba Lumber instead. With the proper trees once again able to be harvested, that exchange has been removed, so players will need to travel to Warlords of Draenor zones to collect the resource as originally intended.
Blizzard
View Original
With the initial release of patch 11.2.7, Draenor trees are not yet ready for harvesting. We expect that they will become harvestable in the coming weeks.

Players looking for Shadowmoon Lumber to craft items with Warlords of Draenor professions should instead harvest Olemba Lumber from Outland. The lumberjack in each neighborhood will trade Olemba Lumber for an equal amount of Shadowmoon Lumber until the trees in Draenor catch up.
The Lumberjack Warlord achievement requires harvesting quite a few of these new trees across any of the Draenor zones. Spires of Arak, Talador, and Shadowmoon Valley seem to have the widest range of spawns, while Gorgrond, Ashran, and the Tanaan Jungle are much more sparse. As expected, you'll typically find nodes in more heavily wooded areas, though this is not an absolute as several trees, mushrooms, and even some odd vines can be harvested.

title: Warband Banks Temporarily Disabled

content:
Updated 2/17 5:15 PST: Warband banks have been reenabled for NA servers, and EU servers will have them enabled again after the weekly reset!


Updated 2/17 4:30 PST: Blizzard has not announced any updates as to Warband Banks returning to functionality, although some players have reported intermittent success in accessing their banks. Currently, the Battle.net launcher has a banner related to the disabled feature, but unfortunately, no more word has been revealed.


Blizzard has temporarily disabled Warband Banks, with an unknown timeframe as to when they'll be up and running again.

We'll update this article with the latest information, so stay tuned for any changes!
Blizzard
View Original
Today, we’ve temporarily disabled the Warband Bank function while we investigate and fix an issue.

We’ll work to get the Warband Bank back up and running as soon as possible.

title: Mounted Lumber Harvesting Achievements Now Available in Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 14 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
With the release of Patch 12.0.1 on live servers, players can finally obtain the Lumber Harvesting achievements that award mounted harvesting.

Midnight Pre-Patch 12.0.1 Patch Notes
Midnight Pre-Patch 12.0.1 Patch Notes
Lumber Harvesting & Decor Crafting
Lumber Harvesting & Decor Crafting
Table of Contents
Mounted Lumber Harvesting Achievements Now Available
How to Harvest Lumber
MOUNTED LUMBER HARVESTING ACHIEVEMENTS NOW AVAILABLE
Patch 12.0.1 brought with it not only an avalanche of class changes but also the new lumber harvesting achievements. Harvest lumber in each of the expansion zones and obtain achievements that award mounted lumber harvesting in those areas. Currently 12 achievements exist that award mounted harvesting for their expansion-specific zones based on their respective lumber types:

Achievement Lumber Type Location
Classically Trained Lumberjack Ironwood Lumber Kalimdor and Eastern Kingdoms
Outlandish Lumberjack Olemba Lumber Outland Zones
Wrathful Lumberjack Coldwind Lumber Northrend Zones
Cataclysmic Lumberjack Ashwood Lumber Cataclysm Zones
Mist-Shrouded Lumberjack Bamboo Lumber Mists of Pandaria Zones
Lumberjack Warlord Shadowmoon Lumber Draenor Zones
Legion Lumberjack Fel-Touched Lumber Broken Isle Zones and Argus
Azeroth's Lumberjack Darkpine Lumber Battle for Azeroth Zones
Shadowy Lumberjack Arden Lumber Shadowlands Zones
Draconic Lumberjack Dragonpine Lumber Dragonflight Zones
The Lumberjack Within Dornic Fir Lumber Khaz Algar and K'aresh
Midnight Lumberjack Thalassian Lumber Quel'thalas and Harandar (Midnight)

For those that retroactively harvested lumber, you should have been granted your respective progress towards those achievements.


Ironwood Lumber Farming Achievement Progress from Pre-Patch Hotfix
HOW TO HARVEST LUMBER
Lumber is a newly introduced crafting material in Patch 11.2.7 used as a required reagent in all Decor crafts in Player Housing. This new material is Warbound, meaning it can be shared across your alts but cannot be sold on the Auction House or traded to another player.


Lumber harvesting does not require a particular profession and is unlocked as part of the Player Housing Intro campaign. Simply complete Lumber For You from Lestia Goldenstrike/Xiz'ro in Founder's Point/Razorwind Shores to receive the Harvesting Hatchet which upon use will allow you to toggle lumber nodes on the mini-map.


Toggling Lumber on the Mini-Map.


For anyone that lost their Harvesting Hatchet and couldn't repurchase, the known bug has been fixed as of February 11th.
Blizzard
View Original
Noting that this bug was fixed at 1:36 p.m. PST today. You can now again purchase a Harvesting Hatchet to learn the Find Lumber ability.
Lumber Harvesting & Decor Crafting
Lumber Harvesting & Decor Crafting

title: Farm Legacy Dragonflight Dungeons and Raids for the Trader's Ottuk Mounts
author: POSTED 15 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
With legacy loot now enabled for Dragonflight dungeons and raids, highly contested collectables like the Trader's Ottuk mounts can now be earned completely solo and with hardly any effort at all!

Sold by Tattukiaka in Iskaara, the Ivory Trader's Ottuk and Iskaara Trader's Ottuk mounts are unique in that they aren't purchased with a common currency or gold. Instead, they require specific accessory drops from Dragonflight dungeons and raids. At the time, most players would need a group to even see them drop, which could make them highly contested for players that either wanted to use the items or collect the mounts for themselves.


It's more of a barter system.


The Ivory Trader's Ottuk requires three specific rings from Dragonflight dungeons, which can be obtained on any difficulty. Note that Al'gathar Academy can only be entered on normal difficulty at this time, perhaps because it is already considered part of the upcoming Midnight Season 1 dungeon pool.
Platinum Star Band from Vexamus in Algeth'ar Academy.
Unstable Arcane Loop from Leymor in The Azure Vault.
Thunderous Downburst Ring from The Raging Tempestin The Nokhud Offensive.



The Iskaara Trader's Ottuk requires two specific amulets from Vault of the Incarnates. Neither should present much of a challenge, though there is quite a bit of running involved.
Terros's Captive Core from the second boss, Terros.
Eye of the Vengeful Hurricane from the nominal fifth boss, Dathea, Ascended.



Due to the nature of random drops, it may still take several tries to obtain the necessary items, though remember that normal dungeons have no lockout outside of the regular instance limit per hour and the raid bosses can be looted separately on each difficulty per week. Players also don't need to complete the entire dungeon or raid, just the individual boss.


Sometimes you're just that lucky!

title: New Keystone Dungeons Tab Added to Adventure Guide in Midnight Beta
author: POSTED 16 DAYS AGO BY IMX3
content:
A new Keystone Dungeons tab was added to the Adventure Guide, giving players a brief introduction to the Mythic+ dungeon system, along with detailing all the seasonal affixes.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
KEYSTONE DUNGEONS ADVENTURE GUIDE
The Mythic+ dungeon system has a lot of moving parts, and sometimes it can be difficult to remember every detail. With the Keystone Dungeons tab, players can get vital information on the many parts of Mythic+, including but not limited to:
Obtaining a Mythic Keystone
Starting a Mythic Keystone
Completing a Mythic Keystone
Upgrading/Downgrading a Mythic Keystone
Especially for newer players, this is a helpful reference when learning how to get started with Mythic+.



Alongside general Mythic+ information, there is an entire section for Mythic+ Affixes. Each specific tab lists a Keystone level, providing information such as when certain affixes become active, change, or get removed. Mousing over any affix provides the spell tooltip, which gives a summary of what that affix does throughout a dungeon.

title: Kexman Talks Delves and Player Housing with Jay Hwang and Maria Hamilton
author: POSTED 16 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Content creator Kexman interviewed Design Director Maria Hamilton and Lead Prop Artist Jay Hwang at the Azeroth Homes event in London, asking a few quick questions about delves and player housing!
DISCUSSING DELVES WITH MARIA HAMILTON
Delves were targeted for a particular type of player, those who can't set aside a big block of time or often get interrupted and need to step away for a moment. That's difficult to do in group content where other players can be kept waiting or a timer is ticking down, so the developers started looking for a less restrictive and more solo-friendly piece of content.
Once they started working on Delves, they started trying new things. Nemesis summons, K'aresh gates, traps that players or enemies could be lured into, and Midnight is adding mount-capable outdoor delves as well.
The developers have been looking at data on how players spend time inside delves, and they think there's room for a longer experience, leading them to Labyrinths. Somewhat inspired by Blackrock Mountain, which had several interconnected but separate activities, the idea is to allow groups to progress at their own pace, rather than requiring them to go through everything all at once.
A team is already working The Last Titan, and they've chosen locations for new delves and talked about what's happening in its zones at this point in the story, what kind of dungeons they should have, what kind of creatures should be there. With so many previous connections to Northrend, there's a lot of nostalgia and easter eggs to explore, but the team still want to make it fresh and exciting for players who never experienced those areas before.
The developers like to try to guess what players will do first when they arrive in a new piece of content and are very interested to see who is right or whether players surprise them.
PLAYER HOUSING WITH JAY HWANG
Watching players engage with housing has been a dream come true. They couldn't be happier with the way players have engaged with the system, making their own tutorials and creating their own communities around the feature.
Housing is starting here. They still have a backlog of creations to release, but seeing players come up with new ideas they didn't even expect has led to even more ideas to support in the future. Developers try to prepare themselves for players to break the game in unexpected ways, but sometimes they become really cool features.
Because they decided not to include any kind of player power with housing, the developers have a lot more freedom to allow bugs and unexpected ways of engaging with the system, even deciding to adopt and support some of them as official features, like adding native support for floating houses.
More exciting than any one individual type of racial architecture coming to housing is seeing how players mix and match them together to create entirely new themes.

title: Silvermoon City Preview and Points of Interest Blue Post
author: POSTED 16 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard is welcoming players to the newly refurbished Silvermoon City in Midnight with a preview of the bustling expansion hub, featuring 74 specific locations of interest!
View Original
With the press of the Void’s incursion and assault on the Sunwell, the forces of the Horde and Alliance fortify within the streets of Silvermoon City. Serving as a hub for both factions, approximately one-third of the city is designated exclusively for the Horde, while the remaining two-thirds are accessible to both faction members as they work together to push back Xal’atath’s forces. The city has been rebuilt from the ground up and the path of destruction the Scourge left behind is mostly a memory.

Soaring towers resplendent in crimson and gold lead to stunning vistas of the surrounding areas. The city is worth exploring in all its many bejeweled facets: the sin'dorei culture soaked in and celebrated having triumphed over many of their trials.
Click to view larger image with key locations, trainers, vendors, and more.

Tip:  Getting around the city can be as easy as taking flight on your own mount, hopping into a well-appointed carriage for a more scenic route, or using an orb of translocation to travel between levels. If you can’t find what you’re looking for, you can always ask a guard for directions. Mailboxes are also plentiful around the city and easy to find when you need one.
Sanctum of Light
The Sanctum of Light sits at the heart of the city. It’s where you’ll find Lor’themar Theron among other notable figures. Your first quests within the city begin here setting you on the path of earning reputation with the Silvermoon Court and discovering what all Eversong Woods and the city itself have to offer. As you progress in the campaign, you will return to the heart of the Sanctum to choose the path you want to take beyond Eversong Woods by using the (1) Scouting Map.
The Bazaar
The Bazaar surrounds the heart of Silvermoon city with the Sanctum of Light at its center. Various vendors, trainers, the Barbershop, auction house, portal room, and more can be found on several levels.

title: WoW Weekly: Second Midnight Pre-Patch, Short Stories, Tours, and More
author: POSTED 16 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
This week in WoW, Love is in the Air, beta changes have made their way to the Midnight Pre-Expansion, and the Arcantina is open for business!
View Original
Dive into the second Midnight Pre-Expansion content update filled with class updates and a host of quality‑of‑life improvements, prepare to venture into Midnight by tackling last-minute tasks in The War Within, learn more about how you can take refuge from the trials and travails of an adventurer’s life in the Arcantina, deck your home out in plushies, and more! Each week meets you with adventurous discovery and fresh rewards—be sure to check in to learn more about what awaits you in Azeroth.
The Second Midnight Pre-Expansion Update is Now Live
With the release of the second pre‑expansion update, we’re rolling out additional class updates and a host of quality‑of‑life improvements—including user interface (UI) enhancements to Damage Meters, Boss Alerts, and more—as we make our final preparations for the launch of Midnight.

Check out the current update notes here to learn more about all the changes.

Whether you’re refining your rotation, dialing in your interface, or just preparing for what’s ahead, this update is all about getting ready for Midnight.
What to Tackle in The War Within Before the Launch of Midnight
As World of Warcraft: The War Within reaches its zenith, we're getting ready to turn the page to the next chapter: Midnight. Before your adventure moves forward, now's the perfect time to wrap up activities around Khaz Algar—especially those rewards and achievements that either go away or become much harder to earn once the expansion content update launches worldwide on March 2, 2026, at 3:00 pm PST.

Learn more about what you need to finish from our previously published article.
Flutter Into the Fight—Love Is in the Air Has Arrived!
Sounds and fragrances of love swirl in the air with a hint of nefarious undertones as a strange love sickness clouds the hearts of the denizens of Azeroth. Uncover the dark secret fueling this plague of passion.

When : February 9–23
Where : Located outside Orgrimmar and Stormwind, with portals to the Gala of Gifts found in major cities around Azeroth.
Currency : Love Tokens

Participate in quests for Love Tokens to buy gifts for yourself and those you love. Also, don’t forget to show a little love to your alts—Love Tokens are Warbound.

There are new items available during the Love is in the Air in-game holiday, and for those looking for love on our Classic Progression realms, Love is in the Air is for you, too! Follow your heart to our previously published article for all the details.
Pull Up a Chair in the Arcantina
A refuge to the road-weary and those seeking a respite from the trials and travails of an adventurer’s life, the Arcantina serves as a crossway for all kinds.

This magical tavern is poised at the crossroads of nowhere and everywhere catering to travelers and heroes from all walks of life. Here you’ll reunite with some familiar faces, share stories, and take on new quests that send you all over Azeroth and beyond.

More details about this cozy tavern atmosphere can be found in our article.
Dress for Every Situation with the Updated Transmog System
In Midnight the transmogrification system has gotten an overhaul to its functionality. Save your favorite looks to Outfit Slots, style multiple outfits, and dress for the moment anywhere your adventures take you in Azeroth.

With the new system, instead of having to reapply your favorite look when you receive a new item, you can apply and save an appearance to a specific Outfit Slot. Whether you’re pushing back the forces of the Void or you’re hanging out in your home after getting your fill of adventure, you can look your very best.

Learn more from our previously published article
Welcome to Silvermoon City
Take a tour of the gleaming capital city of the sin’dorei, restored to its former glory. From artists practicing their crafts to profession trainers and vendors, adventurers will find just what they need.

Whether you’re seeking a respite from the press of the Void or simply passing through, Silvermoon has it all.

Take a glance at our previously published article to learn about where to go and what to do in Silvermoon.
Watch the Xal’atath Animation: Supremacy
Midnight is almost here. Xal’atath has united the warring factions of the Void, unleashing a ravenous army that threatens to consume all of Azeroth.

"Oh, this has been a delight. But our little game is over. When next we meet...I will take everything you love."

Read more about Xal'atath in her previously published character profile. Stand and fight, or be consumed when Midnight launches.
Warcraft Short Story: "Legacy of the Amani"
As the daughter of the Amani chieftain, Zul’jarra has spent her life in preparation to shoulder the heavy burden of leadership. Her grandfather, Zul’jin, lost to the forest trolls what little they had left after centuries at war with their elven neighbors.

Today, the tribes are fractured and friendless, without even the loa to pray to, and when a rival tribe’s leader challenges her claim, Zul’jarra must undertake a desperate journey to prove herself and face the usurper head-on.

Find out if she's up to the challenge ahead in our newest short story written by Graham Roswell and illustrated by Matt Hubel.
Download the PDF
Cuddle Up with the Plushie Decor Pack
Decorate your new home with a pack of eight adorable plushies: four Beloved Lions and four Beloved Wolves. Perfect for shelves, sofas, or cozy corners, they can bring a touch of charm to every room in your home.

These small Housing decor items arrive for the cost of 500 Hearthsteel and contain 4 of each plush in the pack.

The Plushie Decor Pack includes the following:
4 Beloved Lion Plushie (Alliance)
4 Beloved Wolf Plushie (Horde)
You can also purchase the Beloved Lion Plushie and Beloved Wolf Plushie individually from the in-game shop or the Battle.net shop.

Hearthsteel can be purchased in various denominations at the rate of 100 Hearthsteel per $1. Learn more about the Hearthsteel virtual currency in our previously published article.
Buy Now
Return each week to the World of Warcraft news site, where together, we’ll forge your destinies in Azeroth and beyond. Lok'tar Ogar!
