title: Find Your Favorite Class with Account Played
author: POSTED 23 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Account Played is a simple new addon that tracks the total time played across all of your characters and all of their realms, sorted by class to see which you favor the most!

 Account Played Addon


Unfortunately, the addon does require you to have first logged onto those characters in order to add their stats, which could be a burden for players with a significant amount of alts, but this should sort itself out over time. Once populated, the frame will show each class with a distinct colored bar, alongside the total time played and its percentage as a whole.


You'll need to log onto every character or else it may look a little imbalanced... unless you're a proud one trick!


And that's about it. While the frame can be resized or moved across the screen, the addon does exactly what it says and nothing more. For players though, finding out who is the newest and who is the most senior players of the class presents endless opportunities for bragging rights!




Players have always loved checking their /played stats, and this addon is a great way to do the math for you - instead of just knowing how each character stacks up for hours racked up AFK in cities, now you can get the full breakdown at a glance. If you're looking for something to compare individual playtime for each individual character instead of grouping by class, take a look at the very similar Character Playtime addon instead!

title: WoW Weekly: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary, Midnight Story So Far
author: POSTED 23 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
This week in WoW, we venture once more through the Dark Portal in the Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary, and the second Midnight Pre-Expansion update is about to go live!
View Original
Venture once more through the legendary Dark Portal with the Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary, dive into Xal’atath’s dark deeds in The War Within with the Story So Far, and embrace the unwavering resolve of Anubisath’s Guardians by donning their regalia.
WoW®: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition Now Live!
It's time to take the fight to Outland! Prepare to tread once again through the Dark Portal and into a fel-scarred realm. Your journey continues with a level increase to level 70, an increased level cadence, three challenging Raids, two playable races, Guild Bank, and a slew of other features.

Learn more about Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition, including important details about features and character transfers, from our previously published article.
The Second Midnight Pre-Expansion Update Goes Live February 10
With the release of the second pre‑expansion update, we’re rolling out additional class updates and a host of quality‑of‑life improvements—including user interface (UI) enhancements to Damage Meters, Boss Alerts, and more—as we make our final preparations for the launch of Midnight.

Check out the current update notes here to learn more about all the changes.

Whether you’re refining your rotation, dialing in your interface, or just preparing for what’s ahead, this update is all about getting ready for Midnight.
Watch the “Midnight Story So Far” Trailer
Catch up on Xal’atath’s dark deeds throughout The War Within, from the destruction of Dalaran to the return of the Void Lord Dimensius. Remember: Midnight is coming.
Command the Sands with the Anubisath Guardian Pack
Bound to ancient duty, Anubisath’s Guardians stand eternal. Embrace their unwavering resolve by donning their regalia, available in the Anubisath Guardian Pack—a limited-time bundle available through March 2, 2026.

Content within the Anubisath Guardian Pack may arrive in a future rotation of the Trading Post, but no sooner than three months after the offer leaves the Shop. Visit shop.battle.net for more information and detailed promotion end dates.
Visit the Shop
Fresh challenges and new allegiances await you in Azeroth! Return each week to the World of Warcraft news site, where together, we’ll help you uncover your destiny. Lok'tar Ogar!

title: Enhance Your UI and Automate Common Tasks with WilduTools
author: POSTED 24 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Among the surge of new addons released since the launch of Midnight, WilduTools offers a collection of dozens of quality of life tweaks to automate gameplay tasks and enhance the user interface, from color-coded range to target indicators to crosshairs which help keep track of your character in cluttered groups and hectic situations!
 Download WilduTools to Enhance Your UI


Features range from simple rescaling to practical automation, such as skipping confirmation windows when purchasing decor.


Created by the developer of the exceptionally popular Cooldown Manager Centered, which does exactly what it sounds like, WilduTools includes a wide variety of features, using helpful mouseover text and images to explain exactly what they do. Not all of these changes may be to everyone's liking, but all of the options are opt-in, so that players can pick and choose features they like without getting those they don't.



Preview each feature to see exactly what it does.


Other features include more visible friendly player names inside instances, better waypoint indicators, always visible encounter bar values, distinct melee range indicators, and much more!


[/screenshot alt="WilduTools waypoints"]

title: Tickets for Toronto's Northrend Live Event Available Today at 3PM EST
author: POSTED 24 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
Tickets for Blizzard's Midnight Event, Northrend Live, in Toronto, Canada are available starting today at 3PM EST!

Tickets for the February 21st event are free and available first-come, first-served.

 Northrend Live Tickets
Blizzard
View Original
Canadian adventurers, it's time to gather and celebrate the launch of World of Warcraft®: Midnight™!

Mark your calendars for an epic community event. Attendance is free, and the event will stream live for those who cannot attend in person. Northrend Live gathers creators, players, and fans from coast-to-coast-to-coast for one immersive, high-energy experience, both in person and online.

When : Saturday, February 21, 2026, from 11:00 am to 6:00 pm EST
Where : Illuminarium Toronto, 28 Gristmill Lane, Toronto, ON M5A 4R2 (must be age 19 or older for entry)
Livestream : Twitch.tv/warcraft & Twitch.tv/Plkatv
Tickets Available : Thursday, February 5, at 3 pm EST

For all those attending Northrend Live in person, both VIP and General Admission ticket holders can:
Get face-to-face with creators and streamers.
Enter through the Void for an immersive light projection experience.
Chance to attend a meet-and-greet with WoW Design Lead Toby Ragaini (exact timeslot to be announced).
Create new and lasting memories in our Housing photobooth.
Take photos with and meet amazing WoW cosplayers.
Compete and win prizes in our own Darkmoon Faire (while supplies last).
In-Person Attendance
Tickets to Northrend Live are free and available on a first-come, first-served basis while supplies last.

To ensure as many in-person adventurers as possible can experience Northrend Live, two types of tickets will be available.
Free VIP Admission
A limited number of ticket holders will receive full-day access to Illuminarium Toronto from 11 am– 6 pm EST, a free swag bag (while supplies last), and the ability to watch the entire stream from start to finish (entry and exit allowed).
Free General Admission
If you didn't score VIP tickets, general admission is time-slotted. Three time-slot windows will be available to book tickets for Northrend Live.

If you miss out on grabbing a ticket in advance, you can still visit Northrend Live in person on the day of the event. We'll be welcoming adventurers to queue up for the event throughout the day, as venue capacity allows.
Grab Your Tickets
Livestream Details

"Weekly No Leaver" Live Podcast (hosted by Cartridgebros)

title: OPie Addon Updated for Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 25 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Over the weekend, the popular addon OPie was updated for the Midnight Pre-Patch!

 Download OPie


OPie is a very popular addon, and it has now been officially updated for 12.0 and the Midnight Pre-Patch! This addon has been very useful for many players, allowing you to bind things to a radial menu that only shows up when you press a keybind. This means one keybind can equal a number of things.

Some of the most common uses are:
Raid Markers
World Markers
Quest Items
Custom Mounts
Portals
... and anything else you want.

title: Updated: World Quests In, Rares Not So Much: Leveling with the Twilight Ascension Pre-Patch Event
author: POSTED 26 DAYS AGO BY JAYDAA
content:
Leveling a new main for Midnight? Let's crunch the numbers to see how quickly you can level in the Twilight Ascension Pre-Patch event.

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension


Twilight Rare event completion now gives ~35% of a level for a total of about half a level when including Ritualist and Rare kill experience. This makes rare farming a solid repeatable source of experience.

 LEVELING WITH THE TWILIGHT ASCENSION PRE-PATCH EVENT
If you missed out on the leveling extravaganza that was Legion Remix, then you might be looking for the next best leveling option to pad out your roster of alts before Midnight's launch. So how does the new Twilight Ascension Pre-Patch event stack up for leveling? Let's break it down by activity.

The below numbers were obtained while using the experience buffs from Winds of Mysterious Fortune ( Winds of Mysterious Fortune & Surge of Mysterious Wisdom), Warband XP Bonus ( Warband Mentored Leveling maxed at 25%), and the Darkmoon Faire ( WHEE!). Exact numbers will vary by level but should be relatively consistent.
WORLD QUESTS
Scattered across the Twilight Highlands World Quests will be available during the event starting at level 10. Most of these are relatively straightforward requiring less than 5 minutes to complete.

Upon completion players will be granted Resonance Crystals and Twilight's Blade Insignia, and for those under level 80 a fairly lucrative amount of experience. Here's an example on a level 42 character:


  

The experience from a single World Quest completion accounts for 66% of a full level, not factoring in any enemy kill experience, which will vary significantly by World Quest. This seems to hold even at the 70-80 thresholds and is something we'd highly recommend doing if you're looking for a few quick levels. The only downside here is that the World Quests are finite, only refreshing biweekly.
TWILIGHT RARES
With the latest event hotfixes, Twilight Ascension rares now spawn every 5 minutes. Whether you're defeating them all for the Two Minutes to Midnight achievement or farming them for 2-3 Twilight's Blade Insignia, players under level can earn some experience. Here's an example on a level 67 character.

  
The experience from a single Twilight Rare or Ritualist kill equates to ~4% of a full level or ~16% for a full event spawn (1 Rare + 3 Ritualists). You can pick up a little bit extra from killing the surrounding mobs at event start and maybe even get lucky by looting some bonus experience from the Winds of Mysterious Fortune event, but the bulk of the XP will still be from the Rare and Ritualists.
 

It's not a ton of experience, and we wouldn't recommend this option if your only goal is leveling. There's typically better leveling options, but if you're already farming rares for Twilight's Blade Insignia to collect all the cosmetics or to transfer for gear, then this isn't a bad added bonus. Just don't farm in a raid group, as your experience will be cut in half.

title: A Bigger Vision for Warcraft - Interview with The Game Business Show
author: POSTED 26 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Executive Producer Holly Longdale and Senior Game Direction Ion Hazzikostas spoke on The Game Business Show, discussing World of Warcrafts the game’s ageing audience, working with Microsoft, and the future of the Warcraft IP.

The lengthy interview is worth a full watch, but we've gathered some highlights below.
HOW WOW HAS CHANGED & WHERE IT'S GOING
Holly believes that Warcraft is a fantastic IP that has been underutilized, but is poised for growth, and wants to bring it to as many people as possible: a bigger vision than simply an MMO. Ion firmly believes that Blizzard's best years are ahead of them. Chris Metzen has sometimes thought they shouldn't have called it Warcraft, believing the name may sound too intimidating, but Holly contends that nobody really thinks that about Warhammer.
Gamers across the industry don't necessarily have a lot of time anymore, so Blizzard is trying to add more features like housing that don't require specific time commitments and can be engaged with by a variety of players, whether they're working solo or looking to do something with their friends, partner, or children.
Breadth of content is the key to making WoW approachable for everyone, a kind of "third space" that offers content for all players. It isn't about changing what World of Warcraft has to offer so much as offering more content suitable for everyone, whether they're looking for 20-bite bite sized content or can spend entire weekends dedicated to accomplishing something within the game.
These aren't all new motivations either. WoW has had a vibrant role-playing community since its inception, despite the game not offering many tools to enable it. There's now a whole community formed around how to build things in housing, which is the real beauty of MMOs.
A number of players also engage with new expansion campaigns before falling into a more casual space, which the live team is trying to keep engaged with more frequent patch content. 20 years ago, WoW was thought of as a game only for players who could devote dozens of hours per week, but while it still contains that depth, it now offers much more.
DELIVERING MORE CONTENT FASTER
Organizationally, the development team has changed a lot, breaking up into more specialized team that can work in tandem with one another. There are now teams dedicated to each expansion, a team dedicated to live content, and another on housing. The Last Titan and its following expansion are both already well underway. That started with investing in leaders, to reduce the bottlenecks between the creative and approval processes, allowing teams to work on multiple projects in parallel.
Ten years ago, it was almost a given that there would be 6+ months of downtime between each expansion, which is something they've been working to fix for a long time. Releasing just 18 months after the launch of The War Within, Midnight will have the shortest downtime between expansions to date, while still releasing regular eight-week content updates throughout, boasting no cut corners, compromises, or sacrifices in quality (though some players may disagree).
More importantly, they're not sacrificing their team's health to do it either. Midnight is an expansion made without crunch, without telling anyone to work weekends and stay late. Blizzard wants to continue making this game for years and decades to come, and to do that they need to treat it as a marathon rather than a sprint.
An unexpected benefit of working under Microsoft has been the opportunity to speak speak and visit with development teams for other games, such as the Elder Scrolls and Minecraft. Otherwise, Microsoft has been trusting and supportive since their acquisition, with Holly saying that the team hasn't felt their presence.
CATERING TO DIFFERENT AUDIENCES
Part of offering something for everyone has been realized in branching Classic off into several different versions. When they first launched, the mantra was "no changes," and the team took that to heart, even going out of their way to reproduce known bugs. They quickly realized that maybe some changes would be good, which gave the team an opportunity to start experimenting, from small targeted updates to creating standalone hardcore servers, and even wholly new content.
Many Classic players are completely different from modern/retail ones, behaving differently and wanting different things, but it has caused a change in how the WoW team thinks about the content they create.

title: WoW Midnight Event in Toronto, Canada on February 21st
author: POSTED 26 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced a WoW Midnight Event in Toronto, Canada on February 21st called Northrend Live!

Tickets will be on sale on February 5th at 3PM EST.
Blizzard
View Original
Canadian adventurers, it's time to gather and celebrate the launch of World of Warcraft®: Midnight™!

Mark your calendars for an epic community event. Attendance is free, and the event will stream live for those who cannot attend in person. Northrend Live gathers creators, players, and fans from coast-to-coast-to-coast for one immersive, high-energy experience, both in person and online.

When : Saturday, February 21, 2026, from 11:00 am to 6:00 pm EST
Where : Illuminarium Toronto, 28 Gristmill Lane, Toronto, ON M5A 4R2 (must be age 19 or older for entry)
Livestream : Twitch.tv/warcraft & Twitch.tv/Plkatv
Tickets Available : Thursday, February 5, at 3 pm EST

For all those attending Northrend Live in person, both VIP and General Admission ticket holders can:
Get face-to-face with creators and streamers.
Enter through the Void for an immersive light projection experience.
Chance to attend a meet-and-greet with WoW Design Lead Toby Ragaini (exact timeslot to be announced).
Create new and lasting memories in our Housing photobooth.
Take photos with and meet amazing WoW cosplayers.
Compete and win prizes in our own Darkmoon Faire (while supplies last).
In-Person Attendance
Tickets to Northrend Live are free and available on a first-come, first-served basis while supplies last.

To ensure as many in-person adventurers as possible can experience Northrend Live, two types of tickets will be available.
Free VIP Admission
A limited number of ticket holders will receive full-day access to Illuminarium Toronto from 11 am– 6 pm EST, a free swag bag (while supplies last), and the ability to watch the entire stream from start to finish (entry and exit allowed).
Free General Admission
If you didn't score VIP tickets, general admission is time-slotted. Three time-slot windows will be available to book tickets for Northrend Live.

If you miss out on grabbing a ticket in advance, you can still visit Northrend Live in person on the day of the event. We'll be welcoming adventurers to queue up for the event throughout the day, as venue capacity allows.
Grab Your Tickets
Livestream Details
For those who cannot attend in person, Northrend Live will livestream an action-packed program from 12 pm to 5 pm EST on Twitch.tv/warcraft and Twitch.tv/Plkatv, featuring professional hosts, casters, and creators from across the country:
"Weekly No Leaver" Live Podcast (hosted by Cartridgebros)
"Decor Gala" by Raider.io Competition Finals
"Creator" Jump Puzzle Competition
Faction Feud Live Game Show
Those watching from home can also tune in for fun giveaways!
Don't miss out on the chance to celebrate the launch of World of Warcraft Midnight with hundreds of fellow adventurers.

title: February 3rd Midnight Pre-Patch Hotfixes - Pre-Patch Weeklies, Devourer PvP Tuning
author: POSTED 26 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has released hotfix notes, which include per character Pre-Patch weekly quests, updating prices for housing decor items, and significant Devourer tuning in PvP.
View Original
February 3, 2026

Classes
Evoker
Devastation
Fixed an issue allowing Deep Breath to sometimes be castable more often than intended.
Housing
Fixed several decor items that were incorrectly priced. Their costs have been updated to the intended values.
Items
Fixed an issue where Mysterious Satchels could activate group loot rolls for players who were not in a group.
Player versus Player
Demon Hunter
Fel-Scarred: Focused Hatred now increases Demonsurge damage by 10% (was 50%) when damaging a single target, reducing by 2% (was 10%) per additional target in PvP combat.
Fel-Scarred: Demonsurge damage reduced by 20% in PvP combat.
Devourer
Developers’ notes: We’re applying several changes that were made to Devourer in the Beta, to reduce burst during pre-patch. These come along with an overall damage reduction to the specialization.
All damage reduced by 8% in PvP combat.
Consume damage increased by 100% in PvP combat. Does not affect Devour.
Devour damage increased by 30% in PvP combat.
Voidblade damage increased by 15% in PvP combat. Does not affect Pierce the Veil.
Void Ray damage increased by 30% in PvP combat.
Hungering Slash damage increased by 15% in PvP combat. Does not affect Reaper’s Toll.
Impending Apocalypse now increases the damage of your next Collapsing Star by 15% in PvP combat (was 30%).
Focused Ray now increases the damage of Void Ray by 15% in PvP combat (was 50%).
Inevitable End now reduces the cast time of Collapsing Star by 25% instead of increasing its damage done. Please note that the tooltip will not reflect this change during pre-patch.
Predator’s Wake damage reduced by 15% in PvP combat. This is the empowered The Hunt from Demonic Intensity.
Devourer’s Bite now increases the damage your target takes by 5% per application (was 12%).
Surrender to the Void now increases damage dealt while in Void Metamorphosis by 10% (was 20%).
Hunter
Pack Leader: Boar Charge damage is now reduced by 50% in PvP combat.
Quests
Doomshadow should live a little longer before meeting their demise for players who are participating in “The Big Dig”.
Twilight Ascension Event
Spawns in the Twilight Highlands should properly announce their presence to the zone when they spawn.
Fixed an issue preventing Eoin Dunwald from appearing during the Twilight Ascension event.
The weekly repeatable quests “Twilight’s Dawn” and “Disrupt the Call” may now be completed per-character and they now award Twilight’s Blade Insignias to all characters.
Twilight Ascension World Quests now award both Twilight’s Blade Insignias and Resonance Crystals to all characters as their primary reward instead of awarding one or the other.

title: Midnight Beta Patch 12.0.1 Development Notes - More Class Tuning for All
author: POSTED 26 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has updated the Midnight Beta Patch 12.0.1 Test Development Notes with another round of class tuning for all classes! Keep in mind that these changes will not be reflected on live pre-patch servers until a later date.
View Original
The 12.0.1 Beta will be updated with an additional round of class tuning hotfixes later today. Here are the changes:
CLASSES
DEATH KNIGHT
Hero Talents
Deathbringer
Frost
Exterminate first scythe damage increased by 30%.
Reaper’s Mark damage increased by 25%.
Deathly Blows now increases the damage of Frost Strike by 15% (was 5%).
Swift and Painful now grants 8% increased Strength when no enemies are struck by Soul Rupture (was 5%).
Rider of the Apocalypse
Auto-attack damage of all Horsemen is reduced by 50%.
Hungering Thirst now increases the damage of diseases, Frost Strike, Frostbane, and Death Coil by 5%, and now also affects Necrotic Coil.
Frost
Feast of Souls now increases the damage of Runic Power abilities by 10% when you have 2 or more active Horsemen (was 20%).
Unholy
Whitemane’s Epidemic damage increased 150%.
Trollbane’s Icy Fury damage increased by 100%.
Undeath damage increased by 50%.
San’layn
Unholy
Inevitable now increases the damage of diseases by up to 30% (was 20%).
Infliction of Sorrow now causes Vampiric Strike to extend the duration of plagues by 3 seconds (was 2 seconds).
Blood
Death Strike damage increased by 20%.
Blood Boil damage increased by 15%.
Heart Strike damage increased by 20%.
Death and Decay damage increased by 25%.
Consumption damage increased by 30%.
Bone Shield Armor increased by 20%.
Blood Fortification Stamina increased to 35% (was 30%).
Apex Talent: Dance of Midnight (Rank 1) now has 100% chance to trigger.
Frost
Obliterate damage increased by 15%.
Frost Strike damage reduced by 10%.
Frostbane damage reduced by 10%.
Unholy
All damage increased by 8%.
DEMON HUNTER
Hero Talents
Aldrachi Reaver
Reaver’s Glaive damage reduced by 10%.
Reaver’s Mark now increases damage taken by 5% per stack (was 6%).
Void-Scarred
Devourer
Developers’ notes: We’re taking some power out of Void-Scarred’s Metamorphosis window and shifting it to their non-Metamorphosis effects.
Flamebound has been updated – Hungering Slash critical strike damage bonus increased to 125% (was 50%). This does not affect PvP combat.
The damage bonus of Metamorphosis-empowered versions of Voidblade, Hungering Slash, and The Hunt reduced to 20% (was 30%).
Monster Rising now grants 15% increased Intellect when outside of Metamorphosis (was 8%).
Enduring Torment now increases the effectiveness of Mastery outside of Metamorphosis by 20% (was 10%).
Devourer
Reap damage increased by 75%.
Consume damage increased by 30% and now generates 1 additional Soul Fragment.
Shattered Souls now has a 4% chance to cause a damaging spell to generate a Soul Fragment (was 8%).
Entropy now grants you a Soul Fragment every 12 seconds while in combat (was 8 seconds).
Waste Not now causes Void Ray to have a 200% increased chance of generating a Soul Fragment (was 100%).
Fixed an issue causing male Devourer Demon Hunters to sometimes have a female voice during Void Metamorphosis.
DRUID
Hero Talents
Keeper of the Grove
Balance
Spirit of the Thicket now increases Starfall damage by 12% (was 8%).
Restoration
Potent Enchantments increases Incarnation’s duration by 6 seconds (was 3 seconds).
Class
Matted Fur absorption increased by 100%.
Matted Fur lasts 15 seconds (was 8 seconds).
Regrowth direct healing reduced by 15%.
Wild Growth healing increased by 25%.
Balance
Starfire now deals 70% of primary target damage to nearby enemies (was 50%).
Starfall damage increased by 10%.
Starsurge damage increased by 5%.
Sunseeker Mushroom and Wild Mushroom damage increased by 10%.
Power of Goldrinn damage increased by 5%.
Denizen of the Dream damage reduced by 15%.
Developers’ notes: We’re rolling back some of the previous bonus to Denizen to move that damage into your own abilities instead.
Stellar Amplification increases your periodic effect damage on affected targets by 16% (was 20%).
Guardian
Armor granted by Ironfur increased by 10%.
Mangle damage increased by 30%.
Lunar Beam damage increased by 30%.
Moonfire damage increased by 30%.
Red Moon damage increased by 30%.
Innate Resolve now increases Frenzied Regeneration’s healing by up to 80% (was 120%).
Frenzied Regeneration now heals for 32% of maximum health over its duration (was 40%).
Frenzied Regeneration’s cooldown reduced to 26 seconds (was 36 seconds).
Ursoc’s Fury now grants an absorb shield for 30% of damage dealt by Thrash and Maul (was 40%).
Moonless Night increased to 40% (was 20%).
Killing Blow causes Maul and Raze to deal up to 100% increased damage (was 75%).
Restoration
Developers’ notes: The goal of these changes is to reduce Restoration Druid’s power in dungeons while keeping them at a similar power level in raids. This is primarily accomplished through reducing the effectiveness of their Mastery and Regrowth, while bringing up the effectiveness of Wild Growth and some raid-oriented talents. We’re also doing some targeted buffs for some weaker, unpopular talents for Restoration.
Mastery: Harmony effectiveness reduced by 20%.
Intensity increases Regrowth critical healing by 60% (was 75%).
Unstoppable Growth reduces Wild Growth’s fall off by 30% per point (was 15%).
Improved Wild Growth increases the number of Wild Growth targets by 2 (was 1).
Regenesis increases healing done by Rejuvenation or Tranquility by up to 30% per point (was 15%).
Embrace of the Dream healing increased by 85%.
Master Shapeshifter generates 20% more mana from Wrath, Starfire, and Starsurge.
Nurturing Dormancy increases Rejuvenations duration by up to 6 seconds (was 4 seconds).
EVOKER
Hero Talents
Flameshaper
Devastation
Consume Flame now consumed 4 seconds of Fire Breath from Pyre (was 8 seconds) and 1 second of Fire Breath from Disintegrate damage (was 1.5 seconds).
Consume Flame damage is now dealt at 115% effectiveness (was 125%).
Devastation
Disintegrate damage increased by 10%.
HUNTER
Developers’ notes: Beast Mastery is continuing to overperform across all content, so we’re bringing down their baseline power and apex talent damage and making smaller adjustments to individual hero talent trees.
Marksmanship is getting a small bump in AOE paired with a few buffs to underperforming spells like Kill Shot and Volley.
For Survival, we’re buffing up Wildfire Bomb’s single target damage bonus to ensure it isn’t being forgotten about in Pack Leader’s single target rotation and tamping down Sentinel since its already overperforming and poised to gain a significant boost from the Wildfire Bomb single target damage bonus.
Hero Talents
Dark Ranger
Beast Mastery
Shadow Thrash now deals Shadow damage, but its damage has been reduced by 30%.
Blighted Quiver now increases Beast Cleave and Kill Cleave effectiveness by 5% (was 10%).
Marksmanship
Pact of the Hollow now increases Blighted Arrow damage by 25%.
Pack Leader
Beast Mastery
Boar area damage increased by 200%.
Pack Mentality now grants 4 seconds of cooldown reduction to Barbed Shot (was 6 seconds) and increases Kill Command’s damage by 25% (was 50%).
Survival
No Mercy bleed bonus increased to 35% (was 20%).
Boar area damage increased by 200%.
Sentinel
Survival
Sentinel’s Mark has been updated – Wildfire Bomb damage bonus reduced to 40% (was 80%).
Can’t Miss, Won’t Miss has been updated – Tip of the Spear damage bonus reduced to 4% (was 10%).
Moon’s Blessing now grants 4 seconds of Wildfire Bomb cooldown reduction (was 6 seconds).
Beast Mastery
All damage dealt by the hunter and their pets reduced by 12%.
Apex Talent: Nature’s Ally now increases the damage of Cobra Shot and Barbed Shot by 15% (was 30%).
Marksmanship
Volley damage increased by 25%.
Trick Shots effectiveness increased to 75% (was 65%).
Aspect of the Hydra effectiveness increased to 70% (was 60%).
Kill Shot damage increased by 25% (does not affect Black Arrow).
Headshot now increases damage per stack by 3% (was 2%).
Survival
Wildfire Bomb primary target damage bonus increased to 60% (was 40%).
Two Against Many damage bonus reduced to 10% (was 15%).
MAGE
Hero Talents
Frostfire
Fire
Heat Sink now increases Fire Blast damage by 15% (was 30%).
Dualcasting Adept now increases Pyroblast damage by 15% (was 10%).
Frost
Frostfire Bolt damage reduced by 20%.
Isothermic Core’s Meteor now Shatters 1 Freezing stack (was 2).
Duality’s Pyroblast damage increased by 100%.
Molten Chill Ignite effectiveness increased to 30% (was 15%).
Spellslinger
Arcane
Controlled Instincts has been updated – Cleave percentage increased to 60% (was 40%).
Arcane Splinter damage increased by 80%.
Signature Spell now increases Arcane Pulse and Arcane Blast damage by 25% (was 15%).
Polished Focus now increases Arcane Barrage damage by 25% (was 15%).
Frost
Controlled Instincts damage effectiveness increased to 60% (was 40%).
Sunfury
Arcane Phoenix area spell damage increased by 50%.
Arcane
Arcane Soul duration reduced to 4 seconds (was 6 seconds).
Ashes of Inspiration Haste reduced to 15% (was 20%) and duration reduced to 6 seconds (was 10 seconds).
Sunfury Execution now grants Arcane Barrage 5% increased damage during Touch of the Magi (was 10%).
Glorious Incandescence now summons a Meteor for every 6 stacks of Arcane Salvo consumed (was 5).
Fire
Spellfire Sphere chance to trigger increased to 25% (was 20%).
Burden of Power now increases Flamestrike and Pyroblast damage by 5% (was 8%).
Hyperthermia duration reduced to 4 seconds (was 6 seconds).
Ashes of Inspiration Haste reduced to 15% (was 20%) and duration reduced to 6 seconds (was 10 seconds).
Arcane
Developers’ notes: We’re looking to reduce Sunfury Arcane’s single target output significantly, but don’t want to sacrifice too much of what makes the Hero Talent tree exciting, so we’re opting to reduce Arcane’s baseline power while compensating Spellslinger. We’re also giving Arcane’s area damage some help, since they’ve been struggling in dungeon content.
Arcane Barrage damage reduced by 20%.
Arcane Missiles damage reduced by 20%.
Arcane Blast damage reduced by 20%.
Arcane Explosion damage reduced by 20%.
Arcane Orb damage increased by 60%.
Arcane Pulse damage increased by 10%.
Arcane Surge damage increased by 25%.
Arcing Cleave effectiveness increased to 60% (was 50%).
Resonance now increases Arcane Barrage’s damage by 8% for each target beyond the first (was 5%).
Touch of the Archmage has been updated – Damage taken during Touch of the Magi reduced to 15% (was 20%). Rune primary target damage bonus reduced to 75% (was 100%).
Fire
Developers’ notes: We’re reducing Sunfury Fire’s single target effectiveness slightly and compensating with some area damage bonuses by putting some more power into Ignite’s Mastery scaling. We’re also taking some damage out of Fire Blast and Fireball and moving it into Pyroblast to ensure your Hot Streaks are hitting as hard as they should.
Mastery: Ignite effectiveness increased by 33%.
Pyroblast damage increased by 15%.
Fire Blast damage reduced by 40%.
Scald now increases Fireball’s damage by 25% (was 50%).
Ignition now increases Flamestrike’s Ignite damage by 30% (was 50%).
Frost
Developers’ notes: Frost Mage, particularly Frostfire, has been overperforming in dungeon content for quite some time, so we’re looking to reduce Frost’s area damage power while putting some more power into their single target, where they’ve been struggling a bit more.
All damage dealt increased by 5%.
Shatter primary target damage increased by 40%.
Flurry damage reduced by 20%.
Glacial Assault chance to trigger reduced to 12% (was 25%).
Splitting Ice now causes Flurry and Frostbolt to strike an additional target at 80% effectiveness (was 100%).
Frozen Orb damage reduced by 20%.
Comet Storm now Shatters 1 Freezing stack per Missile (was 2).
MONK

title: Pre-Patch Currency Buffed Again: World Quests and Weeklies on Alts
author: POSTED 26 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
With the weekly reset, Pre-Patch World Quests and weeklies now give full event currency on alts!

Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview


Players who are looking to farm any of the rewards from the Pre-Patch event now have a much more efficient way. After today's weekly reset on NA servers, players have noticed that alts who complete the Pre-Patch World Quests also get the Pre-Patch event currency Twilight's Blade Insignia.



Last week, only your first character was able to earn the bonus 120 currency: 40 from World Quests, and 40+40 from both weeklies. Now, each character can get currency regardless if you already did it this week, making it much easier to farm for any of the items you might want such as Decor or the Retrained Skyrazor mount!

title: One Free Transmog for Each Character Now Available
author: POSTED 26 DAYS AGO BY SQUISHEI
content:
One free transmog is available for each character with the weekly reset!

Two weeks ago, Blizzard announced that players would get one free transmog per character as all transmogs were removed in the Midnight Pre-Patch update. This has now been implemented, and allows each player one free swap to save an Outfit slot with a transmog of their choice.



When saving an outfit, you get the option if you want to claim the free transmog or not. If you're only changing a couple pieces of gear, and want to save your Free Transmog for a full save of an Outfit later, you can opt to use Gold now.



Blizzard has also mentioned that there is a known issue where players may experience an error message stating they don't have enough gold - but this is actively being worked on, and should be fixed soon!
Blizzard
This is a known issue with the fix-up to provide the free change. We’re going to hotfix that check on how much gold you have later this week.

Until then, you can work around it by moving gold from a character who has some to the character you want to use the free change on.

title: Some Big Characters Won't Make It Through The Worldsoul Saga - Interview with PC Gamer
author: POSTED 27 DAYS AGO BY POPULARTOPULAR
content:
In a recent interview excerpt from PC Gamer, Game Director Ion Hazzikostas and Design Director Maria Hamilton suggest that some important characters will not survive through the Worldsoul Saga trilogy.

PCGamer said:
Link
"There will be important characters that do not survive the trilogy. It’ll be a lot of death, I’m afraid," Hamilton explains. "Those are hard decisions. We have arguments about, ‘Is this the right time? What will we do with that person gone?’" Hamilton didn't spill on who might be getting merked by Xal'atath—which is fair enough.

Still, it's a big promise, given that The War Within opened with the fakeout death of Khadgar. Knowing that Blizzard isn't always going to be handling its cast with mittens is a relief. Hazzikostas also says the team's planning on getting around to some of those old plot points, like the giant bloody sword stabbed into Azeroth that hasn't really been touched on since Sargeras put it there in Legion.

"Sometimes Chekhov’s Gun, or Chekhov’s Sword as the case may be," says Hazzikostas, "Is going to lie there for a couple of expansions before something comes of it. Get used to that." When are we going to talk about the sword, Ion? I've been dying to talk about the sword.
In this case, Chekhov’s Sword refers to the sword that Sargeras plunged into Silithus during the Legion expansion. Legion was infamous for killing off several high-profile characters, including King Varian Wrynn, Warchief Vol'Jin, and Tirion Fordring.



Thusfar, the Worldsoul Saga has featured popular characters such as Anduin Wrynn, Alleria Windrunner, and Thrall, while the Midnight expansion is set to feature Turalyon, Arator, Lor'themar Theron, and Lady Liadrin.
Do you think all of these characters will make it through The Last Titan expansion and survive the Worldsoul Saga? Let us know your thoughts in the comments down below!

title: Sylvanas Wont Change in Midnight - Interview with PCGamesN
author: POSTED 27 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In an interview with PCGamesN, Design Director Maria Hamilton said Sylvanas' divisive character arc isn't going to change in Midnight.

 Sylvanas in Midnight Interview with PCGamesN


"I don't want to make any promises about when we'll see her next: I knew we would be seeing her before Midnight came out, and we have. I don't think we could leave out such an important character that people care about in the World Soul Saga, but I can't say more than that."
Fresh from the Maw, Sylvanas indeed returns in Midnight, a story set in Silvermoon and intrinsically tied to the Windrunner family. The developers painting Sylvanas as a complex character, despite being characterized as a puppet of the Jailer since long before we met him in Shadowlands. It's a storyline that players had extremely mixed feelings about, though Hamilton says they have no intention of changing the character's established arc. In a previous interview, Executive Producer Holly Longdale also shared that there was a "path out" for Sylvanas, revealing only that it would be "complicated."
"We don't want to change the character," she highlights. "The character has an arc: it's important that the character has that arc and that it's internally logical. But we do want to hear players' thoughts about it - you're right [referring to my question], it is a very divisive character. There are maybe people who don't know her whole story, who maybe don't understand how she got where she got. Maybe they do, but they don't believe that's right.

"People are really passionate about our characters, so we want to hear what they're thinking," she says, before concluding that "we're trying to do justice to every character we have, and the Sylvanas story is certainly not over."

title: Catalyze Warbound Pre-Patch Event Gear to Create Level 70 Tier Sets
author: POSTED 29 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Warbound gear obtained from the Twilight Ascension Pre-Patch event can be catalyzed, reducing its player level requirement to empower alts and unlock tier set appearances a little early!

 Twilight Ascension Pre-Patch Event Overview


Unlike regular warbound gear, catalyzing the pre-patch event armor reduces its level requirement to level 70.


While this is undoubtedly a powerful perk for characters still leveling through The War Within, it does have some noticeable limitations:
Rare catch up gear purchased from the vendor cannot be catalyzed; only the epics.
Epics cannot be purchased by low level characters, but rare drops and the level 80 vendor equipment are Warbound.
The catalyzing character must be able to reach Dornogal to actually use the Catalyst, which normally requires level 68, or make their way to one of the previous expansion catalysts in Zereth Mortis or Thaldraszus.
Lower level characters can be summoned or ported to Dornogal, but the epics still require a minimum of level 70 after catalyzing.

The level requirement is only reduced, so you can't make completely overpowered characters!


This results in a fairly narrow band of usefulness, but players willing to jump through a few hoops can make their alts a full set of item level 121+ armor, complete with set bonuses! Even if you aren't interested in the short-term power spike, it's also a convenient way of unlocking normal and LFR tier set appearances.

title: China Celebrates Lunar New Year with Another Mystery Box of Exclusive Prizes
author: POSTED 29 DAYS AGO BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Thunderfire Games has revealed their second exclusive event celebrating the New Year in China... another Mystery Box containing more of the recently datamined Lunar Festival cosmetics!


Although acquired from a toy rather than truly tamable, this would be the first time Ashes of Al'ar were available as a hunter pet.


Available on February 5th, this new mystery box features the Thunderhoof Celestial mount, Azure Thunder Coil Pillar toy, Ensemble: Scorching Conqueror and Scorching Polearm transmog, and Auspicious Pixiu pet from our recent datamining. This preview doesn't give us all the details on prices or filler prizes yet, except that the Kirin mount and associated toy are guaranteed after only thirteen purchases.

Interestingly, it also highlights two prizes exclusive to WoW Classic and Titan Reforged: a Blazing Quilen mount and the Embers of Al'ar, a toy which transforms Hunter pets into the iconic phoenix from Tempest Keep!





WHAT'S LEFT?
With the previous promotion containing the Stormgilded Celestial and Gilded Coil Spire, this leaves three toys and the thirteen pieces of decor still unaccounted for from our Lunar Festival datamining. It seems as though the toys are almost certainly going to be CN exclusive, but the decor could still go either way - at least it stands to reason that there would be something new to collect in the upcoming Lunar Festival, of which the decor is all clearly themed around.

title: Crunching for Cultists Achievement Made Easy with Simple Strategy
author: POSTED 29 DAYS AGO BY IMX3
content:
Crunching for Cultists is a new achievement with the Midnight Pre-Patch that puts your Tic-Tac-Toe skills to the test. If you're looking for a quick way to get all 50 wins for the achievement, we've got you covered with an easy strategy!

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
 Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension
CRUNCHING FOR CULTISTS ACHIEVEMENT
Crunching for Cultists requires 50 wins of Tic-Tac-Toe at the Strategem in the Twilight Ascension basecamp. For those looking for the quickest way to complete this achievement, here's a simple strategy:

Pick a faction as your designated marker.
With your first three moves, select 3 corners of the board.
Based on the AI's move selections, select whichever remaining marker will secure three-in-a-row (if applicable).


This strategy greatly increases your chances of winning by creating 3 possible victories that your opponent doesn't have enough moves to block. Keep in mind, this does not guarantee wins, but rather greatly increases the chances of it. Depending on the AI's moves, games can sometimes end as quickly as 3 moves, or at worst, draw after 5 moves.


The opponent does not have enough moves to block all 3 possible win conditions

title: Join the Secret Cabal of Mind-Seekers - New Secret Discovery in Progress
author: POSTED 2026/01/30 AT 7:05 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
A new secret has been discovered by the super sleuths of the Secret Finding & Collections Discord, which appears to be the first step in joining the Secretive Cabal of the Mind-Seekers!




Although this is a new secret, it takes us to an old location - all the way back to Vash'jir and the mysterious pearl floating on its outermost border. While the pearl has been an oddity for ages now, Saltine and Nenami discovered that it now has a function in teleporting players to what appears to be an alternate version of the Seat of Knowledge in Pandaria!


The instructions suggest swimming, though others have suggested flying above and using a Felslate Anchor. Upon arriving, simply swim into the pearl!
Secret Finding Discord said:
Best I can suggest is swim to the surface, head to ~16,85 on the Vash'jir map. Then fly due south in normal flight mode until fatigue kicks in. Back up a little and submerge.

Swim straight down from there and nudge yourself further south. If you get fatigue, back off to reset it then swim straight down some more. Eventually, you can't do this any more (roughly as the sea starts to turn dark blue)
At that point, swim straight at the orb.
Filled with oddities that seem related to past secrets, the particulars of this space are still being discovered, though Flauschtatze has already discovered that speaking to a mysterious dark stranger named Anakron (who appears very similar to the Hooded Supplier from MoP and Legion Remix) awards the Mind-Seeker Feat of Strength and associated Mind-Seeker %s title!

It isn't entirely confirmed what the requirements are for this, though it presumed that most if not all previous secret achievements need to have been earned, with the current belief that any combination of 17 completed secrets are required. Uuna is also present, with several lines of dialogue which seem as though they may be related to any uncompleted secrets.


The area appears filled with pedestals relating to previous secrets, which is fitting for the Mind-Seekers.


Upon joining the secret cabal, Anakron has a speech prepared for their newest member. Though never explicitly confirmed, it's often assumed that the Mind-Seekers are responsible for the mysterious secrets found throughout Azeroth. In the behind-the-scenes blog for Riddler's Mind Worm, the Mind-Seekers even address players directly, taunting the organization's inevitable return.


Welcome to the Mind-Seekers!


This discovery leaves us with more questions than answers, but the secret finding community is on the case, so stay tuned for further updates!

title: Tame Magmorax and Tor'go in the Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 2026/01/30 AT 11:30 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
With the release of the Midnight Pre-Patch, Hunters can now tame two new pets with unique appearances!
MAGMORAX, THE LAVA HYDRA
Guarding the way to Neltharion and Sarkareth as the antepenultimate boss of Aberrus, the Shadowed Crucible, Magmorax is a one-of-a-kind hydra made of fire and molten magma. Engaging the fiery-tempered puppy still requires clearing the first six bosses of the raid, but they can all be done on normal difficulty, which should be pretty accessible in the new expansion.


Raised from a pup by Neltharion, the only thing stronger than the loyalty of Magmorax is his appetite. The lava hydra guards the passage into the deepest chambers of the Shadowed Crucible, keeping his master's secrets safely hidden from prying eyes.
TOR'GO, THE SPECTRAL AZERITE DEVILSAUR
The rare-elite Tor'go is a spectral version of the mighty devilsaur, only found during Worldsoul Memory: Primal Predators in The Ringing Deeps, which means you'll need at least one Radiant Echo earned from Bountiful Delves to start the event. be aware that the creature is considered exotic, and therefore only available to Beast Mastery Hunters.

title: New Addon to Track Time Spiral includes Audio TTS and Icon Reminder
author: POSTED 2026/01/30 AT 9:00 AM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Want to easily track when your Evoker presses Time Spiral? A new addon has been released which tracks when Time Spiral is pressed, allowing for a custom Icon and audio Text-to-Speech!

 Download Time Spiral Tracker


Since the release of Evokers, many players created or downloaded a custom Weakaura for a sound and icon notification when Time Spiral was pressed. Time Spiral is very important in high end raiding content, as a free use of your movement ability is incredibly useful, if not required as part of certain strategies. With the disarmament of addons, and no way to easily track Time Spiral buff in the cooldown manager, this has made it easy to miss when Time Spiral was pressed.

Made by Leblond, you can now download an addon to track when Time Spiral has been pressed, complete with customizable TTS sound and an icon. There is one caveat due to the way it's tracked though -- the icon will always be tracked for 10 seconds since that is the default. If for whatever reason that changes, then the icon timer will be wrong.



You can change the TTS and icon location in Options -> Addons -> Time Spiral Tracker.

title: New Enemy Damage Taken Metric Added to the Damage Meter on Midnight Beta
author: POSTED 2026/01/30 AT 7:00 AM BY IMX3
content:
The Damage Meter continues to receive updates to its functionality, now including the ability to track Enemy Damage Taken in the Midnight Beta.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
 User Interface Hub
User Interface Hub
ENEMY DAMAGE TAKEN ON THE DAMAGE METER
While the Damage Meter is great for keeping track of general damage and healing metrics, an uncommon but useful piece of info to keep track of is Enemy Damage Taken. Especially in higher-difficulty content, being able to keep track of how much damage targets are taking can be useful if a specific target needs to be burst down sooner than everything else.

Here is our first look at the Enemy Damage Taken metric on Blizzard's Damage Meter.



There are a few convenient functionality pieces available with this metric, including:
Rarity logos beside the name of the mob (Elites vs. Non-elites)
Both Total Damage and DPS taken are listed, with DPS taken listed in parentheses
Likely for PvP, class icons are displayed next to the name of the target
Clicking a target's name opens up a separate box displaying who did what amount of damage to the target
WHAT FUNCTIONALITY IS NOT INCLUDED?
Being able to track Enemy Damage Taken is incredibly useful; however, certain features could improve this metric of the damage meter.
Having a Boss logo instead of Elite vs. Non-elite would help distinguish which target is the boss during a fight.
The class logos currently show up next to PvE targets, which causes a lot of confusion.
Targets with the same name are not distinguishable and instead are all categorized into one name on the meter. This can present weirdly, with a target taking over 100% of damage taken if there are multiple targets of the same type.

title: Every Easter Egg in the New Midnight Player Housing Trailer
author: POSTED 2026/01/29 AT 5:30 PM BY JEZARTROZ
content:
In the State of Azeroth presentation, Blizzard revealed (and teased) tons of new information about the immediate future of World of Warcraft. With discussions about Midnight's launch, new features, and Player Housing itself, there's plenty to take a look at - but along with that, Blizzard debuted a brand-new trailer for Player Housing!



This down-to-earth (almost slice of life, for Azeroth) trailer really captures the spirit of the community, from hunting collectibles to interacting with guild mates (and seeing their "Elegant" Lawn Flamingo obsession projects). With the announcements from the State of Azeroth video, there's plenty of easter eggs we can find in the video: both those announced, and some fun ones that may hint to future updates to Housing!
TRANSMOG AND WEAPONS - IT'S TIME TO SHOW OFF
While Ion mentioned focusing on showing off cosmetic collections, one thing he didn't directly state was what would happen with weapon and armor transmogs that people want to display. This trailer, however, has several examples of weapons and armor being displayed around the home in the same way trophies are!

  
 

Wall-mounted, inside other decor, or freestanding on the floor - it certainly looks like weapons and armor will be on the table for the future.
PETS AND MOUNTS ARE MOVING IN
Less of an easter egg, more of a confirmation: Ion mentioned that pets and mounts would be coming to Player Housing, and that's certainly on display here! Between the Mimiron's Head in the front yard (does that look like a stable to anyone?) and the Corgi Pup curled up in front of the fireplace, players will soon be able to add their very own pets to their homes.

 

We don't know what kind of limitations will be placed on this - or how this could work with alts in your warband in the future - but for now, this is a huge step to allowing more personalization in your neighborhood.
TOYS ON PARADE
We've seen potential previews of toys in homes before, but never like this - there are clear shots of multiple Pepes from the Trans-Dimensional Bird Whistle, each with different outfits.



It's unlikely that simply using a toy would be able to provide decor like this, but decor inspired by toys? That should definitely be on the table!
COULD CUSTOM PHOTOS BE ON THE HORIZON?
One of the most potentially exciting easter eggs in the video is a lingering shot of a photo - one that looks like an "in-game" screenshot of the Blood Elf Rogue's guild. This "custom" art is certainly not the standard kind you see models for around cities, so is it possible that players will be able to add their own custom photos to their homes?



We recently discovered what looks like a new promotion related to photo sharing - complete with strings enabling the feature - in a recent datamine. It could certainly be just a coincidence, but the timing, and the focus on Player Housing's official launch, could mean that this feature may actually let players take photos in-game and use them in their homes.

Even more strings have just been discovered, leading us to believe this could be called World of Warcraft: Craft Your World!

title: Find Your Community With the WoW Ambassadors Program
author: POSTED 2026/01/29 AT 4:04 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
Blizzard is introducing the new WoW Ambassadors program as a way for veteran players to foster community in-game and beyond. Sign up today!

 WoW Ambassadors Sign Up
WOW AMBASSADORS PROGRAM
Blizzard
View Original
World of Warcraft has always been more than just a game—it’s a universe where friendships are forged, epic stories are written, and heroes unite from every corner of Azeroth. With the official launch of the WoW Ambassador Program, we invite you to find your people and experience the game alongside like-minded players.
What is the WoW Ambassadors Program?
The WoW Ambassador Program is designed to empower dedicated and experienced players who want to help shape the World of Warcraft community, both in-game and beyond. Our vision is simple: foster engagement and connection among players, ensuring everyone feels at home, whether they are a veteran raider or just starting out in Azeroth.
How the Program Works
Community is the heart of WoW, and Discord servers have become the modern taverns where players gather, strategize, and make friends. Through the WoW Ambassador Program, official and partner Discord communities nominate standout members to represent WoW’s spirit of camaraderie. Ambassadors help welcome new players, organize activities, and amplify the sense of belonging that sets WoW apart. Participating Discords enjoy special recognition, access to exclusive events, and direct support from Blizzard—making every server a more vibrant place to connect.
WoW Portal Room: Your Gateway to Community
Launching alongside the program, the WoW Portal Room is the official World of Warcraft Discord server—a hub where Ambassadors and players unite. Here, Ambassadors serve as guides, event organizers, and community champions, ensuring every adventurer feels included. Whether you’re seeking a new guild, tips for mythic dungeons, or simply want to chat, the Portal Room is where you will take the first steps to finding your people. Just like using a portal in World of Warcraft, the portal room is meant to be a way stop on your journey and not the final destination.
How to Sign Up
Ready to become an Ambassador and your Discord community join the program? Signing up is easy. Applications are now available. Once all the applications are reviewed, players will be able to meet up with the chosen Ambassadors in the WoW Portal Room beginning February 26 to find their fit.
Join Now
Choose Your Path
The launch of the WoW Ambassador Program marks a new opportunity for players to connect in meaningful ways and is built for everyone. Whether you’re leading raids, crafting stories, or just seeking new friends, this is your chance to become part of something bigger. Step into the Portal Room, connect with Ambassadors, and help us create a world where every player is recognized, included, and celebrated.
Check back here on the official World of Warcraft site for more information!

title: New Standalone Game Mode Coming in Midnight Patch 12.1.7
author: POSTED 2026/01/29 AT 3:04 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
Blizzard announced a mysterious new standalone game mode would be coming in Midnight Patch 12.1.7. Past info and datamining may hint at what we can expect.

State of Azeroth 2026 Liveblog
State of Azeroth 2026 Liveblog
 WoW 2026 Midnight Roadmap
WoW 2026 Midnight Roadmap
NEW STANDALONE GAME MODE IN PATCH 12.1.7
In the State of Azeroth presentation, a new standalone game mode was revealed currently slated for release in Midnight Patch 12.1.7 due Late Autumn of 2026. We didn't get much more information beyond a concept art for a dark and shrouded town in Azeroth haunted by an ethereal glowing magic reminiscent of the Drust of Kul Tiras.

 

This new standalone mode is being made by the team that brought us Plunderstorm and Remix (of which we saw those highlighted in the presentation) and would be entirely separate from the core WoW game loop.
Ion Hazzikostas
View Original
Later in the year we also have a new standalone event coming from the same group of mad scientists that brought you game modes like Plunderstorm or Remix. We'll have more to share on this as we get closer to the date. This is going to be another separate standalone mode that exists alongside the core World of Warcraft loop.
While that's not exactly a lot to go on, we have gotten hints at potential future game modes in the past. Let's check out what we've learned previously.
PAX EAST 2025
At the Evolving Azeroth Panel at PAX East in May 2025 we learned that there were new experimental game modes in development for future release. The key art showcased at the event depicted the town of Darkshire within the haunted forests of Duskwood which does seem to line up with the above concept art.



Blizzard confirmed at PAX that this upcoming game mode would not be a Battle Royale, like Plunderstorm. Instead, it will have a vibe that is more similar to a "scary story" leading many to speculate about a potential murder mystery party game possibly inspired by the popular Among Us.
VAMPIRE SURVIVORS DATAMINING
Way back at the end of 2023 in Dragonflight, we datamined some new scenescript files which are typically used for various minigames and cutscenes within WoW. Unfortunately, nothing ever materialized from the datamining and information was limited beyond the below entries.
10.2.5 - Vampire Survivors Mode - Game Mode - Client Scene (OJF)
10.2.5 - Vampire Survivor Mode - Data - Creatures
While this may be a long scrapped experiment, we're still hopeful we could see some version of this introduced to WoW in the future. Vampire Survivors tonally fits with the "scary story" vibe they're leaning into and the gameplay loop of automatically attacking characters fighting against continuous waves of monsters could be really fun in WoW.

What type of game would you like to see implemented as a standalone mode in WoW? Does the 'Scary Story' aesthetic and haunted vibes of Duskwood appeal to you?

title: Enter the Labyrinth: Expanded Delve Activity Coming in Patch 12.1.5
author: POSTED 2026/01/29 AT 2:02 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
During the State of Azeroth presentation, Blizzard has revealed that Patch 12.1.5 will include a new expanded type of Delve called Labyrinths!


Enter the megadelve!


In addition to being significantly larger than normal delves, Labyrinths are unique that they can be tackled all at a once or a bit at a time, giving players the freedom to progress at their own pace, rather than the typical one-shot scenario. This combination makes the Labyrinths more of a Megadelve or raid equivalent for solo and small group players.





While sharing similar elements of discovery and exploration, these Labyrinths will also have a separate rewards structure to account for their greater size and time commitment, though we will continue to explore them alongside our new Midnight Delve companion: Valeera Sanguinar.

title: Player Housing Updates in the Works Throughout Midnight - Pets and Mounts in Houses!
author: POSTED 2026/01/29 AT 12:33 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
Utterly overwhelmed by the creativity, Blizzard looks to continue to support Player Housing with some major updates in Midnight including showcasing mounts and pets, importable design sharing, mass select, and so much more.

State of Azeroth 2026 Liveblog
State of Azeroth 2026 Liveblog
 Player Housing Overview
Player Housing Overview
Neighborhood Endeavors Guide
Neighborhood Endeavors Guide
MIDNIGHT PLAYER HOUSING UPDATES
As part of the State of Azeroth presentation, Blizzard expressed not only their awe at all the ingenious and creative designs but doubled down on their continued support for Player Housing.

Throughout Midnight they're looking to build on the outpouring of feedback they've received and provide meaningful updates to Player Housing whether it's economy tuning, UI tools, or entirely new functionality that supports players' creative expression.

Updates to Housing are coming though the exact timeline isn't confirmed beyond a Housing Update note for Patch 12.1 on the Midnight Roadmap. A few of the updates the development team is prioritizing in Midnight include:
Pet & Mount integration - Have your companion pets to wander around or have your favorite mounts hitched up outside. Want more opportunities to showcase your cosmetic collections.
Mass Select Decor - Players have created inspiring pieces cobbled together out of multiple other decor, but moving these items can require full reconstruction. Blizzard's looking to add support for drag and drop selecting of multiple items .
Copy & Paste Decor - Looking to get multiple copies of your designed decor, copy and paste the same item; No assembly required.
Import/Export Design Sharing - Share your most creative designs with quick and easy importable codes that provide a full renovation or a starting point to customize.
Decor Budget Increases - With technical limits expanding, Blizzard wants to reward ongoing progression with larger decor budgets to truly push the limits of player creativity.


Blizzard made it clear that this isn't an exhaustive list of things they're working on for Player Housing. They're looking to continually iterate on player feedback, especially with the upcoming Endeavors. How do players respond to Endeavors and how do they liven up neighborhoods?
Ion Hazzikostas
This is just the ground floor for housing in WoW. We want this feature to be a reflection of your life and times in Azeroth, and we're serious about expanding it and improving it alongside you.”
How have you enjoyed Player Housing since the start of early access? Are you excited for these new updates or what features would you like to see added?

title: Disguise Yourself as Decor in Prop Hunt: New Activity Coming in Patch 12.0.5
author: POSTED 2026/01/29 AT 11:34 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In the State of Azeroth presentation, Blizzard has revealed Prop Hunt, a new 5v5 hide-and-seek inspired minigame coming in Patch 12.0.5!
PROP HUNT COMING TO WORLD OF WARCRAFT




Also called Decor Duel, Prop Hunt is a queued activity pitting two groups of five against each other in instanced versions of Silvermoon City and the Alliance/Horde neighborhoods. One team will disguise themselves as pieces of decor, hiding amongst various props and doodads scattered throughout the environment, while the other team attempts to find them.





Not intended to be a major feature or progress the narrative of Midnight, Prop Hunt serves as a lighthearted diversion, affording players the opportunity to play with one another in a low-stakes environment. While we don't have any indication of what (if any) rewards may be associated with the minigame, there will almost certainly be Trading Post progress, achievements, and fun to be had for all!

title: Midnight Has Additional Mid-Season Raids in Patch 12.0.7 and 12.1.5
author: POSTED 2026/01/29 AT 11:27 AM BY IMX3
content:
Two new raids are coming in the mid-patch updates for Midnight in Patch 12.0.7 and Patch 12.1.5!

State of Azeroth 2026 Liveblog
State of Azeroth 2026 Liveblog
 Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
NEW RAIDS IN PATCH 12.0.7 AND PATCH 12.0.5FOR MIDNIGHT
Blizzard is introducing two new Raids in Patch 12.0.7 and Patch 12.0.5 of the Midnight expansion. These are separate from the seasonal "main" raids coming in major patches and Blizzard are excited to get away from all of the raids being consigned to the massive twice-a-year updates.

The first raid, coming in Patch 12.0.7, is named Sporefall, and will feature as a stand-alone raid with one boss. This raid will take place in Harandar, the lush and nature-filled zone of Midnight.



The other raid is planned for Patch 12.1.5, which is after the release of Midnight Season 2. While we don't have a name, it was mentioned that this raid will feature one boss. Unlike Sporefall, which is its own stand-alone raid, this raid is planned to be a direct continuation of Midnight's overarching story.

The last time we saw stand-alone raids was back in Battle for Azeroth with the Crucible of Storms raid, and before that, the Ruby Sanctum raid that followed after Icecrown. Blizzard looks to be expanding more on new styles of raid content, which is a welcome change.
MID-PATCH RAIDS, A NEW CONCEPT
In recent expansions, raids were never something players expected to see released with a mid-patch update. Instead, we'd see raids released with seasonal updates. This was roughly a six-month window before a new raid would release.

With Midnight releasing raids in mid-patch updates, along with having seasonal raids scheduled, this shortens the time between new raiding content releases.

Much remains to be seen on how these raids will operate from a gameplay perspective regarding things like loot, difficulty scaling, etc. However, what we do know is that more raid-style content is coming to WoW. What are your first impressions? Let us know in the comments below!
