title: Catch Up on the State of Azeroth - Blizzard Presentation Liveblog
author: POSTED 2026/01/29 AT 10:43 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
The State of Azeroth has now concluded, but you can follow along with our recap, as Executive Producer Holly Longdale and Game Director Ion Hazzikostas give us a glimpse into the future of Warcraft!

The State of Azeroth presentation has now ended - take a look below for everything that was talked about in the video!
Table of Contents
State of Azeroth Liveblog
Coming to Classic WoW
The Midnight Pre-Patch
Player Housing Updates
Midnight Patch Updates
Player Housing Cinematic
Catch Up on The State of Azeroth Bluepost
STATE OF AZEROTH LIVEBLOG
The State of Azeroth has begun, teasing Housing, Labyrinths, and BlizzCon! What does it all mean?!
The campaign of Midnight is the kind of story that should find your heart, whether you just made your way to Azeroth or have been here for 21 years already.
Housing has been asked for since the early days of WoW, they wanted to do it and they wanted it to live up to 21 years of dreams. Since early release, we've all spent over 450 years worth of time and 14 million gold spent, mostly on the secretive bookcase wall!
Blizzard is now happy to announce the WoW Ambassador program, meant to help connect players and communities. Helpful friends from within - check out the website for more details.
This year, Blizzard is happy to announce a partnership with Habitat for Humanity, with a new charity pet pack including the adorable Rufus for both Classic and Modern WoW, as well as a doghouse, bowl, and bed decor. He loves to build while idle.
 COMING TO CLASSIC WOW
Over the past 6 years, Classic has been an expression of love, brought in a variety of flavors for all types of different players. Today, MoP Classic continues, with the Escalation update coming in a few weeks. Siege of Orgrimmar and the Timeless Isle will come later this year. The Burning Crusade Anniversary realm release is also coming soon, and they've recently released Seasonal Titanforged Realms for players in China.
 THE MIDNIGHT PRE-PATCH
We're just a month out from the full release of Midnight, but there's still plenty to do and see before then. The repeatable Twilight Ascension Midnight Pre-Patch event will run until launch, featuring rares, world quests, and bosses to farm for new mounts, pets, and transmog, and decor. Other new additions to the game include the Devourer Demon Hunter, Void Elf Demon Hunters, Transmog 2.0, an updated beginner PvP format, and the return of Winds of Mysterious Fortune!
In celebration of Midnight, the Dark Portal housing decor will be automatically granted to all players who log in between now and the full expansion launch.
Blizzard is also hosting a Welcome Back Weekend, making the game free for returning from now through Feb 1st.
Midnight is introducing a new Level 90 cap, 8 new dungeons, 11 new delves, the Prey system in open world gameplay, the Slayer's Rise epic battleground, the Arcantina tavern of stories, and more!
Season 1 will begin a few weeks after launch, featuring The Voidspire as a 6-boss raid, the Dreamrift as a single boss raid, 4 new world bosses, a new pool of 4 new and 4 returning Mythic+ dungeons, as well as new PvP and Delve seasons. A few weeks later, The March on Quel'Danas 2-boss raid will also be released.
 PLAYER HOUSING UPDATES
Blizzard has heard player requests for player housing, so they're happy to announce they're working hard to allow us to release our player pets into the house, hitch mounts outside, and add copy/paste features to quickly create new custom decor!
Import/Export codes for easy sharing of house designs is also coming, alongside a continual increase to interior and exterior decor limits!
Keep telling them what you want, and they'll keep working to deliver!
 MIDNIGHT PATCH UPDATES
Patch 12.0.5 will hit the PTR shortly after launch, featuring Void Assaults across the world!
On the lighter side, there will be a Prop Hunt style event (also called Decor Duel): a 5v5 queued activity where one team uses enchantment and illusion to disguise themselves as props hidden in the environment, and the other attempts to find them.
Patch 12.0.7 will introduce a new single boss raid!
Not much is being revealed about first major update, 12.1, but it will bring a new zone, raid, dungeon, delves, M+ pool, seasonal rewards, world boss, and other updates we expect from a major patch. There will also be an updated friend system.
Patch 12.1.5 will bring a new content type: Labyrinths! More than the bite sized experience currently offered, they are inspired by mega-dungeons as large sprawling delves that players can tackle all at once or on their own pace.
Patch 12.1.5 will also feature a standalone single boss raid continuing the narrative of Midnight and a standalone game mode, similar to Plunderstorm or Legion Remix!
BlizzCon is also coming later this year, which will show us what Blizzard is planning for the next 20 years of Warcraft!
 PLAYER HOUSING CINEMATIC
Before they leave us, the developers are also happy to share that while the story of Midnight may be dire, Azeroth is still our home with a special cinematic showcasing a Blood Elf claiming treasure off some monsters before heading back to the Razorwind Shores! Entering her house, it's filled with player-requested features, such as wall-mounted weapons and armor, in-house pets, and even a custom picture frame. Adventure is calling us home in Midnight!
 CATCH UP ON THE STATE OF AZEROTH BLUEPOST
Blizzard
View Original
From the roadmaps of what’s ahead for modern World of Warcraft and Classic World of Warcraft, to claiming your own Dark Portal Housing decor item to set up in your own home, we’ve rounded up everything you need to know about the State of Azeroth.
Click to view the full size version.
We’re on the cusp of the Midnight expansion launch and all the stories that will be told as we defend Azeroth from the threat the Void presents. Beyond Midnight, players will be able to experience the third and final installment of the World Soul Saga with The Last Titan.

Beyond the looming darkness and perils players will face, there is also the call of home with Housing, offering a place to rest and express themselves creatively. This feature is twenty years in the making and we knew we had to deliver something that could grow and evolve over the years. Players with the Midnight expansion are already delving into this feature with Housing Early Access with the full launch just around the corner.
Introducing Azeroth Interiors
To shine a light on this new feature, we’re bringing in some of the biggest creator names and best builders alike to go head-to-head in a delightful interior design competition! We’re calling up six pairs of prominent creators from around the world to create a concept, execute on their ideas, and produce exemplary player houses worth coming home to.

You don’t have to be a competitor to get in on the fun. Share your own Housing creations on social media by using the hashtag #AzerothInteriors.

Learn more about what’s in store in our previously published article.
WoW Classic and Looking Ahead
Over the past six years, Classic has been an expression of love for the early years of WoW and bringing it to you in a variety of flavors. Between Hardcore, Seasonal, and Progression realms the community has shown us the ongoing potential of Classic are limitless. We take that to heart and we will continue to honor that.

Mists of Pandaria Classic
Today, Mists of Pandaria Classic continues in the original Classic realms with the next major milestone the release of the Escalation update in just a few weeks. It includes a new quest campaign, battleground, scenarios, and more.

Updates for Mists of Pandaria Classic will continue to be released over time as we have with previous expansions—with the Siege of Orgrimmar and the return of Timeless Isle later this year.
Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition
The launch of Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition is on our doorstep. Another round of Illidan taunts and Fel Reaver terror. You’ll play through the chess event in Karazhan, face Magtheridon, and more throughout the year.
Seasonal Titan Reforged Realms Launched in China
We’ve also launched Seasonal Titan Reforged Realms in Classic WoW for players in China. Why? Not only are the audiences between modern and Classic World of Warcraft unique, but so are the players in China. We made those realms for them based on how they play.

This is not all we have cooking up. We can say with confidence all Classic players across the globe have a lot to look forward to. We’ll save that for later.
Our Commitment to You
What is the State of Azeroth? It’s alive in all its forms and we plan to keep it that way with your help and guidance as we listen to your feedback and act on it. We’ll soon be launching surveys within the game to get your thoughts and feedback. We are committed to listening to you and aim to keep proving our dedication to this ideal. We want to continue providing you with the space, place, and tools to play the way you want to play and live how you want to live in Azeroth. That’s our Northstar.

We want our humble universe to be go to when you have free time, when you need to recharge alongside your friends and family after a long day, or when you need an escape. Let off steam by conquering a boss, roleplaying, now decorating, transmogging, PvPing, exploring, and more. A fantasy home in every sense. Where strangers become friends and friends become neighbors.
Find Your People
We have a plan for those of you looking to find your people. Those players who enjoy WoW the same way you do. We’re calling it the WoW Ambassador Program. It’s all about the community helping the community. It’s less about us and more about you. It’s a bit of an experiment to help connect you with players and communities that are filled with others who play how you play—or how you want to play. These Ambassadors are helpful friends within our own WoW community.
New Charity Pet Pack for Habitat for Humanity
As you know, community is our mainstay and in the era of Housing, we wanted to do what we can to support those who need more than pixels when it comes to having a place to live. This year we’re excited to be partnering with Habitat for Humanity with a charity pet pack that we hope, with your support, will help provide safety and shelter to families in need of an affordable home. Habitat for Humanity has helped more than sixty-five million people globally.

This charity pet pack includes the adorable Roofus who will be available in both modern World of Warcraft and Classic WoW. Roofus loves to build—something you’ll see in action if he’s left idle for too long. Those of you who play modern WoW will also get a dog house, a bowl, and a bed.

We’re incredibly excited to be supporting Habitat for Humanity this year and will have more information to share about how you can bring Roofus home soon.
A Thank You!
As always, you are our heroes, our heartbeat, and our gratitude is everlasting.

Thank you for your continued passion, for the time you’ve put into this world, and for your boundless creativity. It has been amazing seeing so many jump into Housing over the last couple of months. On behalf of everyone who had any part in bringing Housing to life, we are in awe of what you’ve already created. We knew you’d make incredible homes, but the community response has blown away our expectations. There is no World of Warcraft without you the community.

We are honored to be a part of this world and humbled to be the caretakers of something that has remained so special and important to so many people for so long.
The Current and Future World of Warcraft
We want to share a bit of what we’re up to for World of Warcraft in terms of the right now, present content, the fast-approaching launch of Midnight, and a small peek at some of the bigger things we have planned for 2026. We aren’t able to get into all the details just yet as many of these plans involve story spoilers and we want to hold on to some surprises to share later. There’s still a lot to look forward to for all residents of Azeroth in the year ahead.
It’s Nearly Midnight
We’re almost a month away from Midnight launch and the development team is hard at work fixing bugs, refining features, and getting the expansion ready for players to dive into in March. Between now and the launch of the expansion, there’s still plenty to see and do in WoW today.
The Midnight Pre-Expansion Update
Twilight Ascension Pre-Expansion Event
The Twilight Ascension pre-expansion event is live now and runs through to the launch of Midnight on March 2. This is where the stakes start to crystallize and the threat we face begins to take shape. It’s also where the Void makes itself felt in Azeroth. This is a repeatable event in which players join Umbric and Grand Magister Rommath to keep the Twilight’s Blade cult at bay.

Players take up World Quests and defeat rare enemies to disrupt the rituals of the Twilight’s Blade cult as well as earn event currency to use to purchase limited-time rewards such as armor sets, pets, a mount, and more. Players will also be able to collect new Housing decor to help furnish their home in Azeroth. Learn more about the event in our previously published article.
Demon Hunter Devourer Specialization and Void Elf Demon Hunters
Also in the pre-expansion content update, players can take up a new Void-themed ranged Devourer specialization. Void Elf Demon Hunters can be unlocked after a short questline. Learn more in our previously published article.
Systems Updates
Some of the baseline system improvements and updates have also gone live including an updated transmog system that includes situational outfit swapping, an improved user interface (UI), and slot-based transmog so that you don’t have to re-transmog every time you get an upgrade.
Training Grounds
For players new to PvP, you can learn the ropes in the training grounds against smarter game-controlled opponents in the Arathi Basin, Silvershard Mines, and Battle for Gilneas battlegrounds.
Winds of Mysterious Fortune
A limited-time event, reputation gains and experience gains are boosted for players below level 80, it’s a great way to get caught up to speed before the Midnight release whether you’re leveling an alternate character to join the fray or fast-tracking some reputation-based Housing decor items that you’ve been aiming to add to your collection. Learn more in our article.
Dark Portal Housing Decor Item
For a limited time, we’re granting a Dark Portal Housing decor item for all players with an active World of Warcraft subscription or Game Time who log in between now and Midnight expansion launch.
Make Your Stand During the Welcome Back Weekend
From January 29 through February 1, we're giving all players with inactive World of Warcraft® accounts full access to return to the game and all your characters without a subscription. Explore the World of Warcraft base game up to level 80 including all level-up content, the pre-launch events, the recent system updates in the pre-expansion content update, the completely overhauled returning player experience, and more than twenty years of Azeroth’s world and story.

We’re excited for the Midnight expansion and we want to make it easy for you to check it all out for yourself. Learn more about the Welcome Back Weekend in our news article.
Midnight Expansion
Increased Level Cap: The level cap in Midnight is increasing to level 90, and players will level through four new and reimagined zones full of new questing content of both campaign chapters as well as local stories with additional chapters waiting for you at maximum level.
Eight New Dungeons: The expansion also introduces eight new dungeons, eleven new Delves and a new Delve companion, Valeera Sanguinar.
Prey System: The new Prey System adds a new layer of depth and challenge to your gameplay.
Slayers Rise PvP: A new PvP feature is arriving in Midnight with the Epic Battleground, Slayer’s Rise, which also exists as an outdoor world space for those who want to take part in world PvP in War Mode.
Arcantina: Spend time in the Arcantina, a mystical tavern where you can rub elbows with some big names in WoW lore, learn more about them, and complete quests on their behalf.
A couple of weeks after the launch of the expansion, Midnight Season One begins, pulling the story forward through a host of new content.
The Voidspire Raid: This six-boss raid is the beginning of the capstone for the story we’re telling at the start of Midnight.
The Dreamrift Raid: A smaller one boss raid encounter.
Four New World Bosses
Updated Mythic+ Dungeon Pool: Four dungeons will be new Midnight dungeons and four will be four updated legacy dungeons.
New Delves Season
New PvP Season
Shortly after the start of Season One, we’ll introduce a third raid zone with two bosses in The March on Quel’Danas. This new raid will cap off the initial chapter of the Midnight story, bringing us closer to what will come next.

The addition of Story Mode to World of Warcraft has made it so that any player can experience these moments for themselves. We want to continue to lean in, giving you ways to experience the story of Azeroth no matter how you choose to play. Whether you want to jump in and tackle raids, take on Delves, or just hang out in the Arcantina and chat. Our goal is to give you lots to do and plenty of options to select from.

We can’t wait to see how you choose to adventure across the unfolding story of Midnight.
Housing Updates
We have been overwhelmed by your creativity and ingenuity during Housing Early Access, along with your feedback. We want you to help us build this feature together. We want to hear more of what you’re interested in, more of what’s working, and what’s not working. Your feedback is directly informing the decisions we make. You’ve already seen some of the quality-of-life improvements go live over the last couple of months, but we’re keeping a close eye on Housing to continually improve the feature from every angle. Whether we’re talking about economy adjustments, UI updates, or all new functionalities.

We want to keep finding opportunities to support the creative expression that you invent, such as making it easier to float your house as an example. We already have a couple of things in mind. We’ve heard from many in the community that your cozy creation doesn’t feel complete without the ability for some of your companion pets to wander around your home. We want to offer this ability to do this along with showcasing more of your cosmetic collections in general such as being able to hitch a couple of your favorite mounts up in your yard outdoors. We’re working on delivering this as well.

We’ve also seen and heard that as you’re expressing your boundless creativity, you want to be able to move creations in their entirety—such as a grand piano made of various decor items—to a new spot in your home without having to deconstruct it. We want to make it so that you can select multiple pieces and move them all together along with the ability to copy and paste.
As you’re browsing other players Housing designs, we’ve heard that you would like a way to copy and import their looks as a starting point for your own creations, or maybe you have created something you would like to share with others. To that end, we’re working on import and export codes for easy sharing and exchanging your house designs.

We’re also continuing to increase indoor and outdoor decor budgets as we learn more about technical limits of the system along with simply wanting to make sure we’re providing opportunities for progression as you level up your house.

This is only some of what we’re working on with an eye on some bigger things for Housing as well. We’re paying attention to how Endeavors are working, how they enliven Neighborhoods, and how players interact with them, so that we can continue to support the things you want to see from the feature.

This is just the ground floor for Housing in World of Warcraft. This is the foundation of a feature that we want to be a reflection of your life and times in Azeroth and we’re serious about expanding it and improving it alongside you, the player, in the years to come.

Thank you for your passion and feedback so far. Keep telling us what you want from Housing and we will keep listening and keep it coming.
Beyond Midnight
We have a few things to share with you beyond the launch of Midnight beginning with a few specific plans and then getting a bit vaguer as we move into the later part of the year.
12.0.5
Shortly after the launch of Midnight, patch 12.0.5 will arrive on the Public Test Realm (PTR). This update finds us in the aftermath of the Season One raids.
Void Assaults
The threat of Xal’atath has yet to be resolved, so we’re going to see Void Assaults begin to appear across the outdoor world. These will bring us new enemies to fight, new reasons to rally to defend Azeroth, and new rewards along the way.
Prop Hunt
On the lighter side, we’ll be adding a prop hunt-inspired event as a small diversion from the battle between the Light and the Void. In this event, you’re going to queue up to join a team of five to face off against another small team of five in a location such as Silvermoon City. One team will use illusion to disguise themselves as innocuous props to hide within the environment. The other team will try to seek them out. You might not defeat the Void and save Azeroth by taking part in this event, but it’s a great way to find a bit of fun in the face of the ever present and growing threat the Void presents.
12.0.7
Our next small update brings a further escalation of Void Assaults and introduces a one boss raid with Sporefall. We’re excited to move away from the idea that raids need to be massive twice a year updates. We want to lean more into the flexibility of matching the content we offer to the ongoing narrative and find opportunities to tell stories of different shapes and sizes.
12.1
This brings us to our first major Midnight content update. It will arrive with a new zone with maximum level content, new raid, and a new season with an updated Mythic+ dungeon Pool, new PvP and Delves rewards, and continued refinements you’d expect from a major content update in World of Warcraft.

The update will also bring with it an overhaul of the Friends system, making it easier to keep up with friends you have and make new friends along the way.
12.1.5
We’re introducing a new content type. To support the passionate community around Delves, we want to add more flexibility and experimentation. For those who love Delve gameplay but want something more than the bite sized experiences that are currently offered, we’re adding a new feature that we’re currently calling Labyrinths.

These are inspired by mega-dungeons. They are large sprawling experiences you can tackle all at once or a bit at a time depending on your own schedule. They will have a different form and reward structure than are currently available in Delves but retain the core elements of exploring and besting foes alongside your Delve companion.

In 12.1.5, we’re also planning another small one boss raid encounter. Unlike the raid in 12. 0.7, which is more of a standalone experience, this raid will be a continuation of the narrative in Midnight. It will serve as a bridge and set the stage for the next major update the following year.

Later in the year, we’ll also have a new stand-alone event from the same wizards who brought you game modes like Plunderstorm or Remix. We’ll share more about this mode later and it will exist alongside the core World of Warcraft game.
This is all accompanied by the steady flow of content that the community is used to seeing such as holiday updates, events like Turbulent Timeways, and much more.
Looking Forward
There’s much more to come in Midnight and World of Warcraft as a whole and we can’t wait for you to see it. BlizzCon is arriving in just under eight months and it’s going to be one for the ages. We hope to see you there so that we can show you more about what we’re thinking for the next twenty years of everything Warcraft. You won’t want to miss it.

Thank you for joining us and thank you for being a part of the journey with us. We know the Void and Xal’atath are dark threats, the stakes are high and the tone of Midnight can be dire. But we also never want to forget that Azeroth is, for all of us, home. It’s a place of light, community, family, and friends. This is a world that can warm the heart of even the Prince of Darkness.
Adventure is calling you home. We’ll see you at BlizzCon 2026 and, of course, in Azeroth.

title: Unlock Medium-Sized Player Houses at Level 8 for Bigger Bolder Buildings
author: POSTED 2026/01/29 AT 9:00 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Now that the Midnight Pre-Patch is here, players can start working their way towards unlocking medium-sized houses at Player Housing Level 8!

 
Screenshots courtesy of persephonaewow.


While Blizzard has recently increased the caps on Housing XP from Endeavors, acquiring uncommon and rare decor is still the best way to level up - encouraging players to seek out old achievements and new vendors throughout the game world. Early estimate is that around 96% of the current decor needs to be collected to reach level 8, so check out our database and start hunting!



Unfortunately, players cannot preview these new exteriors and the customization available to them before level 8, with the option remaining firmly locked and greyed out within the user interface. With such a high requirement, even community screenshots are a little hard to come by, but we're eager to see what cozy creations players come up with.

Remember, the exterior size of your house is independent of the inside, with just as many rooms squeezed into a tiny cottage as a giant mansion. Keep in mind though that editing the exterior of your housing may adjust its configuration and location, so it can be a very good idea to take a few screenshots, video, or create some kind of marker to be sure it can be placed back in the correct position... especially if you already spent time artfully placing decor all around it!

Then, enter a group and defeat Yogg-Saron in Ulduar Timewalking to collect the Titan Prison Fragment that drops from him.

The Cache of Timewarped Treasures should award a 134 ilvl Hero (1/6) piece of gear, with the potential for a rare Wrath of the Lich King mount like Invincible's Reins! Base loot from Ulduar Timewalking drops at ilvl 121 Champion (1/8).

title: Midnight Pre-Patch Hotfixes for January 28 - Endeavor and Pre-Patch Event Updates
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 8:36 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has posted hotfixes for today which includes hotfixes for Endeavors and the Midnight Pre-Patch event.
Blizzard
View Original
January 28, 2026

Housing
Increased the House XP cap to 2250 XP +250 XP per Endeavor completion, for a total of 2500 per Endeavor.
Adjusted diminishing returns for repeatable tasks to increase rewards after multiple repetitions of activities.
Improved the rewards for the non-Endeavor-specific tasks.
For the first (current) month only: significantly reduced the amount of Endeavor XP needed to complete the bar for the first Endeavor cycle.
Developers’ notes: This is intended to compensate for the fact that it’s the first month, and there is no record of player activity.
Items
Fixed an issue that caused Dragonflight raid cloaks to convert to a set item not intended for your class.
Added a sell price to the Ascension Arrestor’s Dagger.
Quests
Fixed a bug that caused returning level 80 players to be directed to the incorrect content.
Fixed an issue causing players to be unable to use the Path of the Chosen to progress quests during Xal’atath Lorewalking.
Players can again complete “Nuke 'Em Norbit” in Mechagon as intended.
Twilight Ascension Event
Resolved an issue where the scenario UI was not available during the Mirrorvise rare event.
Ritualist and boss health has been adjusted to make them last a bit longer for large groups.
The price of gear on the vendor has been reduced to 20 Insignias (was 40).
Champion track Warbound-until-equipped items will no longer generate an ‘Epic Upgrade’ notification when looted from rares.
Equipment drops are no longer restricted to only one per day, per character, from each individual rare.
[With realm restarts in each region] Rares now spawn every 5 minutes (was 10 minutes).

title: Patch 12.0.1 Midnight Beta Test Development Notes - Class Tuning
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 7:17 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has posted Patch 12.0.1 development notes and class tuning for today which includes changes for Unholy DKs and more!
Blizzard
View Original
Hello testers! Here are this week’s changes to 12.0.1:
CLASSES
DEATH KNIGHT
UNHOLY
Fixed an issue that caused Festering Scythe’s debuff effect to stop functioning when diseases get extended from any source.
Forbidden Knowledge rank 4 will now stop summoning a Lesser Ghoul to Putrefy when the pet is set to passive.
Midnight Season 1 Set Bonus – Scourge Strike now grants its Runic Power when the Class Set 4 piece makes it cost no Rune.
EVOKER
Hero Talents
Scalecommander
Devastation
Developers’ notes: We’re reducing the number of times Deep Breath is available through Wingleader for Scalecommander to lessen the amount of time spent airborne which can be difficult to play. We’re offsetting the damage losses with buffs to other Scalecommander talents.
Wingleader now causes Bombardments to reduce the cooldown of Deep Breath by 0.5 seconds (was 1 second), up to 1.5 seconds (was 3 seconds).
Might of the Black Dragonflight increases Black spell damage by 30% (was 20%).
Refined Essence now increases Essence ability damage by 25% (was 15%).
Preservation
Temporal Anomaly no longer shows on raid frames.
HUNTER
Beast Mastery
Dire Command and the Pack Leader Bear will now properly advance Huntmaster’s Call.
Dire Command now benefits from bad luck protection.
Huntmaster’s Call has been updated - Now summons Hati or Fenryr at 8 stacks (was 6).
Fixed an issue where Fortitude of the Bear’s damage reduction effect would persist when switching Pets or Pet Specializations.
MAGE
Arcane
Expanded Mind has been updated – Now additionally causes Arcane Orb to grant 2 stacks of Arcane Salvo.
PALADIN
Holy
Developers’ notes: We’re returning Crusader Strike as a baseline spell for Holy Paladins in this update with the intention of increasing moment to moment decision making and adding more variety to their rotation. We are introducing a second charge to Crusader Strike and look forward to your feedback on how that feels to play alongside Midnight’s other updates.
Holy Shock no longer overrides Crusader Strike.
Judgment cooldown increased by 83%.
Crusader Strike’s charges are no longer decreased.
Crusader’s Might now causes Crusader Strike to reduce the cooldown of Holy Shock and Judgment.
Holy Shock’s cooldown is now 9.5 seconds (was 6 seconds).
Avenging Crusader no longer overrides itself with Crusader Strike.
Crusader Strike damage increased by 20%.
Crusader Strike cooldown increased by 25%.
Shield of the Righteous now reduces the cooldown of Crusader Strike by 1.5 seconds (was Holy Shock by 2 seconds).
PRIEST
Discipline
Piety redistributes 15% of overhealing (was 20%).
Preventive Measures increases damage of Smite, Penance, and Holy Nova by 15% (was 20%).
Divine Aegis no longer shows on raid frames.
Holy
Fixed an issue causing Benediction (Apex Talent) to heal for less than intended.
Shadow
Fixed an issue causing the damage over time effect from Shadow Word: Madness to not properly account for damage modifiers when casting the ability while the debuff is already present.
Fixed an issue causing Tentacle Slam to engage enemies before it deals damage.
SHAMAN
Hero Talents
Farseer
Restoration
Primordial Capacity once again increases the maximum stacks of Tidal Waves by 1.
Stormbringer
Surging Currents now increases the effectiveness of the next Healing Surge or Chain Heal by 16% per stack (was 20%).
Enhancement
Healing Surge healing reduced by 20%.
Raging Maelstrom now causes Healing Surge and Chain Heal to gain 50% of Maelstrom Weapon benefits (was 100%).
Chain Heal healing increased by 100%.
Fixed an issue that caused Maelstrom Weapon spending abilities to incorrectly do damage as if they consumed 10 stacks of Maelstrom Weapon when Raging Maelstrom is talented without Overflowing Maelstrom.
Rolling Thunder now grants the Nature Feral Spirit when Doom Wind activates while Ascendance is talented.
Restoration
Developers’ notes: We’re aiming to add more interesting decisions to Restoration which has felt lacking in Midnight as well as addressing a core talent issue pushing gameplay towards a repetitive playstyle. To accomplish this, we’re returning Tidal Waves to the talent tree, updating Ascendance with a different set of mechanics, and removing Whispering Waves. We feel this will be more exciting to play with and prevent current and future issues with Whispering Waves pushing gameplay too far towards an unfun playstyle. Thank you for your continued feedback!
Ascendance has been redesigned – Immediately heal allies within 20 yards and transform into a Water Ascendant for 15 seconds. While ascended, Chain Heal jumps to 3 additional allies with 10% healing reduced for all jumps, Healing Wave always critically heals and heals 1 additional ally at 50% effectiveness, and the mana cost of Chain Heal and Healing Wave is reduced by 25%.
Tidal Waves returns and has been updated – Each cast of Riptide grants 1 stack of Tidal Waves, reducing the cast time of your next Healing Wave or Chain Heal by 20%. Can accumulate up to 2 stacks.
Lava Surge can no longer trigger when Lava Burst is not talented.
Echo of the Elements and Primal Tide Core have changed positions in the talent tree.
Whispering Waves has been removed.
PLAYER VERSUS PLAYER
DEATH KNIGHT
Unholy
Stitchmaster now heals for 2% of the Lesser Ghoul’s maximum health (was 5%).
Fixed an issue that caused Life and Death healing to not function.
USER INTERFACE AND ACCESSIBILITY
NAMEPLATES
Important cast highlighting is now more obvious.
Under “Enemy NPC Buffs/Debuffs,” the “Shared CC” checkbox is now enabled by default.
The Endeavors progress bar in the Housing Dashboard now has percentage text on it.
The Item Sets tab in the Adventure Guide has been removed.

title: Midnight Season 1 Mythic+ Dungeon Development Notes for January 28 - Damage and Healing of Enemies Increased
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 7:15 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has posted Mythic+ dungeon development notes for today which includes increasing the damage and healing of enemies.
Blizzard
View Original
Greetings!

Midnight Season 1 dungeon testing continues on Wednesday, January 28 at 6:00 p.m. PST (9:00 p.m. EDT / 3:00 a.m. CEST) and continues throughout the first week of Midnight. Please note that this schedule may be adjusted if we encounter any technical issues during testing.

During the test period you’ll be able to acquire and customize Mythic Keystones by talking to the Keystone Vendor in Stormwind, Orgrimmar and Silvermoon. The nearby Dungeon Teleports NPC will assist you with transportation. Throughout beta, all Midnight dungeons will be available for testing on Normal and Heroic difficulty. Seasonal dungeons will rotate with each new test period.

For this round, the following seasonal dungeons will be open for testing on Heroic, Mythic and Mythic+ difficulty:
Magisters’ Terrace
Maisara Caverns
Nexus-Point Xenas
Windrunner Spire
Algeth’ar Academy
Seat of the Triumvirate
Pit of Saron
Skyreach
Provided that the items equipped on your character are at least item level 226, they will scale automatically to a higher level as needed to support Dungeon testing.

Dungeon Updates

General
Creature health and damage has been increased by 10% in Mythic and Mythic Keystone difficulties.
Dev note: Overall, this change represents roughly one keystone level. We’re making this adjustment based on data from beta realms. As player power levels begin to finalize, we’ll continue to closely monitor throughout the remainder of beta and make further adjustments as needed.
Magisters’ Terrace
Timer increased from 33 minutes to 33 minutes and 30 seconds
Seranel Sunlash
Runic Mark
Number of targets reduced from 3 to 2
Degentrius
Unstable Void Essence
No longer afflicts players standing in the area with a damage over time effect
Maisara Caverns
Muro’jinn and Nekraxx
Carrion Swoop
Freezing traps are now highlighted during spell cast
Hulking Juggernaut
Deafening Roar
Damage reduced by 10%
Nexus-Point Xenas
Timer reduced from 30 minutes to 29 minutes and 30 seconds
Enemy forces requirement increased by 4%
Chief Corewright Kasreth
Leyline Array
Damage reduced by 25% on non-Mythic difficulties
Damage reduced by 50% on Mythic and Mythic Keystone difficulties
Arcane Spill
Damage reduced by 33%
Corewarden Nysarra
Lightscar Flare
Periodic damage increased by 16%
Eclipsing Step
Initial damage increased by 25%
Dreadflail
Health increased by 10%
Lothraxion
Divine Guile
Now pulses damage every 2 seconds instead of 3 seconds
Core Exposure

title: Pre-Patch Event Rares Will Be Summoned Every 5 Minutes Later Today
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 5:13 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced that the Pre-Patch event rares will be summoned every 5 minutes (down from 10 minutes) later today.

Many players have criticized this event noting that there's a long time where players sit around waiting for the next rare to spawn.
Blizzard
View Original
There are additional updates coming today. With hotfixes that are already live to all realms:
Ritualist and boss health has been adjusted to make them last a longer for large groups.
The price of gear on the vendor is being adjusted downwards, from 40 Insignias to 20 Insignias.
And later today, we’ll deploy this hotfix:
Bosses will start spawning every 5 minutes, rather than every 10 minutes.

title: Pre-Patch Gear Costs Reduced by 50% in Recent Hotfix
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 4:59 PM BY JEZARTROZ
content:
In a hotfix a short time ago, pre-patch gear costs were reduced by 50%!

Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension


With the Midnight Pre-Patch event, players have access to Champion track catch-up gear from Armorer Kalinovan. Originally, this gear cost 40 each, but this hotfix reduced the price of gear by 50%, to 20.



This, coupled with the buff to both native drop rates of this gear, plus increased currency from rares, brings the time to farm a single piece of gear (if you're lucky and get 3 currency each time a rare dies) down to just over an hour, instead of more than two.

Additionally, Blizzard just released an official update blue post documenting the change:
Blizzard
The price of gear on the vendor is being adjusted downwards, from 40 Insignias to 20 Insignias.

title: New Twilight Ascension Event Rare Tracker Addon for Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 4:39 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
Never miss a Twilight Ascension rare spawn and Get the Two Minutes to Midnight achievement quickly with the Midnight Twilight Ascension Addon by ZamestoTV.

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension
MIDNIGHT TWILIGHT ASCENSION PRE-PATCH ADDON
Created by Russian content creator ZamestoTV, the Midnight Twilight Ascension Pre-Patch Addon is a comprehensive Rare Tracker for the Pre-Patch event. Simply install the addon and upon loading you'll see the Rare Tracker Window.

 

Once any rare monster spawns, the rare tracker will automatically sync the timers for all remaining rares. You'll know exactly when a particular rare is spawning as well as where it's spawning simply by clicking the map button in the tracker which sets a pin on the world map.

Rares highlighted in green are those already killed for your Two Minutes to Midnight achievement while those in white you still need for achievement progress. At the top you'll see a Twilight's Blade Insignia tracker as well as the remaining time for the Pre-Patch event.

Feel free to hit the Pre-Patch button to show/hide the tracking window or you can use the /prepath chat command. Good luck out there Pre-Patch farmers and may every rare drop gear.

Curseforge for Midnight Twilight Ascension Addon
Curseforge for Midnight Twilight Ascension Addon

title: Purchase Past Trading Post Monthly Bonus Rewards in February
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 3:41 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
With the February Trading Post also comes the opportunity to purchase a wide variety of past mounts, pets, toys, and transmog, as well as 12 past Monthly Bonus Rewards!


Get a second chance at the Wings of the Amber Monarch, Golden Mobile Timepiece, Ensemble: Love Witch's Attire, and more!


Last year, the past February Post also resold several previous Monthly Rewards for players who missed them, giving us a fairly strong indication that this will be a recurring event in future years as well. While it may cost a little extra tender, this should also serve as a major relief for players to know they may have a second chance to acquire things they missed. Note that this special sale does not repeat any item from the previous year though, so be warned: if you miss this second chance, there may not be a third!

title: Play WoW for 15 Minutes to Earn the Portable Bakery Backpiece via Discord Quests
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 3:40 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
You have until February 4th to complete a new Discord Quest awards the Portable Bakery Backpiece for playing WoW for 15 minutes with Discord open!

Blizzard and Discord have resolved the issues, and codes should be redeemable again!
View Original
Heya everyone! The Discord quest has recently been updated and the issue should be resolved now. Completing the new quest and getting a new code should provide you with the bready backpack . If the backpack doesn’t appear in your collection right away after redeeming it, try closing and relaunching the game.
Table of Contents
WoW Discord Quest
Portable Bakery Decor
WOW DISCORD QUEST
A new Discord Quest is available until February 4th, and will award players the Piping Hot Portable Bakery backpiece.

Make sure you have Discord installed, and click on the Direct Messages button in the top left corner. From there, you can click Quests and find the World of Warcraft: Midnight Quest.



Click Accept Quest and play 15 minutes with your Discord client open. You do not need to stream it, like previous Discord Quests. Once completed, click Claim Rewards to earn a code which you need to enter into the Battle.net code redemption link.
Portable Bakery Decor
From this quest, you get the Piping Hot Portable Bakery backpiece.

Blizzard
View Original


Play World of Warcraft for 15 minutes with your Discord client open and earn a Piping Hot Portable Bakery backpiece! The World of Warcraft Discord Quest is available until February 4.

Redemption Instructions:
Log into Discord app with any Discord account
Accept World of Warcraft: Housing Quest in Discord Quests
Log into World of Warcraft via the Battle.net app on any WoW account
Play World of Warcraft for 15 minutes
Claim reward on Discord and receive a code
Claim code at [Battle.net](http://Battle.net) or in the Battle.net app
Receive back transmog in World of Warcraft
Link to the Discord Quest main page can be found here and a direct link to the World of Warcraft: Housing Quest can be found here.

How it Works
Get Tender Every Month
At the beginning of each month, players with an active account in good standing will receive 500 Trader’s Tender automatically every month from a chest called the Collector’s Cache. If you don’t have active game time on your account or aren’t a subscriber at the start of a new month, don’t worry. Your Tender will be awarded on the first day of the month when you do have game time or an active subscription. This will be the same amount for everyone, regardless of how you pay for your game time.
The Traveler's Log
Complete monthly activities listed in the new Traveler’s Log. Each month features a rotating, themed set of activities. These activities provide the opportunity to earn up to 500 additional Trader’s Tender. There’s only a set amount of Tender you can earn each month through completing activities, so you won’t need to complete all the ones in the log each month. You’ll be able to pick and choose from a variety of fun in-game activities to easily earn Tender. Players can choose to earn by continuing to play the game as they already do, such as completing quests, competing in battlegrounds, participating in holiday activities, and even running Mythic+ dungeons. But you can also choose to take part in activities uniquely designed for the month.
Freeze an Item
Before the month comes to an end, if you haven’t purchased that one “must-have” item yet, don’t worry. You can “freeze” an item so you can purchase it later. When you freeze an item, it will stay available month over month until it is purchased or replaced.

If this month’s offerings didn’t have the items you wanted, you can hang on to your Trader’s Tender to spend later. It won’t go anywhere, and unspent Tender will continue to accumulate each month.
Learn more about the Trading Post from the news post on the official site.

*The Trading Post feature is not available in WoW Classic titles.
**Requires World of Warcraft Subscription or Game Time
***Requires Level 10 to access the Adventure Guide.

title: (Update) Lua API Changes for Midnight Launch Ending Soon with Release Candidate Coming
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 11:00 AM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard, through the WoWUI Discord, has posted UI development notes. For Midnight launch, Blizzard are working on fixing exploits and high-priority dev requests for the Lua API and the remainder of Lua requests will likely be waiting until 12.0.5.

Edit: Blizzard has clarified further on Redddit that this is for the Lua API and changes to classes, the base UI and more are still planned.
Blizzard
View Original
It's important to note that the message here -- one from a WoW Lead Software Engineer to a community of addon authors -- was talking about changes to the Lua API that they use to write addons, and not anything else such as development on any part of the game or its User Interface or classes, etc. etc.

This is how we've tried to always treat the API. We don't want to modify it in between patches, other than to address extreme bugs/exploits, because doing so would probably break players' addons until they get updated.

We have UI updates in 12.0.1 (coming soon!), and we almost-continuously hotfix the game to address quality-of-life issues, such as damage meter functionality, or class abilities currently missing from the cooldown manager, and bugs, and everything else.
This means that we can and likely will see base UI functionality improve during 12.0.1 and through hotfixes.
This is important as many players are still looking for addon improvements and Blizzard have announced that only exploit and high priority requests are making it to Midnight launch for the addon API. Any other requests are looking to have to wait until Midnight's first major patch 12.0.5.
WoW Devs via WoWUI Discord said:
Blizzard again! Below you will find a list of changes we are making to addon restriction APIs in the coming weeks.

These are not the entirety of the changes we are planning, just the ones already committed to for the immediately upcoming Beta releases.

IMPORTANT: The RC process for 12.0.1 has begun, and while the Beta build this week is likely not going to be the final RC we are getting close. As a result, only very high priority issues are being worked on in 12.0.1 (mostly exploits, but also a smattering of remaining high-priority addon dev requests). Once we get closer to 12.0.1 launch we will release a list of tasks we plan to work on for 12.0.5 so you can have a better idea of the upcoming roadmap (including many of your requests).

Critical changes due to be deployed to live/pre-patch over the next few days
Fixed an issue where the C_UnitAuras.AddPrivateAuraAppliedSound API caused Lua errors at the time of the sound playing.
Fixed an issue causing secret-related Lua errors in Edit Mode.
Fixed an issue causing click casting to get eaten by private auras on raid frames.
Fixed various issues with secrets in tooltips causing Lua errors.
Fixed an issue where responding to a BNet whisper could cause a Lua error in auto complete code.
Fixed an issue causing secret-related Lua errors when addons created dropdowns.
Fixed an issue where arena frames could cause Lua errors when exiting edit mode.
Changes in Beta 8 (ETA 1/28)
Boss Warning APIs
Added a new C_EncounterEvents namespace that allows querying static information about encounter events.
Added a new C_EncounterEvents.SetEventColor API that allows addons to associate a specific color with an encounter event (our UI will use it to colorize text and timer bars).
Added a new C_EncounterEvents.SetEventSound API that allows addons to specify a sound to play when encounter events show text warnings or transition states on the timeline.
Sounds will be played automatically by the client.
Aura Filters
Added 4 new filter types for Aura APIs:
CROWD_CONTROL
BIG_DEFENSIVE
RAID_PLAYER_DISPELLABLE (returns auras the player can dispel)
RAID_IN_COMBAT (returns auras that are flagged to show on raid frames in combat, when used with Player and Helpful filters this should return mostly just HoTs).
Misc Changes
|T string markup inside secret strings now permits the use of local file overrides if the texture is part of the base game.
Referencing custom addon textures using secret strings is still not allowed.
Fixed an issue where tainted calls to CVarCallbackRegistry:GetCVarValue could store tainted values in the cached value table, causing restriction issues.
Fixed an issue in the Restricted Environment ipairs function that caused it to return early when any falsey value was stored in the table.
Added the optional start and end range parameters to unpack in the Restricted Environment.

title: Stormwind and Orgrimmar Updates Considered in Interview with PC Gamer
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 10:15 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In an interview excerpt from PC Gamer, Associate Game Director Paul Kubit and Manager for Visual Development Gabriel Gonzalez indicate that Stormwind and Orgrimmar world updates may be considered if Midnight's Silvermoon City redesign is well received.


PC Gamer said:
Link
"It was a little scary, honestly, to go back in to tackle that zone again," says Gonzalez regarding Silvermoon and the Ghostlands, given their nostalgia-drenched position within the Burning Crusade. "Are we going to make the right choices, visually? Are we going to change it too much? Are people going to feel like we’ve taken something away from them?"

As a matter of fact, Gonzalez admits that "most parts of the city, it was just smoke and mirrors. There were a lot of parts of that city that just were not there." That's not too strange to hear—when Cataclysm rebuilt much of the game's two base kingdoms for flying mounts, there were a ton of zones (previously only accessible via out-of-bounds glitches) that were flat, featureless planes.

Even without that motivation, though, Kubit states that this is a sign of greater things to come: "We’re really anxious to see how players respond," he says, "If it’s positive, I think it opens up a bunch more storytelling opportunities." He then tells Heather that reimaginings for the major cities of the Alliance and the Horde—Stormwind and Orgrimmar, respectively—are an example of something they'd like to tackle.
Updating more old world zones has been a frequent topic of discussion in various interviews over the past year, with the developers expressing interest in telling more stories within them, and also recently spoke about redesigning Northrend for the Last Titan expansion. Another world-wide Cataclysm overhaul doesn't seem likely though, with the developers instead focusing on one area at a time.

title: New Auto Reset Option for Damage Meter Datamined on Midnight Beta
author: POSTED 2026/01/28 AT 9:16 AM BY IMX3
content:
We've datamined a new auto-reset option upon entering a new instance on the Midnight Beta, a feature that players have been requesting for some time now.

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
 User Interface Hub
User Interface Hub
AUTO-RESET DAMAGE METER OPTION
While not currently available, two new spell strings were added to the Midnight Beta, suggesting that auto-reset functionality for the Damage Meter will be coming soon.

NEW AUTO_RESET_DAMAGE_METER
Auto Reset Damage Meter
NEW AUTO_RESET_DAMAGE_METER_TOOLTIP
Toggle on to have the damage meter reset all values every time you enter a new instance.


A common frustration with the current Damage Meter is that statistics from different instances are listed together. That Demon Hunter you ran the previous dungeon with? Their damage will still show up in your current dungeon statistics, even if you aren't grouped with them anymore. Having the ability to auto-reset the Damage Meter will prevent this from happening and thus prevent further confusion.

Alongside the launch of the Midnight pre-patch event is the Winds of Mysterious Fortune event, where players will receive bonus experience and reputation gains!

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview

Winds of Mysterious Fortune Event Guide
WINDS OF MYSTERIOUS FORTUNE EVENT
The Winds of Mysterious Fortune is a leveling-focused event. Throughout the event, characters from levels 10 to 80 will receive +20% Experience from all sources and 50% increased Reputation gains for factions from the Dragonflight and The War Within expansions.

The current iteration of the Winds of Mysterious Fortune event will run from January 27th, 2026 to March 2nd, 2026.


With the Midnight expansion coming soon, now is the perfect time to catch up on any leveling and wrap up any reputation gains from The War Within and Dragonflight.
Blizzard
View Original
Get satchels chock full of valuable and unique items during the Winds of Mysterious Fortune event and take advantage of the Winds of Mysterious Fortune 20% experience buff—catch up on your alts, finish missed content and unlocks, and get ready for the launch of World of Warcraft®: Midnight™.

When : January 27 through February 22
You Get a Satchel, and You Get a Satchel!
The winds of fortune blow in your favor during the Winds of Mysterious Fortune event.

While questing and adventuring, players below level 80 can gain Mysterious Satchels bursting with goodies while looting creatures or as a bonus reward from completing quests.

Mysterious Satchels contain various unique and valuable items, including temporary stat boosts, visual alterations, a chance for bonus gear appropriate to your level and specialization, and 10% experience potions that stack with the 20% experience buff to a maximum of 30%. Experience potions are a Warbound-bound item that can be consumed during the event or saved until after the event is over for use on your other level 10–79 characters. However, any stat or elemental potion effects will not work in Mythic+ Raids or rated PvP content

If you think Mysterious Satchels are amazing, then you’ll love Pristine Mysterious Satchels. The first Mysterious Satchel drop you earn daily will be Pristine quality, giving you a greater chance to earn even more valuable rewards.
A Buff to Speed Things Along
Take advantage of the Winds of Mysterious Fortune 20% experience buff to get characters level 10–79 up to speed. Catch up on your alts, finish missed content and unlocks, and prepare for World of Warcraft: Midnight. This buff also provides +50% to major faction renown in Dragonflight and The War Within zones.
May the road rise up to meet you, and the Winds of Mysterious Fortune be at your back.

title: Farm Rares in Twilight Highlands for Two Minutes to Midnight Mount
author: POSTED 2026/01/27 AT 2:27 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Farm 19 rares in the Twilight Highlands Pre-Patch event to earn the Light-Forged Mechsuit mount!

Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview: Twilight Ascension
Table of Contents
Two Minute to Midnight
18 Rotation Rares
Voice of the Eclipse
TWO MINUTE TO MIDNIGHT
The Two Minutes to Midnight Pre-Patch achievement requires you to defeat 19 rares for the %s, Thorn of Twilight and Light-Forged Mechsuit mount. These are limited time rewards that are Midnight Pre-Patch only so make sure you get them now!


18 ROTATION RARES
There are 18 rares which hard coded into a rotation and will spawn once every 10 minutes. The rotation is listed below:
Redeye the Skullchewer → T'aavihan the Unbound → Ray of Putrescence → Ix the Bloodfallen → Commander Ix'vaarha → Sharfadi, Bulwark of the Night → Ez'Haadosh the Liminality → Berg the Spellfist → Corla, Herald of Twilight → Void Zealot Devinda → Asira Dawnslayer → Archbishop Benedictus → Nedrand the Eyegorger → Executioner Lynthelma → Gustavan, Herald of the End → Voidclaw Hexathor → Mirrorvise → Saligrum the Observer
This means that if you want to do it in one go, it will take you 3 hours. If you miss a rare, you'll need to wait another 3 hours for it to summon again, but at least you'll know exactly when it will spawn again.
VOICE OF THE ECLIPSE
The 19th rare is Voice of the Eclipse. This rare is not on a hard rotation like the others and will be summoned at varying times.

If you have to choose between a rotational rare of the Voice of the Eclipse, prioritize the rotation rare first!

The Twilight Ascension pre-patch event for Midnight begins on January 27th and will last until March 2nd—the official release date for the Midnight expansion. The Twilight's Blade are invading the Twilight Highlands; defeat the ritual leaders and protect the Twilight Highlands!

To gain access to the Midnight pre-patch event, players must first complete the questlines starting with The Cult Within / The Cult Within. Player Housing Overview

In Midnight, we’ve opened the doors to boundless creativity with the introduction of Housing. To shine a light on this new feature, we’re bringing in some of the biggest creator names and best builders alike to go head-to-head in a delightful interior design competition!

You don’t have to be a competitor to get in on the fun. Share your own Housing creations on social media by using the hashtag #AzerothInteriors.

February 19-21- Phase Two: Building
February 24- Phase Three: Walkthroughs and Judging

title: Updated: Big Buffs to Player Housing Endeavors Coming Tonight
author: POSTED 2026/01/27 AT 11:03 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has posted a big update on Housing Endeavors, as they will soon be increasing the experience cap, reducing diminishing returns on completing repeat tasks, improving XP rewards for generic tasks, and reducing the amount of tasks needed to complete the first Endeavor!

 Housing Endeavors Guide


Blizzard has issued an follow-up, revealing that these changes will coming tonight/with the next weekly reset in each region!
Blizzard
View Original
Hello everyone!

We’d like to share a couple of updates regarding the upcoming endeavor changes:

During testing, we identified an issue with the planned adjustment to reduce the XP required to complete the first endeavor to 400 XP. Instead for this first endeavor, we are making it so that tasks now grant a lot more progress towards the endeavor bar per completion.

All the endeavor updates will take effect with the next restart:
EU and Asia realms: During this week’s weekly restarts.
US realms: During an off-peak restart tonight.
Thank you and we’re looking forward to you experiencing the changes!
The rest of the original bluepost still holds true, with one small change in that this first monthly endeavor will not have its XP requirements reduced, but that tasks will instead award significantly more progress towards completing it.
Blizzard
View Original
Hello neighbors!

It’s been a week since Endeavors went live and we’ve been reading your feedback and working through some improvements that we’re bringing to Endeavors in the next few days.

Earning More House XP

The most prominent feedback has been on House XP being capped per Endeavor. This cap exists so that collecting new decor for XP remains valuable, but we agree that a cap of 1000 XP is too limiting when compared with the XP needed to level your house.
We’re increasing the per-Endeavor House XP cap from 1000 to 2250. With the 250 XP from completing the Endeavor, this brings the total possible House XP from an Endeavor to 2500 XP.
With 40 days total for this first Endeavor and then a (roughly) monthly duration once Midnight launches, this change means you’ll gain about 1-2 house levels just from the monthly Endeavor alone.

This change will not grant retroactive House XP, but it will increase the cap for the current Endeavor, so everyone can earn an additional 1250 XP.

Diminishing Returns

The next most common point of feedback is about diminishing returns on task rewards. These exist to encourage you to explore other tasks and not do a single task over and over again. However, we recognize that not all tasks are for all players, and different players have preferences which could lead them into doing a small set of tasks repeatedly.
We’re increasing the floor of the diminishing returns from 20% to 50% as well as slowing its descent.
This change will affect both the rewards (House XP and Community Coupons) as well as the Endeavor XP. This means if you’d previously gone from earning 10 to earning 2 coupons after multiple completions, you’ll now earn a minimum of 5 coupons after this change.
If you’re currently earning the minimum coupon amount due to repeated completions when the update goes live, you may need to log out and log back in to show the new value.

We’re also introducing a weekly reset for the diminishing returns on repeatable Endeavor tasks. Given the timing of this update, this means the first reset will be the week of February 3.

Two Other Changes

Endeavor difficulty scales with your Neighborhood’s activity level. However, given the lack of information for this first month, the system hasn’t yet been calibrated to your Neighborhood activity level. Therefore, for this first Endeavor only:
We’re reducing the amount of XP needed to complete the first Endeavor from 1000 to 400.
This should make things feel much better for smaller / less active Neighborhoods. Additionally:
We’re improving the rewards for generic tasks from 10 to 50 XP.
Generic tasks are those that provide some XP but are not themed to the current Endeavor, and this change applies only to them as a way of making them feel more rewarding.

A Few Notes

The Endeavor user interface (UI) doesn’t mention the XP cap or diminishing returns, but if you see House XP as a reward, you haven’t yet hit the cap.
UI improvements are coming in a future update.
If you’re at the House XP cap, you’ll still contribute to the Endeavor’s progress. Very engaged players can carry a Neighborhood to completion (even more so with the changes above).

Don’t forget – there’s a 20% bonus to rewards if you’re playing in a group with 2 of your neighbors. Enjoy grouping up when you’re endeavoring!
We’re looking forward to more of your feedback and we will continue to adjust as needed. Housing is a long and exciting journey that we’ve only just begun!

title: Chinese Exclusive Lunar New Year Mystery Box Promotion
author: POSTED 2026/01/26 AT 11:31 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Thunderfire Games has announced a Lunar Mystery Box promotion, confirming that at least a couple of the recently datamined Lunar Festival collectables will be exclusive to China.


Team up and spend together to receive great gifts!


Included in the promotion is the newly datamined Stormgilded Celestial mount and Gilded Coil Spire toy, alongside a new Golden Ashened Cataclysm reskin of the Obsidian Worldbreaker. While the other datamined toys, transmog, pet, and decor are not included in this particular mystery box, the announcement also teases an upcoming New Year's Fortune Event and Battle.net store sale, which could include some or all of the remaining datamined items. Therefore, it is currently unclear whether any of the rest will be available to the rest of the world or not.







In addition to the two new mounts are six more rewards, most of which return from the original TCG. A subsequently released infographic details the mounts as having a 1.25% drop rate, while the consumables make up the other 98.75%.
Reins of the Spectral Tiger
X-51 Nether-Rocket
X-51 Nether-Rocket X-TREME
50x Blazing Fire Sandbox Horse (new consumable, presumably similar to the Sandbox Tiger)
25x Azure Dragon Soaring Flame (new consumable, presumably similar to various fireworks)
50x Path of Cenarius
GATCHA!
Similar to last year's Blazing Royal Firehawk and Golden Ashes of Al'ar promotion, prizes are earned by opening Landro's Loot Box, purchased in the store for about ¥100 RMB ($14 USD) each. Eight additional boxes are given out as free rewards for personal purchase milestones, while players can also "team up" with up to nine others to earn up to four more boxes from their purchases.


1 point = 1 RMB, for a total of ¥1,7000 RMB ($245 USD) individually and another ¥5,000 RMB ($719 USD) spent across the team to earn all 12 boxes.


Players can buy as many boxes as they want on top of the twelve freebies though, which not only allows them to play the odds, but also acquire duplicates. Unlike most collectables, these prizes are not bind-on-pickup and can be looted more than once, allowing them to be traded not only between players, but also game modes, with several of the rewards redeemable on Retail, Classic, or Titan Reforged servers. This means a determined player needs to acquire more than one in order to use them in multiple game modes. Considering that these promotional items have not yet returned or been made available since, this also creates a lucrative secondary market for savvy dealers who hold onto their rewards - the Golden Ashes of Al'ar now sells for nearly ¥11,000 RMB ($1,600 USD)!
MORE COMING SOON
While this promotion only accounts for two of the twenty-three Lunar Festival themed items datamined last week, the teaser closes with a banner advertising a "New Year's Fortune" event and Battle.net store sale coming soon. This does not confirm, but does give us a reasonable belief that more of the datamined items will be included: the Thunderhoof Celestial, Azure Thunder Coil Pillar, and Jade Monument being most likely, as counterparts to today's Mystery Box prizes.


New Year's Fortune event / Battle.net sale is about to begin, please stay tuned!


Whether the Auspicious Pixiu pet, Ensemble: Scorching Conqueror and Scorching Polearm transmog, Fortune's Waving Cat and Gilded Fountain toys, or 13 pieces of decor will also be included remains to be seen, though it does stand to reason that at least some of them would be included in the worldwide Lunar Festival event this February.

title: Midnight Pre-Patch Event Begins Tomorrow - Blizzard Blog
author: POSTED 2026/01/26 AT 3:01 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
The Midnight Pre-Patch event starts tomorrow and Blizzard has released a blog about it!

For more details, check out our Midnight Pre-Patch Event Guide.

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Event Overview
Blizzard
View Original
Travel to the Twilight Highlands to push back the threat of the Twilight's Blade cult and stop them from summoning more forces by disrupting their rituals and defeating rare enemies across the highlands. Available to characters level 10 and above, you’ll also take on World Quests and reap the rewards of a job well done during this repeatable event. Alliance players will meet with Magister Umbric in Stormwind to begin their journey and Horde players will meet with Grand Magister Rommath in Orgrimmar.
The Cult Within
Join Magister Umbric and Grand Magister Rommath as they work to keep the growing Twilight's Blade cult at bay. You’ll play through an introductory questline to kick things off and receive the Twilight's Blade Tabard on first completion for your Warband along with the title “Definitely Not a Cultist.”
World Quests and Rares
Continue on after the completion of the introductory quest line to take on two additional weekly quests Disrupt the Call and Twilight’s Dawn, Twilight Highlands World Quests, and defeat rare enemies to disrupt the rituals of the Twilight’s Blade cult.

You’ll earn Twilight Blade Insignias that you can use to purchase items from the vendors at the Ascension Base Camp in Twilight Highlands.
Rewarding the Efforts
You’ll be able to earn two achievements:
Two Minutes to Midnight- Defeat each of the bosses being summoned by the Twilight’s Blade in the Twilight Highlands during the Midnight pre-launch events. Rewards: Light-Forged Mechsuit (mount) and “Thorn of Twilight” title.
Crunching for Cultists- Win the Twilight’s Blade Stratagem game in the Twilight Ascension basecamp.
Visit Armorer Kalinovan to purchase item level 121 (Upgrade Level Champion 1/8) gear and Materialist Ophinell to purchase a variety of cosmetic rewards including pets, a mount, a toy, Housing decor, and transmog sets.

title: ElvUI Now Updated for Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 2026/01/25 AT 7:45 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
ElvUI has been updated for the Midnight Pre-Patch supporting customizations for basic filtering, cooldown manager skinning and much more!

 Download ElvUI
ElvUI is a popular addon that many players use because of how many different customization options there are. If you want customized UnitFrames, Nameplates, BossFrames, Action Bars, Bags, and more, then ElvUI has options for all of those. ElvUI is now on version 15.0.0 which means that it is compatible and usable with the Midnight Pre-Patch.
ElvUI said:
Update
The first official ElvUI 15 build is about to be released to all the usual sources (Website, Tukui Client, WowUp and Wago App).
As mentioned in our original Midnight post, this initial release is not perfectly fine but a very good start to keep working on reports and features. Special shoutout to p3lim and the oUF team for maintaining the library to keep our Unitframes and Nameplates powered for Midnight
Thanks to Nevcairiel for updating the action button library which makes it easier for us to have our Actionbars

Compatibility
We've done our best to test and verify all of our Midnight changes are as compatible with Classic, TBC and Mists as possible
Style Filters and Portraits have been removed for all versions of the game and Cutaway Bars has been removed for Midnight
The new basic filtering for buffs and debuffs can optionally be used in Classic, TBC and Mists (its a performance increase)
Midnight
Basic filtering for buffs and debuffs has been implemented for all frames, based on what Blizzard allows in 12.0
Basic text format tags have been added, logic such as "only show last name" and similar is no longer possible
Basic cooldown manager skinning and font settings implemented in the BlizzUI Improvements tab under General
Cooldown text settings have been reworked from scratch, if you don't see them on Actionbars u have to enable "Show Numbers for Cooldowns" in Blizzard settings
Custom classbar for the Devourer Demon Hunter has been added
Custom classbar tab for "Additional Power" has been added to display Ebon Might for the Augmentation Evoker
Nameplate classification colors have been added for enemy types Melee, Caster, Miniboss, Boss, Rares and more (these work in dungeons and raids as well!)
Nameplates Health Width setting is now separate from Nameplates > General > Clickable Width
Good to know
Added a "Reset Text" button next to all "Text Format" boxes in Unitframes and Nameplates to restore (and fix in case of Midnight) our defaults
Cooldown manager
We have noted down a big list of ideas for features we would like to work on for the cooldown manager
The general long-term plan here is to make a dedicated "Cooldown Manager" tab in the main ElvUI config on the left side
Nothing is set in stone yet, this implementation might take weeks or months or not even happen at all, but the general thought exists
Known issues
Support for the new Diminishing Returns Tracking module on Arena frames has not been implemented yet
The border for Private Auras on Unitframes is not scaled yet, expect it to be very big and overlapping until we get to work on it and test

title: Valorstones Remain Until Full Midnight Launch
author: POSTED 2026/01/25 AT 2:00 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
While gear can still be upgraded during the Midnight Pre-Patch, Valorstones are also sticking around until the full expansion launches in March! Thankfully, they're easier to get than ever before, and can even be transferred between characters.

 

With the regular season ending, some players were concerned that Season 3 items could no longer be upgraded, though of course that is not the case. However, while we learned that Valorstones would be removed in Midnight, this does not appear to be taking effect until the next season, after the full expansion launch.

This means you'll still need those pesky stones to change itemization or upgrade catch-up gear earned during the upcoming Pre-Patch event, so you may want to stock up before gearing any new alts.


Valorstones are now Warbound transferrable!


They're not too difficult to get though. Not only do they drop from practically every activity, Valorstones can now be transferred across characters, allowing you to funnel them to whichever alt needs them the most. If you still need a few more, Worldsoul Memories can be a quick and easy way to farm several hundred, provided you have the Radiant Echoes currency from completing bountiful delves, and any leftover Algari Token of Merit from unwanted Great Vault rewards can be traded for one Golden Valorstone each.


Valorstones can be purchased or farmed, then transferred to whichever character needs them.

content:
The famous owner of Frederick's Fabulous Furniture, Frederick the Fabulous, has been found in the Jade Forest of Pandaria and has a unique Housing Decor reward for finding him!

Player Housing Overview
Player Housing Overview
 Player Housing Decor Items
Player Housing Decor Items
FREDERICK THE FABULOUS
Frederick the Fabulous was datamined back in Patch 11.2.7, yet for some time, players weren't able to find this blob anywhere throughout Azeroth. This name has been teased for quite some time; the Pandaren NPCs who give furniture quests all have the title <Frederick's Fabulous Furniture> following their name.

In a Reddit post made by Wrain_Fox, the location of Frederick was finally discovered! Frederick the Fabulous is in the northeast end of the Jade Forest, located underwater in a flipped ship.


Tip: Click map to zoom
Hide pins
Change floor...
The Jade Forest: [Level 0]


/way #371 57.72 15.68 Frederick the Fabulous
GOLDEN CLOUD SERPENT TREASURE CHEST
A while back, a Golden Pandaria chest was mysteriously listed on a vendor not yet available to players. Many players spent a lot of time trying to figure out how to earn this chest, yet no one was ever able to figure it out.

We now have a conclusion to this mystery. For finding Frederick the Fabulous, players can now receive the Golden Cloud Serpent Treasure Chest.

Before going and finding Frederick, however, players will need to complete the small decor questline from Second Chair Pawdo, starting with Furniture Favor and ending with Last Light. Once completed, the chest can be looted from Frederick.

title: Photo Sharing Coming to WoW - New Strings Discovered in Midnight
author: POSTED 2026/01/24 AT 7:00 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
New strings uncovered on the Midnight Beta reveal that a new Photo Sharing feature may be coming coming to World of Warcraft!

Originally added alongside the S.E.L.F.I.E. Camera MkII in Patch 6.1 of Warlords of Draenor, Twitter integration allowed players to connect their World of Warcraft account to Twitter, in order to easily post screenshots or share in-game achievements. While no reason was ever given, the feature was eventually removed in February 2023, presumably due to then-new Twitter API changes charging third party users a monthly fee.



These new strings appear to match much of the same functionality, and even include a new camera, marked as a promotional item Pin-o-Matic Camera. Exactly what site this feature will post to is still unclear though, simply labeled "Site" without further elaboration. There does not appear to be a string related to selecting a site either, which suggests there will only be one option available, though it is also possible that Blizzard intends to launch their own screenshots gallery for players to upload to. That seems less likely, and the most obvious answer is that integration will be returning to X formerly known as Twitter, but that remains to be seen.

title: Housing Experience from Endeavor Tasks Capped
author: POSTED 2026/01/23 AT 7:00 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
With the release of the Midnight pre-patch, the maximum attainable housing level has increased, awarding players further perks such as an increased their decor limit and unlocking new room types. With this update, however, power players have realized that completing tasks within the newly unlocked Endeavor system appears to be capped at an extra 1,000 experience per Endeavor.


Not to be confused with traditional player experience, the amount of House XP required also increases per level.


Up to this point, unlocking uncommon and rare decor was the primary way of gaining housing experience, which Endeavor tasks now supplement. With each housing level requiring several thousand experience though, earning decor will continue to be the primary method of earning experience, while the extra 1,000 from tasks doesn't even account for an extra level per Endeavor. Intended to rotate each month, this first Endeavor is extended to run throughout February as well, due to the pre-patch launching in the middle of the month.

Learn more about Housing XP and Endeavors in this video by SoulSoBreezy.


Without revealing the precise value, Principal Designer Jesse Kurlancheek confirmed that experience is capped per Endeavor, but noted that the team was keeping an eye on feedback.
