title: A Terrible Workaround Solution for Tracking Buffs on Raid Frames in Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 2026/01/23 AT 12:31 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Healers and raid frames have been a constant source of feedback in the Midnight Pre-Patch, and plans have been made for a way to track buffs on raid frames in a terrible workaround solution.

We would advise against doing this without explicit knowledge of the systems involved. This video is a proof of concept and will likely be made into a full addon if nothing changes.


This proof of concept was made by Harreks, our Restoration Shaman Writer and Voulk, our Restoration Druid and Preservation Evoker writer.
Table of Contents
What's the Problem
What's the Solution (Base UI)
The Terrible Workaround
Raid Frame Example 1
Raid Frame Example 2
What's the Problem
One of the biggest problems for healers and raid frames in the Midnight Pre-Patch is the lack of buff filtering -- the lack of a whitelist or blacklist. As a most basic case, friendly buffs (for example: HoTs) are usually cast on your party members and tracking them is very important so you know when to reapply them. When these buffs are in a sea of other buffs, and with no way to filter them, this can make it very difficult for healers to track important buffs and know when to reapply or consume them. For Preservation Evokers, knowing who has your Echo is important so you know where your healing spells are going to duplicate to.


Find the Echo symbol


The lack of buff filtering makes important buffs hard to see, as they are shown in the exact same fashion as buffs that you don't want to see. Not all buffs are equal priority for healers, and there are always some spells that are more important than the others. Does a Preservation Evoker need to see who has the Temporal Anomaly shield?
What's the Solution (Base UI)
There is no solution in the base UI.

The current raid frames (and enemy debuffs but that's a different story) have absolutely no filtering whatsoever meaning a lot of players will have to just deal with this. This is a massive change from addons where people could customize the exact buffs and debuffs they wanted to see, and nothing else.
The Terrible Workaround
Harreks and Voulk have found a "terrible solution" to the problem, and it involves destroying the base UI to make this work and making it look very awful. Every person will need to decide whether or not this tradeoff is worth it.

The main tradeoffs are:
A way to track buff and filter buffs using colors.
However, it removes the icons of every ability that applies a buff onto your raid frames that you need to filter out.
We advise people not to do this without explicit knowledge of the systems involved as there may not be a way to undo this easily.


For this to work, Harreks and Voulk have found that you need to remove and replace all the spell icons of abilities that apply a buff to a transparent icon that you don't want to see. This will make it so that these icons will just be hidden on your raid frames, as a soft filtering option. This will make the icons on your bars and the Cooldown Manager completely transparent as well -- a pretty nasty tradeoff. You can then apply a colored texture that you want to the spell(s) that you want to track to make it easier to see. This colored texture can take up the entire health bar, or a small colored square as well. You can see some of these examples in Harrek's video below, but he's also provided some more examples below.



Harreks is looking to turn this into an addon in the future if no functionality is changed. It doesn't look great, but if healers want buff tracking, this is one way that currently works.
Raid Frame Example 1
In this example, Dream Breath is a Green Square and Echo is a Red Square.

Raid Frame Example 2
In this example, Echo turns the entire health bar orange when they have it as it is a very important buff to track for healers.

title: Keep Clear Names While Hiding Friendly Nameplates in Midnight
author: POSTED 2026/01/23 AT 9:00 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
A newly added variable on the Midnight Beta allows players to hide the bulky bars that come with friendly player nameplates, while keeping the names clearly visible.

/run C_CVar.SetCVar("nameplateShowOnlyNameForFriendlyPlayerUnits", 1)
Simply enter the command into the chat window to remove the bulky colored nameplate frame from friendly players, or change the 1 to 0 to restore it. Keep in mind that nameplate addons such as Plater and Playnator may impede these settings with their own functionality!
NO NAMEPLATES
With plates disabled, names are shown in a faint blue, which can be very hard to see.


FULL NAMEPLATES
Enabling nameplates makes names clearly visible, but adds blocky colored bars which can limit visibility.


NAMES, NO PLATES
By using the variable, the colored plates are hidden, while character names remain more clearly visible.


Depending on your role within a group and how your UI is setup, these plates could still be useful, so there are pros and cons to each setting. The full frames make it easier to tell exactly who is standing where and the colored frames help better distinguish players from one another, but they can also get in the way of seeing other things, particularly with larger group sizes.

On the other hand, players who rely more on party frames for healing, aren't capable of healing, or just aren't concerned with the state of their party members, turning the frames off can create a lot more visibility, while still making friendly player names more visible, which can be very helpful for precise positioning.


The setting only affects friendly units, so enemy nameplates may still be shown normally.


Currently, this option is only available through entering the command into your chat box. It is a persistent setting though, so it should remain through reload or logout, rather than having to re-enter it every time.

title: Everything To Know About Midnight's Transmog System
author: POSTED 2026/01/23 AT 7:00 AM BY WHISPYR
content:
Midnight is bringing big changes to the Transmog System, which will impact how players spend gold, collect gear, and interact with cosmetics in general. Alongside core feature revamps, the UI has also undergone a significant overhaul. Now that players have had a few days to play with the new system with the Prepatch, we've got everything you need to answer questions you might have about the system.

Everything about Transmog
Everything about Transmog
NEW TRANSMOG UI IN MIDNIGHT
One of the first things players will notice upon logging into 12.0 is the revamped transmogrification user interface. Some aspects remain familiar, such as the list of saved outfits on the left, or the window on the right to select an item. There are, however, some new additions to this window, such as the "Situations" tab, which will be discussed later.


Although the visuals have changed, much of the functionality players have grown used to remains the same.
TRANSMOG OUTFITS IN MIDNIGHT
One of the biggest changes to Transmog comes in the form of Outfits. Instead of Transmog applying to a specific item, changing item appearances affects the item slot itself, regardless of the item equipped. While a gold fee is required to unlock an outfit, swapping between saved Outfits is entirely free once they are set up. There is still a small fee for those looking to change the appearance within an Outfit.

An Important note about Outfits slots is that these are unlocked for your entire Warband, making transmogs across multiple characters much easier. In addition to that, Blizzard announced cost reductions to the unlock costs, with the first 20 outfits costing a combined 162k gold.
TRANSMOG SITUATIONS IN MIDNIGHT
The other major system coming to Transmog in Midnight is Situations. Situations are a new type of setting that can be applied to a saved Outfit, automatically applying that Outfit to your character if the conditions are met.


Conditions that can be used include:
Location (including inside Player Housing)
Movement (such as being mounted)
Specialization
Equipment Set
Racial Form
Professions
Outfits can have multiple conditions at once, but all conditions must be met for the Outfit to be applied. This new feature allows players to swap between their battle-ready transmog set and their lounge set for home. Maybe you would prefer to wear different outfits depending on your spec, even though the same items are equipped. Situations lets you do it all for free!

title: Transmog Costs Will Be Reduced by 50% & One Free Transmog Soon
author: POSTED 2026/01/22 AT 7:17 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced that transmog costs will be reduced by 50% soon, and the one free transmog will be available as soon as possible!
Blizzard
View Original
Hello everyone! We have a few more updates on Transmog we’d like to share with you:

We’re going to lower the cost of outfit creation prices by 50% to bring them more in line with what the prices were prior to the Transmog update. This price reduction is currently under development and should be available within the next day.

Additionally, we’re going to try to grant the one free Transmog to all player-characters as soon as we can, before 12.0.1, hopefully via a hotfix. We’ll update the hotfixes article once the free Transmog is available to use.

One more thing. Many of you have been asking about how the new Transmog system will work with the Trial of Style event. When Trial of Style is available, players receive a special Transmog outfit slot that can be worn both inside and outside the competition. All Transmog updates made to this slot are free. While in the competition, this is the outfit players are judged on. Once the Trial of Style event ends, the outfit slot is removed.

Thank you so much for all your feedback!

title: More Addon Changes to Come - Interview with PC Gamer
author: POSTED 2026/01/22 AT 4:25 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In new interviews with PC Gamer, Senior Game Director Ion Hazzikostas confirmed that there are more addon-on changes to come, continuing to tweak addon API and encounters over time in Midnight.

 Addon Changes Aren't Over - Interview with PC Gamer
 Not Backtracing on Addon Plans - Interview with PC Gamer


Discussing the why and wherefore, the Game Director acknowledges that while addon support has been around since the inception of World of Warcraft, what they're capable of has ballooned well beyond intent or expectations. Hellfire Citadel marked a turning point, with addons trivializing major mechanics checks to dynamically solve problems for players mid-combat.
Ion Hazzikostas, via PC Gamer said:
"Add-ons have been part of the game since literally day one, since beta," he says. "What we started to see 10 years later in Warlords of Draenor was increasingly bespoke add-on solutions that were designed to simplify and solve specific mechanics."

"We've probably let this go farther than we should have," Hazzkostas says. "But we really just boiled down to the question of, do we want this to be what WoW is forever? We probably should have done something sooner, and it would have been a less-jarring transition for the community."
Since then, the computational power of addons has only grown, leading to an arms race as developers made encounters more demanding and players created more complex tools to solve them. Even Blizzard's attempt to block addons from reading so-called "private auras" was met with further creative problem solving, with players using manual inputs to trigger the computation instead, ultimately with the same result of addons handling most major group coordination.
Ion Hazzikostas, via PC Gamer said:
"I see discussion in the community to the effect of, 'Blizzard's backtracking on this. Blizzard's walking back their plans.' This was always the intent: to swing the pendulum to the other end and then creep it back in a measured way," Hazzikostas says. "Our goal was never to stamp out the add-on ecosystem. It was to move away from it being something that feels like a required competitive aspect of the game."
The sweeping addon changes came as a shock to many, but while Blizzard was upfront about what kinds of addons they intended to break, and what they intend to leave available, they've taken a rather circuitous route to get there. Many addons that weren't meant to be impacted were initially blocked, though by this point, most functionality has been restored - the majority of collection, roleplay, and user interface customization should continue to work, as long as they don't interact with combat.

There are a lot more players than developers though, so Blizzard is fully expecting and prepared to deal with loopholes, as players find more creative solutions to work around their new limitations.
Ion Hazzikostas, via PC Gamer said:
"Players are numerous and creative, and there will doubtless be some loopholes, some workarounds, some clever solutions. We'll make adjustments to both encounters and to the add-on API as needed in close to real time to keep things fair and to keep things fun."

If the player workaround feels both required and frustrating, "we're going to take action quickly."
Likewise, Edit Mode and the Cooldown Manager continue to see updates, though they still pale in comparison to what was achievable with addons. With a slew of new addons dedicated to improving those tools, the game director has also pledged to continue iterating on the amount of baseline customization available to players.
Ion Hazzikostas, via PC Gamer said:
"We don't view this as a one-and-done. We have gotten to a baseline experience that we feel good about, but there's always room for improvement."

title: Crest Turn-In Achievements Hotfixed: Gilded of the Ethereal for Everyone!
author: POSTED 2026/01/21 AT 5:52 PM BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Update 7:30pm PST: It seems this is fixed - alts (and mains) that log in and aren't eligible are no longer receiving the Feats of Strength. Those who earned them during the brief window seem to have kept them.


Feeling weak after the item level squish? A recent hotfix to the Crest upgrade achievements has made it so that everyone should be able to get a bit of an item level boost!

When hitting item level breakpoints in TWW, players can earn achievements that allow them to trade excess Crest currencies for higher-tier (or lower, if you were low) versions. Vaskarn in Dornogal sells several conversion bundles such as Glorious Cluster of Gilded Ethereal Crests, but these are only available with the proper achievement to unlock them - and it seems now, virtually every character will receive all four achievements on login.

Gilded of the Ethereal

Obtain an average item level of 160 during The War Within Season 3.

- Obtain an average item level of 160 during The War Within Season 3. (Outgrow Gilded Ethereal Crests)

Reward: Crimson Valorstone


What seems to be happening is that after the item level squish, characters were "watermarked" at their old item levels - meaning well over the 160 item level required instead of 720, listed on the achievement itself. Even alts that never made it to level 80 had a higher ilevel than 160, so logging in will grant the achievement, giving a boost of Gilded Ethereal Crest if you're willing to grind out trade fodder.

 
Left: A level 80 alt who hadn't been played since Season 1. Right: A level 72 alt that never made it out of Dornogal.

title: One Free Transmog for Each Character in Patch 12.0.1
author: POSTED 2026/01/21 AT 5:07 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced that each character will get one free transmog in Patch 12.0.1 and Legion Artifacts will be applied to more weapon types.
Blizzard
View Original
Hello!

As many players noticed, Transmogs that were previously applied to gear were removed in the 12.0.0 update. This was intentional as a part of the transition from a gear-based to a slot-based system.

Although previously saved loadouts were converted to Custom Sets in the new system, it costs the normal amount of gold to apply the first Transmog to your character, even if you’re simply setting your appearance back to the way it was before. This was an oversight on our part; you should have been able to maintain or reset your existing look without cost.

With the 12.0.1 update before the launch of Midnight, all player-characters will be granted one free Transmog. This will be automatically applied to the next outfit created for each character. We apologize for any inconvenience caused by first-time Transmog costs.

We’re also aware that Legion Artifact appearances can currently only be applied to weapons with compatible weapon categories. We’re working to implement the Legion Artifact override options for the updated Transmog system. You can expect to be able to apply Legion Artifact appearances to more weapon types in the 12.0.1 update.

As always, we’re thankful for your feedback!

title: Patch 12.0.1 Class Tuning Development Notes on Midnight Beta - Hybrid Off Healing Increased
author: POSTED 2026/01/21 AT 4:10 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has posted development notes for today which includes class tuning and an increase to Hybrid off healing!
Blizzard
View Original
Hello testers! Here are this week’s changes to the 12.0.1 beta:
CLASSES
Developers’ notes:In Midnight, player health was increased to help address the pace of incoming damage and healing. As a result, hybrid spec healing became far less effective than intended. We are taking a pass at the hybrid specs to increase the effectiveness of their healing spells so they are more useful both in and out of combat.
DEATH KNIGHT
Hero Talents
Rider of the Apocalypse
Frost
Trollbane’s Obliterate now correctly becomes Frost when consuming a Killing Machine with One-Handed weapons.
Unholy
Necromancer’s Cunning explosion damage increased by 100%.
Virulent Plague damage increased by 15%.
Clawing Shadows now reduces the damage of each subsequent chain of Scourge Strike by 20% (was 25%).
Superstrain now causes Dread Plague to deal 75% of its damage to a nearby enemy (was 50%).
DEMON HUNTER
Devourer
Developers’ notes: We’ve been getting feedback that Collapsing Star’s resource consumption happening at the start of its cast is too punishing, especially in uncoordinated groups. We still want proper planning and execution of Collapsing Star to be a meaningful part of maximizing Devourer Demon Hunter’s damage, but we agree that there is too much tension surrounding an ability that should feel exciting and epic. As such, we’re updating Collapsing Star to now only consume its stacks when the cast is completed. We’re also adding an internal cooldown to Collapsing Star to prevent edge-cases where players may want to cast and cancel Collapsing Star repeatedly to extend their Void Metamorphosis duration. This cooldown will trigger when Collapsing Star begins its cast. Our goal with this change is to make Collapsing Star more forgiving when canceled due to forced movement or crowd control, while giving us some room to avoid abuse cases. We’ll be closely watching the impact of this change and make tweaks if necessary.
Collapsing Star has been updated – No longer consumes the required Souls at the start of its cast. Now has a 5 second cooldown and triggers its cooldown at the start of its cast.
Collapsing Star now generates 50% less threat.
DRUID
Hero Talents
Keeper of the Grove
Balance
Fixed an issue that caused Sylvan Beckoning’s Dryad to cast an incorrect, more powerful version of Starfall than intended.
Fixed an issue for Durability of Nature causing the tooltip to display a value of 50% for the amount it increases treant health, when it is actually 100%.
Wildstalker
Bloodseeker Vines grow at an increased rate when multiple targets are afflicted by your bleeds.
Rampancy causes Bursting Growths to have a 30% chance to trigger (was 20%).
Patient Custodian increases bleed damage by 8% (was 6%).
Fixed an issue that caused Patient Custodian to not increase Bloodseeker Vine damage.
Balance
Meteorites damage reduced by 10%.
Power of Goldrinn damage increased by 25%.
Denizen of the Dream damage increased by 40%.
Solar Bolt damage increased by 40%.
Lunar Bolt damage increased by 20%.
Wrath damage increased by 20%.
Starfire damage increased by 20%.
All ability damage increased by 6%.
Wild Growth healing increased by 30%.
Rejuvenation healing increased by 30%.
Feral
Fixed an issue that caused Unseen Attacks to often fail to find a target when not fighting dummies.
Focused Frenzy increases the damage of Feral Frenzy by 20% (was 40%).
Cat form Swipe damage increased by 20%.
All attack damage increased by 8%.
Wild Growth healing increased by 100%.
Rejuvenation healing increased by 100%.
EVOKER
Fire Breath’s total (instant + periodic) damage should now be consistent at all Empower ranks, with all combinations of Fire Breath-affecting talents.
Panacea healing increased by 60%.
Augmentation
Ebon Might can no longer be aura canceled.
Ebon Might extension range now matches Ebon Might’s application range.
Sense Power now dynamically updates its duration if a cooldown is extended.
Sense Power and Prescience have been updated to correctly detect all major throughput cooldowns, and will no longer activate on tank or healer specializations.
Verdant Embrace healing increased by 50%.
Emerald Blossom healing increased by 50%.
Devastation
Verdant Embrace healing increased by 50%.
Emerald Blossom healing increased by 50%.
HUNTER
Beast Mastery
Fixed an issue that caused the Bestial Wrath granted to your Nature’s Ally Animal Companion to trigger Bestial Wrath effects and procs an additional time.
MAGE
Frost
Icicles visual effects now automatically hide and reappear based on whether you’re in combat or not.
Fixed an issue that caused all Water Elemental glyphs to not be properly applied to the Water Elemental spell.
MONK
Windwalker
Developers’ notes: This round of updates is targeting a few underperforming and overperforming talents, increasing Windwalker’s AoE damage, tweaking Rising Sun Kick’s damage contribution in single target versus Fists of Fury, and adjusting its chi economy in a few ways to be a smoother experience.
Fists of Fury damage decreased by 10%.
Rising Sun Kick damage increased by 20%.
Spinning Crane Kick damage increased by 50%.
Jadefire Stomp damage increased by 25%.
Flurry of Xuen damage increased by 40%.
Airborne Rhythm now generates 1 Chi (was 2 Chi).
Rushing Wind Kick no longer has a Chi cost.
Sunfire Spiral now increases Mastery: Combo Strikes by 40% (was 20%).
Universal Energy increases magical damage done by 8% (was 15%).
Memory of the Monastery now increases Combo Breaker’s effect chance by 75% (was 25%) and increases Tiger Palm’s damage by 30% (was 15%).
Communion with Wind increases the damage of Strike of the Windlord and Whirling Dragon Punch by 20% (was 10%).
Tigereye Brew has been updated – Now only reduces its stacks down to 10 while out of combat in a raid instance and now grants 10 stacks or reduces stacks down to 10 at encounter start and mythic+ start.
Vivify Healing increased by 100%.
Vivify Healing is reduced by 33% in PvP combat (was 66%).
Expel Harm healing increased by 30%.
PALADIN
Holy
Light of Dawn’s healing effect is now capped at 5 targets. Healing increased by 10%.
Liberation now causes 12% of Word of Glory and Light of Dawn’s healing to be transferred into damage.
Beacon of the Savior now functions with Commanding Light, Beacon of the Lightbringer, and Moment of Compassion.
Hammer of Wrath now costs the same amount of mana as Judgment.
Hammer and Anvil healing now increased by Mastery: Lightbringer. Healing reduced by 30%.
Holy Light healing increased by 25%.
Divine Resonance added to the Cooldown Manager.
Divine Resonance now correctly activates from Holy Bulwark and Sacred Weapon.
Retribution
Flash of Light healing increased by 85%.
Word of Glory healing increased by 100%.
Haste now correctly reduces the global cooldown after a Templar Strike or Templar Slash.
Fixed an issue that caused Empyrean Power to trigger more than intended in AoE scenarios.
Fixed an issue that caused Quickened Invocation to reduce the cooldown of Divine Toll by 15 seconds instead of 30 seconds.
PRIEST
Discipline
Atonement heals for 45% of damage done (was 50%).
Searing Light applies a damage over time equal to 20% of Penance damage (was 30%).
Shadowfiend chance increased to 100% (was 40%).
Twinsight Penance no longer applies Atonement to allies.
Holy Ray can no longer be chained between multiple Penance casts.
Shadow Mend healing increased by 45%.
Borrowed Time and Harsh Discipline have swapped locations.
Borrowed Time grants 5%/10% Haste (was 4%/8%).
Developers’ notes: Please note that this change will be applied as a hotfix, so it might not be immediately data-minable.
Shadow
Flash Heal healing increased by 70%.
Power Word: Shield absorption increased by 50%.
ROGUE
Hero Talents
Fatebound
Fixed an issue that caused Ravenholdt Mint’s benefit to not be applied or even reduce overall damage.
Assassination
Fate Intertwined increases the percentage of damage spread by Caustic Spatter by 5% (was 10%).
Fatebound Coin damage increased by 10%.
Outlaw
Fate Intertwined’s increase to the percentage of damage caused by Blade Flurry reduced to 6% (was 8%).
Assassination
All damage increased by 10%.
Caustic Spatter causes 15% of your Nature damage to hit nearby enemies (was 35%).
Outlaw
All damage increased by 15%.
Blade Flurry causes your abilities to strike nearby enemies for 25% of damage dealt (was 35%).
Menacing Rush increases Adrenaline Rush’s damage bonus to affected abilities by 20% (was 15%).
Heightened Rush increases Adrenaline Rush’s duration by 4 seconds (was 3 seconds).
Midnight Season 1 2-Set Bonus – Updated the tooltip to more accurately reflect the increase to Blade Rush’s damage to your primary target. The displayed number has changed to 15% (was 25%), though the power of the set has not changed.
Fixed an issue in Precision Shots’ tooltip. The correct damage percent increase of 20% is now shown, instead of 10%.
SHAMAN
Elemental
Heailng Surge healing increased by 130%.
Healing Stream Totem healing increased by 50%.
Earth Shield healing increased by 50%.
Enhancement
Healing Surge healing increased by 130%.
Healing Stream Totem healing increased by 50%.
Earth Shield healing increased by 50%.
WARLOCK
Affliction
Haunt duration increased to 18 seconds (was 15 seconds).
Only 1 application of Haunt can be active at a time.
Demonology
Fixed an issue where Grand Warlock Alythess and Lady Sacrolash would not continually attack enemies.
Destruction
Apex Talent: Embers of Nihilam (Rank 4) has been updated – Casting Chaos Bolt, Shadowburn, or Rain of Fire evokes an echo of the Dark Titan’s at 50%, 50%, or 60% effectiveness respectively. If no target is found, a random target suffering from your [Wither/Immolate] will be selected.
WARRIOR
Hero Talents
Colossus
Colossal Might increases damage of your next Demolish by 5% per stack (was 10%).
Demolish damage increased by 33%.
Developers’ notes: We want Demolish to feel satisfying to use even at low or no Colossal Might stacks, so we’re moving some of the Colossal Might damage into the ability’s baseline damage.
Arms
Apex Talent: Master of Warfare – Chance for Slam to be upgraded to Heroic Strike increased by roughly 50%.
Apex Talent: Master of Warfare – Heroic Strike reduces the cooldown of Colossus Smash by 2 seconds (was 5 seconds).
Master Tactician now increases Tactician’s chance to trigger by 5% (was 10%).
Battlelord now also reduces the Rage cost of your next Heroic Strike by 33%.
Fury
Midnight Season 1 4-set Bonus – Rampage reduces the cooldown of Odyn’s Fury by 2.5 seconds (was 1 second).

title: Midnight Beta Season 1 Mythic+ Dungeon Tuning for January 21
author: POSTED 2026/01/21 AT 12:22 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has posted Midnight Season 1 Mythic+ dungeon tuning for today!
Blizzard
View Original
Greetings!

Midnight Season 1 dungeon testing continues on Wednesday, January 21 at 3:00 p.m. PST (6:00 p.m. EDT / 12:00 a.m. CEST) and runs until Monday, January 27 at 10:00 a.m. PST (1:00 p.m. EDT / 7:00 p.m. CEST). Please note that this schedule may be adjusted if we encounter any technical issues during testing.

During the test period you’ll be able to acquire and customize Mythic Keystones by talking to the Keystone Vendor in Stormwind, Orgrimmar and Silvermoon. The nearby Dungeon Teleports NPC will assist you with transportation. Throughout beta, all Midnight dungeons will be available for testing on Normal and Heroic difficulty. Seasonal dungeons will rotate with each new test period.

For this round, the following seasonal dungeons will be open for testing on Heroic, Mythic and Mythic+ difficulty:
Magisters’ Terrace
Maisara Caverns
Nexus-Point Xenas
Windrunner Spire
Algeth’ar Academy
Seat of the Triumvirate
Pit of Saron
Skyreach
Provided that the items equipped on your character are at least item level 226, they will scale automatically to a higher level as needed to support Dungeon testing.

Dungeon Updates

Magisters’ Terrace
Devouring Tyrant
Devouring Strike
Heal absorb reduced by 16%
Degentrius
Unstable Void Essence
Travel time increased from 3 sec to 5 sec
Initial damage from soaking an essence reduced by 16%
Periodic damage reduced by 20%
Stygian Ichor
Max radius reduced by 22%
Maisara Caverns
Vordaza
Lingering Dread
Initial damage reduced by 10%
Unstable Phantom
Movement speed increased by 25%
Windrunner Spire
Restless Heart
Tempest Slash vulnerability increased by 25%
Storming Soulfont
Size increased by 10%
Damage increased by 33%
Commander Kroluk
Fixed an issue causing the Phantasmal Mystic and the Spectral Axethrower to appear at the same location
Apex Lynx
Ferocious Pounce
Increased damage by 15%
Seat of the Triumvirate
Zuraal the Ascended
Coalesced Void
Movement speed reduced by 12.5%
Pit of Saron
Location of the first captive camp has been adjusted
Garfrost
There are 2 additional seconds between Orebreaker and Glacial Overload
Cryostomp
Damage reduced by 18%
Glacial Overload
Frost damage vulnerability reduced to 50% per stack from 75% per stack
Duration of vulnerability increased to 14 seconds from 10 sec
Plungetalon Gargoyle
Plungegrip
Fixed an issue where the channel wasn’t displaying target of target
Fixed an issue that was removing and preventing shapeshifts during the channel
Fixed an issue that could prevent the ability from functioning if the creature was crowd-controlled prior to the cast
Rotting Ghoul
Fixed an issue that could prevent threat generation upon emergence from the ground
Thanks for testing and sharing your thoughts with us! We’ll be reviewing your feedback closely and will continue to post updates throughout the beta as changes roll out.

title: Realm Maintenance in 45 Minutes - Old Lockouts (Made Before Pre-Patch) Will Be Removed
author: POSTED 2026/01/21 AT 12:15 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced realm maintenance in 45 minutes which will wipe old lockouts that were made before the Midnight Pre-Patch.
Blizzard
View Original
We’ve been investigating an issue where some raid instance lockouts are inaccessible, and it’s a difficult issue to solve, but we’ve got a fixup ready to deploy and we have to take realms offline briefly to fix it.

This will wipe players’ older lockouts (any lockouts created before about 17 hours ago).

Thank you for your patience and understanding!

title: Artifact Weapons No Longer Transmoggable for Unusable Weapons in Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 2026/01/21 AT 8:31 AM BY SQUISHEI
content:
With the Midnight Pre-Patch, artifact weapons are no longer transmoggable in the new Transmog UI if the weapon is not part of the normal weapons they can use.

Everything about Transmog
Everything about Transmog


This original article originally mentioned that this was for all artifact weapons and this was incorrect. We apologize for the mistake.
With the Midnight Pre-Patch, all transmogs have been reset as part of an update to the new transmog system. Players have noticed that Artifact weapons are no longer able to be selected in the weapon dropdown menu, but are able to be transmogged if they match a weapon type that the character can use, even if they were part of a previous custom set. The problem is for weapons that are not part of a weapon type that players can use such as Claws of Ursoc for Druids as they are fist weapons.

It's likely that this is a bug, and for the time being you will have to use a different transmog on your weapon.

title: Blizzard's Issues with the Midnight Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 2026/01/20 AT 7:54 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced some issues with the Midnight Pre-Patch including Charter Neighborhoods and Endeavors and more.
Blizzard
View Original
Hello!

It’s been a long day of testing and fixing and we’re finally going live with the 12.0.0 Midnight Pre-Patch.

During our final internal testing, we discovered an issue that could frequently cause saved transmog loadouts to be cleared, resulting in the loss of saved outfits and the gold spent on them. This issue resulted from a quirk of our live database configuration and never appeared in Beta, PTR, or other test environments, but it was an example of an issue that we have to keep realms offline for, and investigate and fix, before we could open the realms to the public.

We have a couple of other issues that we’re going live with, and working to fix as soon as possible:
Charter Neighborhoods weren’t able to start Endeavors properly, so Charter Neighborhoods will remain disabled until we can resolve this. Endeavors should work as expected for other kinds of neighborhoods.
Due to an unforseen issue with the item-level squish, some commodities in the Auction House (e.g. some profession materials) aren’t purchasable. We’ve got the issue identified and a fix on the way in the next day or so.
We thank you for your patience, and are excited to welcome you into the Midnight pre-expansion update!

title: Boss Mod Addons in Midnight - Teaching Old Mods New Tricks
author: POSTED 2026/01/20 AT 4:26 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
With the Midnight Pre-Patch, players will be happy to learn that many of their favorite addons will continue to be available, including the most popular boss mods!
Still Available No Longer Available
 Big Wigs
 Better Timeline
 Deadly Boss Mods WeakAura Packs
OLD FAVORITES FIND NEW PURCHASE
Although the addon changes have greatly affected the capability of boss mods to show specific information, all of the popular boss mods continue to work in the expansion, with their own layout and customization. Like all addons, they are now unable to parse the combat log, which means they no longer show rename ability casts to common terms like "shockwave" and "soak" or decide which alerts are shown; Blizzard's Boss Timeline now controls all of that.

Despite this limitation, there are still many things boss mods can do that the built-in timeline cannot. In addition to reskinning elements to be more aesthetically pleasing, Boss Mods can they can effectively make their own timers using the reminder system, fully configurable with countdowns and sounds, as well as add custom audio to Blizzard's three main "pools" of minor, medium, and critical alerts, so you can have that familiar airhorn blaring when its time to move. They've also begun adding audio alerts to Private Auras, which now includes practically every encounter debuff in the game.

Mystical showcases the difference between Blizzard's boss timeline and Deadly Boss Mods.


So while their functionality may be much more limited in scope, boss mods are still alive and well in Midnight. However much Blizzard insisted that addons shouldn't provide combat advantages, their main concern is avoiding computational problem solving, but allowing players to see and hear warnings is fair game.
BOSS MOD ADDONS FOR MIDNIGHT
Big Wigs Encounter Tools and Deadly Boss Mods are the two OG boss mods, both with comparable features and a dedicated development team releasing constant updates in a timely manner. Highly recommended, though the choice between them is largely a matter of preference.
Better Timeline is a relative newcomer, created to provide a standalone addon version of the popular RaidAbilityTimeline WeakAura.

title: Custom Sound Options May Be Returning to Addons Due to Private Auras
author: POSTED 2026/01/20 AT 10:30 AM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Private Auras were a problem for raiders in War Within and Dragonflight, but Private Auras might be the saving grace for custom sound functionality after the addon changes in Midnight.
Table of Contents
What are Private Auras?
Private Auras Are So Back
Addon Functionality Workaround
Sounds May Be Returning
What are Private Auras?
Private auras were added in Patch 10.1 and Blizzard's first attempt at dealing with computational addons. Addons were not able to track certain aspects of Private Auras, preventing them from directly making computational assignments. Players quickly found workarounds, including a macro that would inform addons they have the a debuff. Once this happened, Private Auras were just handled in the same way, but were more frustrating due to additional inputs that needed to happen and this was frustrating to many players. Blizzard acknowledged that Private Auras were frustrating and were not improving the player experience and moving into the War Within, they used them in a different way that was relatively non-intrusive.
Private Auras Are So Back
The addon changes in Midnight prevent computational addons from automatically doing assignments, so private auras should theoretically have no reason to exist anymore, especially after Blizzard determined that they were frustrating for players. Despite this, in the Midnight Pre-Patch and Midnight Season 1, there are over 700 new private auras that have been added recently.

There are over 300 new private auras for War Within Season 3 in the Midnight Pre-Patch, with over 400 new private auras for Midnight Season 1.


This includes almost every debuff in the current raid and dungeon pool, and for the upcoming Season 1 raids and dungeons in Midnight. Even if the debuff is a simple DoT, it has been made into a Private Aura.

Why are there so many new Private Auras?
Addon Functionality Workaround
The reason that Private Auras may have been added is for players to be able to work around some of the addon restrictions. Due to the new secret values system, addons and players are not able to add sounds or additional notifications when they get a debuff as addons have no idea what debuffs you have. If you're standing in fire, or receive a targeted DoT effect, you're getting a debuff and this may be hard to see in your debuff list. Many players have asked for a whitelist / blacklist system to be able to filter out debuffs that they don't want to see such as Challenger's Burden and Sated. With a curated list of Private Auras for boss mechanics, this would at minimum filter out non-important debuffs, with all the important debuffs being curated by Blizzard. And that's the important thing to remember if this is intended, Blizzard has to curate the list of Private Auras, and they could just as easily not provide the needed information or Private Auras in a future tier, or even in a hotfix.

With Private Auras, players will be able to create a Private Aura box, allowing them to resize and move where Private Auras show on their screen. This could be really useful for curated boss mechanics.
Sounds May Be Returning
There is one more thing that players can and have done with Private Auras -- adding custom sounds to specific spell IDs. In other words, players should theoretically be able to add specific sounds to specific debuffs.

The word here is theoretically because as of right now the functionality is currently broken, and it's unclear whether or not this workaround is intended or not. On one hand, why else would Blizzard be adding so many Private Auras. On the other hand, this seems to go against some of their addon goals.

If this works how Private Auras used to function, addons could be developed to attached customs sounds to specific Private Auras. This is reliant on Blizzard marking the debuff you're looking for as a Private Aura, of course:
Pop an Air Horn on an important debuff that is super important.
Make a custom announcement reminder people what to do with certain debuffs.
Remind yourself to use a Defensive if you get a deadly DoT effect.
... and so on...
Many addons including Big Wigs, DBM, Northern Sky Raid Tools have or will likely add functionality to support this. This also means that addons such as GTFO could also survive and have a purpose in Midnight.

We'll have to see if this functionality makes it into the Midnight Pre-Patch or Midnight Season 1.

title: How to Play Devourer Demon Hunter in Midnight - Basics and Rotation
author: POSTED 2026/01/20 AT 7:45 AM BY IMX3
content:
The Midnight pre-patch introduces the third Demon Hunter specialization, Devourer. Here's a short introduction to the playstyle and rotation basics for Devourer!

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
 Devourer Demon Hunter Class Guide
Devourer Demon Hunter Class Guide
UNLOCKING DEVOURER DEMON HUNTER IN MIDNIGHT
Luckily, there are no prerequisites to unlocking Devourer Demon Hunter in the Midnight pre-patch. Simply log in, and you're good to go!

Pros of Devourer DH Cons of Devourer DH
 Excellent burst damage in Void Metamorphosis
 High mobility with few stationary casts
 Flexibility between ranged and melee-focused combat
 Engaging and visually-appealing gameplay Extremely dependent on Soul Fragment generation
Limited defensive budget
Difficulties with Void Metamorphosis uptime
High Consume spam throughout rotation
DEVOURER DEMON HUNTER CORE ABILITIES IN MIDNIGHT
Devourer Demon Hunters are a mid-ranged, Intellect-based DPS specialization, with some new abilities, along with void-themed Demon Hunter abilities. Here's a quick look at some of Devourer's abilities:
Reap - A scythe attack that consumes soul fragments.
Voidblade & Hungering Slash - Melee combo with a retreat movement.
Soul Immolation - A void-based Immolation Aura that deals damage to yourself, generating Fury and Soul Fragments.
Consume - Filler cast that deals damage to the target and summons Soul Fragments.
Collapsing Star - A powerful finisher that reduces Fury drain while casting.
Void Ray - A piercing beam of Void energy.
Void Metamorphosis - Void-themed transformation sustained by soul harvesting.

Devourer Demon Hunters have an appropriately void-themed Metamorphosis.
PLAYING DEVOURER DEMON HUNTER IN MIDNIGHT
Devourers use two core resources: Fury and Soul Fragments. The goal of Devourer is to build Fury using Consume, and spend Fury on Void Ray to generate Soul Fragments. Players can grab Soul Fragments by either running over them or being close by and using Reap.

At 50 Soul Fragments, players will be able to cast Void Metamorphosis. In Void Metamorphosis, the goal is to prevent Fury from hitting zero for as long as possible. The reason is that we want to cast as many Collapsing Star casts as possible while in Void Metamorphosis. There are two main ways to slow Fury drain while in Void Metamorphosis:
Cast Collapsing Star
Cast Void Ray
Casting Collapsing Star is only available if the player is in Void Metamorphosis and has gathered 30 Soul Fragments. Because of this, we strongly recommend talenting into Predator's Thirst for additional Soul Fragment generation.

Building and maintaining Fury in Void Metamorphosis is the same as before, by casting Consume and gathering Soul Fragments.

HERO TALENTS FOR DEVOURER DH IN MIDNIGHT
Devourer DHs will have the choice between Void-Scarred (originally Fel-Scarred from TWW) and a new set of Hero Talents, Annihilator.

Annihilator focuses around Voidfall, a stacking buff that rains meteors once at 3 stacks and the player casts Reap. Other passive talents in the Annihilator tree include bonus movement capabilities, buffs to Voidfall damage, and more bonuses to Void Ray and Collapsing Star damage.

Player Housing features various Decor recipes for the many Professions of World of Warcraft. These Decor recipes require Lumber, a new crafting reagent players harvest in the open-world of WoW. Recently, a new currency was added to the Midnight Beta, the Essence of Lumber.

Essence of Lumber
Item Level 1
Binds when picked up
"A mote of magical energies obtained from harvesting lumber. Used to requisition additional materials from your neighborhood lumberjack."

While currently not available for harvesting on the Beta, it looks like players will be able to earn Essences by simply harvesting lumber in the open-world.
REQUEST ADDITIONAL MATERIALS WITH ESSENCES
The neat feature of the Essence of Lumber is that it allows players to request "additional materials from your neighborhood lumberjack". While not directly confirmed as of this moment, there are a few different ways this could work:
Use Essences to request other Profession materials for crafting purposes.
Spend Essences on any Lumber of your choice, saving you the trouble of farming for more.
Possibly new Lumber harvesting upgrades? Stronger tools, faster harvesting, etc.
What we do know is that harvesting Lumber will provide more benefits than just being able to complete crafting recipes, which is a nice bonus. What do you think Essences of Lumber will be used for? Let us know in the comments below!

title: Boss Frames with Debuff Tracking and More: Enhance QoL AddOn

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
 User Interface Hub
User Interface Hub

ENHANCE QOL FEATURES
One of the more noticeable features, Enhance QoL offers simple boss frames equipped with debuff tracking, allowing players to track damage-over-time spells and more, all with the simple click of a button.

Besides Boss Frames, Enhance QoL offers many quality-of-life improvements, including:
Create your own resource bars with many customization options.
Display character item level when browsing bags, bank, inventory, etc.

Action Bar and Frame behavior settings, such as auto-hiding bars in/out of combat, etc.
And so much more!
More information regarding Enhance QoL features can be found on R41z0r's announcement post on Reddit. For those seeking a simple and lightweight QoL AddOn for Midnight, Enhance QoL is the ideal choice.

title: Action Bar Addons in Midnight - Very Few Changes, Most Addons Supported
author: POSTED 2026/01/18 AT 2:00 PM BY ANSHLUN
content:
With the Midnight Pre-Patch very close on the horizon, let's take a look at the impact of the addonpocalypse for addons that change Blizzard's default Action Bars.
Still Available No Longer Available
 Bartender4
 Dominos
 OPie*
 ConsolePort No Major Addon Impacted
LITTLE CHANGES FOR ACTION BAR ADDONS IN MIDNIGHT
Overall, action bar addons haven't been affected much by Midnight. The biggest change right now, according to addon authors, seems to be spell cooldowns. While the restrictions imposed early during Beta have been lifted, the ability to display a spell cooldown in these addons can be tricky and break from time to time.

With that said, Blizzard's default action bars have been in a good state since the latest updates they've received, with eight bars available in the base UI. If you're still looking for action bars for Midnight, the most popular options are as follow:
ACTION BAR ADDONS FOR MIDNIGHT
Below you can see a small summary of the status of each addon in Midnight's PTR realms and their download links.
Bartender4
One of the original Action Bar addons, it will still be supported in Midnight, with active Alpha versions that work on the PTR realms.
Dominos
Together with Bartender, Dominos is one of the original Action Bar addons. The addon will still be supported in Midnight, with its live version already working when used on the PTR realms.
OPie*
Player favorite for creating different Action Bars for portals and teleports, OPie will still be supported in Midnight.
*OPie developer announced that while the addon will be supported in Midnight, they don't plan to rush support by fixing temporary issues on Blizzard's end. Full post on their page.
ConsolePort
Players looking to take advantage of this addon to play the game, with this addon allowing you to keybind many different spells by using popular controller keys not supported in the default UI.

title: New Mapzeroth Addon Helps You Find the Fastest Route to Reach Your Destination!
author: POSTED 2026/01/17 AT 12:00 PM BY POPULARTOPULAR
content:
While many combat addons are being discontinued ahead of the Midnight release, many non-combat addons have continued to pop up. Mapzeroth is a new addon that allows players to find the quickest route to their destination in World of Warcraft - almost like a personal GPS!

Download the Mapzeroth Addon From CurseForge
Download the Mapzeroth Addon From CurseForge


With this addon, players can search for and save specific Destinations, including all Cities, Dungeons, and Raids!

 

After setting the destination, Mapzeroth will give players instructions on the fastest route from their current location. These instructions can be found as part of the addon itself, as well as on the in-game chat window. In addition to directions, Mapzeroth also estimates your arrival time!





Players may find this addon useful when zipping around the world, looking for a specific piece of Transmog or your perfect Housing Decor item. Just plug in your destination, hop on your mount, and the addon does the rest!
tr0tsky said:
Link
Description
🗺️ Mapzeroth - Google Maps for Azeroth
Never waste time figuring out how to get somewhere again.
Mapzeroth is an intelligent travel routing addon that calculates the fastest path from your current location to any destination in World of Warcraft. It considers all available travel methods—portals, teleports, flight paths, hearthstones, class abilities, racial abilities, and manual travel—then tells you exactly what to do, step by step.
✨ Features
Smart Pathfinding - Uses Dijkstra's algorithm to find the optimal route based on travel time
Comprehensive Travel Network - Supports portals, mage teleports, hearthstones, flight paths, boats, zeppelins, the Deeprun Tram, dungeon teleports, and more
Class & Racial Abilities - Automatically detects and uses your Death Gate, Dreamwalk, Teleport: Moonglade, Mole Machine, and other special abilities
Waypoint Integration - Route directly to your active map waypoint with /mz waypoint
Faction-Aware - Only suggests routes available to your faction
Clean Material UI - Modern, polished interface with intuitive navigation
🎮 How to Use
Opening Mapzeroth
Click the minimap button (compass icon)
Type /mz show
Main Commands
/mz waypoint - Find the fastest route to your active map waypoint (outputs to chat)
/mz sethearth - Set your manual hearthstone location (use this command while standing at your inn)
/mz help - Show all available commands
Quick Start (GUI Method)
Open the addon with /mz toggle or click the minimap button
Click "Choose Destination" to pick where you want to go
Click "Navigate" to see your optimal route in the window
undefined
Quick Start (Chat Method)
Set a waypoint on your world map
Type /mz waypoint
Your route will be printed step-by-step in chat
undefined
Hearthstone Setup
For Mapzeroth to route using your hearthstone, you need to manually tell it where you're bound:
Bind your hearthstone at an inn
Stand at that location and type /mz sethearth
Mapzeroth will now include your hearthstone in route calculations
🧭 Travel Methods Supported
Portals - Portal rooms in major cities
Mage Teleports - All mage teleport spells
Class Abilities - Death Gate, Dreamwalk, Zen Pilgrimage, and more
Racial Abilities - Mole Machine (Dark Iron Dwarves)
Hearthstone - Your inn-bound hearthstone and Dalaran/Garrison hearthstones
Toys & Items - Jaina's Locket, Argent Crusader's Tabard, and other teleport items
Engineering Gadgets - Wormhole Generators and engineering teleports
Flight Paths - Automated flight master routing where needed to access isolated zones
Boats & Zeppelins - Cross-continent travel
Deeprun Tram - Stormwind ↔ Ironforge
Dungeon Teleports - M+ and Challenge reward teleports
Manual Travel - Walking/flying when it's faster than alternatives
Important Notes
Travel Network Assumptions: Mapzeroth currently assumes you have access to all major portals and travel networks. Routes may include destinations you haven't unlocked yet, such as:
Opposite-faction BfA zones (Zuldazar for Alliance, Boralus for Horde)
Cataclysm Earthshrine portals (requires zone unlock quests)
Vashj'ir portals (requires questline start)
Character-specific unlock detection is planned for a future update!
Time-Phased Zones: Routing can get a bit screwy in zones with multiple time phases (looking at you, Darkshore and Silithus). The WoW map API doesn't always play nicely with temporal shenanigans, so if a route seems odd in these areas, blame the Bronze Dragonflight. We're working on teaching the pathfinding algorithm to respect the timestream.
☕ Support Development
Mapzeroth is a passion project I built to solve my own travel frustrations in WoW. It's completely free and always will be, but if you find it useful and want to support continued development, you can buy me a coffee on Ko-fi. Every bit helps fund late-night coding sessions and keeps me motivated to add new features!
📝 Feedback & Bug Reports
Found a bug or have a feature request? Please report it on the GitHub issues page or leave a comment. I read everything and actively work on improvements.
Safe travels, and may your hearthstone always be ready when you need it! 🌟
— tr0tsky
The Mapzeroth Team

title: Blizzard's Damage Meter Shortcomings in Midnight Pre-Patch & Addon Alternatives
author: POSTED 2026/01/17 AT 9:00 AM BY JAYDAA
content:
To disarm Addons in Midnight, Blizzard's created several UI elements including a new Damage Meter. Does it stack up to previous options and what Addon alternatives exist?

Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight Pre-Patch Overview
Midnight User Interface Hub
Midnight User Interface Hub

In the latest Midnight Beta build additional functionality was added to the Damage Meter, but it's still unknown if players will see these changes for the Midnight Pre-Patch.
Table of Contents
Damage Meters in WoW
Blizzard's Damage Meter
Addon Alternatives
Details!
DAMAGE METERS IN WOW
With the advent of Midnight, Blizzard seeks to eliminate the competitive advantage provided by addons and offer intended functionality across the spectrum of base UI elements so Addons don't feel mandatory for playing WoW. Damage meters are one example of functionality that has been exclusively available to Addon users.

Specifically, Damage meters are combat tools designed to help players perform quick in-game combat analyses and performance comparisons through a wide array of metrics without the need to open a third-party website. With over hundreds of millions of downloads, popular damage meter Addons like Details, Skada, Recount have cemented themselves as near-required essentials for most players for WoW multi-decade history.
BLIZZARD'S DAMAGE METER
Starting in the Midnight Pre-Patch, Blizzard has not only created their own damage meter as part of the base UI but also restricted the combat log data available for Addons to parse; essentially attempting to provide the level playing field between Addons and the base UI.

 

The first iteration of Blizzard's Damage Meter does manage to replicate a lot of the base features that players are used from modern Addons, but does fall short in a lot of categories:

Included Features Unavailable Features
Access to several throughput metrics (Damage, HPS. etc.)
Segments for individual pulls or overall encounters
Breakdowns of player deaths with damaging events
Customization of the meter's appearance
Missing combat timer
Common metrics unavailable (Overhealing, CC Breaks, Buff Uptime, Resource Generation)
Specific or priority enemy damage taken breakdown
Pet Breakdowns segmented at bottom of breakdowns
Breakdowns frequently don't merge spells
Unable to report metrics in chat
Segments frequently reset on instance change or loading
Limited customization (no font changes, spec icons tied to spell icons, etc.)
Simple click navigation instead of dropdown navigation,
Limited information on death breakdowns; missing defensive, externals, healing events
As you can see above, there's a lot of customization, metrics, and features that at present do not exist for the Blizzard Damage Meter. While it's not game breaking, players who require more thorough breakdowns may need to more heavily rely on Warcraftlogs for additional information.

Blizzard's damage meter will likely continue to be iterated upon with more functionality hopefully added in future updates or restrictions on log data relaxed. In the latest Midnight Beta build additional functionality was added to Blizzard's Damage Meter, so it's definitely something they're continuing to look at and address based on feedback.
ADDON ALTERNATIVES FOR BLIZZARD'S DAMAGE METER
The most popular damage meter addon, Details!, looks to be sticking around in Midnight albeit with a reduced set of features due to Blizzard's new limitations.
DETAILS!
While Details metrics and breakdowns are much more limited due to Blizzard's restrictions, they still offer a suite of customization options in a UI that players will find incredibly familiar. If you're looking for a more advanced damage meter beyond the base UI with easy setup and exceptional customization, you can't really go wrong with Details.

 

Details was made by Terciob (developer of Plater) and can be downloaded through Curseforge.
What other Addons have you found that augment Blizzard's damage meter? What features do you miss the most compared to live and hope get implemented soon?

title: Useful Add-Ons to Customize the Cooldown Manager with in Midnight
author: POSTED 2026/01/16 AT 2:39 PM BY IMX3
content:
With no more Weakauras in Midnight, the Cooldown Manager is an important tool to track your class buffs and spells. Add-Ons can further improve the Cooldown Manager by adding additional features, including custom glow effects, more trackable spell support, and more.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
Table of Contents
Cooldown Manager Basics
Addons for the Cooldown Manager
Arc UI
My Essential Buff Tracker
COOLDOWN MANAGER BASICS
The Cooldown Manager is an in-game combat tool designed to help keep track of your essential cooldowns, relevant combat buffs, and utility, including interrupts, CC, and movement-based abilities. This tool comes equipped with basic customization options and Edit Mode support.

The Cooldown Manager also has Import and Export string support, allowing you to grab your favorite streamer or friend's Cooldown Manager options and directly copy them over to your profile.

For players who previously used Weakauras to help track their cooldown abilities, you will notice that the Cooldown Manager can accomplish a lot of the same functionality, but a few things are still missing.

Included Features Unavailable Features
 Freely move spells between Essential and Utility sections.
 Basic glow effects for buffed spells.
 Hide inactive combat buffs to prevent UI clutter.
 Edit Mode support for editing spell icon sizes, orientation, etc.
 Display buffs as either a spell icon or a trackable bar. Glow effects are pre-determined and cannot be customized.
No combat-potion or trinket tracking.
The list of abilities to track cannot be changed by the player.
No custom sounds for buffs, when spells become available, etc.
Zero tracking support for external buffs such as Time Spiral or Power Infusion.
ADDONS FOR THE COOLDOWN MANAGER
Here are two of the most popular addons for the Cooldown Manager. These Add-Ons offer a little more customization for features unavailable with the base Cooldown Manager.
ARC UI
Arc UI offers complete customization of the Cooldown Manager, all with relatively easy-to-navigate settings. Arc UI takes the base function of the Cooldown Manager and adds more customization, including:
Custom Glow Colors for Buffs/Active Spells
Extensive options for the Orientation of Bars/Buffs
Desaturation options for when Spells are on cooldown
Bars designed to keep track of DoT spells, spell charges, etc.
And so much more!
Arc UI was made by ArcForge and can be downloaded through CurseForge.

MY ESSENTIAL BUFF TRACKER
If you're looking for a simple and easy way to track your most important buffs and cooldowns, look to MyEssentialBuffTracker! While Add-Ons like Arc UI offer more customization, MyEssentialBuffTracker keeps things simple and easy to understand. Here are some features of this Add-On:
Easily monitor buffs, cooldowns, and charges in a clean way
The ability to edit icon sizes, icons per row, and more with simple ease
Fine-tune the position, size, and visibility of charge/count on spells and cooldown text


MyEssentialBuffTracker was made by Ckraig and can be downloaded through CurseForge.

The first shadows of the Void fall upon Azeroth as the Midnight Pre-Expansion Content Update goes live January 20. Players can explore the new User Interface (UI) updates, experiment with new combat design changes across all classes, walk the path of the Demon Hunter Devourer, and more.

WoW®: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition Pre-Patch Now Live
Prepare to tread once again through the Dark Portal and into the fel-scarred realm of Outland with the WoW®: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition on February 5 at 3:00 pm PST globally, and included with your WoW Subscription or Game Time.

Your journey continues with a level increase to level 70, an increased level cadence, three challenging Raids, two playable races, Guild Bank, and a slew of other features.

Learn more about Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition, including important details about features and character transfers, from our previously published article.
Embrace the Chaos of the Final Weekend of Legion Remix
Log in anytime from Friday, January 16, 8:00 am PST through to the end of Remix to earn the Feat of Strength, Cloudy With a Chance of Infernals.

When the dust settles and you find yourself returned to the main timeline in Azeroth, any Remix-specific items, equipment, and quests will be removed. Link

title: Three New Mounts on the Midnight Beta - Grimlynx, Chloroceros, & Dragonhawk
author: POSTED 2026/01/16 AT 9:00 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Three new mounts have been added on the Midnight Beta, including an umbral black Dragonhawk!
NEW IVORY GRIMLYNX
Alongside the Fierce Grimlynx and Rootstalker Grimlynx variants, this new family of cat-like creatures are described as the ultimate hunters of Harandar and the Rift of Aln. Like the Reins of the Slatestone Ramolith in The War Within, the Ivory Grimlynx is awarded by completing the Allied Race: Haranir achievement, which should make it available to all players, rather than Haranir-specific.
Description: Ivory grimlynxes have a strong independant streak. It takes an especially brave Haronir to ride them.
Source: Achievement: Allied Race: Haranir

NEW VIVACIOUS CHLOROCEROS
The rhinoceros-like chloroceros is another exotic blend of plant and animal seen throughout Harandar, featuring leaves for fur and a mighty horn that screams trouble for anything that gets in its way. Unlock it by discovering all the Treasures of Harandar.
Description: Catching a chloroceros mane leaf mid-air is considered a sign of good luck among the Haranir. The more vivacious the leaf, the greater the luck.
Source: Achievement: Treasures of Harandar

NEW UMBRAL DRAGONHAWK
As befitting the court of Silvermoon, there's a new dragonhawk on the block, featuring a rich midnight color scheme. Unlocking it will require some wining and dining though, so be prepared to become the Life of the Party.
Description: Cloaked in blackened tones, this Dragonhawk glides with calm precision, its somber coloring lending it an air of quiet distinction.
Source: Achievement: Life of the Party

Legion Remix Overview

Legion Remix is set to end at 10 AM PST on January 19th, 2026.

Previously, we had datamined the Cloudy with a Chance of Infernals achievement, hinting at a potential send-off event for Legion Remix. Cloudy With a Chance of Infernals

COLLECTING LAST MINUTE ENSEMBLES AND APPEARANCES
When Legion Remix comes to a close, Timerunner characters will be transferred over to the Live game. With Legion Remix, certain appearances and transmog items will unlock only after the character is moved over. However, equipped items get transferred into boosted gear during this process, and will prevent you from collecting the appearance on Live servers.

To prevent this from happening, unequip any desired gear before transferring. This will ensure you unlock all appearances on Live.

AddOns like All The Things can help keep track of any uncollected Legion Remix transmog.

With the last 48 hours of Legion Remix, you might be asking, "What should I do before the event ends?" Here are a few quick things to do before Legion Remix goes away:
Level any desired characters to 80 with the fast-leveling of Legion Remix.
Purchase any desired mounts, transmog, or housing decor with Bronze.
Grab any achievements that might be easier to earn in Legion Remix, such as Naxt Victim.

title: Improved Death Recap on Midnight Beta
author: POSTED 2026/01/15 AT 7:48 PM BY IMX3
content:
Deaths are now a trackable option in the in-game Damage Meter. Is it a step up in comparison to the live death recap? Let's take a look.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
FEATURES OF THE DAMAGE METER DEATH RECAP
The new death recap in Blizzard's Damage Meter provides a few more metrics that help players get a better understanding of how a death exactly happened.
Damage events showcase the mob that dealt damage, what spell was used, and how much damage the player took.
Mousing over the damage number or the tombstone provides information on when the damage was taken, leading up to the death (i.e., 0.5 sec before death).
Clicking the death on the Damage Meter pulls up the Death Recap window.
Deaths displayed on the Damage Meter provide the exact time at which the death happened.
The Death Recap tracks the last few seconds leading up to the death.
 MISSING FEATURES OF THE DAMAGE METER DEATH RECAP
With the Damage Meter now tracking instance deaths, this allows players and groups to be on the same page about how someone may have died. While a step in the right direction, there are a few missing components that could make these death recaps better.
Death Recaps do not give any information regarding defensive utilities used, healing potions used, etc.
The Deaths window of the Damage Meter removes the general in-combat timer.
Outside of larger display numbers, there's no telling what spells Critically Striked the player.
While hovering over spells provides a timestamp of when the damage was taken, there's no immediate display of it in the Recap.
No option to publicly share a Death Recap in Party chat or Instance chat.
When looking at the Details! AddOn Death Recap, their recap provides all of the features mentioned in the list above. On top of that, the Details! Death Recap tracks a significantly longer period of time leading up to the death. When analyzing how a player died, every second matters, and having longer than a 1.6-second window to determine how a player died will prove much more useful.

title: Nameplates in Midnight: What's Changing and What Add-Ons Can I Use?
author: POSTED 2026/01/15 AT 6:30 PM BY IMX3
content:
With the Midnight Pre-Patch soon, you might be wondering, what's changing with my Nameplates, and what AddOns are still available for me to use? Let's dive into everything you need to know.

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
NAMEPLATES IN MIDNIGHT
From how they look to what information they display, nameplates in Midnight received many significant changes in comparison to their older counterpart. The most immediate change is in how nameplates look, offering a cleaner look with many different styles to choose from.

Nameplates also received many different customization options, allowing players to customize their Nameplates in a way that works for them.



Outside of looks, Nameplates come equipped with a few more features in Midnight, but may not have everything you're looking for.

Nameplate Features Unavailable Features
 6 different styles of Nameplates to choose from.
 Glow effects for important spells and spells cast on you.
 Detailed options for displaying the health of the NPC.
 Aggro display options for all roles.
 New CC field added to see stuns, stops, etc. More restrictive color-coding that requires Addons to solve.
Buff and debuff icon locations cannot be moved.
No font options outside of the default.
Text cannot be moved outside of the Style options.
Nameplates only track certain buffs/debuffs.
NAMEPLATE ADD-ONS IN MIDNIGHT
Listed below are two different Nameplate Add-Ons designed to offer you more customization outside of the base nameplate settings and styles.
PLATER NAMEPLATES
One of the more popular nameplate Add-Ons, Plater, is receiving an update for Midnight. With many Plater profiles already designed through Wago.io, players can easily import their favorite profiles into Plater, saving the time of editing the nameplate themselves!

Keep in mind, not all profiles will work with Midnight, but keep on the look for new profiles developed for the Midnight expansion! Plater Nameplates can be downloaded through CurseForge.
PLATYNATOR
Platynator is a newly developed Add-On featuring very simple customization options built entirely to work with the Midnight Add-On constraints. This Add-On offers a clean, fresh look for the new Nameplates style if players are finding something different than the base UI nameplates.

Platynator can be downloaded through CurseForge.
