title: Patch 12.0.1 Hotfixes on Midnight Beta - Death Knight, Shaman and Priest
author: POSTED 2025/12/12 AT 6:05 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has announced Death Knight, Priest and Shaman hotfixes for today on the Midnight Beta.
Blizzard
View Original
The following Unholy Death Knight changes were hotfixed to the 12.0.1 Beta realms:
DEATH KNIGHT
Unholy
All Lesser Ghouls damage reduced by 20%.
Outnumber Lesser Ghoul Sweeping Claw damage reduced by 30%.
Pet Ghoul Sweeping Claws damage reduced by 30%.
Scourge Strike and Vampiric Strike damage reduced by 20%.
Infected Claws damage reduced by 20%.
Graveyard damage reduced by 20%.
Necrotic Coil damage reduced by 20%.
Morbidity now increases the damage the target takes per disease by 1% (was 2%).
Dread Plague and Virulent Plague damage reduced by 15%.
All damage reduced by 15%.
Forbidden Knowledge Rank 4 now has a 25% chance to cause a Lesser Ghoul to Putrefy (was 15%).
Blizzard
View Original
The following Priest changes were hotfixed to the 12.0.1 Beta realms:
PRIEST
Holy
Developers’ notes: Holy Priest has fallen behind other healers in output. We are increasing some controlled sources of healing with this change and including some reductions in power to Cosmic Ripple as well as reductions to the power of a couple Archon talents to better maintain Hero Talent balance and keep the healing experience consistent with more healing from directly casted spells.
Flash Heal healing increased by 12%.
Prayer of Healing healing increased by 12%.
Holy Word: Serenity healing increased by 12%.
Holy Word: Sanctify healing increased by 12%.
Renew healing increased by 12%.
The second and third rank of Benediction now increases Cosmic Ripple healing by 15%/30% (was 25%/50) and increases all healing done by 6%/12% (was 5%/10%).
Archon: Empowered Surges increases the healing of spells affected by Surge of Light by 5% (was 15%).
Archon: Halo healing reduced by 20%.
Shadow
Developers’ notes: Shadow has been overperforming relative to other specializations, particular as Voidweaver. We’re reducing the power of some of its talents to bring its power level more in line with other specializations and the Archon Hero Talent tree.
Fixed an issue causing Void Torrent to have a 45 second cooldown instead of the intended 30 second cooldown.
Voidweaver: Voidheart now increases Shadow damage by 5% (was 10%).
Voidweaver: Overwhelming Shadows now increases Void Torrent damage by 30% (was 50%).
Voidweaver: Inner Quietus now increases the damage of Vampiric Touch and Shadow Word: Pain by 20% (was 35%).
Blizzard
View Original
The following Restoration Shaman changes were hotfixed to the 12.0.1 Beta realms:
SHAMAN
Restoration
Developers’ notes: We’re pulling back on the power of some healing effects that are harder to control, particularly Stormstream Totem, and increasing the healing of controlled sources with the goal of a more consistent healing experience. We also made a recent change to allow Stormstream Totem to also be usable after casting Nature’s Swiftness which works in tandem with these goals.
Healing Rain healing increased by 30%.
Chain Heal healing increased by 10%.
Healing Wave healing increased by 10%.
Riptide healing increased by 10%.
Stormstream Totem now causes Riptide to have a 6% chance to upgrade Healing Stream Totem to Stormstream Totem (was 10%).
Stormstream Totem now heals for 10% more than Healing Stream Totem (was 30%).
Totemic: Primal Catalyst chance for Earthliving to trigger is now 8% (was 20%).

title: No Curio Omni-Token Drops Until March on Quel'Danas Raid in Midnight
author: POSTED 2025/12/12 AT 3:14 PM BY IMX3
content:
Players will not be able to earn Curio Omni-Tokens for their Tier Sets until the release of the March of Quel'Danas raid in Midnight.

Midnight S1 Raid Release Schedule
Midnight S1 Raid Release Schedule
NO CURIO DROPS IN THE VOIDSPIRE RAID
When looking at the dungeon journals for the raids releasing with Season 1 of Midnight, the Voidspire raid's final boss does not drop a Curio token. Typically, the last boss will drop an Omni-Token Curio for players to use towards completing their Tier Set bonuses.

Instead, a Curio token will drop from the last boss of the March on Quel'Danas raid, Midnight Falls.



The Voidspire raid will release March 17th, 2025 with the start of Midnight Season 1. The March on Quel'Danas raid releases two weeks later on March 31st, 2025. In this two week period, players will need to look to defeat bosses in the Voidspire and Dreamrift raids in order to earn their Tier Sets.

title: More Bronze and Faster Legion Assaults in Legion Remix
author: POSTED 2025/12/12 AT 1:46 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In a new bluepost, Blizzard has announced that Legion Remix players will immediately begin earning more bronze from a variety of sources and Legion Assaults will cycle significantly faster!
View Original
Q: More Bronze?
A: Yes, let’s have more Bronze!

With hotfixes that are now live on all realms, we’ve cranked up the Bronze for the last few weeks of patch 11.2.7. We’re putting extra Bronze all over the Broken Isles:
Raid bosses on all difficulties now drop a full Minor Bronze Cache (contains 2500 Bronze) in place of their previous Bronze offerings.
Lost Legion Infernals now drop a Greater Bronze Cache (12500 Bronze) once per day, per account.
Infinite Research quests now also grant a Lesser Bronze Cache (4000 Bronze).
Legion Assaults now grant a Bronze Cache (7500 Bronze).
Emissary Quests now grant a Greater Bronze Cache (+8500 Bronze) (was a Lesser Bronze Cache).
World Bosses now drop a Lesser Bronze Cache (4000 Bronze).
Additionally, we took the opportunity to speed up the recurrence of Legion Assaults:
Legion Assaults now cycle approximately 375% faster.
Bronze, Bronze, Bronze 'til the breaks of the dawns.

Please say hi to Pythagorus!

title: WoW Weekly: Throne of Thunder for Mists of Pandaria Classic & Infinite Echoes Update for Legion Remix
author: POSTED 2025/12/12 AT 12:02 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
This week in WoW, Mists of Pandaria Classic received the Thunder King content update complete with the Throne of Thunder raid. The Infinite Echoes update came to Legion Remix offering faster leveling and increased item level.

Throne of Thunder Raid Overview
Throne of Thunder Raid Overview
Legion Remix Overview
Legion Remix Overview
Blizzard
View Original
The time has come to spearhead the assault on the Thunder King’s stronghold in the Thunder King content update for Mists of Pandaria Classic, bring a stop to Lei Shen and his Zandalari allies in the Throne of Thunder raid, and claim your legacy in Legion Remix Infinite Echoes!
Mists of Pandaria Classic: The Thunder King Content Update is Now Live!
Gather heroes, sound the drums, the Thunder King comes!

The Thunder King content update delivers two new zones—Isle of Thunder and Isle of Giants, a new gear upgrade system—Thunderforging, a new legendary chapter, two new world bosses—Oondasta and Nalak, and the Throne of Thunder raid.

In addition, this content update ushers in the continuation of the Legendary questline—continue your journey with Wrathion to earn a Legendary meta gem—introduces new Scenarios, an update to the Challenge Mode system, and includes the latest updates to Celestial Dungeons.

Head over to our previously published article to learn everything you need to know about Mists of Pandaria Classic: The Thunder King.
Take on Lei Shen: The Throne of Thunder Raid is Now Live!
Emperor Lei Shen, the Thunder King, has returned to wreak his vengeance on Pandaria. It falls to the heroes of the Alliance and the Horde to stop the newly resurrected tyrant and his Zandalari allies in the Throne of Thunder. Join the Shado-Pan Assault in their singular drive to see Lei Shen, the Thunder King, defeated once and for all, and gain access to impressive rewards.

The Throne of Thunder is a sprawling citadel housing 12 raid encounters, and players who defeat Emperor Lei Shen in Heroic mode can earn the opportunity to face an added 13th foe, Ra-den.

We look forward to seeing you delve into the Thunder King’s stronghold to take on it’s fearsome denizens, and Lei Shen himself—be sure to read through our raid overview to ensure you're prepared.
Legion Remix: Claim Your Legacy in Infinite Echoes
Glide into fast frenetic fun in Legion Remix as you meet up with the infinite dragonflight once more, level new characters, and earn a variety of items to add to your collection. As the Legion Remix timeline begins collapsing, say your goodbyes and embrace the chaos.

The infinite hourglass turns once more! As the echoes of the Legion reverberate through the shifting sands of destiny, the world trembles with anticipation. It is time to step boldly into Legion Remix: Infinite Echoes, where every heartbeat is charged with new possibilities and the timelines themselves bend to your will.

Legion Remix is available to everyone, and no expansion purchase is needed, but a World of Warcraft Subscription or Game Time is required to play this fast-paced adventure through the Broken Isles. This means that Classic players can also participate in the Fel frenzy by simply installing the modern (live) World of Warcraft client.

Head over to our previously published article to learn more about what you can expect in Infinite Echoes.
Let us adventure alongside you, regaling you with tales of valor, casting a light on epic loot, and directing your eyes to legendary raids. Return each week to the World of Warcraft news site, where together, we’ll forge your destinies in Azeroth and beyond. Lok'tar Ogar!

title: Are New Race Options for Paladins Coming in Midnight?
author: POSTED 2025/12/12 AT 10:15 AM BY PORTERGAUGE
content:
Midnight sees the return of existing Night Elf, Void Elf, and Kul Tiran paladins -- but will players be able to play these combos soon?

WARNING: Midnight spoilers in this post!
Table of Contents
Returning Paladin Characters
Paladin Chargers for Void Elves and Kul Tirans
Other Unavailable Paladin Races
RETURNING PALADIN CHARACTERS
At the start of Midnight's story, we join paladins and priests from across Azeroth as they defend the Sunwell against Xal'atath and her Void army. This group has many of our usual suspects, with priest and paladin trainers from the capitol cities joined by notable major characters like Lady Liadrin, Turalyon, and Anduin Wrynn. Amidst the army of Light wielders, however, there are also a few very interesting faces returning to the spotlight -- paladins from races who do not have them as a player option.

Most notable of these is Delas Moonfang, a Priestess of the Moon who joined the Order of the Silver Hand to become a Night Elf Paladin during their order hall campaign in Legion. We also journeyed with her to meet her ancestral ghosts, who were also presented as Night Elf paladins. In Midnight, she shows up with a totally new armor set befitting her night elf culture, but also making her quite distinct from the typical ranged priestesses we see.
Long have I followed the path of the Light, and for many years I studied under the Priestesses of the Moon. Yet, I have always felt my education incomplete, and a yearning to know more.

When I walked into these halls, I finally felt a spark of what was missing. I believe that a new path lies before me.

I wish to become a paladin of the Silver Hand. Will you allow me to join you?


Also returning is Hadwin, a Kul Tiran paladin that we met during Dragonflight. Originally born in Drustvar, Hadwin felt a calling to the Light that led him to join the Silver Hand, and eventually become a member of the reformed Tyr's Guard. Despite coming from a culture like Kul Tiras with a religious background worshipping their mysterious Tidemother, Hadwin wields the Light in a more traditional sense during our quests with him, making him very distinctly a Paladin.
My dad wanted me to join the navy, and my mum hoped I'd be a tidepriest. But I'm cracking good at swinging a blunt object, if I do say so myself, and got on with the Order of Embers when Lady Waycrest asked for volunteers to fight witches in Drustvar.

But strange as it seems, I started to feel a connection to the Light working as an Inquisitor. I can't explain it. I worship the Tidemother! It caused me to seek out the Silver Hand and train as a paladin. But even then I wasn't satisfied. I came to the Dragon Isles to forge my own path and follow my own code, righting wrongs wherever I found them.

Travard and Koranos found me, and the rest is history.


Less notable in main story quests than the other two, Aeonara Dawnshade was introduced in the prologue patch for The War Within. During our brief visit to Telogrus, we meet a number of new void elf characters, including the iconic Lady Darkglen and a few former blood elves from TBC Silvermoon. Among those new faces is Aeonara, sporting the title "Wielder of the Eclipse" and later seen with paladin abilities like Consecration when Telogrus comes under attack by the Shadowguard.
Some look out into the night sky and see only darkness. But that is the only time the stars can shine.

So too is it for the ren'dorei. Our greatness comes from the fact that even within the darkest of shadows, we can still find ourselves.
PALADIN CHARGERS FOR VOID ELVES AND KUL TIRANS
The mere presence of these NPCs likely would not be enough for speculation. After all, in an army of paladins from all previous expansions, it makes sense for even lesser known oddities like these three to show up.

In addition to these cameos, we also have some very conspicuous new spells: Chargers themed after Void Elves and Kul Tirans.

Starcursed Voidstrider
Instant
Requires Paladin
Leap atop your Charger for 3 sec, increasing movement speed by 100%. Usable while indoors or in combat.
 Waycrest Gryphon
Instant
Requires Paladin
Leap atop your Charger for 3 sec, increasing movement speed by 100%. Usable while indoors or in combat.


These spells are classified as Paladin spells, and contain the modifications for their various talent options like Cavalier and Divine Spurs. They do use existing mount models, however, and are very clearly coordinated to Aeonara and Hadwin's respective backstories.

These spells might just be for NPCs, but the amount of integration they have into the Paladin talent tree is certainly interesting. It wouldn't be the first time we've seen Blizzard teasing upcoming class features, as seen with Devourer Demon Hunters and Augmentation Evokers.
OTHER UNAVAILABLE PALADIN RACES
These NPCs in Midnight aren't the only time we've seen non-playable paladins either. Dating back even to Naxxramas in vanilla WoW, we have had undead like Zeliek wielding the Light in spite of their affliction. The appearance of undead paladins continued in Cataclysm when many Scarlet Crusaders were raised into undeath, complete with Judgment tier sets.

 

We've also seen these kinds of unique combinations in Hearthstone, where Gnomes and Worgen have paladin characters dating back to 2019. Hearthstone obviously acts as an alternate-universe twist on many canonical WoW storylines, but their inclusions are played very straight in their respective expansions.


Commander Rhyssa and the Lightfang Enforcer cards.

We even saw the addition of a Gnome Paladin hero portrait to Hearthstone's most recent expansion with none other than their leader, Gelbin Mekkatorque. While the premise is about a far-future Gnomeregan embracing the Light under the guidance of Gelbin's mechanical form, he is also shown alongside an army of mechanized gnome paladins.



While they don't have explicit instances of Paladin characters, we've seen groups like the trolls and orcs gain more affinity for the Light in recent years. In Dragonflight, we saw the orcs gain playable Priests for the first time. Through the connection to the 'elements within' as contrast to their typical Shaman practices, the mag'har seer Nakha was able to call upon the Light to become a Lok'osh: an orc priest. With the combination of the militaristic culture of the Orcs and the tendency for Shamans to engage in melee combat, it would be quite easy to justify an extension of the Lok'osh into player Paladin options.

Likewise, the Zandalari trolls have paladins through the worship of the loa Rezan -- a loa whose essence now resides inside of Vol'jin, former chieftain of the playable Darkspear trolls. When Vol'jin inevitably returns from his slumber in Ardenweald, it seems likely that his status as a loa will bring change to the Darkspear and their customs.
Winter Queen says: Intriguing... a mortal soul entwined with the essence of a loa.
Winter Queen says: I cannot say what fate will befall you, Vol'jin of the Darkspear. But I offer you this chance to one day be reborn anew.
Spirit of Vol'jin says: I be deeply honored, ya majesty. Champion, thank ya for all ya done for me... in life, and in death.
Spirit of Vol'jin says: Looks like ol' Vol'jin be startin' a new hunt... after I be gettin' some rest.
Despite being one of WoW's most iconic classes for over 20 years, Paladin continues to have some of the most restricted race options. Midnight is shaping up to be the ultimate spotlight for Paladin lore, and there is perhaps no better time than now to expand its options to the rest of the roster.

Do you think these NPC spells and appearances are a hint at a future patch reveal? What race would you most like to see gain Paladins as a class option? Let us know!

title: Earn World of Warcraft Housing Decor by Pre-Ordering Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred
author: POSTED 2025/12/11 AT 10:51 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
The newest expansion for Diablo 4, Lord of Hatred, has just been announced and its pre-order bonuses are three pieces of World of Warcraft housing decor!

 Pre-Order Diablo 4 on Battle.net
 Pre-Order Diablo 4 on Steam



Any edition of Lord of Hatred will unlock these pieces of decor, and although the expansion does not release until April 28th, the three pieces of decor are marked as instant unlocks. Unfortunately, pre-orders through Battle.net have been a bit buggy since their announcement, so it may take some time for the order to go through, but your new decorations should be available as soon as it does.


Decide the fate of Sanctuary over a nice game of chess.


While these items are the only bonus for World of Warcraft, pre-ordering any edition of Lord of Hatred also unlocks immediate early access to the long-awaited Paladin class for use in Season 11!


Higher tier editions also award a wealth of other Diablo 4 goodies.

title: 12.0.1 Midnight Beta Test Development Notes for Pre-Patch
author: POSTED 2025/12/11 AT 7:20 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has released a separate set of Midnight Pre-Patch Test Development Notes for Patch 12.0.1, which will launch with the pre-expansion update.
Blizzard
View Original
The following changes will be released in the 12.0.1 update that launches a few weeks after the Midnight pre-expansion update, but before the launch of Midnight.
CLASSES
DEATH KNIGHT
Hero Talents
Deathbringer
Frost
Deathly Blows now has an additional effect – Reaper’s Mark grants 3 stacks of Bonegrinder if it is known.
Frost
Apex Talents: Chosen of Frostbrood rank 2 and 3 now have an additional effect – Enduring Strength increases your Strength by an additional 4%/8%.
Apex Talents: Chosen of Frostbrood rank 4 now has an additional effect – Additionally increases all Frost damage by 10%.
Breath of Sindragosa and Frostwyrm’s Fury cooldowns are no longer reset after an encounter.
Unholy
Developers’ notes: In this week’s build, we’re looking at a couple of topics that we’ve seen a lot of feedback around. Overall, we’re focusing on the power distribution between abilities and the talents that empower them. Some abilities such as Army of the Dead lacked baseline power and relied heavily on other talents to make them feel impactful. Another example is the Apex Talents. While the first node provided a good amount of power, the rest of the nodes did not add much. With this power redistribution in mind, we are also aiming to resolve some rotational quality of life issues such as the permanent Outbreak override, button bloat during cooldowns, the reduced global cooldown of Apex Talents, and some talents that are feeling like they should be baseline such as Rapid Variant and Necromancer’s Cunning.
New Talent: Scythe of Decay – Festering Scythe casts a Death and Decay at the target location. Can only occur every 30 seconds. Reduced to 15 seconds with Death’s Echo. Replaces Death and Decay.
Superstrain has been redesigned – Dread Plague deals damage to an additional nearby enemy at 50% effectiveness and has a chance to grant you 4 Runic Power.
Reanimation has been updated – Putrefy now summons a Magus of the Dead for 15 seconds (was 8 seconds).
Pestilence has been updated – Dark Transformation upgrades your next Outbreak to Pestilence. The rest of the effect remains the same.
Pestilence now deals 150% of the remaining damage of Plagues instantly (was 100%).
Magus of the Dead has been updated – Army of the Dead now again also summons a Magus of the Dead for 15 seconds.
Army of the Dead has been updated – Summons a legion of 8 empowered Lesser Ghouls that last for 15 seconds. Generates 40 additional Runic Power. Reduced to 4 Lesser Ghouls in PvP combat
Raise Abomination and Gargoyle no longer putrefies 2 Lesser Ghouls.
Forbidden Knowledge rank 1 now lasts for 25 seconds.
Forbidden Knowledge rank 2/3 has been redesigned – Putrefy causes your next Scourge Strike to summon a Lesser Ghoul and grants 3/6% Mastery for 12 seconds. Multiple applications may overlap.
Forbidden Knowledge rank 4 has been redesigned – Each Magus of the Dead under your command increases the damage of Necrotic Coil and Graveyard by 8%. Dread Plague has a 15% chance to rouse the dead, causing a Lesser Ghoul to unearth and leap at the target to Putrefy at 60% effectiveness.
Soul Reaper increases Putrefy damage.
Soul Reaper debuff tooltip updated to show disease damage increase.
Soul Reaper now consumes all available Putrefy charges to cast Putrefy at its target.
Unholy Devotion now lasts for 12 seconds (was 5 seconds).
Rapid Variant is now an inherent effect for Dread Plague and now takes enemy bounding box into account for distance calculations.
Putrefy now summons a Lesser Ghoul inherently. The rest of Necromancer’s Cunning’s effects remain the same.
Dark Transformation now lasts 15 seconds (was 12 seconds) and is extended by Death Coil and Epidemic by 1 second (was 0.5 seconds).
Necrotic Coil and Graveyard now costs 30 Runic Power (was 20).
Necrotic Coil and Graveyard no longer have a reduced global cooldown (was 1 second).
Graveyard damage increased by 40%.
Blightfall now increases all disease damage by 5% (was 10%).
Sudden Doom now reduces the cost of Death Coil and Epidemic by 15 Runic Power (was 10).
DRUID
Restoration
Renewing Surge has been updated – Swiftmend cooldown is reduced by 15%, increasing up to 30% on lower health targets. (Benefit is maximized when healing a target at 50% or below.)
Renewing Surge no longer reduced by 25% in PvP combat.
Fixed an issue where the Abundance tooltip would say Regrowth’s cast time is reduced by 0%.
HUNTER
Beast Mastery
Developers’ notes: Our goal with this iteration of Beast Mastery’s Apex Talent is to add more rotational direction and depth. We’re also having it pull an Animal Companion from your Stables to help the Apex Talent synergize with more builds, playstyles, and player fantasies.
Apex Talents: Nature’s Ally have been redesigned –
Bestial Wrath summons an Animal Companion from your Stable to fight alongside you for 15 seconds. The summoned Animal Companion obeys your Kill Command, Wild Thrash, and Stomps alongside your other pets.
Damage dealt by your pets is increased by 5%/10%. Bestial Wrath strikes 2/4 additional targets.
Barbed Shot and Cobra Shot damage increased by 30%. Barbed Shot, Cobra Shot, and Black Arrow increases the damage of your next Kill Command by 30%.
PALADIN
Hero Talents
Herald of the Sun
Retribution
Developers’ notes: In this week’s updates, we’re focusing on rotational bloat during major cooldowns, tuning and Herald of the Sun updates. For Herald of the Sun we are aiming to make quality of life changes that have tuning implications such as the Sun’s Avatar effect width and the way Dawnlight’s are applied by Divine Storm. For Herald of the Sun’s rotational clarity, we are making Hammer of Wrath clear choice as the Holy Power builder to use during Avenging Wrath. Our ultimate goal is to have Herald of the Sun be a viable option against Templar for those who enjoy its playstyle or are looking for a change of pace.
Walk Into Light now has an additional effect – During Avenging Wrath, Hammer of Wrath consumes an available charge to pierce the enemy with Blade of Justice.
Endless Gleam now has an additional effect – When 2 Dawnlights are struck by Divine Storm, their durations are extended by an additional 0.3 seconds.
Dawnlight radiates 5% of the its damage and healing to nearby allies or enemies.
Dawnlight damage increased by 100%.
Sun’s Avatar now deals reduced damage beyond 8 targets (was 5).
Sun’s Avatar damage spell now takes enemy bounding box into account for distance calculations.
Sun’s Avatar damage spell rectangle is now 5 yards wide (was 3 yards).
Sun’s Avatar period increased to 1 second (was 0.5 seconds).
Sun’s Avatar damage increased by 20%.
Divine Storm will now apply the second Dawnlight to an unaffected enemy hit.
Fixed an issue that caused Blessing of An’she to not activate from Judgment casts.
Fixed an issue that caused Blessing of An’she to not increase the damage of Hammer of Wrath.
Fixed an issue that caused Divine Toll to grant 2 additional charges of Dawnlights.
Templar
An issue causing Divine Toll to grant Hammer of Light has been resolved.
An issue causing Divine Resonance to extend the duration of Divine Hammer with each cast of Judgment has been resolved.
Holy
Fixed an issue that caused Veneration to now grant absorb shields from Hammer of Wrath casts.
Retribution
Midnight 4-set bonus has been updated – Final Verdict now applies Expurgation at 100% effectiveness (was 50%). Divine Storm now applies Expurgation at 50% effectiveness (was 25%).
Quickened Invocation now reduces the cooldown of Divine Toll by 30 seconds (was 15 seconds).
PRIEST
Class
Shackle Undead has been renamed to Shackle Horror and can now additionally be used against Aberrations.
Holy
Benediction (not the Apex talent) has been renamed to Light’s Resurgence.
SHAMAN
Enhancement
Surging Currents now grants a charge per cast of Tempest.
Raging Maelstrom stacks no longer reset with each Maelstrom Weapon spender casts.
Restoration
The final rank of Stormstream Totem has been redesigned – Casting Ancestral Swiftness or Nature’s Swiftness grants a use of Stormstream Totem and Stormstream Totem no longer consumes a charge of Healing Stream Totem when used.
The second and third rank of Stormstream Totem now increase the healing done by Healing Stream Totem and Stormstream Totem by 20%/40% (was 30%/60%).
Healing Rain healing increased by 40%.
WARLOCK
Demonology
Dominion of Argus no longer has a chance to summon a Doommaiden but now has a chance to summon Grand Warlock Alythess or Lady Sacrolash.
WARRIOR
Arms
Apex Talent: Master of Warfare – Middle node now increases Mortal Strike, Cleave, and Overpower damage (was only Mortal Strike and Cleave) by 2.5%/5% per stack (was 5%/10%).
TRANSMOG
With the introduction to the updated transmog system, Venthyr Covenant members who have unlocked the Bewitched Wardrobe will receive a discount on outfit changes while participating in the Ember Court feature.

title: Leveling Experience Increased in Midnight Beta Test Development Notes
author: POSTED 2025/12/11 AT 7:10 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has released new Midnight Beta Development Notes, which includes an increase in the amount of experience required to level from 80 to 90!
Blizzard
View Original
Hello Beta testers! Here are this week’s changes to the Midnight Beta, including the 12.0.1 development notes:
NEW FEATURES
The following Midnight Season 1 dungeons will be open for testing on Heroic, Mythic, and Mythic+ difficulty:
Pit of Saron
Skyreach
Seat of the Triumvirate
Algeth’ar Academy
Visit the Season 1 dungeon threads for more information and to submit feedback: NA thread | EU thread
CHARACTERS
The amount of experience required to level up from 80 to 90 in Midnight has been increased by 33%.
Developers’ notes: Based on data from Beta playtesting, we’ve seen that the current rate of leveling is faster than we anticipated, with some players hitting level 90 before finishing the Campaign or entering Voidstorm. To that end, we’re going to be adjusting the experience requirements based to make leveling time more consistent with past expansions.
CLASSES
DEATH KNIGHT
Hero Talents
Deathbringer
Blood
Exterminate damage increased by 110%.
Reaper’s Mark damage increased by 110%.
Wave of Souls damage increased by 110%.

Calamitous critical damage bonus increased to 240% (was 220%).

title: Why Do Players Have to Buy Decor More than Once?
author: POSTED 2025/12/11 AT 12:25 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Housing has proved to be a massive success, though not without some friction as players travel across Azeroth and beyond in order to collect various pieces of decor.


How many rocks will I need?
EXPLORING A LIVING WORLD
While many players would like to just sit down at their plot and experiment with everything the system has to offer, the developers have a different point of view. At its core, World of Warcraft is an MMORPG, and the designers want to give players reasons to go out into the world. For some players, that's a simple trip to a vendor, but for others its a reason to complete an activity or achievement they may not have done before... though they shouldn't be strenuous grinds.

They commentedon this recent interview with Meggoart, expressing that these shouldn't be strenuous grinds, but that there should be a bit sweat equity involved in earning new parts for player homes. The intent is to foster a feeling of accomplishment



Taliesin & Evitel similarly raised the question of individual use decor, rather than one-time purchases that can be used repeatedly. In this case, the developers said they want a certain amount of friction, so that a player who decides to purchase a thousand lamps to cover their house can feel proud of their effort, since it requires an amount of investment that not every player can or will replicate.

The crafting economy also weighs into this, as making decor items unlimited one-time unlocks would significantly reduce the value of recrafting, and the team didn't want to create confusion by making some items limited and others unlimited.

FINDING A MIDDLE GROUND
While the developer's point of view isn't unreasonable, players are still left with a lot of potential back and forth as they fine tune their homes. Requiring players to travel to a remote part of the world to acquire a piece of decor is one thing, though traveling back and froth as they figure out exactly how many lampposts or windows they need can start to get tedious. There are a few ways players can make this easier, though they still threaten to take players out of their creative flow.
Decor is warbound, so alts can be used as shopping mules while your decorator stays home.
Each neighborhood is connected to its faction capital's portal room, allowing quick travel to most areas of the game.
The housing dashboard provides a hearth back to your house, but will also return you to the previous location, similar to Death Gate or Nostwin's Voucher.
The middle ground would seem to be some sort of shopping catalogue - a new NPC within the neighborhood or an element within the collections UI which allows players to purchase duplicates without schlepping back to wherever the piece initially came from.


Perhaps some new NPC vendors could sell items players have already collected.

title: The Cult Within - Midnight Pre-Patch Rewards and Playthrough (Story Spoilers)
author: POSTED 2025/12/11 AT 4:13 AM BY PORTERGAUGE
content:
The Midnight pre-patch brings us back to the Twilight Highlands to investigate rising cult activity. In this post, we've documented the rewards from this questline, and a complete story playthrough.

WARNING: Midnight Spoilers in this post!
Table of Contents
Rewards
Story Playthrough
World Quest and Rare Unlock
REWARDS
Players who complete this questline will recieve two rewards:
Completing the quest Cult It Out will award players with Twilight's Blade Tabard.
Completing the quest Cult It Out also grants players the title <Name>, Definitely Not a Cultist.
STORY PLAYTHROUGH
Our quest starts differently between the two factions. For the Horde, they are called by Grand Magister Rommath to the Cleft of Shadows in Orgrimmar. For the Alliance, they are called by Magister Umbric to Old Town in Stormwind. For both sides, there are rumors swirling about the resurgence of Twilight cultists in the cities who are taking advantage of the Radiant Song to recruit new members.
Grand Magister Rommath says: Champion, I have heard unsettling rumors that Xal'atath's Twilight's Blade cult is recruiting within Horde cities.
Grand Magister Rommath says: Meet me in Orgrimmar so we may root out this evil. We cannot suffer a single cultist within our walls.
Magister Umbric says: Champion, rumors suggest that Xal'atath's Twilight's Blade cult has been recruiting in Alliance cities.
Magister Umbric says: Meet me in Stormwind so we can conduct an investigation. If the cult is there, then we must stamp it out at once.
Grand Magister Rommath says: Over here, champion. I've tracked the cultists' activities to the caves below.
Grand Magister Rommath says: We must root out these cultists before they spread their corruption. Find and eliminate them.
Magister Umbric says: Over here, champion. The rumors I mentioned began in this part of the city.
Magister Umbric says: Please be discreet. Not everyone who seems suspicious or practices void magic is necessarily a danger.
 

Exploring their respective capitol districts, players speak to Suspicious Citizens, many of whom speak of the promise of dark power. After determining who is a cultists versus a regular citizen, we return to Umbric or Rommath with a clear truth: the Twilight cult is back.
There's always another struggle on Azeroth. What if you could leave it all behind?
<They look left and right before leaning in too close.>

Know anything about the Radiant Song? It just never ends.
This world doesn't deserve you. How often do you run to its rescue?

We value you. We know what you could be with the right help.
Has the Light left you in the dark? You're not alone in these shadows.

She's here to show us a better way.
Do you have experience with otherworldly creatures? Would you be interested in part-time work as a sitter of sorts to something with six or seven tentacles?
Grand Magister Rommath says: So the Twilight's Blade are indeed recruiting here. I suppose I have to work with Umbric after all.
Grand Magister Rommath says: It's imperative that we stop the Twilight's Blade at its source: the Twilight Highlands.
Grand Magister Rommath says: This portal will take you to our base of operations. I'll meet you there.
Magister Umbric says: So Rommath was right for once. The Twilight's Blade have been recruiting here.
Magister Umbric says: Now that we have confirmed the rumors, we must stop the Twilight's Blade at its source: the Twilight Highlands.
Magister Umbric says: This portal will take you to our base of operations. I'll meet you there.
Despite their differences, Umbric and Rommath agree to work together, converging at a camp in the Twilight Highlands to investigate the resurgence of cultists at its source. This camp acts as the base of operations for the pre-patch event, containing blood elf and void elf characters from both factions as well as vendors with new cosmetic goods and catch-up gear.

 

With the two faction stories converging, we are asked by Umbric and Rommath to infiltrate the Twilight's Blade in the region to learn more about their aims. Before we leave, the two bicker in a short conversation about the merits of practicing Void magic.
Grand Magister Rommath says: Yet more minds twisted by the Void. This must seem quite familiar to you, Umbric.
Grand Magister Rommath says: Infiltrate the cult and see what you can learn. But do not succumb to the allure of their sermons.
Grand Magister Rommath says: We may have removed the cult's presence from Orgrimmar and Stormwind, but their numbers still grow. This is a staging ground.
Magister Umbric says: Arator told me of a larger force being assembled by Xal'atath. The Twilight's Blade seems a lesser threat by comparison.
Grand Magister Rommath says: Every practitioner of the void is a threat, Umbric. Have your own mistakes and misadventures done nothing to teach you that?
Magister Umbric says: Becoming ren'dorei has proven to me the opposite. The Void can be harnessed for good, and its wielders left in control of their minds.
Magister Umbric says: If you want to speak of "mistakes," look to your own Magisters. How many times has their pursuit of power nearly led to ruin?
Grand Magister Rommath says: I will not be lectured by you! Not when you forced me to exile you from Silvermoon for refusing to abandon your forbidden research!
Magister Umbric says: The knowledge gleaned from that research has saved our world countless times! You're merely too stubborn to acknowledge it!
Grand Magister Rommath says: Enough! Like everything else you do, this plan to infiltrate the cult is foolhardy and dangerous. It had better work, Umbric.
Magister Umbric says: Oh it will, Rommath. The question is where or not you'll admit it when it does.
In the nearby town of Dunwald, we find a small army of Twilight acolytes in the midst of training. After nabbing some of their gear to create a disguise, we meet their Master of Acolytes, Gehl Harrowtongue. From there, he instructs us to undergo acolyte training by assisting in rituals, sparring with cultists, and reading through their sermons.

 
  Gehl Harrowtongue says: You. Acolyte. Haven't you finished your training? Where is your tabard?
Gehl Harrowtongue says: Prove yourself as one of Xal'atath's faithful. Only then may you attend the initiation ceremony.
Tenets of the Twilight's Blade
First, Xal'atath is to be obeyed and worshipped in every action.

Second, herald the end of Azeroth with every breath so that all may find her.

Third, seek deep knowledge and grasp onto dark powers as you find them or they find you.

Fourth, delight in agony as you have the opportunity to cause it.

Fifth, fight to maintain your station with every ounce of your being.

Sixth, protect the image of the Blade by never using the name of the Hammer.

<The list continues for multiple pages.>
Her Weapon Reforged
Xal'atath has blessed our great leader Mor'duun with the power to bring the void to Azeroth.

We do all things to gain Xal'atath's favor. We work to earn her blessings. She can bring an end to all our suffering.

We have never had a greater purpose than serving Xal'atath in her unending malevolence. We shed the name of Twilight's Hammer to become her shining, reforged weapon. The Twilight's Blade!
Mor'duun: Master of the Profane Arts
Mor'duun held great power before he met Xal'atath, but with her strength, he has ascended!

He leads the Twilight's Blade to greatness. Every act draws her eye to his devotion. She notices every word!

May all follow in his footsteps as we chase oblivion and transformation together!
Once our acolyte training is complete, we are sent into a nearby cellar to hear the sermon of the new leader of the Twilight's Blade, an ogre named Mor'duun. While his image speaks to the gathered cultists about their newfound alliance with Xal'atath, we set up a trap to cause their rituals to backfire and kill everyone inside.


 Gehl Harrowtongue says: Take heed of Mor'duun's speech, Our leader's words have set many cultists' hearts afire. I'm sure they will yours as well.
Projection of Mor'duun says: We are the Twilight's Blade. The void-black dagger Xal'atath wields against Azeroth.
Projection of Mor'duun says: We cut deep. Blood. Carnage. Chaos! This is what gains her favor. This is our glorious purpose!
Projection of Mor'duun says: We shall strike at the heart of Azeroth and end this flawed world. For Xal'atath! For the Twilight's Blade!
Upon leaving, we are joined by Umbric with a much simpler task: kill the remaining Twilight cultists in the area. While he heads back to camp, we pick the town clean, including our 'mentor Gehl Harrowtongue.

 Magister Umbric says: Excellent work, Raphah. I believe we have learned enough here. Time to tear this camp apart.
Magister Umbric says: I will meet you back at the basecamp. Our fight against this cult is only beginning.
Gehl Harrowtongue says: You still haven't learned. The Twilight's Blade will be your end!
Gehl Harrowtongue says: I didn't... teach you that.
Grand Magister Rommath says: I maintain that this plan was a risk, Umbric. Luck has once again spared us from your recklessness.
Back at camp, we gather our thoughts to affirm what we set out to know: the Twilight's Blade has been formed to usher Xal'atath into Azeroth. The time for infiltration is over, and the pre-patch fully begins as we head out to fight the Twilight cultists.
WORLD QUEST AND RARE UNLOCK
Completing this questline will also unlock new World Quests and Rare in the Twilight Highlands for the duration of the pre-patch. Completing these rewards Twilight's Blade Insignia, which can be turned in back at the basecamp for mounts, pets, and housing decor.

title: Pandaren Heritage Armor Questline Walkthrough - Lore and Rewards
author: POSTED 2025/12/10 AT 9:51 PM BY IMX3
content:
With the release of Patch 11.2.7, Pandaren can get their hands on the Pandaren Heritage Armor by helping Li Li Stormstout and others with setting up the Spirit Festival!

For more information on this questline, including each exact quest required to earn the armor, check out our guide below.

Pandaren Heritage Armor Guide
Pandaren Heritage Armor Guide
QUESTLINE REQUIREMENTS
This questline is a racial activity specific to Pandaren characters, meaning you have to be on a Pandaren character to pick up, and complete the entirety of the questline.

Here are all the requirements in order to begin this questline:
You must be on a Pandaren character (either faction).
You must be level 50 or above.
When you are ready to begin this questline, Horde Pandaren will seek out Ji Firepaw in Orgrimmar. For Alliance Pandaren, you will find Aysa Cloudsinger in Stormwind. These two NPCs drop the first quest, Invitation to the Spirit Festival / Invitation to the Spirit Festival.
REWARDS FROM QUESTLINE
Completing the questline rewards the Pandaren Heritage armor ( Ensemble: Heritage of the Wandering Isle) and a Festival Hot Air Balloon toy.

The Ensemble will reward 3 color variants of the Heritage armor: one with Horde colors, another with Alliance colors, and one colored Neutral gray.

Click Here to View the Pandaren Heritage Armor Models




LORE WALKTHROUGH OF QUESTLINE
After accepting the first quest from your respective embassy NPC, it's time to head out in preparation for the Spirit Festival! The Spirit Festival is an annual festival celebrated by the Pandaren in honor of Liu Lang, the first Pandaren to ever leave Pandaria.

When arriving at the Hot Air Balloon, we are met with Li Li Stormstout, all grown up! She mentions it being a long time since she last celebrated and wanted to invite her friends to come celebrate with her this year (that's you!).



When arriving on to the Wandering Isle, things are... off. The festival grounds are rather empty, which leaves Li Li confused. Where is everyone? The Pandaren seem uninterested in celebrating this year; it's time to turn that around! Chen Po has made effort to try and rally people for the festival, time to go see what people are up to. At this point, the questline breaks off into two chains: one happening in Morning Breeze and the other at Dai-Lo Farmstead.

 AT MORNING BREEZE
At Morning Breeze, Li Li's little brother, Shisai Stormstout is a panic. The most special fireworks specially reserved for the festival are missing! This short questline involves trying to solve where the fireworks could have been.



Uncovering clues about the missing fireworks, Aysa Cloudsinger claims the Hozen are responsible for the stolen fireworks. Why else would fur and bananas be found in the village? Ji Firepaw however, thinks otherwise. The fur is too soft, and Hozen eat bananas, not hoard them! Quick to jump to conclusions, Aysa insists we confront the Hozen Chieftain, Yuk Yuk. Turns out we were all wrong. The fireworks were not stolen, Jia and Kee Kee just wanted to play! It was all a misunderstanding, and to make it up to the Hozen, Ji Firepaw extends an invitation to the Hozen to come and join the festival.

AT DAI-LO FARMSTEAD
At Dai-Lo Farmstead, the townsfolk are surprised to see Chen Stormstout back in town, curious to see what brews he's discovered in his travels. Both Chen Po and Chen are ready to give the townsfolk a sampling of their brews, in hopes it will encourage them to come out to the festival. However, the townsfolk are dealing with bigger issues. The harvest season is in full swing, and the vermin are causing trouble. While the two brothers get to brewing, we help the townsfolk with the fields.

Once the vermin situation is handled, we see the townsfolk lining up ready to sample the brews made by Chen Po and Chen. After some sampling, a lot of townsfolk simply agree: the taste of home is best. Or is it? Despite the townsfolks' reluctancy, Chen feels confident they will come around. New taste takes time to appreciate!

 A SPIRIT FESTIVAL WITH NO SPIRITS?
After completing the two quest chains above, Li Li summons us to the Temple of Five Dawns. None of the spirits are in the temple. This part of the questline involves helping the many ancient, elemental spirits: Dafeng, Shu, Wugou, and Huo. With the spirits gathered, the festivities can begin, but Li Li has one more thing she'd like to do. We are then sent to the Wood of Staves, where Li Li wants to gather some flowers and pay a visit to her lost family.



Our first visit is to Strongbo, where Li Li expresses how much she misses Strongbo, and that she wishes he was here. Bo was a martial artist on the Wandering Isle, often considered as one of the greatest there was. Li Li was one of his students, and the two shared many adventures together. Many of their adventures can be seen in the World of Warcraft: Pearl of Pandaria graphic novel, released back in 2012.



The second visit is to Li Li's mother, Xiu Li Stormstout. Shocked by the surprise, Chon Po just so happens to be visiting the memorial right when Li Li is. Li Li was so young when she lost her mother, making note of how she doesn't even recall how she looked, or what she sounded like. Despite having a rocky relationship in the past, the two are able to bond over their mother, with Chon Po making mention of how much he's reminded of Xiu Li anytime he looks to Li Li. It's a heartfelt conversation, where the two are able to leave the past behind, and instead look to what lies ahead and find homage in one another.

 LET THE FESTIVITIES BEGIN!
With everyone ready and accounted for, let the Spirit Festival begin! With a light of the fireworks, a cutscene plays showing the Pandaren together, celebrating the festival. We are even graced with a visit from Yu'lon, with Lorewalker Cho flying on the back! Shen-zin Su has finally returned to the waters of Pandaria after so long, making this Spirit Festival to cherish by all.


"And from my home to yours, Lorewalker. Welcome."


Enjoy the festivities and surprise visit of Lorewalker Cho and Yu'lon. Light fireworks, share drinks with friends, the joy of Spirit Festival has returned to its people. There are a few additional dialogue cutscenes players can enjoy, including ones from Lorewalker Cho, plus Aysa Cloudsinger and Ji Firepaw.

title: Player Housing Decor Hotfixes - Patch 11.2.7 Hotfixes for December 10th
author: POSTED 2025/12/10 AT 6:47 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has posted hotfixes for today which includes Player Housing hotfixes.
Blizzard
View Original
Here you'll find a list of hotfixes that address various issues related to World of Warcraft: The War Within, Mists of Pandaria Classic, Season of Discovery, WoW Classic Era, and Hardcore. Some of the hotfixes below take effect the moment they were implemented, while others may require scheduled realm restarts to go into effect. Please keep in mind that some issues cannot be addressed without a client-side patch update. This list will be updated as additional hotfixes are applied.
Hotfixes
December 10, 2025

Dungeons and Raids
Vault of the Incarnates
Resolved an issue where Raszageth was not spawning in the correct location.
Housing
House Level 3 now awards an exterior decor placement budget of +50 items, in addition to existing rewards.
Margrave's Stitched Leather Rug can now be properly crafted with reagents obtainable from Shadowlands Skinning.
The Dornic Mine and Cheese Platter, the Dornic Sliced Mineloaf, and the Earthen Hospitality Cheese-Like Brick recipes are now properly Warbound and the crafted items they make are now properly unbound and can be bought and sold on the Auction House.
MOTHER's helpfulness knows no bounds. She now sells additional copies of the decor rewards from A Farewell to Arms, MOTHER's Titanic Brazier and N'Zoth's Captured Eye, for 10,000 Echoes of Ny'alotha each (a cost reduction of 90%).
Class Order Campaign achievements now retroactively award decor. Players who previously purchased one or more additional copies of decor earned from these achievements will see this retroactive award made available with the release of the Midnight pre-expansion update.
Players should now be correctly awarded the Drained Dark Heart of Galakrond for completing the achievement Worldsoul-Searching. Additional copies of this decor will become available for in-game purchase from a vendor with the release of the Midnight pre-patch.
Fixed an issue preventing some players from purchasing additional copies of Corruption Pit decor.
The Fortified Alliance Banner may no longer be placed in your house exterior.
Quests
Fixed an issue where “The War Within Recap” did not have a waypoint for Valdrakken.
December 5, 2025

Dungeons
Sanguine Depths
Resolved a Timewalking issue that could cause General Kaal to fail to appear if the gauntlet event was progressed through too quickly.
Spires of Ascension
Resolved a Timewalking issue with Devos, Paragon of Doubt where Archon's Spear sometimes could not be aimed properly.
Horrific Visions
Players can once more share their sanity to revive downed party members.
Syncronized Mind Stabilizer will allow for the first revive this way to be free.
Housing
The Battle for Azeroth achievement A Farewell to Arms now properly, retroactively rewards both the MOTHER's Titanic Brazier and N'Zoth's Captured Eye décor to players who have earned that achievement. Players who have already purchased additional copies of these décor from MOTHER in the Chamber of Heart will see them made available in a future patch.
Tocho and Lyssabel should no longer offer neighborhood renaming outside their respective neighborhoods, and players should no longer have the option to rename their neighborhood while in a public one.
Traveling Book Shop owners Lonalo and Solelo should now have books for sale more frequently, having learned the art of supply and demand.
Sizzling Cinderpollen will drop once again if you have already purchased the Soaring Meaderbee mount. The drop rate for Sizzling Cinderpollen has been significantly increased.
Players can now use the "Teleport to Plot" button while shape-shifted.
Player versus Player
Solo Shuffle
Resolved an issue that could cause Dampening to increase faster than intended.
Quests
Alleria Windrunner once again offers the quest "Manaforge Omega: Dimensius Looms".
Fixed an issue where players could not access Dragonflight Story Mode raids above level 70.
Fixed an issue that caused a herd of windsteeds to go missing on the Wandering Isle.
World
The portals to Valdrakken in Orgrimmar and Stormwind can now be used by new players on the Azure Span campaign.
Added portals to Stormwind and Orgrimmar in the Xal'atath Lorewalking Ny'alotha area.

SPEC POPULARITY IN MYTHIC+ FOR TWW SEASON 3 - WEEK OF DECEMBER 2ND
The meta comp for the Season, Frost DK, BM Hunter, Havoc DH, Prot Warrior, and Resto Druid has done it; they've completed the first +23 key for the Season in live servers.

KEY LEVEL DISTRIBUTION IN SEASON 3 - WEEK OF DECEMBER 2ND
The gap between +10 and +12 keys continues to drop, with most players being done with their Keystone Legend achievements, and most only doing +10s to fill in their weekly Vault slot.

MYTHIC+ PARTICIPATION PER WEEK IN SEASON 3
While new heights are being reached in the high end, overall interest in M+ continues to decline, with not even 400k keys being completed this week.

Legion Remix
DISCLAIMERS AND SOURCE
Data for this article is sourced from Raider.io statistics for Mythic+ dungeon participation, collected during the week of December 2nd. Data encapsulates all key levels, looking to paint a complete picture of what the M+ community is doing during the period.

Note that, since the data reflect only participation, there are many implicit biases in the numbers.
Since all metrics shown deal with popularity, they aren't directly attached to spec balance, but to community perception of spec balance.
Specs considered bad by the community will generally be played less and tend to have lower participation.
Highest keys tend to have a very low participation, meaning specs played by these players will be overrepresented, despite any actual balance.

title: Brawler's Guild Season 5 Now Live - New Broker Challenges & Full Strategy Guides
author: POSTED 2025/12/09 AT 9:08 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
Inactive since Battle for Azeroth, the Brawler's Guild makes its triumphant return in Patch 11.2.7. Learn about all the Season 5 changes and climb the ranks fast with our full strategy guides.

Brawler's Guild Overview
Brawler's Guild Overview
Brawler's Guild Strategies
Brawler's Guild Strategies
Brawler's Guild Rewards
Brawler's Guild Rewards
Table of Contents
What is the Brawler's Guild?
Gaining Access
Locations
Fights & Strategy Guides
WHAT IS THE BRAWLER'S GUILD?
The Brawler's Guild is a special event in which players can take on solo challenges against enemy NPCs. There are many tiers of fights, each increasing in difficulty and complexity. As you progress through the ranks of the Brawler's Guild, you will gain access to many cosmetic rewards including mounts, pets, transmogs and even new housing decor.

  ACCESSING THE BRAWLER'S GUILD
How players will access Season 5 of the Brawler's Guild remains unknown. It's likely that invitations will be found somewhere in Dornogal, but we'll be sure to provide an update as we learn more.
BRAWLER'S GUILD LOCATIONS
The Brawler's Guild takes place in different locations based on faction. Alliance players can participate at Bizmo's Brawlpub while Horde players can join at the Brawl'gar Arena.

ALLIANCE

The Alliance Brawler's Guild is Bizmo's Brawlpub, located inside Stormwind's Deeprun Tram. Once you enter the Deeprun Tram, you'll see the entrance to Bizmo's Brawlpub under the first set of trains.
/way #84 66.5 34.8 Deeprun Tram SW Entrance
/way #499 52.2 46.3 Bizmo's Brawlpub Entrance
HORDE

The Horde Brawler's Guild is Brawl'gar Arena, located in Orgrimmar. The arena is accessed from an instance portal in the Valley of Honor, going up from the Auction House.
/way #85 70.1 31.5 Brawl'gar Arena Entrance
BRAWLER'S GUILD FIGHTS & STRATEGY GUIDES
Once you've received your invitation and been accepted into the guild, you're able to head to either Bizmo's Brawlpub or Brawl'gar Arena to start fighting and climbing the ranks. Simply queue yourself into a fight by talking to either a Bizmo's Brawlpub Bouncer or a Brawl'gar Arena Grunt.

You can challenge bosses in the Brawler's Guild in a couple of ways:
Ranked Fights - The default option that requires you the progress through 7 ranks of 4 bosses per rank in a set order.
Challenge Cards - After reaching Rank 8, purchase cards that allow players to face off against a specific Ranked boss or previous season encounters.
Random Brawls - Purchasing a Bag of Chipped Dice allows players to queue into random fight from any season.
NEW Broker Challenges - Increase the difficulty of fights by adding affixes.
Looking for strategies for the Brawler's Guild fights? Then check our dedicated strategy guide!
Brawler's Guild Strategies
Brawler's Guild Strategies

title: Remix Decor is Available to All After Event - But Will Be Costly
author: POSTED 2025/12/09 AT 4:37 PM BY WHISPYR
content:
Blizzard has announced that Legion Remix Decor will be available after the event ends, but for a much higher price, so make sure to stock up now! After the event ends, all decor will be available from Val'zuun in the Legion Dalaran Underbelly, but will cost a large amount of Order Resources and Veiled Argunite.

The prices are already visible on the Midnight Beta client Val'zuun, with prices for decor ranging upwards of 50 Veiled Argunite and 18,000 Order Resources! With such a steep cost after Remix ends, players should be looking to farm all their Legion Remix Decor before the event ends on January 19, 2026. For more information about Legion Remix Decor Farming, check out our decor farming guide for the event!
Blizzard
View Original
Legion Remix players! As you finish up and reach your goals on the Broken Isles (and Argus), it’s worthwhile to visit Domelius in the Infinite Bazaar to pick up whatever decor you may want for your house. After Legion Remix ends, all decor from that event will be sold by Val’zuun in Legion Dalaran’s Underbelly (even if you didn’t get the original Legion Remix achievement) for Order Resources and Veiled Argunite… but his prices will be steeper than Domelius’s. So if you’re looking to line a large room with Eredar Lord’s Fel Torches, it’s worthwhile to pick them up while Remix is still going on.

Chimes
BUYING LEGION REMIX DECOR
Players looking to get their Housing Decor from Remix can do so via Domelius, located in the Remix version of Dalaran. Aside from the Decor itself, Domelius' stock of items are all Uncommon or Rare quality, meaning that he can be a great source of Housing Experience for players looking to upgrade their homes!

To purchase the full shop from Domelius and get the maximum Housing Experience, 247,500 Bronze is required to get one of each decor item.

Additional copies of popular items, such as the Tome of the Corrupt or Legion's Fel Torch will cost players between 5000 and 10,000 Bronze each, with bigger altars and tables costing a full 30,000 Bronze.

title: Mythic Crown of Cosmos Could Feature a Secret Phase in Mythic Difficulty
author: POSTED 2025/12/09 AT 3:45 PM BY IMX3
content:
The dungeon journal on the Midnight Beta hints that we could see a secret phase on Mythic difficulty for the last boss of the Voidspire raid!

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
DUNGEON JOURNAL PREVIEW
The last boss of the Voidspire raid features 3 stages on both Normal and Heroic difficulty. When looking at the dungeon journal for Mythic, the entirety of Stage 3 is missing. Also, the Stage 2 for Mythic has the same name as Stage 3 of its Heroic counterpart. Why would they make Stage 2 of Mythic the same name as Stage 3 of Heroic?

 
Heroic difficulty (left) vs. Mythic difficulty (right) dungeon journals for the Crown of Cosmos boss fight.
DOES THE CROWN OF COSMOS HAVE A SECRET MYTHIC PHASE?
Stage Two for Mythic Crown of Cosmos is labeled The End of the End and all we have is a ominous line regarding the Voidspire.

Wild cosmic energy spirals out of control, sealing the fate of all atop the Voidspire...


While it could be very possible the dungeon journal isn't fully updated, this is quite reminiscent to what we saw with the Mythic Gallywix Secret Phase. On the PTR leading up to the release of the Liberation of Undermine raid, there were many alterations in the dungeon journal for Mythic Gallywix versus Normal and Heroic.

With Gallywix's boss fight, Phase 3 of Heroic was moved to be the first Phase of Mythic, but with some additional mechanics added in. With Heroic, it took until Phase 3 for Gallywix to jump into his mechsuit. On Mythic, Gallywix immediately jumps into his mech at the start of the fight.

Blizzard has implemented many secret phases in Mythic boss fights before, including Sire Denathrius, The Jailer, and Gallywix. What do you think? Is the Crown of Cosmos fight going to have secrets in Mythic? Let us know in the comments below!

title: Midnight Mythic+ Season 1 Development Notes - Large Changes to Returning Dungeons

During the test period you’ll be able to acquire and customize Mythic Keystones by talking to the Keystone Vendor in Stormwind, Orgrimmar and Silvermoon. The nearby Dungeon Teleports NPC will assist you with transportation.Throughout beta, all Midnight dungeons will be available for testing on Normal and Heroic difficulty. title: Lost Legion Infernals in Legion Remix - New Spawning Repeatable "World Boss"
author: POSTED 2025/12/09 AT 1:55 PM BY ANSHLUN
content:
Fire has been set to the rain in Remix, and Lost Legion Infernals are invading the Broken Isles! Let's take a look at these new foes, a new weekly quest, and what you get from defeating these heated rocks.
LOST LEGION INFERNALS
While in outdoor zones in Remix, you'll sometimes see the message Lost Legion Infernal begins phasing into this timeline.... This message indicates a Lost Legion Infernal is spawning in your zone. You can see its location on the minimap.



As the vignette suggested, Lost Legion Infernals have health pools similar to World Bosses and scale with the number of players in combat with them. They have different affixes every time they spawn, similar to Obelisk Doomguards. Unlike world bosses or rares, however, you receive loot from Lost Legion Infernals every time you defeat them. Their loot is as follows:
20x Fragmented Memento of Epoch Challenges
4x Flawless Thread of Time
Stamina Mementos for your Artifact
1250x Bronze
1100 to 1200 (Currency #3268)
Their spawn rate seems to be tied to the amount of activity in the zone. This means realms with a lot of players will see Infernals spawning back to back, while smaller realms might have breaks between spawns. Overall, they are up very often.
WEEKLY QUEST
Before going on an Infernal spree, head to the Infinite Bazaar, where Moratari has a new quest for you: Out of Time. This is a new weekly quest that asks you to slay a Lost Legion Infernal.



Defeating the Lost Legion Infernal will complete that step and drop a Blank Doomsayer's Pamphlet, which you can bring back to Moratari. By doing so, she will have a couple of dialogue options explaining what the pamphlet is.



Picking any options unlocks the next step to burn the pamphlet at the bonfire in the middle of the Bazaar. When doing so, you'll be prompted to put a message in the pamphlet before burning it. Depending on what option you pick, a different message will be broadcast to everyone in the Infinite Bazaar. These range from showing off your stats in Legion Remix, to silly ones.



Dialogue implies that this is a weekly quest, so you will have the opportunity to send at least a couple more of these messages to your realm before the end of Legion Remix. What message are you sending to your realm?

title: Legion Remix Phase 5 Patch Notes - Faster Experience, Higher Item Level
author: POSTED 2025/12/09 AT 12:04 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has released patch notes for Legion Remix Phase 5 which includes faster leveling and an increased item level.
Blizzard
View Original
Glide into fast frenetic fun in Legion Remix as you meet up with the infinite dragonflight once more, level new characters, and earn a variety of items to add to your collection. As the Legion Remix timeline begins collapsing, say your goodbyes and embrace the chaos.
Infinite Echoes Updates Inside
The infinite hourglass turns once more! As the echoes of the Legion reverberate through the shifting sands of destiny, the world trembles with anticipation. It is time to step boldly into Legion Remix: Infinite Echoes, where every heartbeat is charged with new possibilities and the timelines themselves bend to your will.

Designed to make progression smoother, offer more ways to earn rewards, introduce new challenges, and provide closure to ongoing storylines, here’s a list of what you can expect.
Quick Facts:
Feature/Update Description
Flawless Threads of Time New upgrade currency replacing Motes of a Broken Time; used to enhance gear to item level 779.
Erus’s Scrapping Service Allows conversion of excess motes/threads into Fragmented Mementos.
Timerunner’s Mayhem Stacking experience bonus (+3% per stack, up to 10 stacks, 12 hours each) from Infinite Research.
New Quests from Moratari “Until Our Next Hello” and “Out of Time”.
Farewell to the Infinites Event Ritual at Infinite Bonfire; burn Blank Doomsayer Pamphlets to conclude the Infinites storyline.
Lost Legion Infernals New enemies for additional challenges and rewards.
Rotating World Bosses Six bosses appear every 18 hours in the Broken Isles, each with unique rewards.
Increased Loot & Obelisks More frequent loot drops, Timewarped Obelisks, and upgrade opportunities.
Flawless Threads of Time: Weaving Your Legacy: Seek out the luminous Flawless Threads of Time—your key to empowering your armaments to a resplendent item level of 779. Gather them through your exploits, and by dismantling max-level gear.

Erus’s Scrapping Service: When your satchels overflow with motes and threads, seek out Erus. His new scrapping service lets you convert surplus motes and threads into Fragmented Mementos of Epoch Challenges—precious tokens of valor, sold in stacks of ten.

Timerunner’s Mayhem: The relentless pursuit of knowledge bears new fruit! Embark on Infinite Research quests to earn the potent Timerunner’s Mayhem—a stacking boon of +3% experience for twelve hours per stack and stacking to ten.

New Quests from Moratari: All roads lead to the enigmatic Moratari at the Infinite Bazaar. For those who have braved the introductory quests, the quests “Until Our Next Hello” and "Out of Time" await, offering closure and hope as the infinite saga wanes.

Farewell to the Infinites: As the infinites' mission draws to its poignant end, heroes are invited to participate in the ritualistic burning of Blank Doomsayer Pamphlets at the Infinite Bonfire. This symbolic act marks the passage of eras and the forging of new destinies—a moment for all Timerunners to reflect and embrace the adventure that lies ahead.

Lost Legion Infernals: The battlefield trembles as Lost Legion Infernals crash down, heralds of chaos from forgotten timelines. These rare elites now stalk the land, each guarding with new loot to claim.

World Bosses from Other Timelines: Every eighteen hours, six world bosses from myriad timelines descend, each presenting a fresh challenge and the promise of rewards.

Accelerated Revelry: The tempo of Legion Remix picks up the pace. Expect bountiful loot drops, a proliferation of Timewarped Obelisks to empower your journey, and the chance to secure Flawless Threads of Time by scrapping your highest-level gear.
What is Legion Remix?
Legion Remix is a time-limited seasonal event where players can create a Timerunner as one of the original 12 Legion classes and replay the entire Legion expansion at an accelerated pace from level 10-80 with some major gameplay twists. Players will collect power, face epic challenges, and earn a mountain of cosmetics such as pets, mounts, and transmog gear.
Available to Everyone
No expansion purchase is needed, but a World of Warcraft Subscription or Game Time is required to begin this fast-paced adventure through the Broken Isles. This means that Classic players can also participate in the Fel frenzy by simply installing the modern (live) World of Warcraft client.

WoW Trial Accounts will also be able to experience this World of Warcraft Remix without a subscription or Game Time through level 20. Purchase a subscription or Game Time to continue beyond level 20.
It’s All Inside
Druid Class Order Hall
Players can take part in all twelve dungeons, one mega-dungeon, five raids, all the Legion Campaign quests, local stories, World Quests, Class Order Hall campaigns and Artifact questlines, Legion Assaults, Invasion Points, Withered Training, and more!

The infinite dragonflight is accelerating all weekly lockouts, reputation gains, and Class Order Hall missions, so get in and have fun!
Our Remix Story So Far
Eternus of the infinite dragonflight
In Remix: Mists of Pandaria, Eternus from the infinite dragonflight was trying to learn more about the past. Now with the Worldsoul Saga™ heating up, she’s looking into some of the events that have led up to what’s happening now to try to understand more of what really saved Azeroth from the Legion.
What’s New in Legion Remix?
We’ve added some new gameplay systems with an eye toward capturing the spirit of the original Legion experience. Our goal is to create opportunities for challenge and strategy that players will eventually dominate with epic power.
Remixed Artifact Weapons
Artifact trait tree on Ebonchill, Greatstaff of Alodi
Instead of the cloak and gem systems from Remix: Mists of Pandaria, we’re going all-in on Artifacts. Every Artifact weapon shares a powerful trait tree, and you’ll choose a path to go along—Nature, Fel, Arcane, Storm, or Holy.

You won’t be locked in and can respec anytime, now made easier with our new Quick Assign feature—you can Ctrl-click to spend up to your remaining Infinite Power purchasing toward your selected point, or Ctrl-right click to refund back to the selected point.

You’ll also find jewelry along the way that lets you add Ranks to traits from any path, taking Ranks from other paths or boosting your chosen path beyond its natural limits! Mix and match to customize your Artifact weapon.
Heroic World Tier
Inspired by Diablo, Heroic World Tier is a new way to play outdoor content. Eternus is experimenting to prepare the champions of Azeroth for what could happen if things get worse. She’s empowering creatures to have more health, more damage, and sometimes Affix-like buffs, empowering their difficulty and rewards. While you’ll encounter tougher foes, if you can overcome them, you will reap the rewards in the end.
Timewarped Obelisks
Power up with Timewarped Obelisks found throughout Legion Remix. These grant temporary but powerful buffs to players as they explore the world.
Timeworn Keystone Dungeons
For players who love Mythic+, we’ve remixed things a bit more to create Timeworn Keystone Dungeons. We’re bringing back most of the Affixes from the original launch of Legion and adding four new Affixes specific to Legion Remix called Eternus’s Trials.
Infinite Research
Each day, players can choose new research assignments to undertake, such as killing demons, running a dungeon, collecting bronze orbs while Skyriding, and more. With over 100 quests available and the ability to unlock access to take on multiple at once, you’ll have plenty of options.

You won’t need to check in every day and can bank up to six days of quests that you can do when you’re ready to take them on. So whether you are taking part in Legion Remix daily, or only have time on the weekends, you’ll be ready to go.
Get in the Mix with Legion Remix Rewards
Housing Decor
Legion Remix opens up opportunities to add a variety of new and returning items to your collection along with new Housing decor you can collect and use once Housing goes live in the Midnight expansion. To help you set your sights on the items you want most, we’ve rounded up a complete list of how and where to get them. See the full list of available items in our article.

Players can also gain new titles such as “Chronoscholar,” “the Infernal,” and “of the Infinite Chaos.”
The Cost of Loot
Bronze is making its return as a currency players can use to purchase cosmetic items only. Instead of upgrading your gear with Bronze, upgrades will drop from all content. Harder content will provide a higher drop chance for upgrades.
More Character Slots and Early Character Conversion
We’re also adding five new character slots to all World of Warcraft accounts so you can make even more characters. You also don’t have to wait for the end of Legion Remix to move your characters over to play with them in The War Within.

This is a one-way trip, so make sure you choose wisely before transferring timelines. Once a Remix character is converted into a standard character, that character can no longer access Legion Remix content.

Transferring your character will remove all Remix-specific items and abilities, clear your quest log, and grant you level-appropriate gear and gold. Any legacy items you collected, including any unopened cosmetics, will be mailed to you.

To convert your Timerunner character, you’ll need to select it in the character selection screen and click on the icon next to it to convert. Please note that characters must be 24 hours old before they can be converted to a standard character in World of Warcraft. The War Within expansion is currently required to play above level 70. Converting a Timerunner above level 70 to a standard character requires The War Within or Midnight expansions.

To learn more about converting your character, visit our support article.
Legion Remix: Content Rollout
We’re rolling out new Legion content to Legion Remix every two weeks. We’ll start things off with the original launch content from Legion and end with Argus and Antorus. Each release will bring new content and new rewards to earn.
Here’s a schedule and overview of the content you can expect to experience:
October 7- Skies of Fire: Take to the skies of the Broken Isles with the original Legion content and the Emerald Nightmare and Trial of Valor raids.
October 21 - Rise of the Nightfallen: Continue the Nightfallen’s story with the Insurrection campaign and new World Quests across the Broken Isles. Re-experience the Legion mega-dungeon with the Return to Karazhan, and take on the Nighthold raid.
November 4 - Legionfall: Establish a foothold on the Broken Shore, take on Legion Assaults, and fight your way into the Cathedral of Eternal Night and the Tomb of Sargeras.
November 18 - Argus Eternal: Replay the Argus campaign across three deadly zones. Take on the Seat of the Triumvirate and Antorus, the Burning Throne.
December 9- Infinite Echoes: As the Legion Remix timeline begins collapsing, say your goodbyes and embrace the chaos.
Legion Remix Content Update Notes
Learn more about all the additions, changes, and updates in our Content Update Notes.
Watch the Legion Remix WoWCast
If you missed the original Legion Remix announcement, don’t worry, we have you covered.
We can’t wait to see you on the Broken Isles in Legion Remix!

title: Level Even Faster in Legion Remix with Timerunner's Mayhem
author: POSTED 2025/12/09 AT 11:35 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
With Legion Remix Phase 5: Infinite Echoes now live, players can level even faster with a new buff called Timerunner's Mayhem!

 Remix Overview
 Artifact Abilities
 Remix Rewards

Gained by completing Infinite Research quests offered by Eternus in the Infinite Bazaar, a unique form of daily activities in Legion Remix, which gives players a choice between several different objectives to pursue. Unlike normal daily quests, they aren't strictly limited to one per day either - the limit is defined by research level with a daily refresh to afford players freedom in playing when and how they want.


Research level increases over time to ensure players have objectives to pursue even after completing main quests.


Starting at just 3% increased experience, the buff can be stacked up to 10 for a total of +30%, but this is also in addition to a massive reduction in the amount of experience required to level from 70 to 80 in Patch 11.2.7, so players should have quite an easy time finishing up any remaining characters.

title: Housing Exterior Decor Budget Increased to 250 at House Level 3
author: POSTED 2025/12/09 AT 10:23 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard has surprised us this week by adding a new perk that increases the housing exterior decor limit from 200 to 250!


Make sure you progress your housing level to unlock this bonus.


This increase comes in the form of a new perk unlocked at housing level 3, though it is not yet clear whether further increases will be found along future ranks of the track. In a recent interview with Taliesin & Evitel, the developers commented that there were technical constraints to increasing exterior decor limits, wanting to ensure that even minimum spec computers would be able to navigate a heavily built-up neighborhood, but that the team would try to increase the budget as they evaluate performance over time.



Blizzard has also now commented in a bluepost, acknowledging the increase and offering some guidance on aerial housing development.
Blizzard
View Original
Maintenance is complete for this week, and we have two development notes for Housing.
Up
Almost immediately after the 11.2.7 patch went live, we saw players using keybinds to elevate houses off the ground, even though there weren’t UI controls for that. We quickly began work to enable the controls so that it would be easier to move a house upward.

We have two concerns, of course:
The underside of houses aren’t modeled or textured. Players who lift their houses may decide to hide that part behind other things.
If you lift your house up, clicking on the door could become difficult. You might want to consider building a ramp or a jumping puzzle or a mount landing spot, etc.
“I have just met you, and I love you.”
Inside Out
One of the biggest pieces of feedback we’ve gotten over the past few weeks was that the exterior decor budget felt too restrictive. The was intentionally set to 200 to address performance concerns.

Now that we have some data from live servers to look at, we’re happy to conclude that we can increase this limit by 25%. At House level 3, the exterior decor limit will increase from 200 to 250.

We’ll continue to monitor everything, keeping an eye on performance impacts (especially as other features are added to Housing) and adjust accordingly.

“She has great new friends, a great new house. Things couldn’t be better.”

title: Legion Remix Final Phase Breaks Item Level Cap for Even More Power Fantasy
author: POSTED 2025/12/09 AT 9:59 AM BY SQUISHEI
content:
With the launch of Legion Remix Phase 5, players can now increase their item level past 740!

Through scrapping and content in Legion Remix, players can now obtain Flawless Thread of Time and Artifactium Sand to grow past item level 740! The very first pieces of gear that were opened on a 740 character were 753 gear, but now this gear is increased by +4 item levels above the watermark.



Here's the known drop rates of Flawless Thread of Time.
Mythic bosses drop 4
Scrapping purple drops 1-3
It shouldn't take too long to upgrade your gear to this new item level assuming you were at 740. Complete a couple Mythic raids and scrap more gear and you'll get there in no time!
