MANAFORGE OMEGA RAID TIER LISTS
Looking for more insight into the raid balance? Check out our Tier Lists, which include more information besides pure throughput for Manaforge Omega.

DPS Tier List
DPS Tier List

Healer Tier List
 Tank Tier List
Tank Tier List
THE WAR WITHIN SEASON 3 DPS RANKINGS
To start our look back into Season 3, we will be looking at Devastation, Beast Mastery, and Elemental.

Beast Mastery started Manaforge Omega in a dire situation, looming at the bottom for the first few weeks. However, tuning changes swapped the landscape quickly, with BM leaping to the top 7 in the week after the tuning changes, all the way from the last spot in the week prior. Ever since then, Beast Mastery has stayed in the top 5, displaying remarkable results.

In contrast, Devastation had a strong start in Manaforge, occupying the top 4 spot in week 1. However, this was short-lived, with Devastation immediately starting to lose spots the following weeks. Throughout the tier, lizards have floated around the middle of the pack, between 15th and 20th place in the rankings, a large change from Liberation of Undermine, when Devastation had amazing results.

Similar to BM, Elemental Shaman had a really good tier. Elemental hasn't dropped from the top 7 the whole tier and has been leading the race ever since Turbo Boost about two months ago.

We've invited our writers for the three specs to contextualize their journeys better, and you can read them below, after the rankings.

For Rankings, without any new tuning or changes, all changes below are just noise changes, with specs that are close enough balance-wise, just swapping positions.

95th Percentile Statistics

Class Writer Commentary
To help us better understand the charts above, we invited our Class Writers for Devastation Evoker, Beast Mastery Hunter, and Elemental Shaman to provide insights about the journey their specs had in Manaforge Omega.
Devastation Evoker
Preheat said:
Warcraft Logs tracks overall raid statistics for damage dealers, based on the highest performing logs in Manaforge Omega. Position in this chart is based on average spec performance for each encounter with a large enough sample size. How each spec is graded depends on the specifics of what Warcraft Logs determines is eligible damage. Based on these statistics Devastation's performance through this tier was solidly average. Despite this, there has been a bit of downward movement as the weeks have gone on. Let's take a moment to discuss some of the reasons why:

Throughout every tier, tuning will occur. Specs that are overperforming will be nerfed and underperformers will get buffs. Specs in the middle don't typically get a lot of attention, but the ripples of the other spec changes can still be felt. In addition to this, there are bug fixes to consider. Scalecommander received some hefty buffs in the form of bug fixes, but the end result was not enough to really push the spec ahead of its peers.

When a spec has very little movement, its rank becomes more about what is moving around it. Devastation has felt a little downward pressure from Augmentation, which received enough changes (and farm optimizations) and to push it above Devastation as the higher performer. Changes to what Warcraft Logs considers acceptable damage have also not helped. Devastation is a spec with a lot of incidental cleave, meaning that its rank is lowered overall when what counts for ranking is strict.

Overall, Devastation has been consistently average throughout this tier. In a world where people focus on statistics and what is "Meta" or "Best" this can be seen as a negative. The real performance and value of Devastation (and Evoker utility) in actual content is hard to miss, even if it can't be qualified.
Beast Mastery Hunter
Tarlo said:
Beast Mastery Hunters journey through Manaforge Omega has been an explosive one. It started off the the tier lacking the proper tools to really do much. It lacked burst, had lost its only execute, lacked cleave and didn't really have a strong enough raidbuff to keep it in on bosses. It didn't really excel at anything and was often passed over.

But everything changed when the Dark Ranger nation attacked. On September 9th, the Dark Ranger hero spec was given massive buffs (and a few Simc fixes were found around the same time). Suddenly, the spec had a significant amount of burst, which now meshed well with the burst windows found on Nexus-King Salhadaar and Dimensius, as well as the Unyielding Netherprism trinket. The burst was crucially also on a 1 minute cooldown, which made it very flexible for these encounters and worked well with Power Infusion. This was exactly what the spec needed to succeed. Initially the tuning was a bit high, so it's been tuned down gradually over time, leaving the spec in a very strong but not overwhelmingly strong position.

As of writing, the spec is mostly held back from the very top of the overall rankings by Forgeweaver Araz (where Withering Fire arrows can hit immune pillars and be wasted) and The Soul Hunters (which is just a poor damage profile for the spec in general). It also doesn't like that a lot of the earlier bosses have become very quick kills for a lot of guilds, so Blighted Quiver and Unyielding Netherprism doesn't have as great an opportunity to stack very high. Beast Mastery generally does not have that strong burst at the start of the pull, since neither the Blighted Quiver nor Unyielding Netherprism have any stacks. Shorter fights mean this initial burst window is more relevant. On the last two bosses, however, the spec remains extremely strong, having cooldowns for every relevant burst moment regardless of how quickly you push due to its flexible 1-minute cooldown. The burst windows with Bloodlust and damage amps are later into the fights, so Blighted Quiver and Unyielding Netherprism both have time to ramp up.
Elemental Shaman
HawkCorrigan said:
It's been a tier, and what a tier it's been for Elemental Shaman. For what felt like the first time in forever, Elemental got played for the World First Kill of a Mythic end boss. And not just one. Three Elementals were in for the fight for both the first and second-place teams. Even for the rest of the playerbase, the spec's performance didn't notably drop. Throughout the tier, Elemental kept itself steadily in the Top 3 of overall DPS on Warcraft Logs. Good place to start, but what exactly did that mean for per-boss performance, and how did our performance change throughout the tier?

Elemental has well-defined strengths, which just so coincide with the requirements of this tier's raid. Right now, Elemental brings excellent single-target damage, good burst AoE, and strong cooldowns, all of which work well with the various wave-based add spawns and vulnerability mechanics in Manaforge Omega. This led to very inflated DPS numbers in week 1, when most players were still stuck on the first few bosses, overemphasizing Loomithar and Soulbinder in the averages, and Elemental topping the statistics for about a week.

In the next few weeks, Elemental dropped to somewhere around 4th-5th place. The fights still suited the spec well, so numbers were still good, and the spec was incredibly powerful on some fights like Dimensius, but overall, it wasn't particularly overtuned. When 11.2.5 was released, Blizzard agreed with that evaluation. Every spec ahead of Elemental got hit with the nerf-bat, and Elemental went into the patch completely unscathed.

Ever since, Elemental is one of the three top specs in overall DPS on the statistics and brings strong damage patterns to fights where it deals damage in a way that other specs struggle with, and is also versatile enough to work in all different types of encounters. It is one of the top specs for Fractillus, Loomithar, and Plexus Sentinel, thanks to its good Single-Target, but also very good for AoE bosses like Soulbinder and Forgeweaver. Dimensius just combines the damage patterns on fight timers that work well for the spec. The only fights where it continues to struggle are Soulhunters and Nexus-King Salhadaar, where it still sits comfortably in the middle of the pack.
DISCLAIMERS AND SOURCE
The data for this article was taken from the Raid Statistics Page on Warcraft Logs for Mythic difficulty during the week of December 2nd. Overall, the numbers shown above represent data for the 95th percentile. For charts, we also included data for all percentiles and boss damage to better represent the current state of balance.

The data presented, however, isn't free of bias, as it is representative of the current meta of the game, which, in itself, is biased by community perception of specs.
This bias comes from players generally flocking to specs perceived as "better", be it either easier to play or dealing more damage, or a combination of both.
The other side of the coin is specs that are too hard to play or too weak will be underrepresented and appear lower than they actually are.
Competitive players will generally prefer specs perceived to do more damage, making the best specs appear higher than they actually are.
While not as prevalent in modern days, strategy differences and parse-funneling may impact rankings. Specs that excel in AoE, spread cleave, or burst windows will appear higher in the total charts.

title: Our Quality of Life Wishlist for Player Housing
author: POSTED 2025/12/08 AT 9:51 PM BY WHISPYR
content:
Player Housing is available as early access on live servers, and while the community has made amazing creations, some features are sorely needed as we approach the full release with the Midnight Expansion in March of 2026.

Player Housing Overview
Player Housing Overview

The rich depth of the housing system has already made for some Xal'atath shrines and other fun builds, but with a staggering amount of remaining quality of life that could be added, we're taking a look at some of the most requested features for the system.
HOUSING QUALITY OF LIFE WISHLIST
Many of the Quality of Life changes and features below will impact players differently, and are not listed in any particular order. Blizzard has also noted multiple times in the past that they are keeping a close eye on community feedback regarding player housing, and they already have multiple feature upgrades planned.
Undo Button
Sometimes you just hit the delete button by accident or move the wrong piece of furniture, an undo button would be a massive help for a lot of perfectionists out there

Decor-Locking
When you have your furniture exactly where you want it, being able to lock it down to avoid any unwanted adjustments is a great help, especially for builds with tiny details.

Group/Multi-select
When making layered builds like the dining table below, even moving the table or rotating it slightly becomes a massive headache. Using a multi-select click-and-drag function or even adding standard Ctrl+click functionality to items would be huge for reorganizing a build.

Plot Borders
Why is it that the exterior grid continues to show grid spaces even well outside of a plot's border? Building a perimeter, or just knowing where there is valid space to build, is a significant visual help, and being able to see the limits of a plot is a no-brainer for a quality-of-life feature.

Import/Export Decor Snippets
I am not the most talented builder in my guild, but there is some incredible work being done in my neighborhood! Being able to get an import for a custom window or an outdoor reading nook to use as a starting point would be a huge help! Even just being able to share base layouts or rough sketches of a room goes a long way in planning out a set of decor.

Inviting to Housing
A lot of players have super cool houses, ranging from visually stunning builds to intricate jump puzzles to complete. But the ability to visit players is currently somewhat limited. The most straightforward way to add visiting functionality is to allow visiting the homes of any player in your party, or to expand the Neighborhood search beyond just Battle.net Friends.

title: How Much Versatility Do You Need to Solo Legion Remix Keys
author: POSTED 2025/12/08 AT 10:48 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
We're entering the final stretch of Legion Remix, which has seen a reduction in the number of players queueing up for Mythic+, but with special rewards for completing up to Mythic+49 Keystone dungeons, we're asking just how much versatility do you need to solo Legion Remix keys?



The real answer is... it varies. Early in Remix, one-shots were a major limiting factor for non-tanks, though that was largely solved by massive buffs to damage dealers and healers. Since then, surviving boss mechanics is viable with much lower amounts of versatility, though healing that damage back up and killing mobs before they wear you down starts becoming a more crucial factor.
HOW MUCH VERSATILITY DO YOU NEED TO SOLO REMIX KEYS?
Unfortunately, there's no specific number, since it varies considerably with class and spec, as well as the content. Some dungeons, like Maw of Souls and Vault of the Wardens are much more difficult than others, while affixes like Sanguine and Necrotic introduce their own challenges limiting long-term survivability. Certain specs are also just more capable than others in dealing and healing varying amounts of damage, with tanks now being particularly disadvantaged.

More power is always better though, and practically any activity will contribute to increasing your overall power level, leading to a greater likelihood of success. There are four breakpoints of rewards from Legion Remix keys, though, so we've come up with some very rough guidelines for each: some players will need more, others will accomplish it with less, though you don't need to solo the dungeons to earn their rewards either and there have been many groups offering free carries just to help players earn all of the limited-time rewards.
Disclaimer: These are very rough solo guidelines that will vary based on class, spec, the dungeon, affixes, and personal skill level. Tackling each challenge with a group can also lower the recommended amount of versatility by as much as half.
+20 PORTALS
Completing a +20 awards a personal teleport to that particular dungeon, just like a normal Mythic+ season. This is the only reward which necessitates completing multiple dungeons, though you may have already earned some of the teleports in previous seasons.
Beyond 700ilvl, this has a fairly low bar, and should be a fairly easy solo with ~50% Versatility by practically any class.
+30 TRANSMOG
+30 awards the Timeworn Keystone Hero achievement and the Ensemble: Sargerei Commander's Hellforged Regalia. This is where you really want to start having closer to 740 item level gear, which can be farmed by using Mote of a Broken Time earned through a variety of activities, including rare mobs and daily lockout raids.
Above 100% Versatility is preferred for DPS, though tanks and healers may want to get closer to 150% to keep up with some dungeon damage requirements.
+40 TITLE
+40 awards the Timeworn Keystone Enthusiast achievement and %s the Infernal title. This will be the big hump for most players and you should definitely have near-max item level gear at this point. Remember that excess Motes beyond ilevel 740 can be scrapped for more Infinite Power, so use that to keep pushing Versatility!
Aim for 350%+ Versatility to solo +40, though at this point how well your class handles various affixes can make a big difference in difficulty, so this could very well push towards 400% or more.
+49 FEAT OF STRENGTH
Completing a +49 awards the Putting the Finite in Infinite Feat of Strength, representing the final reward of Legion Remix! Key scaling makes another big jump beyond +40, so finishing this last achievement will take everything you've got.
Soloing the final Remix challenge will likely require at least 600%+ Versatility, though depending on your class and spec this could just as easily be 700+. Doing it as a group is certainly preferred, not only to considerably lower the requirements, but also just speed up the process. Still, if you're looking for a challenge, this is it!

title: Flip Interior Decor Windows to See Through Them
author: POSTED 2025/12/08 AT 7:42 AM BY WHISPYR
content:
Players exploring detailed interior decor have a great trick up their sleeve: flipping windows to remove the illuminated glass effects, creating see-through windows!

Player Housing Interior Customization
Player Housing Interior Customization
 Decor Farming Overview
Decor Farming Overview

If you're interested in using this trick for your own decorating, check out our Housing Decor Database to pick out a perfect window. Afterwards, make sure to share your creation with the world using Wowhead's Housing Gallery!
WINDOW, WINDOW ON THE WALL
In their standard rotation, windows emit a soft glow that obscures the vision you would typically expect. There's really not much to see here; it's just your standard window!


It's when we look at the window from behind that things get interesting. The window pane is actually see-through! It turns out the layer of light on the windows isn't omnidirectional, so it only has the frosted-window look in the standard rotation. We can make use of this quirk in various ways.


A great usage of this comes courtesy of Omnithic-Area52, who made this cozy indoor garden viewable from behind two windows. Players have also used this trick to create fake outdoor views, glass cabinets, fish tanks, and more.

HOW TO UTILIZE THE TRICK AND QUIRKS
Getting a see-through window is as easy as placing a window down and rotating it to face the "wrong" way. This can be done in both standard and advanced modes, as the only requirement is to rotate it 180 degrees, but it is recommended to use the advanced tools to place it more precisely.


One notable quirk of this trick is that flipping the window does not eliminate the light coming through the window pane, so whatever you put behind your window will be illuminated. This can be worked around, as in the example in the previous section, by using many similarly colored and illuminated plants.

Using an orange-tinted window is also an option, which would give the room a different color temperature and allow for other builds, such as a display case for various trophies, bathed in a regal golden glow.

How are you going to use this in your house? Let us know in the comments below, or showcase it using the Housing Gallery!

title: Take Housing to the Next Level with Decor XP
author: POSTED 2025/12/07 AT 5:55 PM BY WHISPYR
content:
Looking to make extravagant housing builds? Leveling up your House Level will be a crucial long-term progression to unlock new features. From increasing the decor placement budget available to unlocking new rooms and lighting options, leveling your house is an impactful and essential part of the Player Housing system.
EVERYTHING TO KNOW ABOUT HOUSING LEVEL
Housing Level is a renown-style upgrade system with Player Housing that will allow players to incrementally upgrade their various budgets, including interior decor and interior rooms.


Going forward, there will also be special unlocks that will affect things like indoor lighting and other goodies.
EARNING HOUSING EXPERIENCE
Earning Housing Experience to level up your House is as easy as Unlocking Decor! Unlocking any uncommon or rarer decor will grant progress towards the next Housing level.

Leveling up your house grants the following special rewards alongside the incremental Decor Placement Budget and Room Placement Budget:
Initial Housing Unlock
Square Room (Medium)
Daylight Circle Room (Available in Midnight)
Octagon Room (Large)
Square Room (Large) and Evening Circle Room (Available in Midnight)
Level 6 is currently unavailable on live servers, but the beta has the Hearthlight Armillary listed as a reward, allowing players to change the interior lighting of their house.

title: Player Housing Tip! - Use Embedded Chairs to Make Seats on Any Object
author: POSTED 2025/12/07 AT 11:22 AM BY WHISPYR
content:
Lounging around the house has never been easier with this trick; simply clipping stools and chairs into other objects can make for a cool seating area. Being able to sit on your bed or a countertop is a fun way to add some charm to your housing selfies!

Player Housing Interior Customization
Player Housing Interior Customization
 Decor Farming Overview
Decor Farming Overview

For this trick, I used the Zuldazar Stool due to its flat edges, which make it easier to line up and hide in other decor, but this is not required and any chair can be used. For finding stools and chairs for this trick, you can check out the full catalogue of chairs using our Housing Decor Database.


This trick was first popularized on the Midnight Alpha by Ivy, who showed off the trick on social media with an cozy kitchen area as a backdrop, taking a cool selfie with their character sitting on a bar countertop.
TIPS AND TRICKS FOR EMBEDDING CHAIRS
As with most housing tricks, there is a lot of ways to use the mechanics in creative ways, but the basics are very straight forward and can be used in nearly every house. If you come up with a cool setup for this trick, we'd love to see it in our Housing Gallery!
Clipping the chair
This needs to be done in advanced mode for the move tool, which give fine control over the position of objects. It should be noted that once you exit edit mode, it is very difficult to move the clipped chair since it's nearly impossible to select. Luckily, there's a trick for this as well!

Fine Adjustments
In order to select the chair after clipping it into a decor piece and adjusting it, use the scale tool to shrink the object (in this case the bed), revealing the chair. Using the scale tool does not adjust the absolute position of the object, so you don't have to worry about ruining your perfectly placed scenery to add stools!

Tip: Scale the Stool
If you're having trouble hiding your chair properly, it's important to know that scaled down chairs still retain full functionality. The downside to this is that the hitbox to click the chair itself is smaller, and thus sitting in the chair can be more difficult.


This can be worked around by enabling the Enable Interact Key option in your settings window and keybinding Interact with Target. Using this, interacting with the chair does not require a mouse-click at all and the chair can be however small you'd like.

title: Silvermoon Neighborhood Teased - Midnight Concept Art Spotlight
author: POSTED 2025/12/07 AT 12:21 AM BY PORTERGAUGE
content:
Blizzard has released the concept art for the blood elf exteriors coming with Midnight, and may have teased a Neighborhood for Silvermoon as well!
Table of Contents
Blood Elf Concept Art
Housing District in Silvermoon?
BLOOD ELF CONCEPT ART
With the release of player housing in patch 11.2.7, Blizzard has also released a handful of new concept art pieces for the upcoming Blood Elf themed housing exteriors.


Silvermoon Structures by Forrest Imel


Interior Concept Art by Catarina Pulli


Silvermoon Relief by Forrest Imel
HOUSING DISTRICT IN SILVERMOON?
Keen eyes may have noticed that in the bottom left corner of the housing exterior art, there is a label: Silvermoon Housing District.



Currently, we've only been informed of the current two housing neighborhoods, the Horde's Razorwind Shores and the Alliance's Founder's Point. With housing exteriors for both night elves and blood elves coming with the launch of Midnight however, the question of if either race will have a matching neighborhood to go with it arises.

title: Xal'atath Shrines Step Foot Into Player Homes

title: Make Quick Gold with Lumber for Player Housing Decor
author: POSTED 2025/12/06 AT 1:14 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
With many players trying to creating their house and Lumber being a very limited resource, spending your time farming Lumber could net you a lot of Gold!

Professions in Player Housing
Professions in Player Housing
Table of Contents
New Lumber Resource
Crafting Profession Decor
Best Way to Farm Lumber
New Lumber Resource
Lumber is a required reagent in all Decor crafts. This new material is Warbound, meaning you can share this reagent across your alts but cannot sell it on the Auction House or trade it to another player. Lumber can be gathered by any character by heading to a neighborhood and completing the Lumber For You quest. Click the given item and this will grant the ability for you to see Lumber Nodes throughout the world as you explore.

There are currently 12 different Lumber types that are used throughout the professions. Each type of lumber can be found in their specific expansion-related zones, make sure you farm the right one!


Lumber Type Gathering Location
Dornic Fir Lumber Khaz Algar and K'aresh
Fel-Touched Lumber Broken Isle Zones and Argus
Ironwood Lumber Kalimdor and Eastern Kingdoms
Dragonpine Lumber Dragonflight Zones
Bamboo Lumber Mists of Pandaria Zones
Olemba Lumber Outland Zones
Shadowmoon Lumber Draenor Zones
Arden Lumber Shadowlands Zones
Ashwood Lumber Cataclysm Zones
Darkpine Lumber Battle for Azeroth Zones
Coldwind Lumber Northrend Zones
Thalassian Lumber Quel'thalas and Harandar (Midnight)

Crafting Profession Decor
With Lumber being required in all profession decor recipes, not being tradeable, and trees usually dropping 1 Lumber per harvest, crafting profession decor is heavily limited by the amount of Lumber gathered. Lumber is a brand new resource and there is very little of it in the economy, and the demand for these items is currently very high which makes it a great gold-making opportunity.

Take the Beloved Raptor Plushie item, which has the materials: 8 Fel-Touched Lumber, 15 Shal'dorei Silk, 5 Roseate Pigment, 2 Chaotic Spinel, 2 Runic Catgut. This is currently selling for, at minimum ~12k on populated servers, and more on unpopulated servers. The crafting materials other than the lumber for this item, currently cost less than 2k gold as there is a much bigger supply. This makes the 8 lumber that you can farm worth at minimum 1k each.


Cost and Profit Shown via CraftSim Addon


I personally farmed 8 Fel-Touched Lumber in about two minutes. Assuming the decor sells, with each lumber being sold for 1k each, you could make at minimum 100k per hour farming Lumber and crafting, if not way more! Just remember that you also need a character with a profession leveled since Lumber cannot be traded. Assuming you do, head out and craft some profession decor for some gold!

Check out our profession guide for more information on which decor items can be crafted from recipes.

Professions in Player Housing
Professions in Player Housing
Best Way to Farm Lumber
Lumber can be found from any trees and are in great supply. Flying a route around a wooded area and watching your minimap is a great way to collect some Lumber.

There's not much you can do to speed up Lumber farming. The best way to farm Lumber is to farm it on a Druid. As Lumber is Warbound, you don't need to collect Lumber on your crafter. Druids, like other gathering professions, don't need to leave Flight Form in order to collect lumber making them the most efficient gatherers, and this includes Lumber!

title: What Was Left Out of Legion Remix?
author: POSTED 2025/12/06 AT 9:30 AM BY IMX3
content:
While Legion Remix brought ways to earn tons of rewards from the original expansion, some notable rewards got left behind and can only be obtained on Live servers.

Legion Remix Overview
Legion Remix Overview
 Legion Remix: Rewards
Legion Remix: Rewards
Table of Contents
Legion Assault Weapon Ensembles
Pond Nettle Mount
Ratstallion Mount
Of the Doomed Ritual Armor Set
MoP Remix Feedback Reflection
LEGION ASSAULT WEAPON ENSEMBLES
While we saw the return of Legion Assaults with Legion Remix, one detail was left behind, weapon transmog!

In the original Legion expansion, final Assault bosses had a chance to drop different weapon transmog. Some of these weapons are iconic to collectors, like the hilariously long Wrathblade sword.

You can still obtain these weapons on Live, but with long wait times for Legion Assault spawns, mixed with low drop rates, the question remains. Why didn't these get added as Ensembles on the Legion Remix vendors?


Seriously, this sword is LONG.
POND NETTLE MOUNT
For this fishers of WoW, the Pond Nettle was left out of Legion Remix. This mount is a re-color of Fathom Dweller, which interestingly is included with Legion Remix, but not Pond Nettle. What caused it to be removed?

This mount can be obtained on Live servers by fishing in any fel water spot on Argus. Hopefully, it does not take over 20,000 casts to obtain like some other poor collectors had to experience. (Fingers crossed!)


RATSTALLION MOUNT
Rodents of unusual size! Ratstallion is another mount that never made it to Legion Remix. Like the name suggests, it is simply a large rat mount.

To obtain this mount on Live servers, players need to complete the Underbelly Tycoon achievement, which involves collecting 20000 from the Dalaran Sewers.


OF THE DOOMED RITUAL ARMOR SET
Back in the Legion expansion, there were a few different rewards available with defeating powerful demons summoned from their Ritual of Doom.

Certain rewards like the Hateful Eye are available from the Legion Remix Vendors, but there is no Ensemble set for the Of the Doomed Ritual Armor Set. With it already requiring 3 additional people of the same faction to summon a demon, it would be nice to see some transmog drop, but this unfortunately never came to fruition.

 DID BLIZZARD LEARN FROM MOP REMIX?
Legion Remix serves as a huge chance to collect all sorts of goodies without dumping a painstaking amount of hours to get it all. Certain items were even brought back after a long period of being unobtainable, like the Violet Spellwing. While this deserves its recognition, why were certain rewards seemingly forgotten again?

For those that participated in MoP Remix, one of the biggest pains that was a significant point of feedback was the absence of weapon ensembles. Not having weapon ensembles caused a lot of players to miss out on class-specific raid drops. With Legion Remix, this was a perfect opportunity to correct that mistake, yet that didn't happen.

Acknowledging and correcting small mistakes like this can help take a fun game mode like Remix events from good, to great.

title: Blizzard, Let Us Make the Housing Exteriors of our Dreams
author: POSTED 2025/12/06 AT 7:58 AM BY WHISPYR
content:
Player Housing is a wonderful addition, beloved by many players who are building their dream homes, but one issue has arisen: Exterior Decor Limits. The current limits for the interior and exterior decor are vastly different, even though the exterior is the part most people will actually see.

Blizzard's Housing Lead Jesse Kurlancheek has already taken note of the feedback in an interview with IGN, but it is currently unclear what the intended limit is.
Jesse Kurlancheek - Housing Lead
View Original
I think one of the things that gets talked about most frequently is outdoor decor limits are too low. People want to be able to build more stuff outside their house. We're trying. We're going to see where things land and sort of, we want players to be as creative as they want to be. And placing restrictions on folks is never the goal in any of our sort of guiding principles.
PUSH IT TO THE LIMIT
Interior limits are something Blizzard has continually increased and showcased in the various Player Housing trailers, including the incredibly detailed living room and bedroom spaces, which feature multiple small items and layers upon layers of decorations. That same level of decoration shown in trailers for the indoors is not even remotely possible in the current version of the system.

 
Outdoor limits currently (even taking early-access into account) are simply not enough, and players are already struggling to find ways to decorate without immediately hitting the limit. The house below is in my guild's neighborhood, and costs the player an eye-watering 160 decor points, out of the maximum 200.

 
This little reading-nook has quite a bit of smaller bushes and plants, but surprisingly, most of the budget for this particular house is actually spent on the less detailed objects. The perimeter of the house is using Gilnean Stone Wall, which carries a decor weight of 5, and simply enclosing a third of the plot accounts for 70/200 decor points.
INCREASING EXTERIOR DECOR LIMITS
A lot of the frustration with the current exterior decor limit is rooted in the unknown. While the Housing Levels show clear incremental gains for internal decor, with the beta showing a full 2360 budget at level 6, the outdoor decor budget remains unchanged. In fact, it is not even mentioned in the renown-style system. In addition to having no source of added exterior decor, Blizzard has disabled many outdoor decorations, including lamp posts, due to concerns about server load.

Concerns about server stability are valid and righteous, but not having any communication of what the limit is intended to be, where it is earned, and leaving players with barely enough budget to decorate a corner is a heavy limit for a system made available on the live versions of the game.


Simply knowing the intention behind outdoor decor, and when and where more budget will be made available, would go a long way toward alleviating the community's current concerns. With Blizzard already taking note in an interview, players can hopefully see the limit raised or at least have outdoor budget earn its rightful place on the Housing Level track.

title: IGN Housing Interview: Couples Homes, Cut Features, Contests, and More
author: POSTED 2025/12/06 AT 5:58 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
In an interview with IGN, Principal Game Designer Jesse Kurlancheek and Senior UX Designer Joanna Giannullis discussed sharing houses, features that didn't make it into launch, and supporting player-driven content!

 IGN Housing Interview
SHARED HOMES AND INTERIOR DECORATORS
As a part of the recent Housing Early Access Trailer, Blizzard teased the idea of weddings between players, whether official or through community driven events. Though avoiding the question of multiple players sharing possession of a house, the lead developer did relate that the team is thinking about ways players could help decorate each other's homes.
IGN said:
Is there any thought to eventually doing something where two people could have possession of a house together or you could give other people permission to decorate your house? I feel like there's a budding scene of potential interior decorator professions that could spring up around this.

Kurlancheek: Yeah, one comes up a lot is, "I'm not especially creative or I'm not great with a tool set. Can my friend come over and sort of deck out my apartment or deck out my house?" Yeah, it's certainly on our radar.
FEATURES IN DEVELOPMENT
To no surprise, some features didn't make it in time for early access, though that doesn't mean they won't make it into a future update. The full expansion launch already includes endeavors, increased housing level caps, new types of houses, and Night/Blood elf themes, but the developers are also working on restoring some of those cut features, such as customizable exterior lighting!
IGN said:
Was there anything that you had to leave on the cutting room floor for this initial launch that you really wish you could have gotten in? And are you willing to say what it is?

Giannullis: So much. So much. I mean, one of the things that's known is we were able to get the outdoor rooms in for the testing, but those won't be available to players until we actually launch Midnight, just due to some technical reasons, but we were really excited to get them out. We're glad we got them out early so players could give feedback and test them and see what they like about him. But there's some things like that.

Kurlancheek: There's exterior lighting we had to disable during alpha or beta, I can't remember what, just because of the performance impacts. If everybody in your sight line put 100 lights on their lawn, that was going to cause a problem. So, we had to disable those. We're going to fix it up, get them working post-launch, post Midnight launch. Yeah, I think there've been a number of things that sort of people held near and dear to their hearts that we had to just not cut, but just be like, "That's going to wait a little bit and then we'll get to it."
FUTURE FEATURES UNDER CONSIDERATION
And of course, some features are still under consideration. Saving decor layouts to quickly repopulate rooms, copy-paste functions to streamline mass creation, and importing/exporting entire layouts are all popular requests that the team has seen and heard from players, but while there are a lot of features we'd like to see, the team is making an effort to prioritize ones they think will benefit players the most.
IGN said:
Are you looking into any eventual copy-paste functionality or the ability to save custom item combinations? I know that's something a lot of people have been asking about.

Giannullis: A lot of people have asked about that. Definitely copy-paste. Being able, once you get something just right in advanced mode, not having to do it all over again. We're definitely keeping an eye on everything that people are asking for. There's a lot of things that people want. There's a lot of things we want to do, so it's just trying to decide what's going to help players the most.

Yeah, I mean the next question is basically the same one about export strings. If people want to decorate a house, show people and then have someone else copy it if they have those items.

Kurlancheek: Yeah, I think when we talk about any feature that's sort of in housing, it's what is sort of maximally useful across that spectrum of different players. And so, when you talk about something like importing or exporting strings of stuff, that helps the interior designer type player who wants to sort of provide services. It helps the players that are not necessarily super creative, right, are not really jazzed about the mechanical side of decorating. They have a vision but don't want use the tool set.

How are you thinking about things like housing contests and stuff like that? I don't know. I think about Race to World First and how that's a tune-in event, and I feel like I would watch an interior decorating show.

Giannullis: The thing is we've already seen a bunch of creators pop up that are like, "I'm a housing creator, this is what I'm doing." And they're starting to run their own challenges and contests, and so that's really exciting to see. I think we're all kind of watching that to see what sort of activities the players are getting up to.

Kurlancheek: Yeah, and how we can sort of build tools to support whatever that sort of shenanigans of being, right? A Trial of Style kind of thing. What do players need to be able to do that? What sort of functionality makes sense to better enable that?

Giannullis: I remember talking about how many people you can even have in your house at a time, and it was something that we were working really hard to make sure we could get that to a good place because we know people want to have parties, they want to do all these different things and we're trying to make sure that what we're building is supporting that and can handle that.

title: Log on Legion Alts to Earn Decor - Class Order Hall Architecture Decor
author: POSTED 2025/12/05 AT 5:45 PM BY ANSHLUN
content:
With Housing now live, there is a lot of decor to collect and pursue, with some being as easy as one login away to obtain. Let's take a look at the decor unlocked from the Legion Class Halls and what you need to unlock them.
DECOR FROM CLASS HALL ACHIEVEMENTS
For Housing, Blizzard added four achievements for each of the twelve class order halls from Legion. From those, three of them can be obtained as soon as you log in to that character, assuming you've completed their criteria, while the fourth isn't retroactive and might take a few days to complete.
RETROACTIVE ACHIEVEMENTS
The three retroactive achievements are simple; they require you to complete the following with each of the twelve classes present in Legion:
Complete the Class Order Hall Campaign
Unlock any Hidden Artifact Appearance
Complete the Class Hall Research Tree
Note that while credit is retroactive, the Hidden Artifact and Class Hall Research criteria require you to log on the character that completed it. This means logging on every alt you've completed your Legion Class Hall with.

If you haven't completed these before, you can complete the Class Order Hall campaigns and Hidden Artifact Appearance unlocks in Legion Remix at a much faster rate than regular.


The Archmage's Campaign 5 Points
Account-wide progress

Complete the Mage class Order Campaign.
Reward: Tirisgarde Book Tempest

Hidden Potential of the Deathlord 5 Points
Account-wide progress

Locate and unlock a hidden appearance for your artifact as a Death Knight.
Reward: Replica Acherus Soul Forge

Legendary Research of the Dreamgrove 5 Points
Account-wide progress

Research all Druid Class Hall Upgrades.
Reward: Replica Tome of the Ancients



Completing these three achievements for each class will reward decor based on that Class Hall architecture like Tirisgarde Book Tempest, Replica Acherus Soul Forge, and Replica Tome of the Ancients.



Tirisgarde Book Tempest

Replica Acherus Soul Forge

Replica Tome of the Ancients
RECRUITING CLASS HALL TROOPS
The fourth achievement that rewards decor involves recruiting 20 troops of any kind on each of the twelve Legion classes. As it wasn't something tracked, everyone has 0 progress on this achievement and must complete it from scratch.

Raise an Army for the Netherlight Temple 5 Points
Account-wide progress

Recruit 20 Troops as a Priest.

- Recruit 20 troops.

Reward: Netherlight Command Map


Completing this achievement for each class will unlock that class's Legion Mission Map, like Uncrowned Planning Table, Dreadscar Battle Planning Map, and Netherlight Command Map.



Netherlight Command Map

Uncrowned Planning Table

Dreadscar Battle Planning Map


Looking to see all decor available from Class Order Halls? Check out our Legion Decor farming guide:

Legion Decor Farming Guide
Legion Decor Farming Guide

title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Elite – Unboxing & First Impressions
author: POSTED 2025/12/05 AT 5:15 PM BY SAS148
content:
Wowhead is occasionally asked to review products we are affiliated with, and our Sr. Community Support Specialist was the recipient of a free headset - if you're looking for Holiday gift ideas for the gamer in your life, check out the review of the SteelSeries product below!
The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Elite is SteelSeries’ new ultra-premium, “audiophile gamer” headset – a world-first Hi-Res Wireless certified gaming headset offering 24-bit / 96 kHz wireless audio, carbon fiber drivers, and a feature-packed GameHub base station designed to handle your entire gaming setup. SteelSeries was kind enough to send me a sample unit to try out and share my impressions with you. I'm exclusively a PC gamer so I won't be able to speak much to the ability to connect to multiple sources, but I'll cover those features in the sections below. I primarily use my headsets to participate in company meetings, listen to music, watch movies/TV, and play WoW & Overwatch.

 Purchase the Arctis Nova Elite
Table of Contents
Unboxing the Arctis Nova Elite
Box & First Look
What’s in the Box
Design, Comfort & Build Quality
Feature Highlights
Hi-Res Wireless & Carbon Fiber Drivers
OmniPlay & GameHub: One Headset for Everything
ANC & Microphones
Infinite Power: Dual-Battery System
Arctis App, Sonar & Game Presets
Price, Value & Who This Is For
Budget-Friendly SteelSeries Alternatives
Pros & Cons
Final Thoughts
Unboxing the Arctis Nova Elite
Box & First Look


The box was pretty unique in my experience. The earcups of the headset can be turned 90 degrees so you can lay it flat, as a result the box is narrower than the usual, but wider. It had a premium look and feel, even a cloth carrying handle; though I can't imagine why anyone would be carrying this box around like a briefcase.
What’s in the Box


Here’s what you can expect when you crack it open:
Arctis Nova Elite headset
GameHub wireless base station / DAC
Two hot-swappable batteries for the Infinite Power system (one is already in the headset)
3x USB-C cables for connecting to PC/console and power
3.5mm cable for line-in / line-out
Quick-start guide and documentation
Premium carrying bag
Design, Comfort & Build Quality


SteelSeries is very clearly going for a “lifestyle” + “luxury gaming” look here rather than something purely “gamer-y.” The Nova Elite uses:
An all-metal frame and metal control wheel
Plush memory foam earcups
A minimalist, Scandinavian-inspired design co-created with designer Jakob Wagner
The Nova Elite both looks and feels premium. The earcup encases my entire ear (my preferred option) and they do a decent job on their own keeping ambient sound at bay. I've historically been a "keep it all black" sort of user of these products (and let's add RGB on top of it please!), but the Sage & Gold color scheme has really been attractive to me and several friends and family I've shared it with. That said, they do offer it in black if you're completely opposed to a new look.

I've now worn the Nova Elite for a couple weeks, as long as 12 hours at a time, and I can honestly say they're impressively comfortable. Though, on occasion, I have felt a bit overly warm wearing them. Taking them off for a few minutes seems to remedy that. I figure that's just a trade off for having great noise cancelling abilities and long-term comfort. The build quality is exceptional: the frame is entirely metal, it uses metal screws, has magnetic ear plates for swapping batteries or accessing the USB-C port. The microphone slides in and out of the left ear cup very smoothly, not sure what they made this part from but it's endlessly customizable to get the mic where you want it.
Feature Highlights
This section I'm mostly going to share what SteelSeries is saying about the various features, but I'll chime in with my own experience when I have something to elaborate on.
Hi-Res Wireless & Carbon Fiber Drivers
The big headline feature: this is the world’s first Hi-Res Wireless certified gaming headset. Over 2.4 GHz and Bluetooth, the Nova Elite can deliver 24-bit / 96 kHz wireless audio, promising up to 3× more detail and dynamic range than typical gaming headsets. Inside each earcup you get custom carbon fiber drivers with a brass surround. SteelSeries describes these as “pistonic” drivers – moving on a fixed plane like a car piston to minimize distortion and keep the sound as clean and accurate as possible, from 10 Hz up to 40 kHz.
OmniPlay & GameHub: One Headset for Everything


The GameHub is the brains of the operation. It’s a compact base station with:
Three USB-C inputs for PC, PlayStation, and Xbox simultaneously
Line-in and line-out ports
Support for up to 4 sources at once (2× USB-C + Bluetooth + line-in) with OmniPlay mixing
An OLED screen and control wheel for on-the-fly adjustments, including EQ and device mixing
The idea is: chat on Discord on PC, play on console, and scroll on your phone – all at the same time, balancing each source without swapping headsets or cables.
ANC & Microphones


Nova Elite pushes hard on isolation and mic quality:
Active Noise Cancellation (ANC) with a 4-mic hybrid system that adapts in real time and, in SteelSeries’ testing, reduces up to 42% more ambient noise than key rivals.
AI-powered noise rejection that can cut up to 97% of background noise for your mic, keeping keyboard clacks and room noise out of comms.
A retractable boom mic with 32 kHz / 16-bit capture plus a discreet on-ear beamforming mic that takes over when you stow the boom for “street” use.
I've really enjoyed the Noise Cancellation feature. You can turn it up, down, off or even go into transparency mode where it kind of amplifies the sound around you. I dunno, maybe you want to listen in on a conversation without looking like you're doing it, or, more likely... use the feature to allow you to hear people talking to you without having to remove the headset.
Infinite Power: Dual-Battery System


The Infinite Power System uses two swappable batteries:
One battery in the headset, one charging in the GameHub
Up to ~60 hours of playtime in total
Fast charge: roughly 4 hours of use from a 15-minute top-up (per SteelSeries).
The goal is “no downtime” – you swap the battery in a couple of seconds and keep playing. You don't even have to turn the thing back on if you do it fast enough, about 6-8 seconds. This is one of the best features in my opinion, I love that I never have to plug this thing in. The single battery has lasted at least two days in most of my testing, and I'm using it at least eight hours a day.
Arctis App, Sonar & Game Presets

On the software side, Nova Elite naturally gravitates to the SteelSeries’ ecosystem:
Arctis mobile app for real-time EQ changes, game profiles, ANC controls, and mic tweaks while you’re in-game
Access to 200+ game-specific audio presets tuned with audio engineers and pros (Fortnite, CoD, Apex, Minecraft, EA Sports titles, etc.)
SteelSeries Sonar on PC for deeper parametric EQ, virtual mixing, and per-game profiles
Personally I couldn't test out the Arctis mobile app, it claimed to not be available on Android. Still, I don't imagine I'd ever use such a thing. All the controls are readily available to me on my PC or the GameHub. That said, I'm sure there are at least some people out there who would solely use this headset on consoles, in which case the app would definitely be handy. Now, using SteelSeries Sonar unlocked a lot of potential options to customize the sound to what your ear holes prefer.

They also have some built in options for numerous games like OW2. My experience has been pretty neutral here. The preset worked okay, but I still made my own adjustments. Sadly, SteelSeries hasn't solved what seems like a universal problem with getting your headphone and mic sources to default correctly across numerous programs and devices. It's not all on them, there's a lot to consider... but sadly I didn't find it any easier fine tuning every little thing to get these working right everywhere. The options are there though, just expect some set up time if you're using multiple programs, devices and/or want different sound settings when listening to different sources: music, games, voice chat, or movies/TV.
Price, Value & Who This Is For
At $599.99, the Arctis Nova Elite is firmly in “console price” territory. As I mentioned earlier, SteelSeries is emphasizing the luxury look, feel and functionality of this headset.
...for players who care deeply about sound.
...for players who are attracted to the idea of a single headset for everything: PC, PS5, Xbox, Switch, mobile, and whatever else you need. It even has Bluetooth.
...for players who are fashion sensitive or just want to have a "status symbol" on their head.
...and, ultimately, for players with an abundance of disposable income. For those that don't, I'll highlight some more budget friendly SteelSeries options below, several of which I've had over the years.
 Purchase the Arctis Nova Elite
Budget-Friendly SteelSeries Alternatives
If the Nova Elite is out of budget, and no one would be surprised if that were the case, SteelSeries’ Arctis Nova lineup includes more attainable options that keep a lot of the same design language and feature set:
Arctis Nova Pro Wireless – High-end, premium wireless with dual-battery and multi-platform support, but a step down from Elite’s full hi-res and carbon fiber driver package.
Arctis Nova 7 Series – “Best overall” mid-range wireless option for most players; strong feature set without going into luxury pricing.
Arctis Nova 5 Series – Positioned as “affordable luxury” and a great PS5/Xbox headset, if you want a taste of the Nova ecosystem.
Arctis Nova 3 Wireless / Nova 3 (wired) – More budget-conscious, with the wired version especially noted as a strong “best budget/mid-range” pick.
Arctis Nova 1 Series – Entry-level wired headsets for players who just want solid sound and comfort without any wireless or base station extras.
Pros & Cons
Pros
Truly excellent wireless audio with Hi-Res certification, the headset sounds glorious!
Carbon fiber drivers and wide soundstage for detailed in-game audio, and the soundstage is a fun feature to play with.
Infinite Power dual-battery system with effectively no downtime
Comfortable, premium design that doesn’t scream “only for gaming”
I feel like I didn't mention the carrying bag enough: I love this thing. It feels like Prada, or what I imagine Prada feels like.
Cons
Very expensive – more than many full consoles or GPUs
GameHub adds to desk clutter and cable routing, but really not much more than a wired headset would.
Feature set may be overkill if you only play on a single platform
You need to buy into SteelSeries’ software ecosystem to get the most out of it
Final Thoughts
I'm in love with this headset. I never could have afforded it though, and even if I could have I wouldn't have been able to justify it. Perhaps on a payment plan like Klarna or Affirm, and Steelseries does offer Klarna. I don't much care about social clout but I have had numerous people approach me at the coffee shop to ask what it is and where I got it. It's true when I say it doesn't scream "only for gaming". The sound is exceptional. It's AI noise cancellation performs well above my standards and I'm told the mic is crisp & clear, even with the coffee shop's background sound (which most don't even notice).

Have I mentioned the infinite power supply via battery swapping? It's AWESOME. Not only do the batteries seem to last days, once it gets low you just pop it out and pop the fully charged one in: go another couple days. Rinse & repeat. The carrying bag is supple and bespoke, it has a magnetic closure and a zipper pocket inside to store the cables. Though it's admittedly tight to fit anything else; I wish I could get my mouse in there with the headset. So SteelSeries, maybe consider a little extra room for the next generation.



In short: if you can afford it, I highly recommend it. If it's outside your means, I'd recommend the Nova Pro Wireless as a really close second for nearly half the price. It doesn't have a fancy bag or Hi-Res sound, but it does have the infinite battery swap and multi-platform support. If that's still too costly and you're in need of a headset, check out the other alternatives I listed above. If you have any questions, throw them in the comments, I'll do my best to answer any genuine questions you've got about the Nova Elite! Happy gaming (or watching, or listening, or meeting, or recording, or mixing, or...)!

 Purchase the Arctis Nova Elite


Note: This article includes affiliate links to SteelSeries. Purchases made through these links may support Wowhead at no additional cost to you.

title: Cheesy Decor Available to Alliance Players for Completing Northrend Cooking Daily
author: POSTED 2025/12/05 AT 4:23 PM BY IMX3
content:
For a limited time, NA Alliance players can earn the Eversong Party Platter for completing the Northrend Cooking Daily in Dalaran!

Player Housing Overview
Player Housing Overview
 Player Housing Decor Items
Player Housing Decor Items
EVERSONG PARTY PLATTER
The Eversong Party Platter features a simple charcuterie board with some cheese and grapes on it. This decor is available for a limited time for Alliance players - the daily will cycle back again, but for players decorating their house right now, who wants to wait?

To get this decor, head to Northrend Dalaran and pick up Cheese for Glowergold from Katherine Lee in the Silver Enclave. You must have Northrend Cooking learned in order to see the quest.

Once completed, you will have earned your cheese platter, which is a nice, cozy addition to any kitchen or living space in your House!

title: Midnight Pre-Launch Event Testing Rescheduled for December 8th - Play With The Blues
author: POSTED 2025/12/05 AT 1:03 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
The twice delayed Midnight Pre-Launch Event testing has been officially rescheduled for December 8th. Check out how Midnight will begin alongside Blizzard developers.
Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
Blizzard
View Original
On Monday, December 8, please join members of the World of Warcraft development team in the Twilight Highlands on the “Midnight Prelaunch Event” realm to playtest the upcoming Midnight pre-launch event, where we’ll learn more about the Twilight’s Blade with the help of Magister Umbric and Grand Magister Rommath.

The experience will begin with the quest “The Cult Within” in Stormwind if you’re Alliance or in Orgrimmar if you’re Horde, and will eventually lead all players to the Twilight Highlands in the Eastern Kingdoms. The event in the Twilight Highlands has a rapid cadence, and should appear in the Event tracker, which you can view by opening your Map and clicking on the Events tab that appears when the map is minimized. (Default Hotkey: M)
Event Details
Date: Monday, December 8
Start: 12:00 PST (15:00 EST, 21:00 CET)
End: 14:00 PST (17:00 EST, 23:00 CET)
Realm: Nobundo
Requirements: Copy a character from the live servers to participate in the test.
Before we begin, please take some time to get familiar with changes made to your class and specialization. There have been some significant changes to talents, hero talents, and core class abilities.

We look forward to playing with you and reading your feedback! See you there, champions!

title: WoW Weekly: The Warning, Housing Early Access, December Trading Post, and More!
author: POSTED 2025/12/05 AT 12:02 PM BY IMX3
content:
This week in WoW, Patch 11.2.7 went live including the launch of Player Housing Early Access, a new Twitch Drop is available, and the December Trading Post brings a festive collection for the holiday season!

Patch 11.2.7 Overview
Patch 11.2.7 Overview
 Player Housing Overview
Player Housing Overview
Blizzard
View Original
The Midnight Prologue, "The Warning" awaits you in The War Within, stake your claim in Housing Early Access, adventures and challenges await you when World of Warcraft ®: Midnight™ arrives 2 March, and more! Every week brings thrilling new adventures and loot, so rally your allies and forge your legend in Azeroth.
Midnight Prologue: “The Warning” Content Update Now Live!
With the Ghosts of K’aresh behind us, Azeroth stands on the precipice of a future overshadowed by Xal’atath and the relentless advance of the Void. In "The Warning", the stage is set for what comes next before continuing the story in the Midnight expansion. Players will also be welcomed home with the start of Housing Early Access!

Head over to our previously published article to learn more. Take your next step into the continuing story of The War Within and prepare for the arrival of Midnight!
Stake Your Claim with Housing Early Access—Now Live!
With the release of the “The Warning” Midnight Prologue content update, players who have purchased any edition of the Midnight expansion can join a neighborhood, claim a house, and start customizing their new home with Housing Early Access!

If you don’t have Midnight yet, don’t worry. You’ll still be able to begin adding to your Housing collection through a variety of activities and achievements, or by purchasing them from vendors throughout Azeroth.

Our previously published overview will provide everything you need to know to start building your dream home in World of Warcraft.
Housing 101: Getting Started
Join Jay Hwang, Design Lead for Housing decoration, as he guides you through the Housing system in World of Warcraft. You'll learn about picking a neighborhood, purchasing a home, decorating, collecting, and he'll even answer some common community questions.
Prepare to face Xal’atath, the Harbinger and the Void in World of Warcraft®: Midnight™ launching worldwide on 2 March 2026, at 23:00 GMT. Players who have purchased any edition of the Midnight expansion can secure Housing Early Access starting 3 December 2025.

We look forward to you joining us as we begin this new chapter in Azeroth’s story. To dive deeper into Midnight and its many features, you can visit our previously published compilation article.
December’s Trading Post is Cool as Ice!
Mount: Jeweled Sapphire Scarab
“Begifted to a young Tol’vir Princess, this ornate jeweled statue was brought to life by the Gemseers of Ramkahen.”
Brave the chill and explore a trove of frozen treasures when you visit the December Trading Post. Complete a flurry of wintry activities to earn this month’s reward—the Sapphire Resonating Crystal—and welcome the Jeweled Sapphire Scarab mount to your stable.

Visit T&W (Tawney and Wilder) just outside the Mage District in Stormwind or the Zen’shiri Trading Post next to Grommash Hold in Orgrimmar to check out all the latest items they have available.

Players can also visit the Trading Post in the capital city of Dornogal (The Foregrounds) on the Isle of Dorn, where representatives of both Tawney and Wilder and the Zen’shiri Trading Post are open for business.

Here's Some of What's in the December Stock
Ensemble: Sky Witch's Attire
(Head, Shoulder, Chest, Waist, Legs, Feet, Hands)
Soaring Sky Fox
"Take to the sky on the most loyal and loving fox on Azeroth."
Kalu'ak Crest Horn Mount
“The tuskarr do not call these Crest-Horn "Kalu'ak" to claim them, but as a warning they will not tolerate any more lost to poachers seeking their unique antlers.”
Read through our article for a detailed list of all the items available from the December Trading Post and how to get them.

Champions new or returning to World of Warcraft and The War Within who want to know more can take a tour of the Trading Post to learn everything you need to know, from where to find it to how you can earn additional Trader's Tender through the Traveler's Log.
Take a Ride on Turbulent Timeways—Now Live!
Once more, the Bronze Dragonflight has discovered growing disturbances in the timeways, with several rapidly intersecting our own!

Timewalk through a previous expansion each week for 10 consecutive weeks, beginning and ending with the Shadowlands. This grand tour through World of Warcraft's past delivers six new Timewalking Dungeons for players to undertake—De Other Side, Spires of Ascension, Plaguefall, Necrotic Wake, Sanguine Depths, and Halls of Atonement.

Master the Timeways for a new Mount! This season, you'll be granted Ta’readon’s Mount Voucher for completing the “Master of the Turbulent Timeways IV” achievement awarded to those distinguished champions who maintain “Mastery of the Timeways” for any five weeks during the event.

The voucher will grant the “Ta’readon’s Mount Voucher” quest, directing you to Overseer Ta’readon in the Hall of Curiosities, where you’ll choose one of the four Shadowlands mounts—Bronze Wilderling, Bronze Corpsefly, Bronze Gravewing, or Bronze Aquilon—for free, and the ability to purchase the other three mounts from the Timewalking vendor for Timewarped Badges.

To learn more about Turbulent Timeways, including a dungeon schedule and list of rewards, read through our previously published article.
Twitch Drop Now Live! Get the Topsy Turvy Joker’s Mask Transmog
Watch 4 hours of any World of Warcraft content stream on Twitch.tv from 2 December at 18:00 GMT until 30 December at 18:00 GMT while these Twitch Drops are active to claim the Topsy Turvy Joker’s Mask transmog.

Start Time: 2 December, 18:00 GMT
End Time: 30 December 18:00 GMT

To participate, you must link your Twitch account by visiting https://account.battle.net/connections. If you have changed your password or made any changes to your Twitch or Battle.net account, you will have to relink your accounts. When you relink, there will be a 7-day cooldown before you can relink a new Twitch account. After you’ve claimed your rewards on Twitch, please make sure you’re logging into the Battle.net region you’d like to receive the drop(s) on, as the first region you log into will be where your items will be delivered. It can take up to 24 hours to receive your reward(s) in-game after claiming. Read our Frequently Asked Questions section for more information.

Frequently asked questions can be found in our previously published article.
Let us adventure alongside you, regaling you with tales of valor, casting a light on epic loot, and directing your eyes to legendary raids. Return each week to the World of Warcraft news site, where together, we’ll forge your destinies in Azeroth and beyond. Lok'tar Ogar!

title: Earn Free Decor on Multiple Characters with Neighborhood Quests
author: POSTED 2025/12/05 AT 8:42 AM BY WHISPYR
content:
With the release of Player Housing, Neighborhood Treasure Hunts are available, allowing players to earn free decor every single day. The trick? Quests can be completed on multiple characters every day. Doing the daily Treasure Hunt Quests can be a great source of Decor without the steep gold costs ahead of the upcoming full release of the system with Midnight.

Founder's Point Decor Treasure Hunts
Founder's Point Decor Treasure Hunts
 Razorwind Shores Decor Treasure Hunts
Razorwind Shores Decor Treasure Hunts

Housing decor can be quite expensive for the master decorators out there using hundreds of items, but earning it for free couldn't be easier!
DECOR TREASURE HUNT QUESTS
Quests are available once per day, per character, and each character has different quests offered. Additionally, if you have unlocked player housing for both Alliance and Horde, you can get two quests per day, one from each faction's housing hub. To accept the quest, it's as easy as talking to the Last Architect, who is standing in the middle of Founder's Point and Razorwind Shores.

Awnings span river's flow. Gold to the west, darkness east. Look beneath stunted tree.

The Last Architect will give a quest with a riddle attached that points out landmarks in the area or gives directions, eventually leading to a hidden decor object.
TREASURE HUNT DECOR REWARDS
Rewards earned from the Treasure Hunt quests are limited to specific drops. The Orgrimmar Nightstand, for example, can be earned from the Horde Player Housing in Razorwind Shores. These items can also be purchased from the vendors in town for gold, like usual.

The rewards are mainly Stormwind/Orgrimmar-themed for their respective areas, and decorations from other cultures still must be unlocked and purchased from their vendors.

title: Extremely Rare Player Housing Decor Costs 100K Echoes of Ny'alotha Each
author: POSTED 2025/12/04 AT 8:53 PM BY IMX3
content:
Because of the insane resource costs, these two pieces of housing decor are extremely rare to collect, costing players 100,000 Echoes of Ny'alotha per decor item.

Player Housing Overview
Player Housing Overview
 Player Housing Decor Items
Player Housing Decor Items
N'ZOTH'S CAPTURED EYE
The first decor item is N'Zoth's Captured Eye, which features the iconic eye of N'Zoth encapsulated in a twisted glass-like prism.

To be able to purchase this item, players will need to have the BfA Meta Achievement, A Farewell to Arms, completed. Once completed, this item can be purchased from MOTHER in the Chamber of Heart for 100000.

MOTHER'S TITANIC BRAZIER
The other decor item is MOTHER's Titanic Brazier, which is a simple light source with some glow effects towards the top. This item is also available for purchase from MOTHER for 100000. You do not need the Meta Achievement completed in order to purchase this item.
HOW MUCH TIME WILL IT TAKE TO EARN THESE DECOR ITEMS?
When considering needing the meta achievement completed, on top of just how many Echoes of N'Zoth it takes to purchase one piece of decor, the time investment for these items is a lot.

Echoes of Ny'alotha are earned largely through Black Empire Invasions, and defeating bosses in the Ny'alotha raid. Players can earn roughly 1,000 - 1,500 Echoes of Ny'alotha per week. For both items, players will need 200K Echoes of Ny'alotha. That equates to roughly 200 weeks of farming this currency, which is just shy of 4 years worth of time, for two pieces of Housing decor.

Players already have to sink a lot of time and effort in to completing the meta achievement, why weren't these items given to players for free once they earn the achievement?

title: Returning Character Cameo in 11.2.7 Sylvanas Questline
author: POSTED 2025/12/04 AT 3:47 PM BY PORTERGAUGE
content:
While questing with Sylvanas and Arator in patch 11.2.7, you might have missed the sneaky return of a character not seen since the end of Battle for Azeroth!

WARNING: Patch 11.2.7 Spoilers in this post!
Table of Contents
Return of the Blightcaller
Is Nathanos Alive Now?
RETURN OF THE BLIGHTCALLER
During our quests with Sylvanas in the Maw, players might have missed the background appearance of Nathanos Blightcaller.


This NPC shows up during the Determination quest, hidden behind a nearby rock while Sylvanas speaks to the player. This appearance can be finnicky, only appearing in certain circumstances. He has no dialogue or involvement in the quest, and is merely a silent observer.

IS NATHANOS ALIVE NOW?
The is cameo immediately brings up one pertinent question: is Nathanos alive?

Unlike the other spirits we have seen in the Maw, Nathanos is fully corporeal here, with his exact model from Battle for Azeroth. Physical bodies in the Shadowlands aren't uncommon, as seen in Maldraxxus, Bastion, and Revendreth, but all of those zones create new bodies for mortal souls to inhabit. By contrast, this seems to be the very same look Nathanos sported in undeath.

It also brings up another question: if this is Nathanos, who is the Familiar Soul? The obvious assumption of a spirit indefinitely following Sylvanas through the Maw was that it was her lost love, but it seems this might be someone else entirely.

Arator says: Another spirit?
Sylvanas Windrunner says: A silent one. He follows me and then departs, only to return again later.
The simplest answer could be that this is just a mistake. Perhaps this NPC was meant to have a ghostly effect that didn't get implemented, and Nathanos is still dead after all. Given the fickle spawning tendencies of this NPC, it could even be a mistake that it spawns at all, and is a remnant of an older iteration of this questline.

Either way, it does not seem like Sylvanas is aware of Nathanos' presence near her. Dead or alive, it seems likely that we will revisit the Maw at some point in the future to see these two reconnect.

title: New Healing Absorb Texture on Midnight Beta - Many Default Raid Frame Texture Updates
author: POSTED 2025/12/04 AT 9:50 AM BY SQUISHEI
content:
With changes over the last few weeks, the default raid frames have gotten texture updates. Are they better or worse than before?

As a reminder, you can use addons to customize your raid frames if you don't like these textures, and this represents the default raid frames only
Table of Contents
New Healing Absorb Texture
Out of Range Indicator
New Dispel Tint
NEW HEALING ABSORB TEXTURE
As noticed by Megasett on Twitter, healing absorbs placed onto players by enemies have a brand new texture.


New Healing Absorbs texture by Megasett


This brand new texture has "+" signs all over the texture representing the healing absorb. Previously, there was no extra pattern, and instead it was just a simple shaded overlay.


Old Healing Absorb texture on Gorkadin


At this time, there does not seem to be an option to disable this new texture and use the old ones. This new pattern seems very busy, and the old healing absorb function seemed to be what people have gotten accustomed to.
OUT OF RANGE INDICATOR
The new Out of Range indicator has been removed. In the previous Beta build, a new "Eyeball" texture was added that appears in the center of a player's raid frame when they are out of range, in addition to fading out the frame itself. Many players have criticized this as intrusive since it could not be disabled and the frame fading out was enough. It seems that Blizzard has heard this feedback.


Old Out of Range Eyeball indicator on Viectrix
NEW DISPEL TINT
A new dispel indicator has been added on the Beta a couple week ago, to make dispels much more noticeable on raid frames. The entire frame of the player will now be tinted a certain color, which should make it much clearer when a Poison, Curse, Disease or Magic effect is applied. However, the tint was so powerful and so bright that it easily clashed against certain class colors. This has been made slightly better in the latest Beta build.

This effect is turned on by default, but can be disabled in the options.

Old Dispel Tint New Dispel Tint

title: Why Aren't Legion Assaults Solo-Friendly in Legion Remix?
author: POSTED 2025/12/04 AT 6:03 AM BY POPULARTOPULAR
content:
While players await the final Phase of Legion Remix, many are finishing up their collections or completing final Achievements. As they make progress towards their final goals, many are left wondering - Why can't I complete Legion Invasions solo?

Legion Assaults in Legion Remix
Legion Assaults in Legion Remix
LEGION ASSAULTS
Legion Assaults are World Events in Legion Remix that rotate through each zone, where they are active for a period of 6 hours, followed by an 8.5-hour period of inactivity. After completing four World Quests during an active Legion Assault, players receive a quest for a short scenario to defeat the Legion Commander.



While Blizzard increased the cadence of these events, players can be stuck in queues for upwards of 20 minutes or more because there is no way for them to enter the final scenario of this Assault solo.
INCENTIVIZED SOLO PLAY IN LEGION REMIX
Legion Remix has incentivised solo play in several ways, and Blizzard has significantly streamlined many activities for players to complete at a rapid pace. However, Legion Assaults appear to have dodged this philosophy.



For some reason, Legion Assaults require a group of 3 players to enter the ending scenario, and completionists will want to do at least 10 of these for various achievements. While a premade group of 3 players can get into the scenario instantly, solo or duo players have to wait in the queue, and wait times have been shockingly long.
SHOULD BLIZZARD ALLOW SOLO ENTRY?
The solution appears to be an easy answer - if Blizzard wants players to be able to complete content quickly during this limited-time event, allow players to enter these scenarios alone. Many players feel like this content is currently soloable, and Blizzard has removed this barrier to entry for similar content in the past, such as with Island Expeditions.

Defending the Broken Isles III
Account-wide progress

Complete 10 Legion Assaults in the Broken Isles with a Timerunner.

- 11.2.5 Remix - Legion - Journey - Legion Assaults Complete 10
- 11.2.5 Remix - Legion - Journey - Legion Assaults Complete 10 - 11.2.5 Remix - Legion - Journey - Legion Assaults Complete 10
- 11.2.5 Remix - Legion - Journey - Legion Assaults Complete 10

Reward: Vertical Felsteel Chain


Players who require a group because their Infinite Power is low should still have group capabilities, but those who just want to get in and out, or challenge themselves, would like the option to be able to solo this content.
What do you think? Should Blizzard allow players to attempt to solo this kind of content? Let us know your thoughts in the comments down below!

title: Abyss Anglers: New Withered Army Style Event In Midnight
author: POSTED 2025/12/04 AT 12:03 AM BY WHISPYR
content:
The Midnight Beta has been datamined, and there are new details about a mysterious Abyss Angler event filled with achievements and upgrades to earn. This new World Event was added off the coast of Zul'Aman, but it cannot be adequately tested at the moment due to numerous bugs.

In the new build, there were new Achievements that could shed some more light on the event and what it might look like going forward.
WHAT IS THE ABYSS ANGLER EVENT
The Abyss Angler Event could possibly be a new world event that revolves around catching a variety of fish, diving for treasure, and exploring the ocean depths. Along the way, Depthdiver Jeju will help players with upgrades that allow for longer dives with the Nalorakk's Breath Tank, or even unlock new weapons such as the Heavy Harpoon Cannon.

During the event, players' skills are disabled and replaced with a vehicle interface which features, in order, a dash skill, the harpoon skill, a melee attack, using consumables found during the dive, and exiting the event.


There is also a simple meter to show the remaining oxygen. Oxygen is consumed slowly over time, but is also used for some skills. When oxygen reaches zero, the event immediately ends.
EXPLORING THE ABYSS
Currently, casting the Harpoon skill immediately disconnects the player, and there are various issues surrounding the dive suit that make progressing through the event impossible. Through some of the associated achievements on the beta, we might be able to get an idea of what the event is intended to look like.
