title: China to Remove Unearned Gladiator and Raid Mounts in Upcoming Review
author: POSTED 2025/11/22 AT 10:57 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
China continues to crack down on piloted sales, with their World of Warcraft Operations Team announcing another 2,673 characters penalized for account violations over the last week, alongside another 125 manipulating scores and cheating in PvP! Additionally, the team has announced that unearned Mythic Dimensius and Gladiator mounts will be removed by season's end.

 1,820 Season 2 Titles Removed
 2,459 Characters Actioned

 12,994 Characters Penalized
 30,000 More in One Week


These numbers have slow down week-to-week as Lei Huo continues their campaign for fairness, removing ill-gotten gear and issuing suspensions to repeat offenders, with more groups swapping to western-style carry sales instead. So far, there's no indication that the operations team is particularly interested in going after these players, but they do significantly slow the pace of sales, since only so many characters can be carried at a time.
World of Warcraft Chinese Server Operations Team (translated)
View Original
During the third season of The War Within, the Chinese server operations team cracked down on account sharing activities such as PvE/PvP boosting and account sharing, as well as PvP cheating, score boosting, account boosting, and intentional sabotage. The corresponding penalties have now been imposed on the aforementioned violations.

From November 13th to November 20th, a total of 2673 characters violated regulations in The War Within Mythic raids. Last week, 744 characters illegally redeemed Mythic equipment from the corresponding Turbo Boost raid. We will reclaim this equipment and continue to conduct rolling checks and reclaim related equipment. Additionally, at the end of this season, we will conduct a unified review and reclaim of the mount "Unbound Star-Eater" obtained through violations from the Mythic difficulty Dimensius raid. Specific penalties are as follows:
Mythic difficulty Manaforge: Mythic gear picked up in Omega will be reclaimed, and Unbound Star-Eater dropped in Mythic difficulty Dimensius will be reclaimed at the end of the season.
Mythic gear obtained from the first raid row of the Great Vault will be retroactively reclaimed. Furthermore, for players who have not yet claimed their loot, the penalty will be rolled over until all loot is reclaimed.
Mythic quality Turbo Boost equipment obtained from group dungeons will be continuously reclaimed.
All of the aforementioned offenders will be subject to additional account bans.
From November 13th to November 19th, a total of 125 characters were found to have violated regulations in Rated Battlegrounds, Arenas, Solo Blitz, and Blitz modes. Some of these players severely disrupted the gaming experience of others through actions including, but not limited to, intentional sabotage, score manipulation, and cheating. We will permanently freeze these players' Battle.net accounts. Specific penalties are as follows:
The aforementioned offenders will be banned from the competition this season, and some players who commit serious violations will have their Battle.net accounts permanently frozen.
Mounts: Astral Gladiator's Felbat and Toys: Astral Legend Banner will be recycled, and the player will be disqualified from obtaining the title at the end of the season.
Some characters will be subject to bans, and these bans will be applied on a rolling basis. Please note that if one character violates the rules, other characters under the same Battle.net account will also be unable to receive the aforementioned PvP rewards this season.
In addition to the aforementioned key targets, the Chinese server operations team once again solemnly reminds everyone:
Any form of account sharing, account boosting, or account transfer is illegal.
We will continue to investigate and punish any violations that attempt to evade punishment through special means. Please do not take chances!
Have fun playing the game!

You can click the button below to view the list of penalties for this violation.

 PVE Penalty List
 PVP Penalty List
[/quote]

title: Datamining Hints at New Secret in Midnight - Join the Secretive Cabal of the Mind-Seekers!
author: POSTED 2025/11/21 AT 4:33 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
Recent datamining points to a potential new secret in Midnight. Join the secretive cabal of the Mind-Seekers as we explore mysterious achievements, NPCs, zones, and more!

Midnight Overview
Midnight Overview
WoW Secret Guides
WoW Secret Guides
Table of Contents
Who Are the Mind-Seekers?
New Mind-Seeker Datamining in Midnight
Achievement & Title
NPCs
Spells & Zone
Where Could the Secret Begin?
WHO ARE THE MIND-SEEKERS?
For the uninitiated, Secrets in WoW are hidden mind-bending puzzles that often require the involvement of the community in order to solve. Thousands of players scour the far corners of Azeroth in hopes of unearthing the next step often being rewarded with an exclusive mount, pet, or transmog upon completion.

One secret from Legion, the Riddle of the Mind-Seekers required deciphering clues from various pages across Azeroth that were directly connected to World of Warcraft: Chronicle Volume I. Upon completion players were awarded the Riddler's Mind-Worm from a mysterious chest, the Gift of the Mind-Seekers.
Riddler's Mind-Worm
Item Level 14
Binds when picked up
Unique
Mount (Account-wide)
Use: Teaches you how to summon this mount. This is a flying mount.
Summons and dismisses your Riddler's Mind-Worm.
Requires level 1 to 90 90
Requires Apprentice Riding
Sell Price: 1

"It feeds on your thoughts, even the ones you are thinking as you read this."
Drop: Gift of the Mind-Seekers



While not expressly stated, it's often assumed that the 'Mind-Seekers' are the ones responsible for the Secrets. In the behind-the-scenes blog for Riddler's Mind Worm, the Mind-Seekers address players directly taunting them about their inevitable return.
View Original
As a final note, the Mind-Seekers would like us to let you know that they will be seeing you again soon—and that your thoughts are delicious.
The Riddle of the Mind-Seekers
The Riddle of the Mind-Seekers
NEW MIND-SEEKER DATAMINING IN MIDNIGHT
Over the course of Midnight development, various datamining has suggested that we may learn more about the mysterious Mind-Seekers in Midnight, but may also officially join their ranks.
MIND-SEEKER TITLE & FEAT OF STRENGTH ACHIEVEMENT
In particular, the Mind-Seeker feat of strength suggests players will "join the secretive cabal of the Mind-Seekers" with the criteria being a particular quest of which the details remain hidden or unknown.

Mind-Seeker
Account-wide progress

Joined the secretive cabal of the Mind-Seekers.

- Quest #94828


If we're to join this mysterious organization, then perhaps we would don the Mind-Seeker %s title. While details about how or even if players will be able to obtain this new title, a number of new NPCs do sport this new identifier.
JEREMY FEASEL, RIFTAH & MIND-SEEKER NPCS
Jeremy Feasel

While not the first time we've encountered the NPC Jeremy Feasel in-game, this is the first time he's sporting the <Mind-Seeker> title. We last saw this NPC named after the WoW developer Jeremy Feasel during the latest Indecipherable Felcycle Secret which eerily left off on a bit of a cliffhanger.


Riftah

Riftah on the other hand is likely a reference to Griftah, the <Amazing Amulets> vendor and legendary Hearthstone card who frequently shows up every expansion sporting amulets with abilities you ironically already have. What could a similarly named NPC be doing with the Mind-Seekers?
 

Anakron

Much less is known about Anakron though particularly observant players might recognize a similarly garbed NPC known as the Hooded Supplier in MoP and Legion Remix. What this mysterious merchant sells remains unknown especially considering his unusual Unfriendly status.



Interacting with the Hooded Supplier does offer some unique dialogue about his identity and origins:
<The mysterious figure sighs deeply>

Very well. My name is Anakron. I am involved in the cabal known as the Mind-Seekers. There are those who would employ me for my skills in cross-timeline trade. When I have no pressing matters, I entertain myself with the phenomenological, crafting conundrums for those who are willing to find them. You will say no more and tell no one of this. Our business here is finished.
  
As a cross-timeline merchant, his name does indeed appear to be a reference to an anachronism which is something that doesn't belong in the time in which it appears. Beyond this interaction we don't know much else, but we can be certain the Mind-Seekers have been keeping an eye on us for some time.
SANCTUARY OF THE MIND-SEEKER ZONE & SPELLS
Where might we encounter these new NPCs or join the Mind-Seeker cabal? Why the (Zone #16806) of course! Little is known about this zone with no associated map or related data, but it sounds like a good location for a secret cabal.

Additionally, five new spells were recently added all with the same name that might be associated with a new secret:
Mind-Seeker
Mind-Seeker
Mind-Seeker
Mind-Seeker
Mind-Seeker
WHERE COULD THE SECRET BEGIN?
Based on the discovery of a new Jeremy Feasel NPC, it's quite likely this new Secret could pick up where the last one left off. We last encountered Jeremy Feasel during the Indecipherable Felcycle Secret which ends rather abruptly without completing Ratt's full story.

Although the penultimate step Felcycle secret awarded players with the mount, many things suggested the secret was not over. An alternative interpretation of the final code, 84847078, gives the code "TTFN," a common abbreviation for "Ta-ta for now!"



Incognitro, the Indecipherable Felcycle Secret
Incognitro, the Indecipherable Felcycle Secret
Will we finally meet the Mind-Seekers and join their ranks in Midnight? If we become Mind-Seekers, does that mean we'll actively be involved in the creation of the next Secret?

title: Today's Test of Midnight Pre-Launch Event Delayed Till Early December
author: POSTED 2025/11/21 AT 12:39 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
Due to unforseen issues, today's rescheduled 'Play with the Blues' Midnight Pre-Launch test has been delayed again till early December.
Blizzard
View Original
Update
Due to new unforseen issues, we’re forced to delay today’s test.

We will reschedule this test for early December.
This Friday, November 21, please join members of the World of Warcraft development team in the Twilight Highlands on the “Midnight Prelaunch Event” realm to playtest the upcoming Midnight pre-launch event, where we’ll learn more about the Twilight’s Blade with the help of Magister Umbric and Grand Magister Rommath.

The experience will begin with the quest “The Cult Within” in Stormwind if you’re Alliance or in Orgrimmar if you’re Horde, and will eventually lead all players to the Twilight Highlands in the Eastern Kingdoms. The event in the Twilight Highlands has a rapid cadence, and should appear in the Event tracker, which you can view by opening your Map and clicking on the Events tab that appears when the map is minimized. (Default Hotkey: M)
Event Details
Date: Friday, November 21
Start: 11:00 PST (14:00 EST, 20:00 CET)
End: 13:00 PST (16:00 EST, 22:00 CET)
Realm: Midnight Prelaunch Event
The realm will become available when the event begins and will remain open after the event ends for a short period of time for those who still want to test out the pre-launch event.
Requirements: Copy a character from the live servers to participate in the test.
Before we begin, please take some time to get familiar with changes made to your class and specialization. There have been some significant changes to talents, hero talents, and core class abilities.

We look forward to playing with you and reading your feedback! See you there, champions!

title: WoW Weekly: Legion Remix Phase 4, BC Classic Pre-Patch, 21st Anniversary & More
author: POSTED 2025/11/21 AT 12:02 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
This Week in Wow, Legion Remix Phase 4 begins with the addition of Argus and Antorus. Be sure to check out the 21st WoW Anniversary celebration, the upcoming Burning Crusade Classic announcement, and so much more.
Blizzard
View Original
The Burning Throne awaits you in Legion Remix, hop over to Tanaris for the 21st Anniversary in-game event fun, prepare for the WoW®: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition Pre-Expansion Patch arriving January 13, and more! Every week brings thrilling new adventures and loot, so rally your allies and forge your legend in Azeroth.
Legion Remix: Argus Eternal Now Live!
Get ready for fast frenetic fun in Legion Remix as you meet up with the infinite dragonflight once more, level new characters, and earn a variety of items to add to your collection.

Replay the Argus campaign across three deadly zones. Take on the Seat of the Triumvirate and Antorus, the Burning Throne.

Legion Remix is available to everyone, and no expansion purchase is needed, but a World of Warcraft Subscription or Game Time is required to begin this fast-paced adventure through the Broken Isles. This means that Classic players can also participate in the Fel frenzy by simply installing the modern (live) World of Warcraft client.

Head over to our previously published article to learn more. We can’t wait to see you on the Broken Isles in Legion Remix!
Join the World of Warcraft 21st Anniversary Celebration
Join the celebration as World of Warcraft celebrates its 21st Anniversary with a variety of activities and a myriad of ways to earn currency to purchase a plethora of items—and this year, you can don the intrepid threads of the Loyal Watchman ensemble.

When : November 16–December 7
Where : Caverns of Time in Tanaris

Grab the quest from any Holiday Celebrants and hitch a ride to the Caverns of Time through portals located in Orgrimmar and Stormwind.

Joyous Journeys also abound for Mists of Pandaria Classic and WoW Classic Anniversary Edition Realms:

Mists of Pandaria Classic: November 18–December 9

The mystical lands of Pandaria are swirling with danger. For our stalwart champions in World of Warcraft Classic progression (currently Mists of Pandaria Classic), take advantage of the Joyous Journey’s experience buff to enhance your odyssey through Azeroth, increasing experience gains by 50% up to level 90!

WoW Classic Anniversary Edition Realms: November 16–23

Take advantage of the Unrelenting Rallying Cry of the Dragonslayer buff with an increase to critical chance to your spells by 10%, melee, and ranged by 5% and grants 140 attack power. This buff does not stack with Rallying Cry of the Dragonslayer and cannot be Chronobooned.

Learn more about this celebration—including rewards and available quests—from our previously published article.
WoW®: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition Pre-Patch Arrives January 13
Prepare to tread once again through the Dark Portal and into the fel-scarred realm of Outland with the WoW®: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition, launching early next year and included with your WoW Subscription or Game Time.

Beginning January 13, after each regional scheduled maintenance, the Pre-expansion Patch will be released, and each character must choose whether to advance onward to WoW: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition or move to a Classic Era realm.

For those Classic Anniversary players who do not want their characters to continue to WoW: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition, we will open up free character transfers from Anniversary Realms to Classic Era realms for a limited time starting November 25. This choice to move Classic Anniversary characters to Classic Era realms will last until January 12, 2026.

After January 12, 2026, the transfer option will no longer be available, and you will not be able to transfer your Classic Anniversary characters to Classic Era realms. Any characters remaining on Classic Anniversary realms will continue to Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary.

Learn more about what else is coming with Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition from our previously published article.
Enhance Your WoW®: Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition Experience
Are you prepared to tread once again through the Dark Portal and into the Fel-scarred realm of Outland? The WoW Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition pre-expansion patch arrives on January 13. As with WoW Classic, Burning Crusade Classic Anniversary Edition will be included with every WoW player’s subscription or Game Time at no additional cost.

Harness the power of the Nether and embark on your adventure with item-filled packs that include Burning Crusade-themed cosmetics, mounts, pets, and more!

Outland Epic Pack: includes everything in the Heroic Bundle, along with a Level 58 Character Boost (Anniversary) and 30 Days of World of Warcraft Game Time.

Outland Heroic Pack: includes a Netherdrake Flying Mount, a Phase-Hunter Ground Mount, a Whelping Pet, an Exodar Toy, and a Naaru-themed Hearthstone Effect for various WoW collections, and more!
Choose Your Edition
Take a Ride on Turbulent Timeways Starting December 2
Once more, the Bronze Dragonflight has discovered growing disturbances in the timeways, with several rapidly intersecting our own!

When : December 2–February 3

Timewalk through a previous expansion each week for 10 consecutive weeks, beginning and ending with the Shadowlands. This grand tour through World of Warcraft's past delivers six new Timewalking Dungeons for players to undertake—De Other Side, Spires of Ascension, Plaguefall, Necrotic Wake, Sanguine Depths, and Halls of Atonement.

Master the Timeways for a new Mount! This season, you'll be granted Ta’readon’s Mount Voucher for completing the “Master of the Turbulent Timeways IV” achievement awarded to those distinguished champions who maintain “Mastery of the Timeways” for any five weeks during the event.

The voucher will grant the “Ta’readon’s Mount Voucher” quest, directing you to Overseer Ta’readon in the Hall of Curiosities, where you’ll choose one of the four Shadowlands mounts—Bronze Wilderling, Bronze Corpsefly, Bronze Gravewing, or Bronze Aquilon—for free, and the ability to purchase the other three mounts from the Timewalking vendor for Timewarped Badges.

To learn more about Turbulent Timeways, including a dungeon schedule and list of rewards, read through our previously published article.
Mists of Pandaria Classic: The Thunder King Arrives December 9
Gather heroes, sound the drums, the Thunder King comes! The time is nigh to spearhead an assault on the Thunder King’s stronghold. This update delivers two new zones—Isle of Thunder and Isle of Giants, a new gear upgrade system—Thunderforging, a new legendary chapter, two new world bosses—Oondasta and Nalak, and the Throne of Thunder 12-encounter raid.

When : December 9 at 3:00 pm PST

In addition, this content update ushers in the continuation of the Legendary questline— continue your journey with Wrathion to earn a Legendary meta gem, introduces new Scenarios, an update to the Challenge Mode system, and includes the latest updates to Celestial Dungeons.

The Throne of Thunder content update is currently being tested on the PTR—feel free to join. Until then, head over to our previously published overview for all the details about the next content update coming to Mists of Pandaria Classic.
Now Available! World of Warcraft: Blood Ties by Christie Golden
Officially licensed by Blizzard Entertainment, World of Warcraft: Blood Ties is an original prequel novel to the highly anticipated World of Warcraft: Midnight game expansion, the second chapter in the Worldsoul Saga.
Read the Chapter Excerpt
Written by revered World of Warcraft author Christie Golden, this novel chronicles the journey of Arator the Redeemer, son of High Exarch Turalyon and the legendary Alleria Windrunner, as he embarks on a journey investigating rumors of a strange glow emanating from the ruins of a long abandoned Legion base, which leads him to uncover more about himself, his parents, and their heroic legacy.

World of Warcraft®: Blood Ties is now available wherever books are sold.
Get Your Copy Now
Now Available on the Shop: The Trader’s Gilded Brutosaur Mount
“Morten and Killia never realized how much their new trading partnership across faction lines would pay off. That is until the day they were able to purchase this fine creature to aid them in their travels. And of course do it in gilded style.”
Celebrate camaraderie between the Alliance and the Horde with the return of the awe-inspiring Trader’s Gilded Brutosaur, adorned with an Anniversary themed harness encrusted with gems representing World of Warcraft’s expansions. Brave the world of Azeroth on this magnificent Zandalari beast with Morten and Killia, bringing the auction house and the mail to you on the go!

Add this new mount to your collection by visiting the Battle.net Shop or in-game shop through January 5, 2026.
Visit the Shop
Don’t forget you can invest in your new gilded age with WoW Tokens. WoW Tokens purchased from the Auction house for gold can be redeemed for Battle.net Balance. Learn more in our support article.
Let us adventure alongside you, regaling you with tales of valor, casting a light on epic loot, and directing your eyes to legendary raids. Return each week to the World of Warcraft news site, where together, we’ll forge your destinies in Azeroth and beyond. Lok'tar Ogar!

title: Join the Housing Early Access Celebration with Warcraft Cares
author: POSTED 2025/11/21 AT 6:20 AM BY JEZARTROZ
content:
Updated 12/3/2025: There are some exciting updates to the Early Access Celebration - check out Dravvie's update below!
IMPORTANT UPDATES:
Based on player feedback we are opting to extend the deadline for signup period from November 28th to December 10th!
This will give you 14 days to complete your homes!
We are updating the stream Judging portion of the event dates to December 16th and 17th, to align with other fun events Warcraft has going on during December!
If you cannot make it those days do not fret! You can still win! Full schedule updates coming soon!


Warcraft Cares is hosting a block party - and you're invited! In a partnership with the World of Warcraft team, the launch of Player Housing will kick off with a bang: two completely filled neighborhoods showing off featured homes for both Alliance and Horde.

Similar to Wowhead's recent Housing Gallery Sweepstakes, players will be able to win some soon-to-be-announced prizes, show off their home designs, and celebrate the launch of one of the most requested features in World of Warcraft to date.

Dravvie of Warcraft Cares revealed all the details of the celebration in a post on the WoW Forums, as seen below - if you're interested in signing up, applicants will still be accepted until November 28th, so don't delay!

 Warcraft Cares Housing EA Celebration Sign-Up Sheet
Dravvie said:
Link
Hi everyone! Dravvie of Warcraft Cares here! I’m so excited to be finally sharing this!
I am partnering with the World of Warcraft team to host a Neighborhood Block Party in December, when Early Access Housing goes live.

We want to see all of your homes and provide a venue for everyone to gather and celebrate the launch of housing!

You can sign up to be one of our up to 100 homes featured during the event via the form below.

The form will be active until 10:00 am PST on the 28th of November so don’t delay replying!
Housing Event Sign Up Form
Event Info
What: A housing contest and Neighborhood Block Parties that celebrate the wide variety of things players can do with housing.
When: Submissions are open now until the end of November for our Neighborhood Block Parties, which will take place from December 19th to 21st in-game.
Where: Founder’s Point and Razorwind Shores.
How: Submit your designs in this form and bring your home to our neighborhoods for the week!
Who can apply: Anyone with a NA account
How many people will be selected to show off their homes?: Up to 50 people Alliance and up to 50 people 50 Horde Side.
Any home? YES! Show us all of your creative ideas! We want to see the silly, the cool, the platformers, the themed, the magical, the “how did they do that!”, the NPC-style homes and shops! We genuinely want to see everything you are creating!
Selection and Judging
Anyone is welcome to apply: all applicants have an equal chance of being selected. Your home will not be chosen based on your popularity or how well-known you are; the homes selected will be based on the merit of creativity.
What do I win?: Both physical and digital prizes. Every player selected, as well as our alternates, will receive a prize.
Judges: Special guest judges will pick their favorites of various categories, and award some very cool IRL prizes on two live streams while touring both neighborhoods!
Fan Judging: We won’t just be letting our Guest Judges decide on homes, though! We’re going to open up community judging, allowing everyone to vote on their favorite homes and award additional prizes and awards!
This early access housing contest will serve a multitude of purposes:
It will help showcase a wide variety of what players can do with their homes, and give players who now have access to housing and are stuck in the “now what” stage lots of fundamental, tangible ideas that they can use.
Plus the Block Parties will allow them to walk through and see fun ways they can use their homes, not just browse picture on a website.
Plus, there will be YOU! Our chosen “House Designers” that they can talk to about how you did such extraordinary things in person!
An opportunity to gather people who are highly creative with this new feature together, and is a possibility for us to create a record for people to use for future inspiration. For example, walk-through videos, interviews about the different items you use in your homes, how and the tips you have and so on.
A fun way to celebrate a feature all of us have been longing for many years with a unique community gathering around the end of the year
Anyone is welcome to apply, and all applicants have an equal chance of being selected. Your home will not be selected based on your popularity or how well known you are; the chosen homes will be based on the merit of creativity and presenting the widest variety of homes across two neighborhoods.
Bonus:
The event continues to spotlight Warcraft Care’s efforts this winter to raise funds for Feeding America and The Global Foodbanking Network.
I only have my PTR/beta house designs right now:
We know! Show us your ideas of what you’re planning to build for the event!
As long as you don’t go wildly off course once you show up, we’ll be judging your submissions based on that!
If you DO make changes once things are live that are enhancements to your submission, it’s okay!
If you make considerable, massive changes, ie, an entire theme change, contact us, and we’ll check how your designs impact others. We’ll check how your designs impact others that we may be placing you near!
There will be additional details on the form about what we consider to be extreme changes, as opposed to expected ones so you aren’t stressing about the difference
Player Requirements:
Players must follow the in-game code of conduct during the event and any events surrounding it.
Because the social aspect of the event, where those selected get to show off their houses will be open to anyone with a World of Warcraft account and will be streamed and shared on various social platforms by us and many players visiting the event, we ask that you do your best to keep everything family friendly and below the teen rating on the game’s box.
We know that you will encounter many different types of players during the event, and we will provide assistance. However, being able to show patience and kindness to others is a must.
Additionally, we expect players to maintain good standing both online and in their respective communities.
We expect those selected to demonstrate a history of good sportsmanship towards the other players.
Additionally, being aware of how their chat communications are visible in-game, including addons, and utilizing these communications appropriately and in the same family-friendly manner.
Changes to plot assignments should be made through organizers, not directly from player to player, to avoid players feeling pressured to switch.
If players are under 18, we won’t reject you/your home, but please let us know so we can make appropriate arrangements for your sake.
Event Timeline:
Houses will be selected during the Alpha, PTR, and Beta Periods. The Beta will be opening up on November 11th, 2025, for players with any copy of Midnight.
Submissions will be selected via screenshots, and not via GIFs or videos, to keep submissions equitable. Screenshots should not be edited to the best of the user’s abilities.
Applications will close at the end of November ahead of the December 2nd patch, and participants and backup participants will be announced by December 1st-ish.
These participants will build their homes on live servers in their public, guild, or charter neighborhoods and then move into the two Horde and Alliance themed “party neighborhoods” the week before the events on the nights of the 14th and 15th.
Players will have from December 14th/15th (depending on their move in day) to December 18th to complete their yard décor to their liking based upon their assigned plots, and make any internal adjustments as well. If any alternates need to be switched in during this time, we will do so.
December 19-21 will be the days of the Neighborhood Block Parties, with the Horde and Alliance each getting an evening of focus on our streams and one night being just for enjoyment. Fan voting will open, and the judges on stream will pick their favorites from several categories. Times TBD, will be in the afternoon/evening PST.
December 22-23, we may take some screenshots before you move back out, especially of the winners, but mainly we’ll be giving out awards to winners and celebrating how much fun it was to party with everyone!!
FAQ
What if I can’t attend? Will I/My home still be eligible to win? Yes! We know you may have to work some/all of the nights. We just ask that you leave your home open to visitors. The question about availability has no impact on your selection, just giving us a heads up on finding an alternative rout to allowing people to ask you questions about your home if you’re open to it.
What kind of Prizes? Updating soon, but we’re really excited about them!
Who are the judges? We’re still loading the list of judges, but some of them include: Layria, Meggoart, MissMarvel & Shojo_AIE.
Housing Event Sign Up Form

title: Turbulent Timeways Returns December 2nd - Bronze Covenant Mounts, Heroic Caches & Shadowlands Timewalking
author: POSTED 2025/11/20 AT 12:06 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
Turbulent Timeways returns from December 2nd to February 3rd. Explore the new Shadowlands Timewalking, earn 4 new Bronze Covenant Mounts, and gear up with Heroic Manaforge Omega Caches!

Patch 11.2.7 Overview
Patch 11.2.7 Overview
Turbulent Timeways Guide
Turbulent Timeways Guide
Blizzard
View Original
Once more, the Bronze Dragonflight has discovered growing disturbances in the timeways, with several rapidly intersecting our own!

When : December 2–February 3

Timewalk through a previous expansion each week for 10 consecutive weeks, beginning and ending with the Shadowlands. This grand tour through World of Warcraft's past delivers six new Timewalking Dungeons for players to undertake—De Other Side, Spires of Ascension, Plaguefall, Necrotic Wake, Sanguine Depths, and Halls of Atonement.

Players can also shop at a new Shadowlands vendor, Collector Ta’steld, located in the Ring of Fates inside Oribos, just outside the Hall of Curiosities.

Also, for a limited time, the Timewalking quest available from the weekly quest giver will offer an increased reward—a Heroic-level Cache of Kareshi Treasures awarded to players at item level 694. These upgraded rewards are only available during this special event.
Time to Buff Up
Those who run any Timewalking dungeon during the event get the “Knowledge of the Timeways” buff, granting a 5% increase to experience when killing monsters and completing quests.

Completing Timewalking dungeons increases the effect and also extends its duration. This is a stacking buff, and after four applications, it transforms into “Mastery of the Timeways,” increasing the experience gained from killing monsters and completing quests to 30%.

The buff lasts through death and is account-wide, which makes for an excellent opportunity to level up any alts.
Master the Timeways for a New Mount
This season, you'll be granted Ta’readon’s Mount Voucher for completing the “Master of the Turbulent Timeways IV” achievement awarded to those distinguished champions who maintain “Mastery of the Timeways” for any five weeks during the event.

The voucher will grant the “Ta’readon’s Mount Voucher” quest, directing you to Overseer Ta’readon in the Hall of Curiosities, where you’ll choose one of the four Shadowlands mounts—Bronze Wilderling, Bronze Corpsefly, Bronze Gravewing, or Bronze Aquilon—for free, and the ability to purchase the other three mounts from the Timewalking vendor for Timewarped Badges. The Covenant restrictions for the Shadowlands mounts have been lifted. For example, if you choose a Venthyr Mount (Bronze Gravewing) but are not part of the Venthyr Covenant faction, you will still be able to ride this mount.
Bronze Gravewing Mount
Don’t Let Time Pass You By
Timewalking dungeons have a chance to drop items that usually drop when you run them on Heroic, and you'll earn Reputation with a faction associated with the dungeon. Enemies and loot will automatically scale to your level.

While Timewalking is active, you will gain a 50% bonus to reputations earned from quests and creature kills for all reputations related to the corresponding expansion.

To get to any one of the dungeons, open the Group Finder (default hotkey: “I”) and then select Dungeon Finder and Timewalking in the “Type” dropdown menu. Selecting “Find Group” will match you with other players and send you to one of the available Heroic Dungeons.

Blizzard
View Original
Outfit slots should be warband wide.

In a future build of the Midnight Beta, the outfit slot unlocks will become Warband-wide. And costs to unlock these spots will not go up.

title: It Costs Up to 5,700 Gold to Save a Full Set of Transmog to an Outfit Slot in Midnight
author: POSTED 2025/11/20 AT 9:44 AM BY SQUISHEI
content:
The transmog system in Midnight has outfit slots for purchase, but it still costs 5,700 Gold at level 90 to save a transmog to each slot.

Blizzard has announced that the costs for unlocking Outfit slots will be changing. This section is now outdated.
In Midnight, there are two types of Transmog costs:
Purchasing Outfit slots
Assigning a set to an Outfit slot
Players will still be able to create outfits like they do with the current transmog system under a system called "Customs Sets". Custom Sets are free to use, but equipping an outfit in Midnight will require you to assign it to an "outfit slot".
Purchasing Outfit Slots
Outfits slots allow you to swap between all the different assigned loadouts for free. Swapping between Outfit slots is as easy as pressing a button on your action bar and after a brief animation, your character will have a new transmog!



Outfit slots can also be assigned to automatically swap in various scenarios using the "situations" system. Want to swap when you're in a certain spec, location or even mounted? You can.

If you only use one or two transmogs, the free outfit slots might be good enough for you. Outfit slots come with a bonus -- when you upgrade your gear, you won't need to transmog them at all which will be fantastic for leveling and at the start of a Season.

If you want to buy more Outfit slots, they start rather cheap at 500 gold, but quickly scale in price up to 100k gold.
Costs of Outfit Slots
Slot # Gold Cost
1 Free
2 Free
3 100
4 500
5 1,000
6 5,000
7 10,000
8 15,000
9 20,000
10 25,000
11 30,000
12 40,000
13 50,000
14 60,000
15 70,000
16 80,000
17 90,000
18 100,000
19 100,000
20 100,000
Saving Outfit Slots
In order to use any transmog, you need to "save" it onto an Outfit Slot. If you want to create bunch of transmogs, you're able to do that for free -- but in order to see them on your character in game, you must save them to an Outfit Slot.

Saving an Outfit slot is very similar to live, you pick what you want and then you pay gold based on the slots you're changing. You can continue to change it, but you will need to pay every time you change it. However, once a set is saved to an Outfit slot, it's free to swap between Outfit slots.

Saving an outfit slot seems to cost up to 5,700 gold for a full set.

Live Costs of Saving Transmogs
It's first important to note that costs of transmog scales with level, even on the live game. At level 73, a full set of transmog costs ~200 gold.

 

Comparing this to a level 80 on live servers, which costs around ~930 gold. There is slight variance in costs.

  Midnight Costs of Saving Transmogs
For a level 80 in the new Midnight Transmog system, a full set costs ~4,084 gold which is around a 4.4x as much gold. This increases to 5,700 gold for a level 90 character.

  

If you want to save some gold, you'll want to make sure to get save your transmogs to Outfit Slots at the lowest level possible.
Are You Saving Gold with the Midnight Transmog System?
The big question for a lot of people is are you saving gold? That's really hard to answer and will change based on each player's individual habits. We can however take some common examples and see how it would effect them.

We'll do rough calculations for level 80 -- 930 gold for a full set on live, 4084 gold for a full set on beta with the Outfit system.

And remember this is per character as the system is NOT account-wide.
One-Transmog
If you only use one-transmog on a character and never change, this system looks to be neutral at worst or a better case scenario for you. In Midnight, you will not need to buy any additional Outfit slots and you will only need to pay a one-time cost of ~4,084 gold for your initial saving of your transmog, totaling just over 4k gold.

In the current live system, you only pay 930 gold up front, but every single time you do a full swap out of your gear, you are paying an additional 930 gold. This means that after 5 gear swaps, you have overpaid the amount you would pay in the new system. I assume most people will replace their gear at least once while leveling, and at least once per season making you break even after the end of Season 3 of Midnight. The longer you play, the more times you change your gear out, the more money you're saving in the long run.
Two-Transmog
The two-transmog user is very similar, except with a higher up front cost. No additional outfit slots needed, but a slightly higher up front cost. Considering how often you would typically swap transmogs, you'll quickly save much more gold in the long term because the savings stack up when you would swap your gear or transmogs.
Multi-Transmog
This is where it gets much more complicated and is going to be dependent on the user. The choice here is buying more outfit slots or paying the save fee multiple times. The choice here comes down to how often you are going to be saving over an Outfit slot and if that cost is going to be more than just buying a new Outfit slot outright. If you're going to swap 50k on saving loadouts in the next year, and the next Outfit slot is 500g, chances are you should probably just buy the Outfit slot. The first few additional outfit slots are very cheap and should usually be the choice for players.

There's also a level of convenience that occurs -- if you have a lot of situations that you want transmogs for, you might just like the ability to swap automatically every time.

title: Fast Track Darkfuse Solutions Reputation During WoW's 21st Anniversary
author: POSTED 2025/11/20 AT 8:15 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
One of the biggest reputation grinds of The War Within is getting a little bit easier, as several events converge to substantially boost the amount of Darkfuse Solutions rep you can earn over the next few weeks! So, if you're still working towards the Darkfuse Lowdown Coat or searching for the Bronze Goblin Waveshredder, this is the perfect time to be rewarded for your efforts.

Unlock Darkfuse Solutions Reputation
Unlock Darkfuse Solutions Reputation
The Cartels of Undermine Renown Guide
The Cartels of Undermine Renown Guide


Although these bonuses apply to any faction you still may be working on, Darkfuse Solutions is semi-unique in that the bulk of its reputation is earned through turn-ins rather than activity completion. This gives players the opportunity to spend the next couple weeks farming rare mobs and S.C.R.A.P. events to stockpile Market Research, holding their turn-ins until events overlap to substantially reduce the amount of farming required.
WOW'S 21ST ANNIVERSARY
From now through December 7th, all players automatically gain 10% extra reputation from WoW's Anniversary, which can be increased even further by completing Celebrate Good Fun! each day for Blessings of the Bronze Dragonflight.


Get at least 10% just for logging in, though the daily quest is also very quick to finish.
PILGRIM'S BOUNTY
From November 23rd through November 30th, the The Spirit of Sharing will grant another 10% increased reputation. Gained by sampling five of each food from a bountiful table located in any capital city, it's a good option for players who already have currency farmed up and ready to be turned in.

THE DARKMOON FAIRE
Those who already done farming may want to wait until December 7th. For one morning only , players can stack the Anniversary reputation bonus on top of the Darkmoon Faire. There's about a 10-hour period between the Darkmoon Faire beginning overnight and the Anniversary ending on Sunday morning, so save your turn-ins for another +10% reputation from either WHEE! or Darkmoon Top Hat.


Saving your turn-ins for this short window in which the Darkmoon Faire and Anniversary overlap can gain a lot of extra reputation!
MISSING WINDS OF MYSTERIOUS FORTUNE
Originally set to return with the launch of Patch 11.2.7 on December 2nd, the Winds of Mysterious Fortune buff offers another huge increase in reputation gained, however, the Winds have since been removed from the roadmap.


Original Image
New Image


It's possible this was an oversight, though between Legion Remix, the 21st Anniversary, and Turbulent Timeways, it seems more likely that Blizzard decided Winds on top would be too much of a good thing all at once. We'll just have to wait and see whether the winds return, but it's probably not worth counting on.

Table of Contents

Mount Reward

Account-wide progress

These achievements were originally intended to be done as a group, and while you can solo some of these achievements, it can be tedious to do so. With Legion Remix, this presents the perfect opportunity to host / join any achievement groups who might be hunting the same ones as yourself.

There are a few notable achievements that can be made easier with a group or simply cannot be solo'd:
Not For You - Simply burn the boss before this mechanic casts.
Buggy Fight - More people to squish bugs during Heart of the Storm cast.
Infinitesimal - This one requires having a Infinite Whelpling battle pet.

I Attack the Darkness - DH's Spectral Sight and Warlock's Eye of Kilrogg simplifies this achievement greatly.
MOUNT REWARD
Completing the Glory of the Legion Raider achievement drops the Defiled Reins, a Nightmare-corrupted moose.

author: POSTED 2025/11/20 AT 3:28 AM BY IMX3
content:
Completing previous expansion meta achievements is no easy feat, especially with Glory of the Legion Hero. Thankfully, players can use Legion Remix to assist with their achievement hunting!

This meta achievement requires players to do a ton of unique challenges throughout the dungeons of Legion on Mythic Difficulty.

While many of these achievements can be done solo, some of them are painful, often requiring interesting strategies, certain classes, or specific utility. For example, I Got What You Mead can be done solo, but is much easier to do with a coordinated group. Why throw four mugs, when four people can just throw one?

Thanks to Legion Remix, this is a great opportunity to host / join a group of players looking to complete some of these achievements.

Who's Afraid of the Dark? - No one picks up Elune's Light and everyone just burns boss.

title: What Are Invasion Points in Legion Remix?
author: POSTED 2025/11/19 AT 10:21 PM BY IMX3
content:
With Phase 4 of Legion Remix, Invasion Points make their return on Argus. Here's what you need to know:
Table of Contents
Unlocking Invasion Points
What Are Invasion Points?
Achievements
UNLOCKING INVASION POINTS IN LEGION REMIX
Unlocking Invasion Points in Argus will require players to secure the Sigil of Awakening in the Argus campaign. You will know you have done this if you have completed up to the "Dark Awakenings" portion in the You Are Now Prepared! achievement.
WHAT ARE INVASION POINTS IN LEGION REMIX?
Found on Argus, Invasion Points are open-world content where players complete objectives and defeat Legion Commanders.

Invasion Points come in two forms: Lesser Invasion Points and Greater Invasion Points. Lesser Invasion Points are 3-10 player scenario-based instances containing more segmented objectives, with a boss at the end.

Greater Invasion Points are similar to raid or dungeon instances and contain what is essentially a World Boss for groups to defeat. Players will be able to host / join Greater Invasion Points in the Group Finder.

These different Invasion Points can be found on your map of Argus. At this time, we are unsure on the specifics for these event timers in Legion Remix.


Look for Legion Portals throughout Argus to start Invasion Points!
INVASION POINT LEGION REMIX ACHIEVEMENTS
There are a few achievements in Legion Remix tied to Invasion Points, 4 of which reward (Currency #3292).

title: Blizzard Wants Many Addon Options for Customization in Midnight and Beyond - Ion Hazzikostas on Reddit
author: POSTED 2025/11/19 AT 6:21 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Ion Hazzikostas has responded on Reddit discussing how the base UI is seeing ongoing polish and wanting as many possible addon options for customization in Midnight and Beyond.
Blizzard
View Original
Earlier today we pushed a client hotfix to beta to change the way nameplate stacking/overlapping works, as we know it's been way off the mark. We'll make further changes as needed based on playtesting/feedback in the coming beta updates.

Nameplates, unitframes, and raid frames are all seeing ongoing polish (and in some cases we need to set up designer logic for better defaults on buff/debuff visibility). When it comes to UI features, players on beta will see more things go live in a more work-in-progress state than we'd usually target for, say, a questline or a raid boss, because we want to get feedback as quickly as possible even while we know we still have work to do on our end.

There's a lot of discussion in this thread about people trying to go into Midnight (or "preparing" in live TWW) with zero addons after using a bunch of different ones for years, and feeling frustrated. But our goal has never been to get people who enjoy the customization that addons offer to stop using them entirely. We want to keep improving the base UI such that fewer people feel addons are necessary to enjoy the game, but there's a reason last week's blog was about "Addon Disarmament" and not "Addon Removal." The focus has always been on limiting combat performance advantages, and anything else that has been affected was collateral damage that we are working on repairing.

When it comes to aesthetic customization, we could put in years of extra development on the UI and still not fully capture the thousands of variations of personal preference that addons enable. And if we tried, the base UI interface settings and edit mode would become a mess of endless niche options.

While we have UX designers focused on refining the base UI offerings that are being tested now (and we know a lot of refinement is still needed), a major focus for our UI engineers, which isn't captured in patch notes, is working with addon developers to provide new functions and other support to help re-enable a wide range of cosmetic addons that touch unit frames, action bars, and more, so that they can still work in Midnight. If you think the default UI is ugly, or you prefer your secondary resources displayed in a certain way, that's entirely your prerogative, and we want there to be as many possible addon options to allow you to change the size, shape, color, texture, and location of every element (as long as those changes aren't driven by real-time combat logic) in Midnight and beyond.

title: Ion Hazzikostas Responds to the Community's Concerns on Addons - Game Informer Interview
author: POSTED 2025/11/19 AT 1:30 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Gameinformer released an interview with Game Director Ion Hazzikostas, discussing his recent blog post on addons and directly responding to various Reddit comments from the community.

Please watch the below video for the full context, but we've summarized and quoted some responses below.



Follow Gameinformer:
Bluesky
Instagram
TikTok
Twitter
Facebook
Table of Contents
Why now?
Was it Rushed?
Bugs in Previous Patches
Accessibility
Real or Perceived Problem?
Feedback
Why now?
A big question in the community is "Why now?" Could Blizzard have waited until their in game replacements were completed before doing this?
It's like the saying "The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago, the next best time is today."
Part of the change is because of the range of what addons are capable of from players using no addons, to RWF guilds who have professional coders for addons.
These RWF addons trickle down through the rest of the ecosystem and they need to create an experience in meeting players where they are that is adequately challenging, stimulating and understandable.
That's a very big challenge and honestly impossible when looking at certain types of content. If they design without taking into account addons, then people who do use them will trivialize them. If they design with taking into account addons, then people who don't use them will feel overwhelmed.
The goal here is to build content that better serves all players.
The best time to do this is now.
They've been hard at work over the last year, changing what addons are capable of, to build up new capabilities in the base UI, and to rethink how they design raid, dungeons and classes so that it's a uniform experience for everyone.
They understand this is a controversial change, a big change. If you are someone who relied on addons, and have viewed the world through this lens for many many years, the idea of perspective changing can be an unsettling one. But they are doing everything possible to preserve the aesthetic customization, where you want information on your screen, and the convenience and organizational tools people use to track their collections. They want to disrupt none of those.
They want to only limit the functionality that is required to stop addons from giving you real-to-real combat assistance. There is some collateral damage that results from preventing these, but they're in the process of working with addon devs to try to support them the best they can.
Ion Hazzikostas via GameInformer said:
We probably should have done more along these lines 8 years ago when we started seeing addons pop up for the first time that weren't just making it easier to parse what was going on in a raid encounter, but were very directly trying to solve problems and tell players to "run to star", "run to diamond", or "this is how the mechanic is going to work" because we should have been able to see where it was going to lead. Ultimately, it's something that we've had to grapple with designing the game.
Was it Rushed?
Another question from the community was "Was it Rushed?" How long has this been worked on for?
It's something that they've been thinking about and saying they've needed to do this for 2-3 years.
They've been planning how they would make that transition during this time.
They went public with it late last year and wanted to get a reading on it from the community, as if the community responded poorly, they wouldn't have proceeded.
They completely acknolwedge and own the fact that they need to be able to pull this off, as that is a major concern.
Both Blizzard and many players agree that the game should be playable out of the box.
Ion Hazzikostas via GameInformer said:
In late last year, I think we started talking publicly about our philosophy here and where things might be headed with one of the final checks in our plan being [...] wanting to take the community's temperature on this, wanting to make sure that as much as we could see as developers that is limiting our ability to serve our players, if what we heard from players was "do not touch my addons, this is perfect, we don't want any of this to change". If that is what we had heard across the board, we would not have proceeded with any of this. What we have heard from a lot of players is "yeah I shouldn't have to download these 3rd party tools in order to feel like I'm playing the game correctly. I hate joining a guild or pick-up group and having to download and configure this thing because everyone expects me to. A lot of the concerns rested in, will Blizzard be able to pull this off? And that is 100% fair, we want to own that, we want to continue to own that, but if where players land is, if this is a good change for the game, but the proof is in the pudding for whether they can deliver, we'll take that at the end of the day. We want to do things that are right for the game and we need to show and prove to our players over the course of actually doing it that we are able to pull it off in a nuanced way, with their best interest in mind. But what we've heard from our players is that the game should be playable out of the box without needing to turn to external assistance and we agree.
Bugs in Previous Patches
One of the questions was in response to this comment on Reddit, talking about how lots of previously patches have been buggy, and trust.
They have the their built-in UI improvements up on the Beta and are just iterating and augmenting them based on feedback.
WoW is a very complex game with 25 years of engineering framework and sometimes when you have millions of players hitting a system, sometimes it falls over or a change has unintended consequences.
They do everything they can to prevent those.
The UI stuff is pretty self contained and they are confident with all the attention on it and it being the number 1 priority for all their UI engineers up to launch and beyond, that they will be able to deliver.
Where they are falling short they are going to react and prioritize based on the response from the community. It's not a one-and-done thing.
Accessibility
When responding to this comment on Reddit, Ion Hazzikotas discusses accessibility and the updates that they've done on the beta so far.
Accessibility was one of our primary areas of focus. Even if it's a minority of players who are using these tools for those reasons, they couldn't go ahead with those changes if it was going to force those players out of the game.
They've made a lot of changes to their addon logic to support as many accessibility tools as possible, while pulling many of those tools into the base UI.
They've also added new things like the One-Button Rotation and Assist.
Healbot directly is an addon that can be used to click on a frame and heal that player directly, they actually added the ability to bind mouse clicks directly to raid frames as part of the base UI a few years ago.
There's also Clique still works and will offer more fully fledged customizations if you have an MMO mouse, etc.
For DBM, they've built a boss timeline into the base UI that's curated by the encounter designers. They've been working with authors, like the author of DBM, so that they can visually modify that display. If you want bars, or a sound, you'll still be able to hook those up.
They've also added a range of text to speech alerts focused on visually impaired players.
Real or Perceived Problem?
Gameinformer brought up this line from Ion Hazzikostas' blog post:
WoW’s history is littered with examples of the dev team trying to solve perceived “problems” that most players weren’t particularly concerned about, and we wanted to reassure ourselves that we weren’t headed down that same path here.
In WoD, when they decided that they shouldn't have added flying to the world and they restricted flight. That was not a popular decision. They continually walked it back slowly until Skyriding and now it's a baseline part.
In Shadowlands, Ion felt like the ease of completely changing who your character is at the drop of a hat was taking away from RPG identity. There's something compelling about one personal having different strengths and weaknesses than another. Covenants were meant to bring back some "meaningful choice", and that was out of step with what people wanted. Players want flexibility. It took a minute to get the message, and once they did they changed and aren't looking back. Ion completely owns this one.
Part of this might be single player game vs multiplayer game. You might like the idea of having a flaw, but when there's a group and you have a flaw in the content we're looking to do, you don't get invited.
Feedback
Gameinformer also brought up this line from Ion Hazzikostas' blog post:
So we are looking forward to seeing players have that holistic experience, and we are looking for feedback not just on our UI or evolving addon capabilities, but on aspects of endgame combat that might feel unclear or unfair without some of the tools you are accustomed to using.
They're interested in all the feedback: delves, dungeons and raids.
Over the years, they've had feedback on mechanics that weren't sufficiently clear. In an addonless world, they would get clear feedback, but addons would help fill in those gaps.
They feel like they have been shoring up those gaps on their end, but there are probably places where they're missing something.
If there's a pack where it feels like there's too much going on, they want to hear that feedback.
There will UI updates in nearly every patch coming up.

title: Unlock the Lightbound Sargerei Commander Set With Phase 4 of Legion Remix
author: POSTED 2025/11/19 AT 11:04 AM BY JAYDAA
content:
The Lightbound Sargerai Commander set is finally available this week with the release of Antorus, the Burning Throne in Legion Remix Phase 4. Complete all the remix raids on Mythic difficulty to add this unique transmog to your collection.

Legion Remix Overview
Legion Remix Overview
Legion Remix: Rewards
Legion Remix: Rewards
Legion Remix: Artifacts
Legion Remix: Artifacts


UNLOCKING THE SARGERAI COMMANDER REGALIA
Featuring incredible cloaks with persistent particle effects, the set comes in four color variants, each unlocked through the completion of an endgame Remix achievement.

Because Legion Remix content has been released in two-week intervals, the Lightbound variant has not been available until now with the release of the Antorus raid in Legion Remix Phase 4.
Mythic Legion Remix Raids awards Ensemble: Sargerei Commander's Lightbound Regalia
Complete all Legion Remix Raids on Mythic difficulty.
To Fel and Back awards Ensemble: Sargerei Commander's Felscorned Regalia
Complete the Legion Remix campaign, ending with Shape a New Legend.
Heroic Broken Isles World Quests IV awards Ensemble: Sargerei Commander's Voidscarred Regalia
Complete 100 World Quests in the Heroic World Tier.
Timeworn Keystone Hero awards Ensemble: Sargerei Commander's Hellforged Regalia
Complete any Mythic+ Timeworn Keystone Dungeon on +30 or higher.
ENSEMBLE: SARGEREI COMMANDER'S LIGHTBOUND REGALIA
ENSEMBLE: SARGEREI COMMANDER'S FELSCORNED REGALIA
ENSEMBLE: SARGEREI COMMANDER'S VOIDSCARRED REGALIA
ENSEMBLE: SARGEREI COMMANDER'S HELLFORGED REGALIA

MORE LEGION REMIX PHASE 4 REWARDS
With the release of Antorus in Legion Phase 4, players will also be able to earn the new Felscorned Scythe of the Unmaker, one-handed Taeshalach, and reprised Scythe of the Unmaker!

title: Mid May Not Be Bad - The War Within Season 3 DPS Rankings for Mythic Manaforge Omega Week 14
author: POSTED 2025/11/19 AT 5:56 AM BY ANSHLUN
content:
Today, we'll be looking at some of the middle-of-the-pack specs on Manaforge Omega, Augmentation Evoker, Subtlety Rogue, and Windwalker Monk. Throughout the whole tier, all three specs have been around the middle of the overall charts, is that a curse? Or could it be a blessing in disguise?

Manaforge Omega Statistics Page
Manaforge Omega Statistics Page
Table of Contents
Manaforge Omega Raid Tier Lists
DPS Rankings
Expert Commentary
Augmentation Evoker
Subtlety Rogue
Windwalker Monk
Disclaimers and Source
MANAFORGE OMEGA RAID TIER LISTS
Looking for more insight into the raid balance? Check out our Tier Lists, which include more information besides pure throughput for Manaforge Omega.

DPS Tier List
DPS Tier List
 Healer Tier List
Healer Tier List
 Tank Tier List
Tank Tier List
THE WAR WITHIN SEASON 3 DPS RANKINGS
With Manaforge already past the midpoint of its life cycle, let's take a look at three of the specs that have been constantly in the middle-of-the-pack for the tier: Augmentation Evoker, Subtlety Rogue, and Windwalker Monk.

Since the first few weeks, all three specs have revolved around the middle of the rankings, without many changes, just ebbing and flowing with slight variations each week. However, in the Blizzard tuning rollercoaster, that isn't always a bad thing. If anything, it guarantees consistency for your spec, as outliers, especially on the high ends, tend to fall from grace rather quickly, while those at the bottom wait weeks to receive 3% buffs.

Without any new tuning or changes, all changes in rankings for this week are just noise changes, with specs that are close enough balance-wise just swapping positions.

95th Percentile Statistics

Overall Damage
Damage to Bosses
Overall Statistics

Overall Damage
Damage to Bosses
Position Spec and Class Population Size Change from
Last Week
1 Elemental Shaman 26492 0
2 Arcane Mage 33851 0
3 Marksmanship Hunter 5687 ↑2
4 Beast Mastery Hunter 59626 ↓1
5 Assassination Rogue 9711 ↓1
6 Destruction Warlock 39768 ↑1
7 Fire Mage 2707 ↑2
8 Frost Death Knight 47829 ↓2
9 Fury Warrior 30353 ↓1
10 Subtlety Rogue 5826 0
11 Augmentation Evoker 5178 ↑2
12 Windwalker Monk 8235 ↓1
13 Unholy Death Knight 4587 ↓1
14 Balance Druid 19071 0
15 Shadow Priest 15083 0
16 Devastation Evoker 10815 0
17 Feral Druid 4500 0
18 Retribution Paladin 35776 0
19 Havoc Demon Hunter 32178 ↑1
20 Affliction Warlock 1696 ↓1
21 Demonology Warlock 4719 ↑1
22 Enhancement Shaman 2604 ↑1
23 Outlaw Rogue 1421 ↑3
24 Arms Warrior 1328 ↓3
25 Survival Hunter 1392 ↓1
26 Frost Mage 8101 ↓1

Class Writer Commentary
To help us better understand the charts above, we invited our Class Writers for Augmentation Evoker, Subtlety Rogue, and Windwalker Monk to provide insights about their specs and explain their specs' position in Manaforge Omega.
Augmentation Evoker
Jereico said:
After Augmentation Evoker's strong start to The War Within in Season 1, followed by a flurry of nerfs heading into Season 2, it seems Blizzard has finally found their footing when it comes to balance tuning in Manaforge Omega. Augmentation has enjoyed a position squarely in the middle of the raid rankings throughout Season 3, just in time for the Midnight expansion to come along and mix things up.

This expansion Augmentation saw a portion of its damage in raid shift away from ally-reliant buffs and into its personal damage profile. Though the split still generally favors buff damage, and varies by build and playstyle, the net effect is a spec that is somewhat easier to tune alongside more traditional DPS specs in raid. Improvements to Ebon Might uptime have also reduced the importance of carefully spreadsheeting buff timings, and Augmentation players have found that it's quite possible to perform well by simply maintaining buffs on just a few key players.

While Augmentation itself may not directly bring most impressive target scaling or burst damage potential, it lacks any real weaknesses when it comes to the encounters in Manaforge Omega. Though changing in Midnight, the spec continues to enjoy considerable versatility in its damage profile through its buffs, enhanced by Ebon Might target selection during specific, key moments. On a fight like Dimensius, the phase changes provide obvious spots to reset buff targets and pick up some extra value. Augmentation can buff Elemental Shaman during Phase 1, and later swap to Balance Druid or Beast Mastery Hunter during the damage amp in Phase 3.

Against all odds, Augmentation Evoker has landed in a solid, reasonable place for raiders of all skill levels in Season 3 of The War Within, and I expect the upcoming changes in Midnight to largely maintain this status quo, all else equal.
Subtlety Rogue
fuu1 said:
Subtlety Rogue is positioned middle of the pack in the current statistic, which is not a bad position to be in. This is a consequence of fight design and tuning. Subtlety offers a very burst heavy damage profile, something very valuable on fights with adds or vulnerability phases. Trinkets like Unyielding Netherprism and Araz's Ritual Forge increase these burst phases. This burst is a blessing and a curse, it allows Subtlety to be valuable on the most important fights of the tier. The problem or downside is tuning is done considering this strength, leaving Subtlety with weak pure single target and cleave damage. While this seems fair, it leads to a sensitive situation. Passive damage buffs to the raid as well as higher gear levels indirectly reduce the power level of the specialization due to faster add kill timings as well as potential timing shifts on Nexus-King Salhadaar and Dimensius. The Blessing of Autumn external is often used on these fights to help getting better cooldown alignment.

The result of everything mentioned above means Subtlety is within the worse specs on all fights without adds or short vulnerability phases. This means Plexus Sentinel, Loom'ithar, Fractillus and to a certain degree Soul Hunters. But can make up decently well for it on Soulbinder Naazindhri, Forgeweaver Araz, Nexus-King Salhadaar and Dimensius, even though slightly worse on the last two lately because of changes in timings.

If there was an incentive to improve Subtleties position, the easiest thing to do would be a baseline buff, the mentioned trade off the balance team decided on makes sense in isolation but does not hold up well with increases in power level. This principle is also applied inconsistently between classes, where examples like Arcane Mage or Hunter specializations seem to be allowed to have fewer weaknesses and overall higher tuning. Buffing Secret Technique would be another approach to increase burst damage, something that's also valuable in mythic+, the spell was already nerfed twice during this expansion and did not feel overpowered before nerfs. A higher tuning would reward people for playing the specialization, because it is, at least till midnight, the potentially hardest to learn in the game and punishes you heavily for mistakes.
Windwalker Monk
Babylonius said:
For much of Windwalker's life, it has struggled to reach the level of "middle of the pack" in raids, constantly fighting just to make it back to average. Since Dragonflight, Windwalkers have enjoyed relative mediocrity (absolutely a step up from many times in the last 10 years) and even representation in the Race to World First where players like Trill and Boomie demonstrated what Windwalker can be capable of in the right circumstances. Unfortunately, these highs haven't tended to continue through the rest of their respective seasons, with Windwalker losing some ground as the tier continued.

Looking back at the history of Windwalker Monks in raid, problems frequently stem from poor single target, and the current state of things is no different from this long-standing trend. Generally, as a tier goes on, adds on fights are live less or a fight is over before more spawn. This can "hurt" specs, like Windwalker that lean toward the AOE/padding side of the damage spectrum. The past two tiers this has meant that Windwalkers went from roughly 2% above the statistical average to about 4% below the statistical average over the duration of Liberation of Undermine and from around 1% above the average to almost perfectly at the statistical average in [zone=16178. *Note* The statistical averages may not match what is going on in your raid or what your favorite streamer is doing, just the data from all the Mythic boss kills.

On a personal level, I have been tracking the balance of DPS specs and classes in raids for more than 10 years. While the community has long been mostly swayed by the order of the colored bars, I've always been more interested in how much a spec or class is overperforming or underperforming. A blue post from many years ago, and now lost to time, once said that Blizzard's design goal was for spec performance to be within 10% top to bottom. While this goal has only been met twice, in Aberrus, the Shadowed Crucible during 10.1.5 and Amirdrassil, the Dream's Hope during 10.2.5, there has been a clear trend toward that direction over the years, with Dragonflight being the most "balanced" of any expansion, followed by The War Within. The introduction of Augmentation Evokers, with the chaos it wrought, and Hero Talents in The War Within certainly made reaching that 10% goal more difficult, but overall balance inside raids, at least for DPS specs, has been statistically very good over the past few years. Thankfully, this means that most of the time even the bottom of the colored bars is close enough to the top that everyone can raid on what they enjoy. Now if only we could change human psychology...
DISCLAIMERS AND SOURCE
The data for this article was taken from the Raid Statistics Page on Warcraft Logs for Mythic difficulty during the week of October 21st. Overall, the numbers shown above represent data for the 95th percentile. For charts, we also included data for all percentiles and boss damage to better represent the current state of balance.

The data presented, however, isn't free of bias, as it is representative of the current meta of the game, which, in itself, is biased by community perception of specs.
This bias comes from players generally flocking to specs perceived as "better", be it either easier to play or dealing more damage, or a combination of both.
The other side of the coin is specs that are too hard to play or too weak will be underrepresented and appear lower than they actually are.
Competitive players will generally prefer specs perceived to do more damage, making the best specs appear higher than they actually are.
While not as prevalent in modern days, strategy differences and parse-funneling may impact rankings. Specs that excel in AoE, spread cleave, or burst windows will appear higher in the total charts.

title: Keys For the Vault - TWW Season 3 Mythic+ Rankings Week 14
author: POSTED 2025/11/19 AT 12:56 AM BY ANSHLUN
content:
Mythic+ continues to wind down, with participation taking another, albeit smaller, week-over-week drop. The gap between +12s and +10 has started to reduce, indicating more and more players are only doing Mythic+ to fill in one or more Vault slots.

 Raider.IO TWW Season 3 Dungeon Statistics
Table of Contents
Spec Popularity
Depletion per Dungeon
Key Levels
Participation per Week
Legion Remix
Disclaimers and Source
Tier List
SPEC POPULARITY IN MYTHIC+ FOR TWW SEASON 3 - WEEK OF NOVEMBER 11TH
Spec popularity continues to be stable on week 14, with the meta of Frost DK, BM Hunter, Havoc DH, Prot Warrior, and Resto Druid comprising over 70% of the characters in keys above +19.

DEPLETION RATES FOR SEASON 3 KEYS - WEEK OF NOVEMBER 11TH
As participation continues to drop, most players are doing fewer keys, likely only for Vault slots, leading to stable depletion rates.

KEY LEVEL DISTRIBUTION IN SEASON 3 - WEEK OF NOVEMBER 11TH
The gap between +10 and +12 keys is starting to shrink, with fewer players completing their Keystone Legend achievements and most only doing +10s to fill in their weekly Vault slot.

MYTHIC+ PARTICIPATION PER WEEK IN SEASON 3
We've observed an almost 50% drop in keys completed this week when compared to four weeks ago, when Turbo Boost started. This drop is expected as we've reached the 14th week of Season 3.

Legion Remix
DISCLAIMERS AND SOURCE
Data for this article is sourced from Raider.io statistics for Mythic+ dungeon participation, collected during the week of November 11th. Data encapsulates all key levels, looking to paint a complete picture of what the M+ community is doing during the period.

Note that, since the data reflect only participation, there are many implicit biases in the numbers.
Since all metrics shown deal with popularity, they aren't directly attached to spec balance, but to community perception of spec balance.
Specs considered bad by the community will generally be played less and tend to have lower participation.
Highest keys tend to have a very low participation, meaning specs played by these players will be overrepresented, despite any actual balance.
