title: Solo Delve Hall of Fame Reduced to 4.000 Players
author: POSTED 2025/11/18 AT 9:57 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
After increasing the Solo Delve Hall of Fame to 10,000 last week, the solo delve hall of fame achievement has been reduced back down to 4,000 players.
Fabled Let Me Solo Him: Nullaeus  Be one of the first 100004000 players in your region to defeat Nullaeus in his lair on Tier ?? without any other players in your party, before the release of the next season of delves.
This is really interesting as last week, Blizzard increased the amount of players who could obtain the achievement to 10,000.

Going back to down to 4,000 players may contribute to a pre-existing problem; most Nemesis Delve bosses we've seen so far are much easier to complete on certain classes / specs in comparison to others. The difficulty of doing these fights with each role has strong variation. Tanks can take more upfront damage, healers have more sustainability, and DPS can simply kill faster. All of these differing aspects will have some affect on who can defeat the Nemesis boss easier depending on whatever set of mechanics is present in the Nemesis fight.

title: Revamped Transmog System in Midnight - Outfit Slots, Situations, and More!
author: POSTED 2025/11/18 AT 8:15 PM BY PORTERGAUGE
content:
The new Transmogrification system has been added to the Midnight Beta for testing, complete with custom outfit slots and conditional situations to change sets automatically!
Table of Contents
Outfits
Situations
What Happens To My Current Transmog?
OUTFITS
Similar to on live, talking to a Transmogrifier will open up a window that allows you to choose an appearance for each armor slot, but instead of applying to an individual item from your bags or character, it now applies to an Outfit Slot.


By default, you start with two Outfit Slots, and can purchase up to 20 per character. Each slot costs an increasing amount of gold, starting at 100 gold and capping out at a massive 100,000! Once purchased, Outfits can be freely swapped between at no cost. Your Outfits can even be dragged to your action bars for a quick way to change between them!


 
The only gold cost once purchased comes from saving the appearances within an Outfit Slot, which comes at a very hefty price on the beta right now!

SITUATIONS
The main new feature of the revamped transmog system are Situations, new effects that you can apply to each individual outfit to automatically apply to your character in specific scenarios. This system can be entirely ignored if you wish to retain the current system of a permanent outfit, but open up a massive amount of customizability with the features listed below.

Locations
Locations include all forms of instanced content for both PvE and PvP, rested areas like capitals and inns, your House, and even the ability to have a specific set for the character select screen!
Movement and Mounting
Movement options allow you to change your outfit depending on your travel state. You can equip different outfits depending on if you are mounted or unmounted, as well as customize it for different mount types. Perhaps in future, Blizzard could even allow us to have different sets for individual mounts?

Specialization and Talent Builds
Specialization options allow you to not only apply different outfits for each specialization like on live, but also apply different outfits for individual talent builds that you have saved in your talent tree!

Equipment Slots
Equipment slots can now be customized to have different appearances when equipped, even if two different slots use the same item!
Racial Forms for Dracthyr and Worgen
In addition to all of these default options, Dracthyr and Worgen also have a unique Situation to allow them to change outfits depending on their racial form! So long to worrying about if a cool belt or shoulder item matches the gear in your Dracthyr form!
WHAT HAPPENS TO MY CURRENT TRANSMOG?
Compared to live, there are no changes to the Appearance tab, item collection, or the Dressing Room to interfere with your current transmog sets. Your existing saved sets will continue to be available when this system launches, and unobtained sets can still be saved as a Custom Set.

This new Transmogrification system offers a massive amount of customization for different forms of content. How will you use these situations for your characters? Are there any Situations you think Blizzard should add? Let us know!

title: Purchasing Outfit Slots are Not Account-Wide - All 20 Slots Costs ~800k Gold
author: POSTED 2025/11/18 AT 6:40 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Transmog is available on the Midnight Beta, and you can buy outfit slots which have increasing gold costs. Buying all 20 outfits slots costs ~800k gold!

Blizzard has announced that the costs for unlocking Outfit slots will be changing. This section is now outdated.
Transmogging Tied to Slots in Midnight
In Midnight, transmogging your gear is tied to an outfit slot. When you get a new piece of gear, you no longer need to pay to transmog that piece -- no more looking mismatched while leveling up and getting new gear!

In addition, players are able to buy outfit slots which allow you to save and swap sets, and change them automatically for different activities with the "situations" system. Want a set while you're in your house? While you're mounted? For Swimming or different specs? You can!

The old functionality of saving pre-determined transmog sets is not going away and is still do that for free. The new Outfit slots are only used for the situations system.
Outfit Slots
In order to save all these different transmogs, you'll need to buy outfit slots for each character.
These outfit slots are NOT account-wide, meaning you need to buy them on each character.
Each character gets 2 outfit slots for free.
Players can buy up to a total of 20 slots, with the cost starting at 100 gold and increasing to a maximum of 100k
The total amount of gold is almost ~800k for all 20 slots.

title: Blizzard Damage Meter Now Available on the Midnight Beta
author: POSTED 2025/11/18 AT 6:29 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
Blizzard's built-in damage meter is now functioning and available for testing on the Midnight Beta!

 
The normal meter looks about how you'd expect, offering a simple class, name, and numerical readout. Right clicking on the bar will expand it out into another tab, showing additional details. If you're a lower-right corner of the screen enjoyer, don't worry, the damage meter will grow to the left instead.


Right click the meter to expand its details, giving a small breakdown of damaging abilities, etc.


The damage meter can be enabled and positioned in Edit Mode, though if it still does not appear for you, try using this command to make it show:
/run DamageMeter:Show()
Blizzard has also outlined a few of its features in today's Beta Development Notes.
NEW INTERFACE: DAMAGE METER
Allows players to track combat statistics for themselves and their party members in a variety of categories. It can be enabled under Options > Gameplay Enhancements > Damage Meter.
Once enabled, various settings for the Damage Meter are available in Edit Mode.
Current categories include: Damage Done, DPS, Damage Taken, Healing Done, HPS, Absorbs, Interrupts, Dispels.
Supports tracking within individual combat pulls or across all pulls.
Supports up to 3 windows at a time (primary window can be moved/resized in Edit Mode, secondary windows adjustable outside of Edit Mode).
Right-clicking on an individual entry in the damage meter will open a separate window containing a per-spell breakdown.
Developers’ notes: This feature is still in active development. Various pieces of the UI are still WIP and the combat statistics shown may not be completely accurate in some cases.

title: New End Game Realm on Midnight Beta - Development Notes for November 18th
author: POSTED 2025/11/18 AT 6:19 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Blizzard has posted development notes for today which includes a new end game realm on the Midnight Beta.
Blizzard
View Original
END-GAME REALM: XALATATH’S ENDGAME
‘Xal’atath’s Endgame’ is a separate Beta region that is focused on end-game content and has multiple vendors setup to allow players to quickly and easily test out content.
Profession Vendors and Trainers
Class Set Vendors
PvP Gear Vendors
Trinket Vendors
This environment is locked to using a max level template only, and players will not be able to access the Midnight outdoor zones as the focus of this environment is on instanced content (raid, dungeon, and pvp content).
Character copy is not available on this realm.
NEW FEATURES
Updated Transmog System
Midnight Season 1 – Available on the end-game realm, Xalatath’s Endgame
Raid testing begins. Visit https://us.forums.blizzard.com/en/wow/t/midnight-raid-testing-schedule/2198120 for more details.
Mythic+ Season 1 legacy dungeons and Mythic+ updates. Visit https://us.forums.blizzard.com/en/wow/t/feedback-midnight-season-1-dungeon-testing-november-18th-december-2nd/2201147 for more details.
CLASSES
DEATH KNIGHT
Blood
Melee auto-attack damage has been increased by 150%.
Mastery: Blood Shield now appropriately displays the absorb amount is up to 50% of your maximum health.
Blood Boil damage reduced by 12%.
Deadly Reach now deals 75% of Death Strike damage to additional targets hit.
Bloodshot no longer only increases physical damage. Now increases all damage dealt by 8% while Blood Shield is active.
New Talent: Bloody Reflection – While Blood Shield is active, taking direct damage reflects it back dealing Shadow damage to the attacker. Blood Shield’s absorb is increased by up to 65% of your maximum health.
Frost
Auto-attack damage increased by 400%.
Unholy
Necromancer’s Bargain has been renamed to Necromancer’s Cunning and moved to the specialization tree – Putrefy now summons a Lesser Ghoul to attack instead.
Outnumber has been redesigned and is now the capstone talent – Each Lesser Ghoul increases the damage of your permanent Ghoul by 10% and Dark Transformation additionally empowers the Claw attacks of your Lesser Ghouls to Sweeping Claws. No longer grants 4 seconds additional duration to Lesser Ghoul.
Reaping secondary effect has been updated – Dark Transformation now resets the cooldown of Soul Reaper and allows it to be used on any target.
Commander of the Dead now has a secondary effect – The duration of Lesser Ghouls are increased by 25%/50% of your Mastery.
Auto-attack damage increased by 300%.
Army of the Dead now summons 6 Lesser Ghouls.
Lesser Ghouls now last for 6 seconds (was 8 seconds).
March of Madness now increases the damage of Lesser Ghouls by 15% (was 30%).
Summon Gargoyle no longer grants 50 Runic Power.
Gargoyle Strike now always critically strikes.
Raise Abomination and Summon Gargoyle now Putrefy 2 Lesser Ghouls (was 4).
Magus of the Dead no longer causes Army of the Dead to summon a Magus.
Blightfall now increases all disease damage by 10% (was 20%).
Reanimation now has a 100% chance to reanimate a Lesser Ghoul when Putrefied.
Sweeping Claws damage increased by 20% and now deals 35% reduced damage to secondary targets.
Unholy Aura now grants 1%/2% Haste for each Magus of the Dead active.
Infected Claws will now be applied to the permanent Ghoul without the need to resummon.
Infected Claws now scales with Mastery.
Reaping damage bonus is now correctly applied to Scourge Strike.
Fixed an issue that caused Scourge Strike damage to not scale with Mastery.
Army of the Dead and its follow-up talents Raise Abomination and Summon Gargoyle have been moved to the center of the talent tree.
DEMON HUNTER
Hero Talents
New Hero Talent: Annihilator
New Talent: Harness the Cosmos – Voidfall Meteor damage increased by 15%. Choice node against Doomsayer.
Final Hour has been updated – Now causes Voidfall bonuses to linger for 8 seconds (was 12 seconds).
Fixed an issue causing all Voidfall Meteors to benefit from World Killer’s radius increase.
Devourer
Doomsayer has been updated – Buff is now removed after 10 seconds of entering combat (was 5 seconds).
Voidfall Meteor damage reduced by 50%.
World Killer Meteors now generate 1 Soul Fragment (was 2).
Fixed an issue where World Killer Meteors were not dealing appropriately increased damage and not creating Soul Fragments for Devourer Demon Hunters.
Vengeance
Doomsayer has been updated – Now summons 1 Voidfall Meteor (was 3).
Meteoric Fall has been updated – Now also triggered by Soul Cleave.
Voidfall Meteor damage reduced by 25%.
Void-Scarred
Devourer
Student of Suffering has been updated – Now grants 3 Fury per tick (was 5).
Devourer
Developers’ notes: Devourer is still overperforming by a wide margin, so we’re tamping down Annihilator’s power while also reducing the damage of their core spells. We are intentionally not reducing The Hunt’s power to help its power relative to the rest of the Devourer kit.
Eradicate has been updated – Damage now increases proportionally when Cull replaces Reap.
Entropy has been updated – Now forces your Soul Fragments to 25 when a raid encounter begins.
Mastery: Monster Within effectiveness reduced.
Void Ray damage reduced by 25%.
Reap damage reduced by 25%.
Collapsing Star damage reduced by 25%.
Voidblade damage reduced by 25%.
Hungering Slash damage reduced by 25%.
Voidstep damage reduced by 25%.
DRUID
Hero Talents
Druid of the Claw
Claw Rampage has been updated – Attacks during Berserk only have one chance to grant Ravage, instead of one per target hit. No longer triggers off the Cat Form version of Thrash.
Feral
Ravage damage to its primary target reduced by 20%.
Dreadful Wound damage reduced by 20%.
Strike for the Heart increases Shred, Swipe, and Rake critical strike chance by 5% (was 8%).
Wildstalker
Bloodseeker Vines damage reduced by 15%.
Bursting Growth damage reduced by 20%.
Vigorous Creepers causes Bloodseeker Vines to increase damage victims take from you by 4% (was 6%).
Class
Thrash can no longer be used in Cat Form.
Developers’ notes: Cat Form Thrash does not have a strong identity or distinctive gameplay for druids in Midnight. To free up a keybind and reduce the number of debuff auras on enemy nameplates, we are removing Thrash from druids in Cat Form.
All Druids learn Bear Form Thrash at level 11 (was a talent).
Swipe is now a Class talent (was learned at level 10).
Shred damage increased by 40%.
Cat Form Swipe damage increased by 25%.
Thrash has been removed.
Feral
Chomp has been updated – Chomp your target, causing Physical damage. Chomp critical strikes deal 3 times normal damage. Chomp can only be used when you fall below 30% Energy or for 2 seconds afterward.
Developers’ notes: These changes to Chomp are intended to make being able to use it once you’ve met its conditions more reliable and to help its damage keep pace with abilities that benefit from Mastery as your gear improves.
All ability damage reduced by 27%.
Melee damage increased by 115%.
Moonfire with Lunar Inspiration damage increased by 15%.
Feral Frenzy damage increased by 10%.
Frantic Frenzy damage increased by 15%.
Chomp damage increased by 10%.
Unseen Slash bleed damage reduced by 30%.
Unseen Swipe damage increased by 30%.
Merciless Claws increases the damage of Swipe by 20% (was 15%).
Midnight 4-set bonus has been updated – Now increases Moment of Clarity damage by 15% (was 20%).
Wild Slashes no longer increases Thrash damage.
Veinripper no longer increases the duration of Thrash’s bleed.
Fixed issues with Frantic Frenzy that could cause it to target far away enemies or generate fewer than five combo points.
Thrashing Claws has been removed.
Guardian
New Talent: Killing Blow – Maul and Raze may consume up to 40 additional rage to deal up to 150% increased damage.
New Talent: Natural Resilience – When Frenzied Regeneration would heal you above maximum health, 80% of its excess healing is instead converted to an absorb shield.
New Talent: Dream Guide – When you take non-periodic damage, you have a chance to gain a charge of Dream Guide. When you or a nearby ally drops below 40% health, you consume a charge of Dream Guide to automatically cast Regrowth on them.
Wild Guardian has been updated –
Rank 1: Now activates after you cast Berserk or Incarnation: Guardian of Ursoc.
Rank 2/3 has been redesigned: Mastery increased by 3%/6%. Maul is always empowered and deals 20%/40% additional Nature damage over 12 seconds.
Rank 4: When you cast Wild Guardian, you gain 2 charges of Dream of Cenarius or Dream Guide. Wild Guardian’s echos are now cast at 100% effectiveness and each echo an additional 2 times over 8 seconds.
Dream of Cenarius has been updated – 20 second lockout has been removed, converted to 3.0+Haste RPPM. Now stacks up to 4.
Ursoc’s Fury now generates an absorb shield based on 40% of the damage dealt (was 25%).
Ursoc’s Fury, Raze, and Vulnerable Flesh have changed locations in the talent tree.
The following talents have been removed:
Circle of Life and Death
Mangle
EVOKER
Augmentation
Eruption cast by Duplicate damage reduced by 30%.
All spell and ability damage reduced by 10%.
Preservation
Fixed an issue where Dream Breath was incorrectly calculating its upfront healing amount when empowering beyond rank 1. The overall healing amount is now equal no matter which empower level is chosen.
HUNTER
Hero Talents
Dark Ranger
Wailing Arrow has been updated – Damage increased by 100% and cast time reduced to 1 second (was 2 seconds).
Developers’ notes: We feel that Wailing Arrow needs an increase to usability and damage.
Beast Mastery
Through the Eyes has been updated – Now causes Black Arrow to deal 10% increased damage to enemies affected by Black Arrow’s periodic damage.
Pack Leader
Pack Mentality has been updated – Howl of the Pack Leader now grants 100% increased Kill Command damage.
Howl of the Pack Leader has been updated – Wyvern damage bonus increased to 15% (was 10%).
Marksmanship
Developers’ note: We’re buffing Kill Shot and Rapid Fire to ensure they’re feeling like valuable parts of your kit. We’re increasing Aimed Shot’s cooldown to reduce its power and help with moments of Aimed Shot overflow. Lastly, we’re reducing Aimed Shot’s cast time slightly and having it grant you some additional maneuverability after its longer cast time via a reworked Ammo Conservation.
Trueshot has been updated – Now grants 60% cooldown reduction to Aimed Shot and Rapid Fire (was 40%).
Ammo Conservation renamed to Quick Draw and has been updated – Now causes Aimed Shot to grant 50% increased movement speed for 2 seconds, decaying rapidly over its duration. No longer grants Aimed Shot cooldown reduction.
Aimed Shot cooldown increased to 15 seconds (was 12 seconds).
Aimed Shot cast time reduced to 2.5 seconds (was 3 seconds).
Kill Shot damage increased by 75%.
Rapid Fire damage increased by 100%.
Fixed an issue causing Deadly Insight to trigger from Marksmanship abilities.
Survival
Strike as One has been updated – Now additionally increase your pet’s damage dealt by 30%.
Kill Command damage increased by 200%.
Auto-attack damage dealt increased by 150%.
MAGE
Hero Talents
Frostfire
Flash Freezeburn has been redesigned –
Fire: Meteor damage increased by 25%. Meteor now grants Frostfire Empowerment.
Frost: Glacial Spike damage increased by 25% and it now explodes on impact, dealing 50% of its damage to up to 5 nearby enemies. Additionally, Glacial Spike now grants Frostfire Empowerment.
Frostfire Empowerment has been updated – Now triggered from casting Frostfire spells (was Frostfire spell damage). No longer affects Glacial Spike.
Developers’ notes: We’re moving AOE Glacial Spike to Flash Freezeburn to make the effect clearer and not tied to Frostfire Empowerment for increased consistency and reduced aura tracking. Frostfire Empowerment triggering from casts should reduce the situations where you gain Frostfire Empowerment mid-cast.
Frostfire Bolt direct damage reduced by 40%. No longer deals increased damage in PvP combat.
Developers’ notes: We recently fixed some issues with Frostfire Bolt, Frostbolt, and Fireball that were causing all 3 spells to deal significantly less damage than intended. This fix paired with the damage bonus should bring Frostfire Bolt’s damage to more reasonable levels.
Fixed an issue causing Frostfire Empowerment to be granted significantly more often than intended.
Frost
Developers’ notes: Our goal is for Shatter to make up a meaningful part of Frost’s damage in all content, and our last change pushed it too far out of relevancy when paired with the Fractured Frost target count reduction. We’re partially reverting our Shatter damage reduction as well as our Ice Lance Freezing stack increase to help create a better balance between Freezing generation and consumption.
Thermal Void has been updated – Now triggers from consuming Brain Freeze (was casting Flurry).
Shatter damage increased by 50%.
Developers’ notes: The net change from our previous tuning will be a 25% damage reduction in Shatter’s damage.
Flurry damage reduced by 15%.
Ice Lance now Shatters 4 stacks of Freezing (was 5).
MONK
Hero Talents
Shado-Pan
Weapons of the Wall has been redesigned – Zenith Stomp deals 20% more damage. Niuzao’s stomp damage increased by 20%.
Mistweaver
Enveloping Mist now costs 3.6% base mana (was 4.2%).
Mana Tea now restores 12.5% more mana.
Rushing Wind Kick’s mana cost reduced by 50%.
Soothing Breath healing increased by 100%.
Enveloping Mist healing increased by 50%.
All healing reduced by 5%.
Jadefire Teachings now transfers 180% of damage done (was 215%).
Ancient Teachings now transfers 25% of damage done (was 30%).
Revival and Restoral healing increased by 100%.
Windwalker
Singularly Focused Jade now increases Jadefire Stomp’s damage by 300% (was 500%).
Celestial Conduit damage reduced by 75%.
Zenith Stomp damage reduced by 33%.
Jadefire Stomp damage reduced by 66%.
Auto-attack damage increased by 200%.
All spell and ability damage reduced by 13%.
PALADIN
Holy
Avenging Crusader has been updated:
Now grants access to Crusader Strike for its duration.
Crusader Strike now transfers damage into healing for its duration.
No longer increases the damage of Judgment.
Auto-attack damage is now increased by 1000% for its duration (was 30%).
Crusader’s Might has been updated – Now additionally causes Crusader Strike to decrease the cooldown of Judgment by 1.5 seconds.
Retribution
Crusading Strikes has been redesigned and is being reintroduced as a choice node to Templar Strikes – Crusader Strike replaces your auto-attacks and deals Physical damage, but you auto-attack 50% slower.
Crusading Strikes damage increased by 200%.
Auto-attack damage increased by 300% (does not affect Crusading Strikes).
Hammer of Wrath damage has been increased by 30%.
Fixed an issue that caused the Hammer of Wrath tooltip to display wrong damage information.
PRIEST
Hero Talents
Voidweaver
Depth of Shadows has been moved to the Shadow and Discipline talent trees.
Voidwraith has been redesigned – When Entropic Rift ends, it summons a Voidwraith for 5 seconds that casts Void Flay on a nearby enemy.
Discipline
New Talent: Depth of Shadows – Casting Shadow Word: Death has a 25% chance to summon a Shadowfiend for 5 seconds when damaging targets below 20%.
The number of Void Shields allowed active at once is now uncapped (was limited to 3). Void Shield still applies to 3 targets per cast.
Fixed various incorrect interactions between Void Shield and Unfolding Vision.
Atonement heals for 50% of damage done (was 60%).
Mind Blast damage reduced by 30%.
Shadowfiend has been removed.
Shadow
Developers’ notes: With this update, we’re removing Shadowfiend and its variants and replacing them with proc versions to reduce the launch sequence of cooldowns for the specialization. We’re also looking to add DoT extension gameplay to the specialization after the removal of Void Bolt from Voidform. We want this to be something that functions consistently and against multiple targets and felt Shadowy Apparitions were a good source for this effect. We’re also undoing a previous change to have Misery override Shadow Word: Pain which caused issued with solo pulling and having an ability to use on the move. Finally, we’re adding a choice against Misery for low target situations where managing both DoTs can once again be a part of expected gameplay. We expect in high target situations for Misery to be the stronger option, but DoT management is still core to Shadow and low target and spread targets situations are where Shadow Word: Pain management can continue to live.
New Talent: Depth of Shadows – Casting Shadow Word: Death has a 25% chance to summon a Shadowfiend for 5 seconds when damaging targets below 20% or 35% with Deathspeaker talented.
New Talent: Haunting Shadows – Shadowy Apparition damage increased by 10%. When Shadowy Apparitions deal damage, they extend the duration of Shadow Word: Pain and Vampiric Touch on the target by 1.5 seconds.
New Talent: Invoked Nightmare – Increases the damage of Shadow Word: Pain by 150%. Choice talent with Misery.
New Talent: Maddening Tentacles – Tentacle Slam afflicts your target with Shadow Word: Madness at 25% effectiveness.
New Talent: Crushing Void – Tentacle Slam deals 50% increased damage and Void Volley deals 15% increased damage. When Voidform ends, you can cast Void Volley 1 time for free within 30 seconds.
Mindbender has been redesigned – Casting Void Eruption summons a Mindbender to attack your target for 10 seconds.
Mental Decay no longer extends the duration of Shadow Word: Pain and Vampiric Touch when Mind Flay deals damage.
Mental Decay now increases the damage of Mind Flay by 25% (was 15%).
Surge of Insanity now increases the damage of Mind Flay by 15% (was 60%).
Mind Flay and Mind Flay: Insanity damage increased by 40%.
Ancient Madness now causes Shadow Word: Madness to increase the duration of Voidform by 3 seconds (was 2.5 seconds), but its effect now diminishes by 25% per cast of Shadow Word: Madness during Voidform.
Misery no longer replaces Shadow Word: Pain.
Shadowfiend has been removed.
ROGUE
Class
Subtlety
Improved Ambush now has an alternate effect – Shadowstrike damage increased by 8%.
Outlaw
Gravedigger has been updated – When you Dispatch you have a 12% chance per combo point spent to hide a bullet up your sleeve. When you’ve hidden 6 bullets, your next Between the Eyes consumes them and costs no Energy, generates 6 combo points, and immediately resets its own cooldown.
Developers’ notes: We weren’t happy that the previous version of this talent could result in Between the Eyes being highlighted for a significant period while it was on cooldown.
Subtlety
Developers’ notes: The changes for Subtlety in this update look to improve upon initial goals with further adjustments based on gameplay testing and community feedback received thus far. Some previous changes swung the pendulum too far, like overemphasizing Haste as an outlet for cooldown reduction on too many abilities and talent effects, and removing all generic damage bonuses from Shadow Dance. Other changes did not go far enough, like leaving circumstances of unnecessary cooldown overlap between 2 buttons (Symbols of Death and Shadow Dance) when 1 combined button would serve better, and where core abilities (notably Rupture and Shuriken Storm) had muddled mechanical identity. Finally, adjustments to resource pacing and damage profile are baked throughout the total changes with the intent that overall combat flow remains satisfying and damage output feels controlled and potent with the updated lineup of streamlined abilities and shorter-but-more-frequent cooldown pacing. We hope you enjoy Subtlety in the Midnight Beta and look forward to receiving community feedback that will continue informing iterative improvements.
New Passive Ability: Shuriken Storm (Rank 2) – Shuriken Storm deals 100% increased damage while Stealthed. Learned at level 20.
New Talent: Improved Secret Technique – Secret Technique damage increased by 10%.
Dark Brew has been redesigned – Your weapon poisons have 10% increased application chance and now deal Shadow damage instead of Nature. Shadow damage increased by 10%.
Shadow Focus has been redesigned – Abilities deal 10% more damage while Stealth or Shadow Dance is active.
Weaponmaster has been redesigned – Backstab and Shadowstrike have a 15% chance to create a Shadow Clone that repeats the attack for 50% of normal damage as Shadow.
Perforated Veins has been redesigned – Backstab, Gloomblade, Shuriken Storm, and Shadowstrike have 10% increased damage while Find Weakness is active.
Replicating Shadows has been redesigned – Shadow Clones deal 20% increased damage and your effects that have a chance to create them have 5% additional chance to occur.
Shuriken Tornado has been redesigned – Shuriken Storm has a 15% chance to create Shadow Clone that repeats the attack for 50% of normal damage as Shadow.
Death Perception has been redesigned – Find Weakness, Shadow Dance, and Shadow Blades each increase the damage of finishing moves by 10%.
Dark Shadow has been redesigned – Shadow Dance increases all ability damage by 5%/10%, and Shadowstrike and Shuriken Storm damage by an additional 5%/10%.
Secret Technique now learned at level 25 (was a talent).
All ability damage reduced by 17%.
Melee auto-attack damage increased by 82%.
Shadowstrike damage reduced by 10%.
Backstab damage increased by 20%.
Gloomblade damage increased by 20%.
Shuriken Storm damage increased by 50%.
Eviscerate damage increased by 14%.
Black Powder damage increased by 9%.
Secret Technique damage increased by 26%.
Danse Macabre effect damage increased by 110%.
Shadow Dance duration reduced to 6 seconds (was 8 seconds).
Shadow Dance cooldown reduced to 20 seconds (was 30 seconds).
The First Dance bonus to Shadow Dance duration reduced to 3 seconds (was 4 seconds).
Master of Shadows now generates 30 Energy over 3 seconds (was 25 Energy).
Planned Execution effect is now granted by Shadow Dance (was Symbols of Death).
Warning Signs effect is now granted by Shadow Dance (was Symbols of Death).
Deepening Shadows effect increased to 150% but now only benefits from the value of your Haste stat (was 100% of all Haste buffs).
The Rotten effect now triggers when Shadow Dance is activated (was Symbols of Death).
Shadowcraft now increases the frequency of Shadow Techniques by 40% while Shadow Dance is active (was 75%)
Many talents have changed locations and the talent tree structure has been adjusted, including removal of some talent nodes and updated connections.
The following talents have been removed:
Improved Shadow Techniques
Inevitability
Rupture
Swift Death
Symbols of Death
SHAMAN
Enhancement
Auto-attack damage increased by 300%.
WARLOCK
Affliction
Developers’ notes: We have modified the overall structure of Affliction’s talent tree to reduce the friction between selecting a build geared towards single-target damage or multi-target damage. In addition, we are reducing the throughput of Unstable Affliction and removing Soul Miser and Cloying Power to highlight Seed of Corruption as Affliction’s multi-target spender.
New Talent: Potent Soul Shards – Increases damage dealt by Unstable Affliction and Seed of Corruption by 5%.
New Talent: Niskaran Methods – Increases the damage of Agony and Corruption by 10%.
New Talent: Eye Contract – Increases the duration of Summon Darkglare by 5 seconds.
Cull the Weak has been redesigned – Casting Seed of Corruption reduces the cooldown of Dark Harvest by 5 seconds.
Developers’ notes: We are redesigning Cull the Weak to realign with our goal of keeping pooling tied to Darkglare specifically. This effect should underline Dark Harvest as a powerful tool for Affliction in multi-target situations.
Malefic Grasp has been redesigned – While Darkglare is active, your Shadow Bolt/Drain Soul becomes Malefic Grasp.
Malefic Grasp: Binds the target in twilight, dealing Shadow damage over 4 seconds. When Malefic Grasp deals damage, it causes all of your other periodic Affliction damage effects to instantly deal 30% of their normal periodic damage.
Developers’ notes: Malefic Grasp being tied to Nightfall creates moments where you want to pool Unstable Affliction casts. We want to avoid a situation where it is optimal to not press your filler and wait for optimal Unstable Affliction stacks.
Creeping Death has been updated – Your Agony, Corruption, and Unstable Affliction deal damage 10%/20% faster.
Withering Bolt has been updated – Shadow Bolt/Drain Soul deal 5%/10% increased damage, up to 15%/30%, per damage over time effect you have active on the target.
Shadow of Nathreza (Rank 4) has been updated – Damage dealt by Haunt has a chance to release the demonic soul haunting its target. When released, it unleashes a devastating attack before being banished back to Nathreza.
Wrath of Nathreza has been updated – Calls down a meteor formed from the ruins of Nathreza that explodes upon impact, dealing Shadow damage to all enemies within 10 yards. Damage reduced beyond 8 targets.
Unstable Affliction damage reduced by 75%.
The position and pathing of several talents have changed.
The following talents have been removed:
Cloying Power
Hex Weaver
Relinquished
Soul Miser
Demonology
New Talent: Empowered Felstorm – Increases the damage of your Felguard’s Felstorm by 20% and its duration by 4 seconds.
Blighted Maw has been updated – The fangs of your Dreadstalkers are coated in corrosive bile, causing Dreadbite to deal additional damage equal to 50% of damage dealt as Plague damage.
Summon Demonic Tyrant has been updated – Summon a Demonic Tyrant that damages your target for 15 seconds. Damage dealt by Demonic Tyrant is increased by 10% for each Wild Imp and Dreadstalker.
Developers’ notes: Rather than only gaining power from active demons upon his arrival, Demonic Tyrant now will gain power from any demon that is active while he is. This change should make ill-timed Tyrant summons less punishing. In addition, we are decoupling Vilefiend from Tyrant to avoid any future situations where Vilefiend feels like a required talent choice when taking Summon Demonic Tyrant.
Dominion of Argus (Rank 1) now summons a subjugated demon every 2 casts of Hand of Gul’dan (was 3).
Implosion damage increased by 500%.
Aura of Contempt has been removed.
Developers’ notes: We want to avoid situations where you need to micro-manage your Felguard’s Felstorm so we are introducing a new talent to further enhance it to take the place of Aura of Contempt.
WARRIOR
Hero Talents
Slayer
Unrelenting Onslaught now triggers from using Sudden Death (was any Execute).
Arms
All ability damage reduced by 20%.
Auto-attack damage increased by 150%.
Fury
Auto-attack damage increased by 200%.
Apex talent Rampaging Berserker’s final node changed – Rampage extends the duration of Berserk by 1 second (was 2 seconds).
PLAYER VERSUS PLAYER
MONK
Mistweaver
Enveloping Mist’s healing is no longer increased by 50% in PvP combat.
USER INTERFACE AND ACCESSIBILITY
In Edit Mode, added Raid Frame settings for adjusting opacity and debuff icon size.
Raid Frames now have a few layout presets called Templates.
NEW INTERFACE: BOSS WARNINGS
Added a state for queued boss abilities.
Added Icons indicating role (tank, healer, damage), effect type (poison, magic, etc.), and if the spell is deadly.
Several options added: Hide Queued Countdowns, Hide Countdowns for Other Roles, and Spell Support Iconography.
Added a shortcut button in Edit Mode to open Boss Warnings settings.
NEW INTERFACE: DAMAGE METER
Allows players to track combat statistics for themselves and their party members in a variety of categories. It can be enabled under Options > Gameplay Enhancements > Damage Meter.
Once enabled, various settings for the Damage Meter are available in Edit Mode.
Current categories include: Damage Done, DPS, Damage Taken, Healing Done, HPS, Absorbs, Interrupts, Dispels.
Supports tracking within individual combat pulls or across all pulls.
Supports up to 3 windows at a time (primary window can be moved/resized in Edit Mode, secondary windows adjustable outside of Edit Mode).
Right-clicking on an individual entry in the damage meter will open a separate window containing a per-spell breakdown.
Developers’ notes: This feature is still in active development. Various pieces of the UI are still WIP and the combat statistics shown may not be completely accurate in some cases.
UPDATED NAMEPLATES
Nameplates of non-targeted enemies are now darker.
Added a new nameplate style called “Health Focus.” It’s similar to the “Modern” style, but places text above the health bar rather than inside of it.
ADDON INTERFACE
Made improvements to the ability for addons to colorize text strings that contain secret values.
Addons must now be marked by their authors as supporting 12.0.0 in order to be allowed to load in Midnight (to prevent players’ UI breaking due outdated addons).
Made improvements to secret-related Lua error messaging (to make it easier for addons to diagnose issues).
Made performance improvements to the recently updated AbbreviatedNumbers APIs (which allow addons to split up and format secret numbers).

title: Previously Unobtainable AOTC Mount Now Available in Legion Remix
author: POSTED 2025/11/18 AT 2:12 PM BY IMX3
content:
Phase 4 of Legion Remix brings us Antorus, the Burning Throne and the chance to earn an iconic AOTC mount, the Violet Spellwing!
Table of Contents
How to Earn the Violet Spellwing
Mount Model
HOW TO EARN THE VIOLET SPELLWING
The Violet Spellwing was previously a mount earned back in Legion for earning Ahead of the Curve in the Antorus, the Burning Throne raid. Once BfA released, this mount was no longer obtainable, but Legion Remix gives players the chance to earn it once again!

By defeated Argus the Unmaker on Heroic difficulty or higher, players will earn the Blood of the Unmaker which begins the Blood of a Titan quest. To complete this quest, simply return the blood back to Khadgar in Dalaran. Completing this quest will give you the Kirin Tor Summoning Crystal and you're all set! Congrats on your new mount!

MOUNT MODEL
This mount is a beautiful arcane crow that glows with all sorts of blue and purple colors.

title: Mythic Antorus Tips & Tricks: You Might Want a Healer, It's Not Faceroll
author: POSTED 2025/11/18 AT 1:00 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Mythic Antorus is out and with the buffs last night, you're going to want some people that know the mechanics and are geared up.
Table of Contents
Mythic Antorus Tips
Eonar
Imonar
Kin'garoth
Trash - Tarneth
Coven of Shivarra
Aggramar
Argus
Last night, Blizzard buffed Mythic Antorus by 45% health and 30% damage which has increased the challenge of many of the bosses in Antorus. There are some bosses (and even trash) which you will have to do some mechanics and can't completely overgear the content. For groups that are not attempting this at max gear item level with Infinite Power traits, this raid is likely to provide some challenge.

For a full 740 group, each boss lasted 30-40 seconds and the longer you take on these bosses, the more mechanics they'll do. The mechanics on Mythic Antorus actually hurt as well and classes without self-heals or groups without a healer may struggle on certain bosses.
Mythic Antorus Tips
Below, we've noted down some of the things you should be aware of for Mythic Antorus:
Eonar
The Paraxis' raw AoE damage via Paraxis Artillery does about 4M damage every couple seconds to the raid. This adds up very quickly on an encounter where a lot of the time you're sitting around waiting for something to happen. You likely want at least one healer for this boss.
The crystals on the ship can be clicked by one person, but the add on the ship has 2.12B health. You may need to send multiple people for this reason.
Expect to have to complete 2 Final Doom ship phases.
Imonar
The boss drops Sleep Canisters on people which stuns everyone nearby in an AoE every 8 seconds. A healer for dispels or (Item #2179329) scrolls is good here if the players don't spread out.
The boss swaps sides every 20% forcing players to cross sides and casting Conflagration. You must interrupt this to continue dealing damage.
When the boss lands on the other side, he knocks all nearby players off the platform. Don't be in the area where he lands -- be on the bridge if you don't want to swap sides every time.
Kin'garoth
When the boss goes immune, kill an add to end the phase.
Trash - Tarneth
When fighting Tarneth, he casts Flames of Reorigination. This is a DoT that spreads like a plague (literally). The debuff will spread to any nearby targets, infinitely. Until your raid group can spread perfectly until the debuff drops, expect to have this debuff for a long time.
Coven of Shivarra
Make sure to separate the two active bosses.
Aggramar
For Empowered Flame Rend make sure the active tank turns this into the group to split the knockback, or uses a leap ability to stay on the platform.
Argus
You can completely skip the Mythic Phase by DPS'ing him hard enough.

Legion Remix Phase 4: Argus Eternal is now live in North America, featuring more sources of knowledge and new collectables, as we take our fight against the Legion to the Seat of the Triumvirate and Antorus, the Burning Throne!

 Remix Overview
 Artifact Abilities
 Remix Rewards


Once again, there are no new powers or item level increases in this phase, though players will be able to acquire more (Currency #3292), while new activities will give players further opportunity to earn Bronze and (Currency #3268). Finally, a large assortment of new cosmetics and transmog will become available, including Tier 21 armor sets, the Ensemble: Sargerei Commander's Lightbound Regalia, a fel-tinged variant of Felscorned Scythe of the Unmaker, a one-handed version of Taeshalach, and the Violet Spellwing AOTC mount!


The one-handed version is quite a bit smaller, but still no less cool.

The red, blue, and green scythes are also available as staves!


Covering the final season of original Legion content, we have two more weeks before Infinite Echoes, which features featuring some form of catch-up system and brand new content exclusive to Remix.

title: Level a Little Faster in Legion Remix During WoW's 21st Anniversary
author: POSTED 2025/11/18 AT 11:01 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
World of Warcraft's 21st anniversary celebration is here, which means another opportunity to earn several cosmetics, but also an extra 10% experience and reputation buff for Legion Remix characters!



Though the rest of the anniversary event is firmly off-limits to Remix characters, WoW's Anniversary is automatically activate on all characters from now through December 7th. This means they can't complete Celebrate Good Fun!, visit Blackrock Depths, or spend anniversary currencies, but it's still a nice bonus on top of the already greatly increased leveling speed earned by collecting (Currency #3268).

So, if you're still have characters to level, don't forget to take advantage of this small bonus over the new few weeks!
View Original
During the anniversary event, players will automatically receive a 10% bonus to experience and reputation. Players must complete Yelana 's repeatable daily quest, " Celebrating the Good Times! ", to receive an additional 10% bonus.

The anniversary event bonus stacks with other experience and reputation bonuses. During the event, players can enjoy a 75% experience bonus !
Anniversary event grants +20% experience and reputation.
Five Warband Mentors: The War Within: +25% experience bonus for the War for the Earth's Core achievement.
War Mode: +10% XP and Gold
Draught of Ten Lands +10%. This effect applies to characters below level 49 .

title: Blessing of Potency and Zeal Buffs for Delves in Midnight
author: POSTED 2025/11/17 AT 10:59 PM BY IMX3
content:
Midnight is adding some new buffs to Delves that will help make completing your next Delve much easier!
Table of Contents
Blessing of Potency and Zeal
BLESSING OF POTENCY AND ZEAL DELVE BUFFS
These two new buffs are called Blessing of Potency and Blessing of Zeal.

Blessing of Potency
Item Effect
Unlimited range
Instant
Reagents:
Blessing of Potency
In Delves, until you complete your next one, all of your stats are increased by 10%.
 Blessing of Zeal
Item Effect
Unlimited range
Instant
Reagents:
Blessing of Zeal
In Delves, until you complete your next one, your harmful spells and abilities have a chance to cause an explosion on impact inflicting moderate Fire damage split between nearby enemies.


The interesting note here is the phrasing "until you complete your next one", suggesting this is not a permanent buff. Based on datamining, there are a few strings suggesting these buffs will drop as a consumable.
Blessing of Potency  You are now better, faster, stronger, increasing all stats by 10% in the next Delve.In Delves, until you complete your next one, all of your stats are increased by 10%.
Used by Item Blessing of Potency 
Blessing of Zeal  In Delves, until you complete your next one, your harmful spells and abilities have a chance to cause an explosion on impact inflicting moderate Fire damage split between nearby enemies.
Used by Item Blessing of Zeal 
As of right now, there is no confirmation detailing exactly where or what will drop these consumable buffs. There's a few different angles we could see:
Daily / Weekly reward
Vendor Item Available with Delver's Journey.
Random consumable drop from Delve chests/enemies.
Possible catch-up consumable?
Let us know what you think in the comments below!

title: Heroic and Mythic Antorus Buffed Before Phase 4 Launch Tomorrow
author: POSTED 2025/11/17 AT 9:41 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
With some last minute hotfixes, Blizzard has buffed Heroic and Mythic Antorus before the Phase 4 launch tomorrow.

We've datamined some hotfixes that have hit live servers ahead of Antorus the Burning Throne!
Heroic Antorus - Health increased by 10.5%, Damage increased by 15%
Mythic Antorus - Health increased by 45.5%, Damage increased by 30%
These two difficulties for Antorus had the global modifier applied to all encounters. While we don't know the exact health values at this time, it's interesting that Blizzard did this before the release. Could Blizzard be increasing the difficulty of Antorus to account for higher Infinite Power traits?

On release, Mythic Nighthold and Mythic Tomb of Sargeras were steamrolled on release, will Mythic Antorus be the same? We'll have to find out tomorrow.

title: Update: Today's Test of Midnight Pre-Launch Event Rescheduled to Nov. 21st
author: POSTED 2025/11/17 AT 5:20 PM BY JAYDAA
content:
Today's 'Play with the Blues' Midnight Pre-Launch test event has been rescheduled for Friday, Nov. 21st due to unexpected issues with the test realms.
Blizzard
View Original
We will test the Pre-Launch event on Friday, November 21 starting at 11:00 a.m. PST.

See you there!
Blizzard
View Original
Update 11:40 a.m. PST
We’ve decided to cancel this test period for today.

We’re hoping to reschedule it for later this week, and we’ll post here again once we’ve got the timeframe decided.

Thank you for your patience and understanding.
Blizzard
View Original
This Monday, November 17, please join members of the World of Warcraft development team in the Twilight Highlands on the “Midnight Prelaunch Event” realm to playtest the upcoming Midnight pre-launch event, where we’ll learn more about the Twilight’s Blade with the help of Magister Umbric and Grand Magister Rommath.

The experience will begin with the quest “The Cult Within” in Stormwind if you’re Alliance or in Orgrimmar if you’re Horde, and will eventually lead all players to the Twilight Highlands in the Eastern Kingdoms. The event in the Twilight Highlands has a rapid cadence, and should appear in the Event tracker, which you can view by opening your Map and clicking on the Events tab that appears when the map is minimized. (Default Hotkey: M)
Event Details
Date: Monday, November 17
Start: 11:00 PST (14:00 EST, 20:00 CET)
End: 13:00 PST (16:00 EST, 22:00 CET)
Realm: Midnight Prelaunch Event
The realm will become available when the event begins and will remain open after the event ends for a short period of time for those who still want to test out the pre-launch event.
Requirements: Copy a character from the live servers to participate in the test.
Before we begin, please take some time to get familiar with changes made to your class and specialization. There have been some significant changes to talents, hero talents, and core class abilities.

We look forward to playing with you and reading your feedback! See you there, champions!

title: Mythic+ Dungeon Testing Begins November 18th with Large Dungeon Updates
author: POSTED 2025/11/17 AT 1:38 PM BY SQUISHEI
content:
Mythic+ dungeon testing begins tomorrow with large dungeon updates!
Blizzard
View Original
Greetings!

Midnight Season 1 dungeon testing begins on Tuesday, November 18 at 12:00 p.m. PST (3:00 p.m. EDT / 9:00 p.m. CEST) and runs until Tuesday, December 2 at 10:00 a.m. PST (1:00 p.m. EDT / 7:00 p.m. CEST). Please note that this schedule may be adjusted if we encounter any technical issues during testing.

During the test period you’ll be able to acquire and customize Mythic Keystones by talking to the Keystone Vendor in Stormwind, Orgrimmar and Silvermoon. The nearby Dungeon Teleports NPC will assist you with transportation.

Throughout beta, all Midnight dungeons will be available for testing on Normal and Heroic difficulty. Seasonal dungeons will rotate with each new test period.

For this round, the following seasonal dungeons will be open for testing on Heroic, Mythic and Mythic+ difficulty:
Pit of Saron
Skyreach
Seat of the Triumvirate
Algeth’ar Academy
Dungeon Updates
We’ve made a number of updates to bring these returning dungeons in line with modern gameplay expectations while preserving what made them memorable in the first place. Below are some of the highlights:
General
Standard shoot and bolt spells will now attempt to spread their targets more evenly across available players.
Xal’atath’s Bargain affixes have had their tuning adjusted:
Ascendant
Cosmic Ascension’s cast time increased from 6.5 sec to 8 sec.
Voidbound
Dark Prayer’s cast time increased from 15 sec to 18 sec.
Devour
Now also snares the afflicted players, reducing their movement speed by 10%.
Pit of Saron
All creature and boss encounters have been fully reimagined for a modernized experience, including a complete pass on creature spawning.
The dungeon’s captive soldiers are now directly tied into the main objective.
Story scenes have been rebuilt to be shorter and meet modern standards while honoring the original story.
Skyreach
All creatures and boss encounters have been updated.
The wind platform after Rukhran has been updated.
Birds no longer deal damage to players.
The wind current can now be disabled by channeling on Coalescing Winds after crossing the platform.
Seat of the Triumvirate
All creatures and boss encounters have been updated.
All void portals now spawn after Zuraal the Ascended is defeated, and their locations are no longer random.
Story scenes have been rebuilt to be shorter and meet modern standards while honoring the original story.
Algeth’ar Academy
Crawth’s Deafening Screech no longer applies Sonic Vulnerability.
Crawth now begins casting Ruinous Winds at specific health thresholds.
Vexamus no longer gains energy when an Arcane Orb reaches it to prevent unfair overlaps with Arcane Fissure.
Thanks for testing and sharing your thoughts with us! We’ll be reviewing your feedback closely and will continue to post updates throughout the beta as changes roll out.

title: Trader's Gilded Brutosaur Auction House Mount Returns to the WoW Store
author: POSTED 2025/11/17 AT 12:43 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
The Reins of the Trader's Gilded Brutosaur auction house mount has returned to the World of Warcraft in-game store!

 Trader's Gilded Brutosaur Shop Page


Previous datamining suggested this would be the case, and it makes sense that the mount originally sold during WoW's 20th Anniversary would return for the 21st anniversary as well. This sale runs through January 5th, though the mount is otherwise unchanged from last year, featuring the same $90 price tag.


The mount offers access to the Auction House and a mailbox.


Though publicly derided due to the high cost and near-exclusive services provided, the $90 store mount proved to be obscenely popular, speculated to have generated over $15 million in sales over the three months it was originally available.

While the mount has also arrived on the Chinese store bundled with 2,000 Trader's Tender alongside several other promotions, including two customizable mecha-panthers and WoW's first flying vendor mount, the NA/EU version does not offer any extra bonuses. On the other hand, the Chinese price of 888 RMB is also closer to $125 USD, representing a significant mark up over the normal $90 price tag.


The Chinese version comes with 2,000 Trader's Tender, though at an increased price.

title: BlizzCon 2026 Tickets Back On Sale Tuesday, November 18th
author: POSTED 2025/11/17 AT 12:16 PM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
BlizzCon 2026 tickets will be going back on sale at 9am PST, this Tuesday on November 18th!

 BlizzCon 2026






Early bird pricing is officially over, so tickets will now be sold for the full price of $299.99 USD. It's unclear whether Charity Night or After Hours Darkmoon Faire will continue to be available though, as the add-ons previously sold out very quickly.
BlizzCon Pass $299.99
Blizzcon Charity Night (ADD-ON) $499.99
Darkmoon Faire After Hours (ADD-ON) 89.99
The BlizzCon charity night addon includes:
Entry to BlizzCon Charity Night
Reserved seating for Opening Ceremony
Signed Blizzard artwork
Collectible credential
The Darkmoon Faire After Hours:
Entry to Darkmoon Faire After Hours
20 Darkmoon Faire Tokens
Collectible Credential
Entry to BlizzCon Store after show hours
Goodies
Purchasing any ticket will get you some goodies!
BLIZZCON Commemorative Items
Included with purchase of a BlizzCon Pass, these exclusive items for attendees include a retro-style Blizzard metal lunch box with matching thermos, keychain, lanyard, and more.
Activities


BlizzCon 2026 will feature many things:
Opening Ceremony: Major Announcements Revealed, and More
Hands-On Gameplay: Experience the Future
Developer Panels: An In-Depth Look Behind the Scenes
Blizzard Arcade: Challenge Your Friends to Games Past and Present
Community Night: Celebrate Talented Artists from Around the Community
Hearthstone Tarvern
March of the Murlocs
Arena World Championship
Masters Tour World Championship
Portfolio & Resume Reviews
Overwatch World Cup
Mythic Dungeon International
Blizzard Class Cup
Darkmoon Faire
BlizzCon Store
Original Blue Post
Blizzard
View Original
BlizzCon is more than an event: it’s a homecoming. It’s a celebration of the stories we’ve shaped, the worlds forged together, and the friendships and bonds that make this gathering so unforgettable. Whether this is your first time attending BlizzCon or a long-awaited reunion with friends and fellow community members, there’s more time than ever to plan and personalize your experience. We’re thrilled to welcome you to what’s shaping up to be an incredible event. For a limited time, BlizzCon 2026 Passes are launching at a special early bird rate—making it the perfect time to claim your spot.

BlizzCon Passes

Early bird passes go on sale Friday, November 7, 2025, at 9 a.m. PST through Sunday, November 9, 2025, 11:59 p.m. PST, for $249.99 USD each, while supplies last . $249.99 is an all-in price, meaning it includes all taxes and fees. Additionally, we’re raising the maximum number of passes you can purchase from two to four per account.

If you miss the early bird rate, standard rate passes go on sale Tuesday, November 18, 2025, at 9 a.m. PST for the all-in price of $289.99 USD each, while supplies last .

The BlizzCon Pass offers full two-day weekend admission to BlizzCon on both Saturday and Sunday, September 12-13, 2026, including all Halls and Arena, during show hours. Plus, pass holders get in-game goodies* and exclusive BlizzCon commemorative items.

BlizzCon Passes will be sold on Tixr. If you don’t have a Tixr account already, be sure to make one before the early bird rush. Don’t miss out on BlizzCon 2026—get your passes early and lock in the best price before they sell out!

Add-On Experiences

BlizzCon Charity Night

Returning in 2026 is the BlizzCon Charity Night, an exclusive event with Blizzard developers, artists, and community representatives. This year, we’re thrilled to partner with Make-A-Wish® as the beneficiary of the BlizzCon Charity Night. Visit www.wish.org to learn more about Make-A-Wish and their mission to create life-changing wishes for children with critical illnesses.

In addition to entry to this exclusive event, purchase of the BlizzCon Charity Night add-on also includes access to a reserved seating section at the BlizzCon Opening Ceremony, a signed piece of Blizzard artwork, and a collectible credential.

The BlizzCon Charity Night add-on is available for $499.99 USD all-in, while supplies last. Blizzard will donate 100% of the purchase price of the BlizzCon Charity Night add-on to Make-A-Wish less any chargebacks or refunds. The purchase of a BlizzCon Pass is required to purchase this add-on. All BlizzCon Charity Night add-on purchases are final, meaning they cannot be resold. One transfer to another attendee is permitted. This add-on is extremely limited and is expected to sell out quickly during the early bird window.

Darkmoon Faire After Hours

The Darkmoon Faire is setting up stakes at BlizzCon 2026, casting its spell over Level 2 of the North Hall once more. While every attendee may enter its gates during show hours, the Darkmoon Faire After Hours experience offers a late-night treat for those who wish to extend their stay. This optional add-on grants you entry to an exclusive three-hour event Saturday evening, September 12, to both the Darkmoon Faire and the BlizzCon Store. Also included are 20 Faire Tokens and a collectible credential.

The Darkmoon Faire After Hours add-on is available for $89.99 USD all-in, while supplies last. The purchase of a BlizzCon Pass is required to purchase this add-on. All Darkmoon Faire After Hours add-on sales are final, meaning they cannot be resold. One transfer to another attendee is permitted. This add-on is extremely limited in availability and is expected to sell out quickly during the early bird window.

What’s in Store

At BlizzCon 2026, you can expect the return of classic BlizzCon staples alongside some new experiences, including:
Kicking the weekend off is our Opening Ceremony featuring major announcements. Seating for the Main Stage, returning to Hall D, will be on a first-come, first-served basis with plenty of additional spots throughout the convention center to catch all the action. Attendees who have purchased the BlizzCon Charity Night add-on will have access to a reserved seating section at Opening Ceremony.
Hit the show floor for hands-on gameplay from World of Warcraft, Diablo IV, Overwatch 2, Hearthstone, and Diablo Immortal.
The Blizzard Arcade is back! Play games from our past and present like Heroes of the Storm, StarCraft, and other titles with friends and fellow attendees.
For an in-depth look behind the scenes, catch developer panels and other speakers on the Main Stage, as well as on multiple stages across the convention center, including the new Classic Stage in the Blizzard Arcade.
Experience BlizzCon through the eyes of creators from across our communities as they capture the energy, surprises, and stories unfolding throughout the event from the show floor and our newly enhanced media experience center.
Celebrate Community Night with us and the many incredibly talented artists from across the Blizzard community as we host our favorite cosplay contests.
Experience the best in esports with major competition including the Overwatch World Cup, World of Warcraft Arena World Championship, World of Warcraft Mythic Dungeon International, Hearthstone Masters Tour World Championship, and the inaugural Blizzard Classic Cup.
Pick up new gear and souvenirs at the BlizzCon Store. In addition to our click-and-collect ordering system for advance purchases, the BlizzCon Store has been redesigned into a new walkthrough retail shop experience, allowing you to see and interact with the items available like a traditional store. And new to 2026, we’ll also have additional shopping kiosks for select merch around the show floor.
Returning to Level 2 of the North Hall, enter the Darkmoon Faire to snag mystery items, limited edition pins, plushies, and more, or to trade with fellow collectors. To make the most of this bewitching event, consider the Darkmoon Faire After Hours add-on ($89.99 USD).
Gather with your guild or friends old and new at the Hearthstone Tavern. Relax with your buddies, take in an esports match, or catch a panel at the Tavern’s new content stage.
Connect with Blizzard employees and members of our communities at the Inclusion Nexus.
MRRGLRLGLR! Mrggl mrrgl mrgmrlgl March of the Murlocs!
Interested in a career in gaming? Connect with Blizzard hiring teams for personalized portfolio and resume reviews, engaging panels, and exclusive networking opportunities.
Community Night

Community Night is a true BlizzCon tradition! We can't wait to see your master craftsmanship, colossal creations, epic FX, and dynamic duo looks—with the coveted award for Best in Show up for grabs among all category winners!

Full details about Community Night contests, including prizing, will be shared soon. Keep an eye on our social channels for announcements.

Where to Stay

Book your hotel stay on the BlizzCon hotel portal where you can take advantage of discounted rates and preferred availability at hotels near the Anaheim Convention Center. We highly recommend booking early to secure your hotel stay before rooms sell out!

Code of Conduct and Zero Tolerance Policy

At BlizzCon, everyone is part of one big Blizzard community. We ask that everyone at the show be respectful and courteous to their fellow attendees. BlizzCon show management has a zero-tolerance policy for harassment of any kind.

Read our full event policies here.

Accessibility

We are committed to providing an outstanding experience for all attendees at BlizzCon. We strive for access to all activities—to the fullest extent possible—based on an awareness of attendees' needs and appropriate planning. This includes accommodations for aides/personal assistants, American Sign Language and/or open captioning of everything happening on the Main Stage and Classic Stage, service animal accommodations, personal consideration rooms, and priority seating. We ask that all accessibility requests be made by July 10, 2026, so that we can best meet individual needs, by contacting us at blizzcon@blizzard.com. On-site requests will be accommodated to the best of our ability; however, available resources at that time may be limited. For additional information about available services, please visit BlizzCon.com.

Additional Details

You’ll find more information about BlizzCon Passes on Tixr or at BlizzCon.com. Some content at BlizzCon is rated for Mature audiences and is not suitable for children: children under 7 will not be permitted to enter, and anyone under the age of 18 must be accompanied by an adult.

If you are a member of the press or a content creator seeking a media credential, please fill out the Media Request form.

Please note that a BlizzCon Pass, BlizzCon Charity Night add-on, or Darkmoon Faire After Hours add-on can only be transferred to another account once, after which it cannot be transferred again. Transferring a BlizzCon Pass is permanent once claimed by the recipient and will remove the pass from your Tixr account without the option to be recalled.

If you purchase a BlizzCon Pass and your plans happen to change, the public resale market for BlizzCon Passes opens December 1, 2025, on Tixr.** As a reminder, the BlizzCon Charity Night add-on and Darkmoon Faire After Hours add-on cannot be resold.

If you can’t make it to the show in person, the Opening Ceremony and major panels will be live streamed free of charge. Details on how our virtual audience can acquire the BlizzCon in-game goodies will be shared at a later date.

The Countdown to BlizzCon 2026 is on!

We’ll be sharing more event details over the coming months. For the latest news, visit BlizzCon.com and sign up to receive BlizzCon updates. Follow us on social media on X, Instagram, and Facebook.

See you at BlizzCon!
*Battle.net account required. Use of digital goodies requires purchase or download of certain Blizzard games, which are sold/downloaded separately. BlizzCon Pass must be linked to a Battle.net account by no later than September 14, 2026, to receive the in-game goodies. In-game goodies will be delivered directly to the Battle.net account linked to your BlizzCon Pass by no later than September 14, 2026. By linking your pass, you also gain access to the BlizzCon Forums.

**Resale eligibility is limited to residents of the United States, Canada, or the European Union.

title: Customizable Mecha-Panthers and First Flying Vendor Mount Coming in Chinese Exclusive Treasures of Azeroth Event
author: POSTED 2025/11/17 AT 9:45 AM BY ARCHIMTIROS
content:
To celebrate the launch of Titan Reforged servers tomorrow, Chinese players can participate in The Treasures of Azeroth event, offering brand new rewards ranging from customizable mecha-panthers for The War Within and the first flying vendor mount in Classic WoW!


The mecha-panthers are also featured in their own anime trailer!


Like the Blazing Royal Firehawk & Golden Ashes of Al'ar earlier this year, these mounts are earned through another high-cost gatcha-game. Launching on November 18th and 19th, the Treasures of Azeroth event is broken up into two parts, featuring separate prizes for The War Within and Classic Mists of Pandaria.
Table of Contents
Custom Mecha-Panthers
Classic Flying Vendor Mount
All Fun and Gatcha Games
TREASURE TROVE OF AZEROTH - CUSTOM MECHA-PANTHERS IN THE WAR WITHIN
Coming to The War Within are two brand-new customizable flying robot-panther mounts, whose claws tear through the air with agile movements. Themed as a melding of alternate timelines, the Mechanical Prototype Panther MK-0 and Steel Prototype Panther MK-0 are the pinnacle of Steel Horde and Undermine technology, both featuring a variety of visual customizations, which can be freely changed at the Rostrums of Transformation.

Mechanical Prototype Panther MK-0
When Gadetzan citizens pried open Gazlowe Palace's underground lab, caged beasts' roars were suffocating. Undermine tech fused combat modules onto these panthers, granting superhuman strength and speed. Rustbolt engineers reprogrammed them exclusively for warriors campaigning in K'aresh. Remember to swap modules at the Morphing Forum before deployment.


Customizations include the color, goggles, helm, nape, saddle, and shoulders.


Steel Prototype Panther MK-0
The first Steel Horde soldiers crossing the Dark Portal captured a ferocious nightsaber. Blackrock artisans armed it with magma armor, transforming it into a combat weapon. Warriors may customize its parts at the Morphing Forum to suit their fighting style and dominate the battlefield.


Customizations include the helm, nape, saddle, and shoulder.


Obtaining the mounts is a matter of chance though. Both panthers are featured alongside several other rewards in a gatcha-style lottery system, in which players will repeatedly pay to unlock one of the prizes at random.
TITAN REFORGED TREASURE - CLASSIC FLYING VENDOR MOUNT
On Classic Mists of Pandaria and Titan Reforged servers, Chinese players can instead get their hands on Emberbringer, World of Warcraft's first flying vendor mount, a Obsidian Worldbreaker-like dragon flanked by two whelplings offering repair and mailbox services!
Emberbringer is the Worldbreaker-like main mount.
Sparkon is a repair vendor.
Amberia features mailbox services.
Note we haven't yet verified official translations for these names, so they might not be completely accurate.
  
The two faithful whelplings ride along wherever you go!


Once again, earning the mount is a matter of chance, featured in another "treasure chest" of random rewards alongside several other mounts, pets, and toys.
ALL FUN AND GATCHA GAMES
Earning these illustrious new mounts isn't quite as simple as making a purchase though. Instead, players will purchase Treasure Points, which can then be converted into either Azerothian Treasure Coins for TWW or Titan Reforged Treasure Coins for Classic servers. These coins are then spent to draw a random prize.
One RMB (~$0.14 USD) = 1 Treasure Point
1 Treasure Point = 1 Treasure Coin
6 Treasure Coins = 1 random draw
30 Treasure Coins = 10 random draws (once per account)
54 Treasure Coins = 10 random draws (all subsequent draws)
Azerothian Treasure Coins can be spent gambling for The War Within retail server rewards, including the two new customizable mecha-panthers. It's not yet clear exactly how much it will cost to get all prizes, as there will be an element of luck involved, but expected to be somewhere between 1,000-2,000 RMB (~$140-280 USD) for both prize pools.
Mechanical Prototype Panther MK-0
Steel Prototype Panther MK-0
Kaldorei War Wolf
Blackrock Warsaber
Murkablo
Gregarious Grell
Sands of Time*10
Karesh expedition resupply
1 Shard of Azeroth Fragment (exchanged for prize pool items)
2 Shard of Azeroth Fragment
3 Shard of Azeroth Fragment

Randomly awarded treasure Fragment shards allow purchasing specific items outright, as a sort of bad luck protection.


Titan Reforged Treasure Coins can be spent gambling for Classic Mists of Pandaria and Titan Time Reforged rewards, including the Emberbringer flying vendor mount. Although many of these rewards are the same as those offered above, they are not shared between game types and must be obtained separately in each.

title: New "Playstyle Options" Category Required for Posting in Group Finder in Midnight
author: POSTED 2025/11/17 AT 8:17 AM BY IMX3
content:
A new required feature is being added to Group Finder where players must choose a Playstyle for the selected content they are participating in.
Table of Contents
Playstyle Options in Group Finder
Impact of Playstyle Options
Sales Advertisements in Group Finder
PLAYSTYLE OPTIONS IN GROUP FINDER
When hosting a group in the Midnight Beta, there is a new section where players must select a Playstyle option. There are four different options to choose from:
Learning - For those newer to the game or haven't participated in the selected content before.
Relaxed - Just here to have fun? This is the option for you.
Competitive - Looking to push as hard as you can in the selected content.
Carry Offered - Simply put, you will be carried through the content by others who know what they're doing.
HOW WILL THESE AFFECT GROUPS IN MIDNIGHT?
Having Playstyle options be required will have immediate impacts on joining / hosting groups in Midnight.

On a positive note, knowing the expectations of a specific group will help determine if you're a good fit. If you're someone who likes to relax, you know joining "Competitive" groups may not be a wise option. Clearly defining the goal the group has for selected content will generally help with finding players of similar goals and mindsets.

With this being a required option, players who host their own groups can freely set their expectations ahead of time, instead of taking a random dice roll of finding players that fit these expectations.
SALES ADVERTISEMENTS IN GROUP FINDER
A potential drawback with this new feature is the "Carry Offered" option. This can be a slippery slope, especially when dealing with players who host carries in exchange for in-game gold (also known as sales). With the current Group Finder in Retail WoW, there are TONS of sales groups and advertisements that get in the way of looking for other groups.

These advertisements are technically a bannable offense, but no significant action is being taken to remove them from the Group Finder and prevent future advertisements from being posted.


Of these 8 listed groups, 7 are sales advertisements.


Having a "Carry Offered" option almost encourages sales advertisements to be put in the Group Finder, which further amplifies an already pre-existing problem that isn't being handled. For the players who want to help, or for people looking to get some help with content, you may have to scroll through a tons of groups that are just looking to make some gold.
